title: Hybrid CMOS Inverter with 519 V/V Gain Based on Highly Stable LTPS p-TFT and IGZO n-TFT
authors: Peng Dai, Zhou Xu, Xuwei Li, Chaoran Dong, Jinlong Xiang, Yangguang Yuan, Yiming Wang, Jiawei Zhang, Yuxiang Li, Qian Xin, Aimin Song
publish: IEEE Electron Device Letters
abstract: A high-performance CMOS inverter is demonstrated by integrating a p-type low-temperature polycrystalline silicon (LTPS) thin-film transistor (TFT) and an n-type InGaZnO (IGZO) TFT, fabricated using low-temperature processes with a maximum thermal budget of 400°C. The p-TFT exhibits a field-effect mobility (μ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">FE</sub>) of 55.6 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/V·s and a subthreshold swing (SS) of 73.4 mV/dec, while the n-TFT exhibits a μ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">FE</sub> of 17.5 cm²/V·s and an SS of 81.9 mV/dec. Both p- and n-TFTs show excellent bias-stress stability, with threshold voltage shifts below 22 mV after 10,000 s of stress at 125°C. Owing to precise electrical matching between the p- and n-TFTs, the CMOS inverter achieves a record-high voltage gain of 519.1 V/V and noise margins exceeding 47% at a low supply voltage. These results highlight the potential of LTPS/oxide hybrid CMOS for high-performance functional logic in emerging flexible and large-area electronic systems.

title: Performance Enhancement of ISFETs in Standard CMOS Technology Using Anti-Reflection Coating as Ion-Sensitive Layer and FLOTOX Tunnel Devices
authors: Alexander Hofmann, Florian Kögler, Björn Bieske, Uwe Baumann, Peter Kornetzky, Ti-bor Kerekes, Dagmar Kirsten, Eric Schäfer
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: We present the characterization results of ion-sensitive field-effect transistors (ISFETs) fabricated in standard CMOS technology with implemented tunnel structures for bidirectional electron tunneling in terms of sensitivity, drift, signal resolution and trapped charge compensation. The measured CMOS ISFETs exhibit a mean pH sensitivity, drift and signal resolution of 44.7mV/pH, 1.8mV/h and 0.002pH, respectively. To our best knowledge, these are the best performance parameters achieved to date for ISFETs with an ion-sensitive Si3N4 layer fabricated in standard CMOS process without customized modifications. We achieved this performance enhancement by using anti-reflection coating (ARC) as ion-sensitive layer available in standard CMOS technology. This enables the use of our proposed CMOS ISFETs as biosensors in applications with high sensor demands such as molecular diagnostics and DNA sequencing.

title: Trust under Attack: Environment Perturbation-Based Attacks on Trust-Aware Human-Robot Collaboration_supp1-3675732.pdf
authors: Boyuan Du
publish: N/A
abstract: In trust-aware human-robot collaboration (HRC), incorporating human trust into robot decision-making has shown promise in improving collaboration performance. However, most existing research overlooks the potential security vulnerabilities in trust-aware human-robot collaboration methods, which may compromise both collaboration efficiency and human trust. To directly reveal these vulnerabilities and evaluate their impact, this paper proposes an environment perturbation-based trust attack method for human-robot collaboration. The proposed method models the human-robot collaboration process under black-box assumptions and generates adversarial environmental perturbations using the Fast Gradient Sign Method (FGSM). Extensive simulation and real-world experiments show that both traditional and large language model (LLM)-based trust-aware human-robot collaboration methods are vulnerable to such attacks, leading to reductions in collaboration efficiency and human trust. Furthermore, the analysis reveals characteristic phenomena in trust dynamics and system performance under adversarial conditions. These results highlight the importance of considering security vulnerabilities in trust-aware human-robot collaboration.

title: Distributed Online Constrained Convex Optimization With Compressed Communication
authors: Jiaxu Liu, Pengkai Wang, Yixiao Qian, Song Chen, Jinming Xu, Shengze Cai, Chao Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: We consider a distributed online convex optimization problem with inequality constraints, where streaming data is distributed among computing agents over a peer-to-peer network. A key challenge in such settings is the high communication load, which has become a bottleneck for the efficiency of distributed algorithms. To address this issue, we propose a consensus-based primal-dual gradient algorithm with compressed communication (DC-DOPDGD) that employs carefully designed compression operators to significantly reduce the amount of transmitted data, along with an error-compensation technique effectively eliminates compression errors. Both dynamic and static network regret bounds are analyzed for the proposed algorithm, and network constraint violation is used to measure constraint violation. Particularly, we prove that the proposed algorithm achieves a static regret bound of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(T^{\max \lbrace \kappa, 1-\kappa \rbrace })$</tex-math></inline-formula>, while the violation of constraints is bounded by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(\mathit {T}^{1-\kappa /2})$</tex-math></inline-formula> for convex losses, where <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathit {T}$</tex-math></inline-formula> is the time horizon and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\kappa \in (0,1)$</tex-math></inline-formula>, which generalize those of centralized algorithms. For strongly convex objectives, our algorithm achieves a static regret bound of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(\log (\mathit {T}))$</tex-math></inline-formula> and a constraint violation bound of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(\sqrt{\log (\mathit {T})\mathit {T}})$</tex-math></inline-formula>, which also matches that of centralized algorithms and achieves the state of the art in distributed scenarios. Numerical simulations validate our theoretical findings and demonstrate significant reductions in the number of transmitted bits compared to uncompressed distributed counterparts with inequality constraints.

title: A Single-Layer Frequency Reconfigurable Filtering Antenna Utilizing Triple-Mode Resonance for High-Density RF Front-Ends
authors: Kun-Zhi Hu, Maoqin Liao, Dajiang Li, Zhiyuan Chen, Yaqing Yu, Ming-Chun Tang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter presents a frequency reconfigurable filtering antenna (FRFA) with a simple single-layer structure. The FRFA features a square driven patch with two pairs of elongated slots of specific widths and six tuning patches connected via varactors. The slots introduce radiation nulls for filtering performance and reduce the resonant frequencies of the TM20 and TM21 modes, allowing them to combine with the TM01 mode to form an operational band. Continuous frequency tuning is achieved by applying a single set of DC bias voltages to simultaneously control all six varactors, thereby significantly reducing the complexity of the control system. The fabricated FRFA prototype achieves a tuning range of 3.53–4.24 GHz (18.3%), with high peak realized gain (>5.6 dBi), cross-polarization below -25 dB, and radiation efficiency exceeding 81% across the entire tuning range. The integrated filtering performance makes it particularly suitable for high-density integrated RF front-ends that demand dynamic spectrum switching and are sensitive to out-of-band interference, such as customer premises equipment (CPE).

title: Toward Multi-Functional LAWNs With ISAC: Opportunities, Challenges, and the Road Ahead
authors: Jun Wu, Weijie Yuan, Xiaoqi Zhang, Yaohuan Yu, Yuanhao Cui, Fan Liu, Geng Sun, Jiacheng Wang, Dusit Niyato, Dong In Kim
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) has been envisioned as a foundational technology for future low-altitude wireless networks (LAWNs), enabling real-time environmental perception and data exchange across aerial-ground systems. In this article, we first explore the roles of ISAC in LAWNs from both node-level and network-level perspectives. We highlight the performance gains achieved through hierarchical integration and cooperation, wherein key design trade-offs are demonstrated. Apart from physical-layer enhancements, emerging LAWN applications demand broader functionalities. To this end, we propose a multi-functional LAWN framework that extends ISAC with capabilities in control, computation, wireless power transfer, and large language model (LLM)-based intelligence. We further provide a representative case study to present the benefits of ISAC-enabled LAWNs and the promising research directions are finally outlined.

title: A Game of Replay Attack With an Age of Information Perspective
authors: Chiara Foglietta, Valeria Bonagura, Leonardo Badia, Federica Pascucci
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Wireless communication links for real-time monitoring and control are increasingly exposed to subtle yet harmful threats such as replay attacks. Unlike jamming or denial-of-service, replayed packets can pass standard integrity checks and distort freshness of information. This letter analyzes such attacks from an age of information (AoI) perspective, focusing on their impact on wireless update flows and the resulting link layer performance over vulnerable radio channels. We model the transmitter-attacker interaction through a continuous-time Markov chain, capturing how replayed packets alter the AoI evolution. A two-stage game is formulated, in which the transmitter adapts its wireless update rate, while the adversary tunes the replay frequency and staleness to remain stealthy. Closed-form equilibria are derived, yielding design guidelines for adaptive sensing and medium access adaptation in wireless links under stealthy replay. Results highlight how the excess AoI penalty can be interpreted as a virtual delay, and mitigated through rate adaptation at medium-access level.

title: Multilevel Bipolar Pulse-Position Modulation with Level Trimming for Graphene-Based Terahertz Wireless Network-on-Chips
authors: Tae-Ju Lee, Pankaj Singh, Sung-Yoon Jung
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents the modeling of an inter-chip wireless communication channel operating in the terahertz (THz) frequency band and proposes a novel modulation scheme for ultra-high-speed data transmission. Conventional bus-based interconnects and network on chip (NoC) architectures suffer from limitations in scalability and latency,which restrict further improvements in on-chip communication performance. To address these limitations, this study models a THz wireless channel using graphene based nano antennas and introduces a modulation technique that applies level trimming to multi-level bipolar pulse position modulation (ML-BPPM). Simulation results show that the proposed scheme achieves improved bit error rate performance compared to conventional ML-PPM, despite the slight trade-off introduced by level trimming. Moreover, it achieves higher data transmission rates compared to conventional ML-PPM based methods. Furthermore, although reducing the number of available levels leads to degraded communication performance, it enables higher data throughput. This work contributes to the development of efficient modulation techniques for THz-based inter-chip wireless communication systems.

title: Junction Box Wiring Detection Method Based on WSD-YOLO11s
authors: Lei Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Currently, power operation and maintenance as well as metering inspection personnel mainly rely on traditional visual inspection methods to assess the condition of three-phase four-wire energy meter junction boxes. However, this method is prone to misjudging abnormal wiring as normal due to operator distraction or fatigue from prolonged work, which may result in metering deviations, economic losses, and risks to public trust. To address this issue, this paper proposes a YOLO11s-based intelligent detection method for three-phase four-wire energy meter junction boxes. This method adopts a lightweight YOLO11s model combined with a wiring status determination mechanism, so take the first letter of the wiring status determination, and then call it WSD-YOLO11s, enabling accurate identification of the spatial positions of terminal plates and wire labels for each phase and the neutral line (N) within the junction box. By analyzing the positional relationships between terminals and wire numbers, a set of dynamic discrimination rules is established to automatically classify various common wiring faults, including short circuits, miswiring, and loose connections. Experimental results show that the system achieves a mean Average Precision (mAP@0.5) of 97.9 % on a self-constructed three-phase four-wire wiring dataset, representing a 0.8 % improvement over the conventional YOLOv5s model. This approach offers an efficient solution for the intelligent operation and maintenance of electric metering devices, significantly improving power supply reliability while reducing manual inspection costs, and demonstrates strong potential for engineering applications.

title: Machine Learning for Reignition and Restrike of AC Breakers Under Sparse Samples
authors: Bo Zhang, Cong Liao, Siyang Wu, Honglin Lin, Yangliang Zheng, Yueheng Wei
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The key to traditional analysis of reignition and restrike events lies in accurately identifying the zero-crossing point of the switching current. However, this is difficult to find due to factors such as harmonics, zero drift, and the short duration of the zero-crossing point in the waveform. Furthermore, the scarcity of valid samples due to the very low frequency of reignition and restrike events poses significant challenges to the development of other identification algorithms. To address these issues, this paper designs and applies a waveform generation method to generate breaker current waveform samples. This method then investigates a reignition and restrike identification method based on frequency domain features. This method effectively circumvents the difficulty of finding the zero-crossing point in traditional analysis methods and accurately enables analysis and diagnosis of the reignition and restrike phenomena.

title: Design and Verification of Digital Driving System for Vibrating Tube Densimeter with Parallel Dual Straight-Tube
authors: Shao Zhenyou, Tian Junzheng, Hou Lin, Wang Ping
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: To address the online density measurement demands under harsh conditions, a Vibrating Tube Densimeter (VTD) with a parallel dual straight-tube structure is proposed. The parallel dual straight-tube structure adopts a “splitter-parallel-combiner” flow channel design, which minimizes flow resistance to avoid solid deposition and bubble stagnation in cement slurry, while suppressing common-mode interference. The digital driving system integrates comprehensive signal processing algorithms including adaptive lattice notch filter for frequency extraction, zero-crossing detection combined with autocorrelation analysis for amplitude calculation, and Extended Kalman Filter for phase estimation, as well as a dual closed-loop control strategy consisting of adaptive PI amplitude control and phase difference compensation control. Experimental verification was conducted under ambient temperature and pressure with six test media. Results show that the system achieves stable phase difference control with steady-state fluctuation within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.1^{\circ}$</tex>, and amplitude with steady-state error within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.01 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex>. The calibrated densityfrequency model realizes density estimation with absolute error within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.002 ~\mathrm{g} \cdot ~\text{cm}^{-3}$</tex>.

title: Analysis of Absorption Capacity of Distributed Renewable Energy Under Virtual Power Plants
authors: Qikun Feng, Shuai Zhao, Bo Wu, Xian Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: This paper proposes a quantitative evaluation method to address the issue of absorption capacity for the grid-connection of distributed renewable energy (DRE). Firstly, an optimal power flow (OPF) model of a virtual power plant (VPP) incorporating conventional thermal power units, distributed renewable energy, and energy storage systems (ESS) is established. Secondly, considering the instability of distributed renewable energy, multi-period optimal power flow (MOPF) calculation is adopted. The maximum absorption capacity that the system can accommodate is determined based on the convergence of the optimal power flow in each period. Finally, the impact of energy storage devices in virtual power plants (VPPs) on absorption capacity is analyzed. The effectiveness of the proposed method is verified on an improved IEEE 33-bus test case, providing theoretical research support for the energy integration of virtual power plants.

title: Design of Programmable Radio Frequency Multi-Chip Phase Synchronization Circuit
authors: Guanchao Wang, Yong Wang, Xunping Hou, Jialin Zhao, Xiaochen Bai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: This paper introduces a digital circuit for high-precision phase synchronization among multiple RF transceiver chips employing fractional-N PLL-based local oscillator. The design addresses phase randomness and calculation errors by employing two key techniques: calibration of the fractional division ratio to avoid error-prone values, and a stepwise feedback strategy to maintain PLL lock. Simulation results show that the circuit delivers rapid synchronization and high phase accuracy. This performance meets the core requirements for programmable RF arrays.

title: Electron Density Correction in Plasma Time-Sequence Using a Cluster-Based Algorithm
authors: Ziheng Lu, Yuan Yao, Bili Ling, Haiqing Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: Electron density is a key parameter in the diagnostic of nuclear fusion plasma physics. The threshold-based point-by-point cumulative phase unwrapping algorithm is currently widely used. However, when processing large volumes of offline data, the computational efficiency of this algorithm becomes a bottleneck due to its high time complexity inherent in point-wise operations. To address this issue, this paper proposes an optimized algorithm based on a cluster concept. By identifying and integrating continuous phase regions, this method elevates the correction operation from the pixel level to the regional level, significantly reducing the number of data points requiring processing. Consequently, it substantially improves the computational efficiency of the offline inversion of plasma electron density.

title: Harmonic Mitigation Methods Applied to Wind Power Plants
authors: Yun Yu, Yajuan Guan, Sanjay Chaudhary, Juan C. Vasquez, Josep M. Guerrero
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: Harmonic emissions and the associated current flows within wind power plants (WPPs) inevitably lead to heat generation and accelerate the aging of electrical equipment. Especially, in the presence of resonance, harmonic voltage and current may be amplified to detrimental levels. Various harmonic-mitigation techniques have been developed for WPPs to address harmonic distortion. In this paper, these harmonic-mitigation methods are summarized and classified, aiming at presenting a comprehensive survey on harmonic mitigation in WPPs for researchers.

title: A Low Switching Frequency Model Predictive Torque Control for SPMSMs
authors: Xingtian Feng, Yerui Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: Software methods for common mode voltage (CMV) suppression in surface-mounted permanent magnet synchronous motors primarily utilize equivalent zero vectors or avoid the selection of zero vectors. However, both methods result in increased inverter switching frequency. To this end, this paper proposes a model predictive torque control (MPTC) with a low switching frequency. This strategy employs an improved cost function to select the optimal voltage vector and establishes a switch table that applies three adjacent voltage vectors within a single control cycle. The approach reduces the switching frequency while maintaining satisfactory control performance. The transition of voltage vectors between adjacent control cycles often results in the simultaneous switching of two switches. Establishing a conditional domain ensures that the voltage vectors before and after switching are as adjacent as possible, further reducing the inverter switching frequency. Simulation results demonstrate that compared to the MPTC without zero vectors for switch table, the proposed control strategy reduces torque ripple by 41.7% under rated conditions, lowers phase current harmonics to 4.12%, and decreases switching frequency from 6.68 kHz to 5.97 kHz.

title: XDRSN-BiLSTM Based Tool Wear Prediction Method for CNC Milling Machines
authors: Yun Shi, Hui Yi, Ming Lu, Qingao Peng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: Addressing the issues of traditional tool wear prediction methods, this paper proposes a tool wear prediction approach based on an Xception module-enhanced Deep Residual Shrinkage Network (XDRSN) integrated with a Bidirectional Long-Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) network. Firstly, the attention mechanism and soft-thresholding structure within the Deep Residual Shrinkage Network (DRSN) are utilized to perform noise reduction on the input signals. Additionally, the Xception module is introduced to construct the XDRSN, enhancing the model's capability to acquire information and enabling multi-scale feature extraction. Subsequently, two Long-Short-Term Memory (LSTM) units are incorporated to build the BiLSTM network, improving the network's ability to capture both historical and future comprehensive information. Finally, the proposed method is applied to an actual production line in Changzhou, demonstrating excellent performance and validating its efficacy.

title: Enhancing the Dermoscopic Image Clarity through Hair Segmentation and Removal Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Renduchintala Abhiram, CHL.Shyam Reddy, Harshath Raavi, B.Lakshmi Prasanna
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Dermoscopy is a non-invasive imaging method essential for the early detection of skin cancers like melanoma. However, the quality of dermoscopic images is often affected by issues such as hair obstruction, uneven lighting, and low contrast. These problems hinder both clinical evaluation and computer-assisted diagnosis. This paper presents a deep learning framework that improves dermoscopic image clarity and segment lesions. The approach combines traditional image preprocessing with an improved U-Net architecture. First, dermoscopic images from the ISIC archive dataset are resized, normalized, and augmented. Then, they undergo denoising using Gaussian, Median, Bilateral, Non-Local Means, and Wavelet filters. Hair regions are identified using an attention-augmented U-Net featuring residual and multi-scale learning, achieving a Dice coefficient of 0.95 for segmentation. The occluded areas are restored using inpainting methods, with a saliency-driven strategy yielding the most consistent quality. Quantitative evaluation shows excellent performance, with a mean Dice coefficient of 0.93, a Jaccard index of 0.83, structural similarity (SSIM) up to 0.88, and a peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) up to 33 dB; these results outperform existing techniques. The system is implemented in a real-time Streamlit interface that processes an image in 1.5 seconds, making it suitable for clinical use. Both qualitative and quantitative results confirm that the framework effectively improves diagnostic visibility, maintains crucial lesion information, and aids in clinical decision-making. This work shows strong potential for integration into computer-aided diagnosis systems, providing a solid and efficient solution for dermoscopic image analysis in real-world dermatological practice.

title: Leveraging Pre-trained CNN Models for Handwritten Digit Recognition: A Comparison of ANN, ResNet-50 and VGG16
authors: Parveen Dhoundiyal, Rahul Chauhan, Swati Devliyal
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Recognition of hand written digits is one of the early problems faced in machine learning and deep learning era. In this work, the task of recognizing handwritten digits from the MNIST dataset was tackled using three kinds of neural models: a simple Artificial Neural Network (ANN), and two powerful pre-trained Convolutional Neural Network architectures. ResNet-50 and VGG16. While the ANN achieved fairly good performance, it struggled with capturing more subtle or complex handwriting variations. On the other hand, ResNet-50 and VGG16 significantly outperformed ANN: ResNet-50, with its residual (skip) connections, helps the network avoid vanishing gradients and allows deeper layers to improve feature extraction, while VGG16's deep, hierarchical structure captures patterns across many levels of abstraction. Thus, the superior performance of ResNet-50 and VGG16 is primarily due to their capacity to model complicated features and maintain information through many layers.

title: AI-Powered Kidney Health: A Deep Neural Architecture for Chronic Kidney Disease Recognition and Prognosis
authors: Arun Kumar Kandru, S. P. Santhoshkumar, N. Ramajayam, D. Shankar, T. Nalini, Gajalakshmi S
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is a progressive and life-threatening disorder that often culminates in end-stage renal failure, necessitating dialysis or transplantation. Early and accurate identification of CKD is therefore crucial to preventing irreversible outcomes and reducing healthcare burdens. This paper introduces an AI-powered deep neural architecture integrated with advanced ensemble classifiers for CKD recognition and prognosis. By accurately analyzing patient data, artificial intelligence is facilitating the early detection of kidney disease through sophisticated machine learning algorithms. The proposed framework employs feature engineering and deep learning to capture complex, non-linear interactions among clinical variables, while predictive modeling enhances discriminative power. From an initial set of 25 attributes, dimensionality reduction and feature selection strategies identified the most influential subset of parameters, optimizing diagnostic efficiency. Multiple state-of-the-art classifiers, including Deep Neural Networks (DNN), Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), and ensemble models such as XGBoost and LightGBM, were systematically evaluated. Among these, the hybrid deep learning–XGBoost architecture achieved the highest performance, recording 98.3% accuracy, 0.98 precision, 0.98 recall, and 0.98 F1-score. The results highlight that combining deep neural architectures with advanced ensemble methods offers superior predictive capability for CKD detection and prognosis. Furthermore, the framework establishes a scalable paradigm for AI-driven healthcare applications, extending beyond kidney disease toward broader clinical decision support and precision medicine.

title: Precise Time Delay Measurement and Compensation for Tightly Coupled Underwater SINS/piUSBL Navigation
authors: Jin Huang, Yingqiang Wang, Haoda Li, Zichen Liu, Zhikun Wang, Ying Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In multi-sensor systems, time synchronization is particularly challenging for underwater integrated navigation systems incorporating acoustic positioning, where time delays can significantly degrade accuracy when measurement and fusion epochs are misaligned. This paper introduces a tightly coupled navigation framework that integrates a passive inverted ultra-short baseline (piUSBL) acoustic positioning system, a strapdown inertial navigation system (SINS), and a depth gauge under precise time synchronization. The framework fuses piUSBL azimuth and slant range with depth measurements, avoiding poor vertical-angle observability in planar arrays. By combining synchronized timing with acoustic signal processing, the proposed method transforms delay from an unobservable error into a measurable parameter, enabling explicit quantification of both acoustic propagation and system processing delays. Field experiments demonstrate that the proposed approach reduces position RMSE by 44.02% and maximum error by 40.79% compared to the uncompensated baseline, while achieving further RMSE reductions of 37.66% and 35.82% in horizontal directions relative to filter-based delay compensation. The results confirm that explicit delay measurement outperforms filter-based estimation, though instantaneous performance remains sensitive to acoustic signal quality, emphasizing the need for robust signal processing alongside accurate time synchronization in latency-sensitive multi-sensor systems.

title: Optimal Scheduling of Fast Charging Stations and Electric Vehicles via an Uncertainty-Aware Stackelberg Game
authors: Ze Zhou, Ye Chen, Ying Wang, Yutong Xiao, Xingyu Chen, Zhichao Fu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) and fast charging infrastructure (FCSs) have developed at a rapid pace, strengthening the coupling between power distribution networks (PDNs) and transportation networks (TNs). This has created new challenges for cross-domain coordination and sustainable energy management. However, the majority of previous research relies on deterministic assumptions, ignoring the stochastic nature of traffic demand and load, and lacks effective general-purpose algorithms for large-scale optimization problems, which results in poor solution quality and slow convergence in practical applications. This paper suggests an uncertainty-aware Stackelberg game model to address these problems, where the multi-class vehicle users and PDN operator are followers and the FCS aggregator is the leader. To more accurately depict decision-making procedures and system interactions, a multi-scenario stochastic representation is explicitly included. A hybrid surrogate-assisted metaheuristic algorithmic framework is presented to address the intricate bi-level programming problem created by the Stackelberg game. Early on, the radial basis function-based surrogate model is used for quick global exploration, and later on, metaheuristic algorithms like adaptive particle swarm optimization and advanced differential evolution are used for more focused local search. Lastly, case studies validate the efficacy and superiority of the model and algorithm by showing that the suggested approach performs better than pure surrogate or metaheuristic approaches in terms of solution quality, and achieves 0.86%–1.05% higher average profit compared to deterministic approaches while maintaining robust worst-case performance with up to 2.66% improvement in minimum profit.

title: A Multi-Stage Neuro-Symbolic Framework for Trustworthy Agentic Intelligence in ICE Systems
authors: Jiaming Pei, Minxi Feng, Yue Xiu, Yonis Gulzar, Rayan Hamza Alsisi, Minghui Dai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: The rapid evolution of consumer electronics has led to intelligent learning systems that must reason reliably and transparently under dynamic and complex inputs. When abstracted from specific applications, these systems expose fundamental challenges in multi-step reasoning, logical consistency, and interpretability that are not well addressed by purely data-driven models. To tackle these challenges, we propose a Multi-Stage Symbolic Supervised Learning framework that integrates perception, learnable symbolization, and logic-based reasoning within a unified, multi-stage training paradigm. The proposed approach progressively enforces symbolic constraints during supervised learning, improving reasoning robustness and interpretability. Experiments on visual and logical reasoning benchmarks demonstrate that our method consistently outperforms state-of-the-art approaches in both accuracy and logical consistency across increasing reasoning complexity.

title: Legendre polynomial Filtered Microstitching Interferometry for mid-frequency form error of X-ray flat mirrors with sub-nanometer Peak-to-valley accuracy
authors: Zubo Hu, Shuai Xue, Yunfeng Mao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This paper presents a Legendre polynomial filtered microstitching interferometry (LFMSI) method to address the critical metrology gap in measuring mid-frequency (0.2 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> ~ 1 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>) form errors on X-ray flat mirrors with sub-nanometer Peak-to-valley (PV) accuracy. In microstitching using white-light interferometry (WLI), low-frequency measurement errors accumulate severely during least-squares stitching (LSS), preventing sub0-nanometer precision. The proposed LFMSI method solves this by integrating a spectrum-targeted preprocessing step: before stitching, Legendre polynomial filtering is applied to each sub-aperture measurement to actively remove low-order terms, thereby stripping away both the low-frequency measurement errors and the pre-corrected low-frequency surface components. This simple yet effective filtering prevents error propagation in the subsequent LSS, enabling sub-nanometer accuracy solely in the mid-frequency band of interest. Simulations reveal that suppressing errors up to the 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> Legendre order is essential, and filtering must precede stitching to avoid irreversible error coupling. Experimental validation using a certified 180 mm flat with PV 0.67 nm yields an LFMSI-reconstructed profile of PV 0.66 nm, demonstrating excellent agreement with the artifact. The method also exhibits superior repeatability of PV<0.1 nm over 24 hours and a self-consistency accuracy of PV 0.31 nm in the 180° reversal test. LFMSI thus provides a robust, hardware-simple metrology solution for final-stage frequency-partitioned ion beam figuring of advanced X-ray optics.

title: Metaheuristic-Based Optimal Scheduling of the Appliances and EV Charging in Grid-Connected Commercial Building Integrated with HRES and BESS Considering the BESS Discharge
authors: Terapong Boonraksa, Nopprut Saeli, Promphak Boonraksa, Boonruang Marungsri
publish: IEEE Open Access Journal of Power and Energy
abstract: Building energy management has become increasingly important as energy demand continues to grow and the need to reduce operational costs and environmental impacts intensifies. The integration of hybrid renewable energy systems (HRES) and battery energy storage systems (BESS) in grid-connected commercial buildings offers significant potential for improving energy efficiency and cost savings. This paper proposes an optimal appliance and EV charging scheduling strategy based on metaheuristic optimization algorithms to enhance building energy management performance. Simulation results indicate that the Secretary Bird Optimization Algorithm (SBOA) outperforms the other considered techniques. In case study 3, where the BESS is scheduled to discharge during on-peak periods, the building energy cost is reduced to 524.22 USD per month, resulting in a net total cost of 1,124,542.47 USD. Moreover, the optimal scheduling of appliances and EV charging integrated with HRES and BESS achieves the highest energy cost savings, reducing the net total cost by 55.90%. The proposed system attains a payback period of 8.84 years and the levelized cost of energy (LCOE) of 0.0989 USD/kWh, while reducing CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> emissions by 79.84%. Overall, the proposed approach effectively minimizes energy costs, reduces grid dependency, and maximizes renewable energy utilization, demonstrating its economic and environmental benefits.

title: Dual-layer Multi-objective Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm for Partial Destructive Incomplete Disassembly Line Balancing Problem_supp1-3675797.doc
authors: Honggui Han
publish: N/A
abstract: Disassembly lines play a crucial role in the recycling and reuse of end-of-life (EOL) products to promote environmental protection and economic benefits. However, the diverse characteristics of EOL products necessitate the adoption of different disassembly modes, posing challenges to existing models that rely solely on non-destructive or complete disassembly. Thus, a dual-layer multi-objective particle swarm algorithm (DMOPSO) is proposed to solve the partial destructive incomplete disassembly line balancing problem (PDI-DLBP), with optimization of economic profit and energy consumption. Firstly, a tailored encoding-decoding scheme is designed for disassembly sequence, mode selection, and task assignment to obtain high-quality disassembly schedules. Then, a dual-layer learning-based particle updating mechanism is designed, incorporating a constraint-based mode updating operator and a multi-source segment learning strategy to enhance search efficiency and optimization stability. Additionally, a dual-layer local search based on molecular forces is introduced to enhance the exploitation ability. The proposed DMOPSO algorithm is applied to several test cases and a real-world disassembly line of TV. Experiment results show that DMOPSO outperforms state-of-the-art algorithms in solving PDI-DLBP. Specifically, the proposed DMOPSO achieves a 5.7% increase in disassembly profit and a 6.5% reduction in disassembly energy consumption compared to the existing best algorithm for solving the real-world case.

title: Computational Resource Management of Edge Clouds for Vehicle-to-Network Services with Resource Limit
authors: Lei Yang, Baochang Liu, Jinhui Liu, Wangyi Guo, Jiang Wu, Zhanobo Xu, Xiaohong Guan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper studies the online management of the computational resources between multiple edge clouds to minimize the operational cost for a vehicle-to-network (V2N) service provider, subject to stochastic trajectories of vehicles, the quality-of-service (QoS) and the resource limit. Due to the random mobility of vehicles, it poses challenges in real-time migration management when the computational capacity of each edge cloud and the stringent delay requirement of V2N services are both constrained, resulting in strong temporal-spatial coupling of the migration decisions. The problem could even be intractable to solve as the number of vehicles grows. To tackle these issues, we first propose a multi-layered cloud framework to gather and coordinate migration information between vehicles. Then, a multiagent rollout with feasibility construction approach is developed, where the migration decision can be optimized sequentially for each vehicle based on the coordinated migration information from the central cloud. To handle the potentially infeasible solutions caused by the computational resource constraint, we propose a heuristic method to determine the migration priority at each overloading edge cloud, and an integer program is constructed that can be easily solved to produce feasible solutions. The numerical results show the efficiency of the proposed approach in both economical performance and computational complexity compared to the benchmarks.

title: Portable Relative Humidity Sensor Using Agarose-Coated Polymer Optical Fibers
authors: MD Shahadat Hossain, Weiyang Xu, Laraib Unsa Noor, Muhammad Saleh Urf Kumail Haider, Abdul Ghaffar, Zain Ul Abdin, Farooque Ahmed, MD Bipu Arman, Chen Chen
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This work presents a compact smartphone-integrated relative humidity (RH) sensor based on intensity modulation using agarose-coated polymer optical fibers (POFs). The sensing architecture employs a tapered, twisted-coupling configuration of dual POFs to maximize evanescent-field interaction with the surrounding medium. Coating the coupling region with a thin hygroscopic agarose layer imparts substantial humidity-dependent optical loss, enabling sensitive and reversible RH detection. Utilizing the smartphone’s integrated flashlight as the light source and its camera as the photodetector, this system establishes a portable, costeffective, and instrument-free measurement platform. The RH sensor demonstrates a linear response over a broad RH range, 20– 90% RH, with a sensitivity of 0.117 μW/%RH measured using a laboratory power meter and a normalised intensity responsivity of 0.106%/%RH under smartphone readout. The portable RH sensor provides a resolution of approximately 0.12±0.01%RH and maintains excellent repeatability across multiple cycles. These features support practical RH monitoring in settings where conventional optical instrumentation is impractical or unavailable.

title: Meta-Federated Learning for Robust Adaptive Control in Non-Stationary Environments: A Lyapunov-Guided Approach
authors: Vaibhav Sharma, Kamal Raj Pardasani
publish: IEEE Control Systems Letters
abstract: Traditional Federated Learning approaches often fail in control settings due to the instability of the average and lack of rigorous safety guarantees. This letter introduces Lyapunov-Guided Meta-Federated Optimization (LGM-FO), a framework synergizing Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning with Lyapunov control theory to address heterogeneity and non-stationarity in Cyber-Physical Systems. Our framework formulates the problem as meta-learning a robust control initialization integrated with a neural Lyapunov certificate to facilitate rapid adaptation while maintaining verifiable stability guarantees. To mitigate communication overhead, we employ an event-triggered aggregation protocol. Theoretically, we provide convergence proofs for the bilevel optimization, derive dynamic regret bounds of O( √ T) for time-varying environments and establish asymptotic stability under asynchronous delays using the Razumikhin theorem. The framework demonstrates superior robustness and efficiency compared to standard federated baselines.

title: Efficient Silver Usage in Screen-Printed SHJ Cells for the Terawatt Production Era
authors: Sebastian Pingel, Fadi M. Maarouf, Noah Wengenmeyr, Oumaima Mhirsi, Marius Singler, Dirk Eberlein, Thomas Müller, Benjamin Grübel, Jonas De Rose, Florian Clement, Andreas Lorenz
publish: IEEE Journal of Photovoltaics
abstract: Enabling terawatt-scale silicon solar cell manufacturing requires a drastic reduction in silver usage without compromising performance. We demonstrate fine-line screen-printed silicon heterojunction (SHJ) cells using silver (Ag), silver-coated copper (AgCu), and copper (Cu) pastes on industrial precursors, delivering full-cell efficiencies around 23%, comparable to pure-Ag references. Specific Ag consumption drops to 2.1 mg/Wp (AgCu/AgCu) and 1.4 mg/Wp (AgCu/Cu). Fine AgCu grids printed with a nominal 17 μm screen opening on the wafer side achieve high effective Ag utilization of ∼25–30 cm2/Ω·mg. Cells with pure Cu paste metallization on both sides exhibited substantially lower JSC and fill factor (FF). Cells were half-cut and integrated into modules via low-temperature, wire-based soldered interconnection; cutting and module-integration losses were assessed. Under accelerated thermal cycling of modules with cells based on Ag/Ag, AgCu/AgCu, as well as AgCu/Cu metallization, showed ≤2% relative efficiency loss, while Cu/Cu degraded by ∼7%. In module reliability testing, VOC and JSC remained stable, while the degradation was dominated by FF losses due to increased series resistance, partially explained by occasional wire detachment. These results show that SHJ cells with AgCu/AgCu or AgCu/Cu metallization can meet Terawatt (TW)-era Ag constraints and deliver SHJ modules with substantially lower Ag consumption compared to typical tunnel oxide passivated contact (TOPCon) cells and modules, which require ∼7 mg/Wp.

title: A Planning Framework for Power-to-Gas in Multi-Energy Distribution Systems
authors: Thomas Swarts, Johan Morren, Wouter van den Akker, Arjan van Voorden, Han Slootweg
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: Power-to-gas (P2G) systems can provide multifaceted services within Multi-Energy Systems (MES) beyond traditional roles. When strategically deployed, P2G systems can mitigate congestion in the electricity grid, advance the heat transition through the utilization of their waste heat, and optimize the usage of existing gas grids. This research introduces a joint operations and planning model for P2G facilities, including the expansion of electricity, gas, and heat distribution systems. The model is formulated as a mixed integer second order cone program (MISOCP). It incorporates the variable efficiency of electrolyzers and a detailed representation of the electrolyzer waste heat extraction system (EHES), addressing spatial, temporal, and temperature mismatches between heat demand and supply. The model is demonstrated on a 22-node interconnected electricity–gas test network and a 95-node real-world electricity–gas network in the Netherlands. Results reveal tradeoffs in alleviating congestion in the electricity grid, preventing congestion in the gas grid, and maximizing waste heat sales. Optimally distributed P2G reduced congestion in the electricity distribution grid, but induced congestion in the gas distribution grid. Future research should focus on managing such Active Gas Distribution Networks (AGDNs) with distributed P2G and finding a balance between the benefits and challenges of P2G deployment in MES.

title: Optimal PMSM Speed Control under Sensor Faults Using Novel Integral Backstepping Single Neuron PIDD Controller with Filtered Feedforward Compensation
authors: Fadi Alyoussef, Mohammad Irfan Alam, Ashraf Farahat, Ahmad Akrad, Yunes Alqudsi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper proposes a fault-tolerant control strategy for PMSM speed sensors that integrates a novel single-neuron PIDD controller, a backstepping controller, and an extended Kalman filter (EKF). The proposed system parameters are computed using the Zebra Optimization Algorithm (ZOA) to eliminate the need for trial-and-error. The main novelty lies in introducing the first single-neuron implementation of a PIDD structure and embedding it in a cascade with integral backstepping, while utilizing a filtered feedforward branch to enhance transient tracking without compromising closed-loop stability. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed controller outperforms comparable methods in the literature without introducing chattering or oscillations under fault conditions. In addition, the suggested controller exhibits better performance under parameter fluctuations than other reported methods, demonstrating its simplicity and robustness. Finally, two independent real-time evaluations are conducted: (i) hardware-in-the-loop using Arduino and a PMSM model to confirm real-time feasibility, and (ii) a physical test on the Quanser Aero 2 platform to compare the proposed SNPIDD with its closely related counterpart, SNPID. The Aero 2 results show that SNPIDD achieves reductions of more than 10% in overshoot and settling time, providing robust disturbance rejection with smaller control effort, reduced oscillations, and at least 15% improvement in recovery time and maximum deviation compared to SNPID. Thus, SNPIDD enhances closed-loop stability and supports reliable operation in the presence of abrupt sensor outages and disturbances.

title: A Discussion on the Fast Fault Current Injection Capabilities of Grid-Forming Inverters
authors: Wenhao Li, Chuanyue Li
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of inverter-based resources (IBRs) in power systems, grid-forming (GFM) inverters have become crucial for maintaining grid stability due to their ability to support grid voltage and frequency independently. This paper focuses on the fast fault current injection (FFCI) capabilities of GFM inverters, a key requirement for ensuring the correct operation of grid protection systems and facilitating voltage recovery during faults. Firstly, this paper reviews and compares the relevant provisions on fault current injection between the UK grid and China Grid. Next, this paper describes the mechanisms of current injection for voltage support, it is found that at present, many relevant regulations of power grid only consider fault current injection under static conditions. But the requirement of FFCI is to consider the dynamic situation, taking a risk of insufficient voltage support in the dynamic process. Last, the PSCAD simulation results are used to briefly explain the previous conclusion, and at the end of the paper, the expectation for the future improvement of dynamic reactive current injection and grid related rules is put forward.

title: Transient Stability Analysis of a Virtual Synchronous Generator by Time Domain Equal Area Criterion
authors: Yulei Cao, Jinbo Meng, Cunzhi Shan, Jing Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Similar to the synchronous generator, the transient stability analysis of a virtual synchronous generator (VSG) is also of great importance. By solving the acceleration and deceleration areas, the equal area criterion (EAC) can quantitatively analyze the transient stability. However, solving these two areas requires integration with respect to the power angle, while existing models of the VSG are formulated in the time domain, which makes it intractable to apply the EAC when conducting detailed modeling of the VSG. In this paper, a time domain EAC is proposed for the transient stability analysis of the VSG. Based on the proposed method, the calculation of acceleration and deceleration areas is streamlined. Additionally, an iterative algorithm is employed to efficiently compute the critical clearing angle (CCA) and critical clearing time (CCT), which serve as key indices for evaluating transient stability. Further, more dynamic characteristics of the system are captured, and their impacts are analyzed during the transient stability analysis. Finally, case studies are conducted to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Energy-Efficient Adaptive Beamforming for Sustainable 5G Networks Using Machine Learning Optimization
authors: G. Kanagavalli, P. Dhinakar
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Massive MIMO is a key enabler of 5G networks with higher capacity and spectral efficiency. However, conventional beamforming techniques are associated with high computational complexity and high power consumption, which limits their applicability. The primary challenge is the design of adaptive beamforming techniques with energy consumption trade-offs and the ability to sustain high levels of performance. We present an adaptive beamforming technique that is energy-efficient and power-aware, which adapts beam configurations according to real-time channel conditions and user needs. The proposed system applies machine learning-based optimization with hybrid analog-digital beamforming to limit the power consumption without compromising signal integrity. By integrating low-complexity algorithms with smart power management techniques, our technique seeks to reduce energy consumption and maximize spectral efficiency. Simulation results show spectacular improvements in energy efficiency, reduced computational complexity, and enhanced beamforming accuracy compared to conventional techniques. The proposed framework plays a pivotal role in making 5G networks sustainable while preserving peak performance in different wireless environments.

title: AI-Driven Detection and Mitigation of Fake News and Hate Speech in Political Discourse: A Machine Learning Approach
authors: Suman Kumari, Rahul Joshi, Krishna Pandey, Ankita Bharti, Mitali Mohanty, Rhythma Badola
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: This paper explores the design and evaluation of an AI-based framework for detecting and mitigating the spread of fake news and hate speech in political discourse. The problem was framed as a contagion-like process, where misinformation spreads rapidly and extensively through social networks. A reinforcement learning model was implemented to dynamically select between different intervention strategies, such as labeling, downranking, or blocking, aiming to minimize the harmful reach of misinformation while addressing fairness concerns. Simulation results demonstrated that even small delays in detection can lead to a rapid increase in the spread of misinformation, highlighting the importance of low latency. Deploying the AI model at the network edge improved responsiveness, while adversarial robustness training reduced the model’s vulnerability to adversarial attacks. Overall, the proposed approach offers a scalable solution for real-time misinformation containment.

title: Stress Detection in EFL Teachers Using Facial Emotion and Voice Signals with a CNN–GRU Framework
authors: Venkata Kiran Kumar Ravi, B. Sridevi, Nigar Aliyarova Namig, G. Immanuel, S. Ramaprabha, Kumar Raj Chittaranjan Singh
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Teacher stress, especially among English as a Foreign Language (EFL) instructors, negatively impacts instructional quality and student outcomes. Current stress detection techniques frequently depend on singular modalities, constraining precision and applicability. The proposed study presents a hybrid deep learning framework that integrates Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Gated Recurrent Units (GRU) to simultaneously enhance facial emotion identification and vocal stress analysis. Facial expressions and speech samples obtained from Kaggle datasets are subjected to preprocessing, feature extraction, and multimodal fusion, facilitating the acquisition of robust temporal and spatial pattern recognition. In comparison to conventional SVM and single-modality deep models, the CNN–GRU methodology attains much superior accuracy (95.8%), precision (96.1%), recall (94.7%), and F1-score (95.4%), in addition to an AUC-ROC of 0.981. This improved performance illustrates the model's exceptional capacity to identify subtle stress indicators in practical environments. The integrated system offers practical applications for automated, non-invasive stress monitoring techniques in educational settings, enhancing instructor well-being and student success. The suggested study enhances multimodal emotion recognition research by bridging the gap in EFL teacher stress detection with a complete and scalable method.

title: Deep Learning-Based Handwritten Digit Classification Using MNIST Dataset with Custom Video Player Integration for Enhanced Educational Gaming Applications
authors: Mala K, Lithinraj P, Abdul Rahaman, Yashas K S, Sridhar Gowda K, Girish S B
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: This research presents a comprehensive approach to handwritten digit recognition using deep learning techniques integrated with custom multimedia interfaces for educational gaming applications. The study implements a feedforward neural network architecture on the MNIST dataset, achieving robust classification performance through systematic methodology. The proposed system utilizes a flatten layer followed by dense, fully connected layers with ReLU and softmax activation functions, trained using the Adam optimizer with categorical cross-entropy loss over five epochs. The integration of a custom video player interface enhances user interaction and provides real-time visualization capabilities for educational purposes. Performance evaluation demonstrates superior accuracy through confusion matrix analysis and training/validation curve visualization. The model achieves significant classification accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency suitable for real-time applications. The custom video player component, developed using HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript, provides responsive multimedia control with cross-browser compatibility. This integrated approach bridges the gap between deep learning model deployment and interactive educational gaming environments, offering practical applications in digital learning platforms. The methodology encompasses data preprocessing, model architecture design, training optimization, and performance visualization, providing a complete pipeline for educational technology implementation.

title: A Hybrid CNN-ViT COVID-19 Detection Framework with Self-Supervised Learning and Explainability for Chest CT Images
authors: Sumit Kushwaha
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Timely and accurate diagnosis of COVID-19 is still an important need in clinical and setting such as those for remote healthcare especially with the existence of new variants COVID syndrome. Though RT-PCR is the reference diagnostic test, it is delayed, has the potential to give a false-negative result and is not widely available, particularly in resource-poor areas. CT has become a useful adjuvant method due to its capacity to detect pulmonary abnormalities seen in COVID-19 such as GGO and consolidations. In this paper, we present a new deep learning-based approach combining Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Vision Transformers (ViTs) for COVID19 infection identification via CT scans. The framework is trained in a two-stage manner: (1) self-supervised contrastive learning on unsupervised CT data to learn robust and generalizable features; and (2) supervised fine-tuning on labeled COVID-19 cases. For the interpretation of COVID-19 predictions, Grad-CAM++ utilized to produce visual justifications. The framework was tested on a public CT dataset by 20-fold cross-validation and outperformed several state-of-the-art frameworks described in the literature with an accuracy and recall of 94.6% and 95.1%, respecti59vely. The results show that the discussed hybrid architecture provides a more favorable trade-off precision-robustness-explainability than the other techniques.

title: Ensemble Deep Learning Approaches for Brain Hemorrhage Detection from CT Images
authors: Musku Reethika, Thorpunuri Akhila, Pochammala Rashmitha, Bhoomeshwar Bala, Arathi Chitla, Raja Shekar Kadurka
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Brain hemorrhage is a critical neurological condition that can quickly become life-threatening if not diagnosed and treated in time. Rapid and accurate detection is therefore essential to improving patient survival and recovery outcomes. Computed Tomography (CT) imaging is the standard diagnostic tool for identifying hemorrhages due to its wide availability and effectiveness. However, manual interpretation of CT scans is often time-consuming, requires specialized expertise, and is vulnerable to human error, especially in emergency scenarios. To address these limitations, this study proposes an automated detection framework built on an ensemble of deep learning models. The system integrates multiple Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architectures, namely Inception, ResNet, and EfficientNet, while incorporating advanced preprocessing, data augmentation, and feature extraction techniques to enhance classification performance. By employing weighted voting and stacking strategies, the ensemble leverages the complementary strengths of individual models and minimizes their weaknesses, thereby improving accuracy and generalization. Experimental evaluation was conducted on publicly available CT datasets annotated by medical experts, demonstrating that the proposed model achieved a test accuracy of 94.1%, precision of 0.96, and recall of 0.93. These results significantly outperform conventional single-model CNNs and traditional machine learning approaches. In addition, the system processes each image in an average of 1.2 seconds, underscoring its potential for near real-time clinical application. Overall, the research highlights the value of deep learning ensembles in supporting radiologists by reducing diagnostic time and increasing reliability. Future work will focus on expanding the dataset, improving interpretability through explainable AI, and integrating the system into routine clinical workflows.

title: AI-Augmented Quality Assurance for Telecom and Network Management Applications
authors: Srikanth Singireddy, Abinaya Mettupatti Sivagnanam
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Telecom and network management applications are critical enterprise systems that handle billing, provisioning, expense management, and large-scale service delivery. Ensuring their quality and reliability remains a challenge due to the scale of data, complexity of business rules, and evolving network technologies such as 5G and SDN. This paper proposes an AI-augmented framework for software quality assurance (QA) that takes advantage of machine learning (ML) and natural language processing (NLP) to automate defect detection, anomaly analysis, and test generation. Experiments in telecom expense management (TEM) and network operations platforms demonstrate that AI-augmented QA improves defect detection rates by 32%, reduces regression cycle time by 45%, and provides explainable insights for developers and QA teams. The findings indicate that integrating AI into QA pipelines enables more resilient, scalable, and adaptive telecom systems, positioning it as a key enabler for next-generation communication infrastructure.

title: Reasoning-Enhanced GraphRAG-based Large and Small AI Model Collaboration: A Unified 6G Network Management Approach
authors: Jiaqi Yu, Lei Feng, Shiyi Gu, Yu Zhou, Celimuge Wu, Shaoyong Guo
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: With the rapid development of network technology, the synergistic collaboration between large AI models (LAMs) and specialized small AI models (SAMs) offers a new paradigm for intelligent network management. Empowered by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) and graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (GraphRAG) techniques, we propose a reasoning-enhanced GraphRAG-based LAM-SAM collaboration framework integrating high-level reasoning with domain-specific deployability. By leveraging structured knowledge graphs, the framework enhances decision-making accuracy and effectively mitigates hallucinations during inference. To enable intelligent coordination, we further implement policy alignment via Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence between LAM and SAM policies. Comprehensive case studies demonstrate the framework's effectiveness in addressing complex network challenges. The framework improves the throughput by approximately 12% in coverage optimization. Moreover, the proposed framework enhances base station health by over 8% in network operations and maintenance without additional cost overhead. Finally, we outline promising future research directions for practical deployment.
