title: Topology and Control of Series-Shunt Enhanced Soft Normally Open Point Integrated with PV and ES for Medium-Voltage Direct-Linked Grid Connection
authors: Jingyue Wang, Di Zhu, Chuang Liu, Jianwen Zhang, Yu Jiang, Heyang Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The enhanced soft normally-open point (e-SNOP) regulates the power flow of medium-voltage ac (MVAC) feeders, while providing low-voltage dc (LVDC) ports for the flexible integration of distributed energy resources. However, its implementation typically requires a large number of power converters. To address this issue, this paper proposes a series- shunt e-SNOP topology integrated with photovoltaic (PV) and energy storage. The shunt unit of this topology offers multiple LVDC ports, enabling the direct connection of distributed PV generation and energy storage, while the series unit features expendable MVAC feeder interfaces to support flexible power flow regulation. Meanwhile, this topology adopts a single-stage dc-ac submodule design, reducing capacitors and simplifying control strategy complexity. First, this paper introduces the topological configuration and its submodule's working principles. Then, its average circuit model is established. Additionally, the three operation modes and their corresponding control strategies are designed. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed control strategies is verified through a 10-kV/3-MW full power simulation platform, and a 1-kW scaled-down experimental prototype is built to further demonstrate the correctness of the proposed topology.

title: A Survey on Bundle Recommendation: Methods, Applications, and Challenges
authors: Meng Sun, Lin Li, Ming Li, Xiaohui Tao, Dong Zhang, Qing Xie, Peipei Wang, Jimmy Xiangji Huang
publish: ACM Computing Surveys
abstract: None

title: Interpretable ANFIS Framework for Performance Evaluation and Managerial Rule Extraction in Dynamic Supply Chains
authors: Mohamed A. El-Baz, Sama M. A. El-Baz
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Modern supply chains operate in dynamic, multi-stage environments where disruptions in procurement, storage, production, inspection, and assembly propagate across stages, causing cumulative inefficiencies. This paper introduces an interpretable Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) framework designed to enable transparent and adaptive performance governance rather than static optimization. The framework integrates two decoupled fuzzy layers: FIS-1 performs stage-level diagnosis through compact ANFIS models that translate operational signals into tactical rules, while FIS-2 aggregates product-level outcomes into a Strategic Performance Index (SPI) for managerial oversight. The framework is evaluated within an ERP-inspired operational setting that represents a realistic industrial workflow across multiple supply-chain stages. In contrast to conventional neuro-fuzzy approaches, the proposed framework explicitly establishes an interpretable linkage between stage-level performance diagnosis and product-level evaluation within a unified and governance-oriented structure. The results indicate that the framework exhibits adaptive and interpretable behavior in response to operational disturbances, supporting dynamic decision-making across iterative operational cycles. Operationalized within a closed-loop, iterative governance cycle (detect-dispatch-measure-adapt), the framework was evaluated over a 30-day simulated horizon. The rules-enabled dynamic regime demonstrated sustained, cycle-driven performance improvements, including a rolling average 10.3% reduction in total delay, a 1.4 percentage-point containment in cumulative cost deviation, and a 1.85% gain in throughput relative to a static, monitor-only baseline (Section IV). The core methodological contribution is the decoupled, interpretable governance structure that explicitly links stage-level operational diagnostics to product-level strategic decision support within ERP/MES environments. This provides a transparent and auditable pathway for converting AI-generated performance signals into actionable managerial interventions.

title: Identifying Critical Nodes in Smart Grid IoT Infrastructure: A Graph Convolutional Network Approach
authors: Yulei Huang, Yan Li, Tianqi Xu, Mengmeng Zhu, Zhaolei He, Quancong Zhu
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The modern smart grid, a mission-critical Cyber-Physical Power System (CPPS), relies on a vast Internet of Things (IoT) comprised of interconnected sensor terminals, such as smart meters and Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs). Ensuring the resilience of this infrastructure by identifying its most critical nodes is of utmost importance to prevent cascading failures. To address this challenge, this paper proposes the Graph Convolutional Network with Integrated Deformable Depthwise Attention (GCN-IDDA), a novel framework based on graph convolutional networks. The model is specifically designed to fuse heterogeneous, multimodal data streams that span physical-layer readings collected from these sensor terminals, network-layer communication metrics, and the overall IoT network topology. To strictly evaluate performance in realistic scenarios, we employ the SimBench dataset to construct grid topologies and the Susceptible-Infected-Recovered (SIR) model to simulate dynamic threat propagation. Extensive experiments on four distinct smart grid IoT network testbeds show that GCN-IDDA significantly outperforms traditional centrality measures and baseline machine learning methods in identification accuracy. This research not only delivers an effective tool for smart grid vulnerability analysis but also provides a scalable, data-driven paradigm for enhancing the security of other large-scale, mission-critical sensor networks.

title: Semi-empirical Modeling of Hot Carrier Degradation in 12 nm FinFET LDMOS devices
authors: Amit Sarkar, P. Srinivasan, Abhisek Dixit
publish: IEEE Transactions on Device and Materials Reliability
abstract: This paper presents a Semi-empirical Reliability Model for Hot-Carrier Degradation (HCD) in FinFET LDMOS devices. Unlike planar LDMOS, FinFET LDMOS exhibits non-saturating HCD over prolonged operation, due to its distinct three-dimensional geometry, which affects electric field distribution and carrier transport mechanisms. Traditional degradation models, such as the time slope exponent and bond dissociation (BD) approaches do not adequately capture this unique degradation due to the 3D structural complexities of FinFET LDMOS. To address this limitation, an enhanced model to account for HCD in FinFET LDMOS is introduced in this paper, significantly improving predictive accuracy for device lifetime. Hot-carrier Degradation for FinFET LDMOS is also characterized with variation in drain to source stress bias and number of fins (NFIN). When all other design parameters remain same, HCD increases with an increase in the drain-to-source stress voltage and reduces with an increase in the number of fins (NFIN).

title: Speak and Be Known: Authenticating Users via Ear Canal Deformation on Earbuds_supp1-3665282.pdf
authors: Shan Chang
publish: N/A
abstract: With the increasing popularity of smart wearable devices, such as earbuds and smart watches, presents new challenges for seamless and secure user authentication due to their limited user interfaces. Conventional biometric methods, including voice, fingerprints, and facial recognition often face issues such as usability limitations, interference from noise, or vulnerability to spoofing attacks. This paper introduces a novel authentication system called BaroAuth, which utilizes the stable and unique Speech-aware Pressure Sequences (SPSs) patterns captured by a miniaturized MEMS barometer embedded in earbuds. The design of BaroAuth hinges on two important observations. First, the production of speech relies on the coordinated movements of articulatory organs, including the tongue, jaw, and soft palate. These organs, through the activity of the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), alter the shape of the ear canal, thereby causing subtle pressure changes that encode the speaker's unique physiological characteristics and articulatory dynamics. Second, SPSs show significant intra-individual consistency and considerable inter-individual variability, which barometers can effectively measure. Meanwhile, we develop the BaroAuth prototype and carry out comprehensive experiments based on it. The experimental findings reveal that BaroAuth demonstrates a mean false-acceptance rate (FAR) of 0.41% and a false-rejection rate (FRR) of 1.23%, respectively, even under complex attack scenarios.

title: Synthesis of Acoustic Wave Ladder Filters With Flexible Transmission Zeros in Bandpass Domain Using Out-of-Band Resonators
authors: Dan Liang, Ziyang Luo, Beizun Liu, Yi Zeng, Dejin Zhu, Yangyang Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: In this article, out-of-band (OoB) Butterworth–Van Dyke (BVD) resonators are introduced to break the rigid transmission zero (TZ) allocation in acoustic wave (AW) ladder filters, thus satisfying asymmetric and diverse specifications. To avoid distortions in frequency mapping, the synthesis process directly proceeds in the bandpass domain. Hence, to mitigate the severe accuracy problem in bandpass synthesis, the modified Remez-like iterations for filter functions and enhanced circuit extraction strategies are elucidated first. The employed OoB BVD resonators are classified into far- and near-band BVDs. The far-band BVDs are viewed as capacitances in synthesis to form the quasi-ladder circuit, thus breaking the even TZ distribution rule in the conventional ladder configuration. The near-band BVDs are accommodated in synthesis by introducing OoB complex reflection zeros (CRZs). They can provide TZs far from the passband while maintaining the uniform electromechanical coupling coefficient (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${k_{eff}}^{2}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>). Especially, unlike conventional in-band BVDs, near-band BVDs can flexibly generate TZs in either upper or lower stopbands, regardless of whether they are series- or shunt-connected. To provide more guidelines, the semi-analytical synthesis procedures, comprehensive topology assessments, and parameter studies of ladder filters with OoB BVDs are detailed. Multiple examples, including two fourth-order surface AW (SAW) filters and a Band 3 SAW diplexer, are synthesized and fabricated to validate the proposed theories.

title: Automated spatiotemporal response identification and separation for averaged and single-trial EEG and MEG data
authors: Oskari Ahola, Lisa Haxel, Ulf Ziemann
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Objective: Conventional analysis approaches of evoked EEG and MEG typically rely on assumptions of independence or uncorrelatedness, fixed temporal windows, and predefined regions of interest to extract neural responses. However, cortical activity is spatially and temporally overlapping and interactive, meaning that independence or uncorrelatedness cannot be assumed. As a result, these methods often fail to account for overlapping cortical activity and individual variability, limiting the accuracy and interpretability of the results. To enhance the validity of research and clinical inferences, we aimed to reliably isolate spatiotemporally localized neural evoked responses. Methods: We developed Spatiotemporal Event Response ENcoding (”SEREN”), an algorithm that automatically identifies spatiotemporally localized evoked response components by leveraging the spatiotemporal density properties of post-synaptic currents using Gaussian kernels in time and space. SEREN can extract individual evoked response components for both averaged and single-trial data and operates in both sensor and source space. Results: We demonstrate SEREN's effectiveness on auditory and visual-evoked MEG data as well as on simulated datasets. Additionally, we show that SEREN can be calibrated for robust single trial monitoring in noisy EEG systems using transcranial magnetic stimulation-evoked potentials, including simulated in real-time applications. Conclusion: SEREN reliably isolates cortical evoked responses, overcoming limitations of conventional analysis approaches that do not account for inter-response overlaps or individualization. Significance: By improving the precision of neural response extraction, SEREN provides a powerful tool for advancing the analysis of neural dynamics and improving the validity of research and clinical applications.

title: Hybrid SVM–CNN Framework for Obstacle Classification in Smart Driving Assistance Systems for Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Geetha M. N, Tamilarasi Rajamani, Rajavali Guntur, G. B. N. Jyothi, S. Kaliappan, Manoj Senthil K
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Accurate obstacle classification is a necessity in autonomous vehicles and intelligent driver aid systems. Current deep learning models, although proficient in feature extraction, frequently demonstrates difficulties in establishing accurate decision boundaries, particularly in settings characterised by varied object kinds and detailed backgrounds. The proposed article offers a Hybrid SVM–CNN Framework that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for deep feature extraction with Support Vector Machines (SVM) for high-margin classification to tackle this difficulty. The system is analysed using documented urban road problem datasets obtained from Kaggle, which include automobiles, pedestrians, and road obstacles. The CNN derives layered visual characteristics, which are subsequently transmitted to the SVM for conclusive categorisation. The comparative analysis with baseline models VGG16, ResNet50, and YOLOv5 demonstrates the suggested method's higher performance. The hybrid model attains an accuracy of 98.47%, precision of 97.92%, recall of 98.61%, F1-score of 98.26%, and AUC-ROC of 0.992. These findings confirm the system's reliability and feasibility for real-time application in self-navigating contexts. The suggested approach limits misclassification and offers a scalable basis for future advancements in smart transportation systems.

title: Multi-Source Data Fusion-Based Pretraining Method for Power Equipment Time-Series-Text Multimodal Large Models
authors: Xiya Li, Peng Gao, Renyu Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: This study proposes a multi-source data fusion-based pretraining method for power equipment time-series-text multimodal large models, aimed at improving the accuracy of power equipment health monitoring and fault diagnosis. By combining time-series sensor data with text data, a fusion strategy based on cross-modal attention mechanisms is introduced, allowing for deep interaction between time-series data and text data at the feature level. The pretraining method includes Masked Sensor Prediction (MSP) tasks and Time-Series-Text Contrastive Learning (TTCL) tasks, enhancing the model's ability to jointly represent time-series data and textual information through a multi-stage training strategy. Experimental results show that the proposed method significantly improves performance in power equipment monitoring tasks, especially in the Dissolved Gas Analysis (DGA) fault classification task for transformers, where the accuracy reached 96.2%, a 3.5% improvement over the baseline model MCBERT (92.7%). In the equipment health state assessment task, accuracy increased to 94.8%, a 3.5% improvement over TimesNet (91.3%). Additionally, ablation experiments further demonstrate the critical role of the cross-modal attention mechanism, GNN module, and TTCL task in performance improvement. Model interpretability analysis also verifies the transparency and reliability of the model's decision-making process. In summary, the multimodal fusion framework proposed in this study offers new insights and technical support for intelligent operation and maintenance, as well as fault early warning in power equipment.

title: Oral Cancer Lesion Detection in Clinical Images Using YOLOv9-Based Deep Learning Pipeline With Real-Time Augmentation
authors: Kavitha A, Nandan S, Nagaraj V K
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Oral cancer is one of the greatest health issues globally. It occurs mostly at elevated levels because there are no definite early detection procedures. In this thesis, a new deep learning platform of real-time and precise identification of lesions in the mouth in clinical images, with the help of the YOLOv9 object detector system. The existing system uses high-intraoral resolution images and real-time data includes augmentation methods to improve the stability of the model when using different clinical presentations, such as change in lighting, morphology of lesion and angle of the image. The model is very precise and recallable with the help of dynamic label assignment of YOLOv9, attention mechanism, and optimized backbone which provide the possibility of real-time inference. The model is trained and tested using a well selected set of labeled lesion images. Quantitative measures of performance, e.g., mean Average F1-score, mAP, and Intersection-over-Union (IoU) are used. Besides the detection, a lesion module and estimation of area are also provided in the pipeline to aid in clinical evaluation of the severity of the lesions and to plan the treatment. It is compatible with edge devices, therefore, suitable in low-resource or rural settings and this is a good indicator on-site screening. The explanation of AI techniques, including Grad-CAM, that enable the visualization of the focus areas, enhances cognition and confidence in clinical settings. There are continuing learning processes that are applied to improve performance when new information about patients is present. This YOLOv9 deep neural network can be a useful addition to the computer-assisted or oral cancer screening that will result in earlier cancer identification, reduce the time wasted and improve patient outcomes in clinical and community health.

title: A Unified Deep Learning Approach for Multi-Mission Spacecraft Anomaly Detection Using Hybrid CNN-LSTM-Transformer Architecture with Multi-Task Learning
authors: Rabinesh Aravind C R, E. Ahila Devi, S. Lakshmi
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: During space exploration activities, identifying abnormalities in spacecraft systems is essential to guaranteeing mission success and averting expensive mission failures. In order to detect anomalies in spacecraft telemetry streams, this research presents a novel deep learning system that cleverly blends CNN-LSTM-Transformer architectures. Our approach concurrently performs several analytical tasks, such as anomaly categorization, binary detection, severity rating, and predictive forecasting, while processing temporal data from NASA's SMAP (Soil Moisture Active Passive) and MSL (Mars Science Laboratory) missions. On SMAP datasets, the created unified model exhibits outstanding performance accomplishments with 98.0% precision and 97.6% total accuracy. Using an interactive Streamlit web interface, we effectively develop this solution, allowing for instant telemetry processing with advanced visualization capabilities appropriate for operational deployment across a range of space missions.

title: A Parallel Current Sharing Scheme for SiC MOSFETs Controlling Gate Drive Current
authors: Kunbing Shen, Wentao Xu, Jiabin Li, Song Zhang, Quanjiang Wang, Leting Shi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: Paralleling SiC MOSFETs is an effective way to increase current and power rating. Current sharing in parallel operation is an important goal for improving system stability and reliability. However, parasitic parameters and other factors can cause current imbalance when SIC MOSFETs are connected in parallel. If there is severe current imbalance among the paralleled SiC MOSFETs, it may reduce their lifespan or even cause damage, thereby compromising system safety and reliability. This paper proposes an active feedback loop to control the gate current of SiC MOSFETs to achieve dynamic current sharing. The effectiveness of this method is verified through simulation.

title: Corrections to “Dynamic Trajectory Planning for a Group of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles in Unknown Environments”
authors: Fan Yang, Qiang Lu, Botao Zhang, Na Huang, Youngjin Choi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: above article [1], Fig. 21(b) (Flight Controller) contains a description error. In the original figure, the flight controller was described as related to MAVROS. However, the UAV platform used in this study is FanciSwarm, and its flight controller is Mcontroller, which is independently developed by Fancinnov. The flight controller does not directly communicate with ROS through MAVROS; instead, data exchange with ROS is achieved through a self-developed flight controller interface provided by Fancinnov. To avoid confusion and improve reproducibility, the flight controller in Fig. 21(b) has been revised and labeled as Mcontroller-V7, and the communication mechanism with ROS has been clarified accordingly. This correction is intended only to improve the accuracy of the UAV hardware architecture description and does not affect the experimental results, analysis, or conclusions reported in the paper.Fig. 21.Real-world environment. The maximum velocity of each UAV is set as $1.5m/s$ . A remote control car equipped with UWB is used as the dynamic target.

title: Advancing Explainable AI in Deep Reinforcement Learning for Fraud Detection in Financial Systems
authors: Anju Das, Kshamta Chauhan, Parvathy Thampi M S., Perumalraja Rengaraju, M E C Vidya Sagar, Sumit Mohanty
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The paper presents a new model for a fraudulent activity detector in financial systems that can be seen as a contribution to the current state of the art in Explainable AI/Deep Reinforcement Learning frameworks. In the proposed model, the state-of-the-art XAI methods, such as SHAP and LIME, are implemented so that the decision-making process of the DRL agent can be transparent and understandable, which is timely considering both the necessity to detect fraud accurately and comply with the regulations. So that the model can be effective and highly precise, the model is constructed using past information and revised in real-time to the new tendencies of fraud, taking into consideration the intention of imitating the complexity of financial transactions to make the model sound and right. The use of confidence ranks the transactions that bring about suspicion of fraud and creates the image of the probability of fraud. Conversely, the trade-offs between a high rate of False Positives and detecting fraud are managed by manipulating and designing rewards/penalties. It has high accuracy (95%), high recall (90-92%), and costs less since it would remove the human manual review of transactions. In addition, there is the continuous adaptation to emerging forms of fraud via meta-learning techniques to ensure the scaling of the model to various financial institutions and fraud. The method is more accurate in detection, has increased operation efficiency, and is a complete solution to intelligible and flexible real-time fraud detection.

title: UniOMP: Unified Optimization Framework for OpenMP Offload Under Machine Learning Guidance
authors: Jianan Li, Lin Han, Shaoliang Peng, Yingying Li, Wei Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: Heterogeneous programming is a critical approach to unleashing the parallel computing potential of GPUs, playing a vital role in fields requiring massive computational resources, such as scientific computing, artificial intelligence, and computer graphics. While OpenMP simplifies parallel programming and offers greater flexibility and portability compared to CUDA and Triton, it still faces challenges in fully leveraging hardware resources for efficient task execution. We propose UniOMP, an OpenMP-based optimization framework designed to enhance kernel launch efficiency and deeply optimize parallel tasks across diverse computational patterns. By integrating machine learning algorithms to select optimal parameters and orchestrate optimization passes, UniOMP seamlessly combines traditional compilation with ML-driven strategies, achieving holistic coordination of fine-grained and global optimizations. Experimental results demonstrate that UniOMP achieves speedups of 1.74x on AMDGPU and 1.34x on NVIDIA platforms for PolyBench, and 1.33x (AMDGPU) and 1.24x (NVIDIA) for SPEC ACCEL. Additionally, it reduces average register usage by 25 %, maximizing the efficient utilization of GPU hardware resources.

title: Flexibility Estimation of Hybrid Heat Pumps based on Grey Box Model Identification
authors: Xin Li, Haoyang Zhang, Koen Kok, Nikolaos Paterakis
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Hybrid Heat Pumps (HHPs) have emerged as a promising technology on the residential side, but estimating their potential flexibility quantity in extrapolated scenarios with different thermal profiles remains a critical challenge. We currently have real measurement data from a pilot project in the Netherlands, but it is difficult to determine the specific parameters of existing and potential households when assessing the overall flexibility potential. To address this, we propose a greybox identification method to fit thermal parameters from real project data, and apply the corresponding clustering algorithm to generate representative user profiles. Based on these, we formulate both a flexibility provision model and an extrapolation model for future HHP adoption scenarios. Among the tested regression methods, Huber robust regression achieves the best accuracy and robustness. The results indicate that HHPs provide higher flexibility at lower outdoor temperatures, while the extrapolation case shows that the added flexibility benefit of new users is more pronounced in colder conditions.

title: Topology and Control Strategy of MMC-Based Converter Station for Multi-Voltage Operation Scenarios
authors: Bingjie Jin, Zuohong Li, Chu Jin, Xinyue Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the large-scale integration of renewable energy generation and the continuous expansion of power grid scale, future flexible DC grids may exhibit characteristics of multiple voltage levels and multiple infeed points. The interconnection and voltage matching between DC buses of different voltage levels represent a technological development requirement for flexible DC grids. This paper proposes a topology of MMC-based converter station suitable for multi-voltage operation scenarios-the high-low-end structure MMC-based converter station, which adopts a construction approach with separate high-end and low-end stations connected in a series-cascaded configuration. Furthermore, an adaptive droop control strategy suitable for the high-low-end structure MMC-based converter station is proposed, enabling rational power and voltage distribution and enhancing system operational stability and flexibility. Finally, a flexible HVDC transmission system with a receiving-end high-low-end structure is simulated and analyzed on the PSCAD platform. The analysis results demonstrate that the high-low-end structure MMC-based converter station possesses complementary support capability during AC side faults, exhibiting stronger voltage support capability and fault recovery capability. The content discussed in this paper can provide certain technical references for constructing multi-voltage level DC grids.

title: Strategic Input Selection For Deep Neural Networks Reliability Evaluation
authors: Lucas Roquet, Fernando Fernandes Dos Santos, Maria Kastriotou, Angeliki Kritikakou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: Assessing the reliability of large Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) is becoming increasingly challenging as models and datasets grow, making exhaustive reliability assessment unfeasible through either radiation testing or fault simulation. In this work, we propose a robust input selection methodology to assess the reliability of large image classification DNNs. Our methodology is based on the confidence distribution of model predictions. We validate this approach through a cross-layer analysis, combining neutron beam experiments and software fault simulations (SWFS) on state-of-the-art large DNNs. Our results show that DNN confidence has a strong impact on the misclassification rates. Our input selection method exhibits up to 22× higher misclassification rates than the commonly used random input selection approach. These findings highlight the limitations of relying only on random selection when estimating the fault sensitivity of large DNNs and emphasize the need for prior input analysis.

title: RRDNet: Robust Fault Diagnosis of Railway Switch Machines under Imperfect Sensing
authors: Xiaoxi Hu, Kai Zhang, Yuhan Huang, Jingming Cao, Dandan Peng, Zhuyun Chen, Tao Tang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Railway Switch Machines (RSMs) are the actuators that directly control the train route and thus require fault diagnosis for condition-based maintenance. Most existing fault diagnosis research for RSMs assumes clean, fully perfect sensing, which rarely holds for trackside equipment in harsh field environments. In practice, environmental interference and sensor faults jointly lead to imperfect sensing, where multichannel vibration signals are corrupted, partially missing, or even invalid on certain channels. To bridge the gap, this paper first proposes an end-to-end Robust RSM Diagnosis Network (RRDNet) for the RSM diagnosis task under imperfect sensing. First, a Single Channel Signal Transformer (SCST) tokenizes each vibration sequence into patches and learns long-range temporal representations while confining disturbances within each channel. Second, a Dynamic Graph ATtention (DyGAT) fusion layer models channels as nodes, constructs a sample-specific topology, and learns to down-weight perturbed channels while enhancing informative ones to obtain a fused descriptor for the final classification. Experiments on a multichannel vibration dataset collected from three triaxial accelerometers, with 16 RSM states and eight imperfect sensing modes, show that RRDNet achieves a Macro-F1 of 94.56% with balanced Macro-Prec and Macro-Rec, and consistently outperforms other models under a unified protocol. Ablation and comparative studies further confirm that the isolate-then-fuse design of SCST and DyGAT is critical for accurate RSM diagnosis under imperfect sensing.

title: URLLC-Aware Resource Allocation for IRS Backscatter-Enabled Space-Air-Ground Integrated Networks_supp1-3675238.pdf
authors: Jianmei Dai
publish: N/A
abstract: The evolution of 6 G and Internet of Things (IoT) technology is driving the proliferation of computation-intensive and latency-sensitive applications, which impose increasingly stringent requirements on IoT Devices (IDs) for powerful and timely data processing capabilities. Space-Air-Ground Integrated Network (SAGINs) have emerged as a promising candidate for providing ubiquitous computing services for processing various sensing data generated by IDs, particularly in remote areas. However, supporting these applications in SAGINs still faces two key challenges: IDs cannot afford intensive data transmission and computation due to the limited battery and computing capability, and the Ultra-Reliable Low-Latency Communication (URLLC) constraints are difficult to satisfy due to the network dynamics. To tackle these challenges, we propose an Intelligent Reflecting Surface (IRS) backscatter-enabled SAGIN architecture, where battery-efficient, radio-frequency-chain-free uplink transmissions from IDs to Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are enabled, significantly reducing the communication energy consumption of IDs. We aim to minimize the system energy consumption for balancing low-latency requirements against energy-saving demands under URLLC constraints. Considering that resource allocation decisions and URLLC constraints have different time scales, the Lyapunov optimization is employed to decompose the formulated problem into tractable subproblems, which are solved by integrating Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) with fractional programming, semi-definite programming, and the Lagrange method. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly reduces the total energy consumption by 66.67% compared to benchmark schemes, thereby enabling sustainable, high-performance computation for remote IDs.

title: Hierarchical Information Fusion and Diffusion Sampling to Enhance Streaming Session-Based Recommendation
authors: Shunpan Liang, Jixiang Yang, Yinuo Han, Hengchen Xi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Session-based recommendation (SBR) systems have gained significant attention in recent years due to their ability to model user–item interaction sequences. However, traditional models struggle to adapt to dynamic environments, particularly when capturing real-time changes in user preferences. To address this, streaming SBR (SSBR) systems have been proposed, which train long-term user preference models offline and update short-term preferences dynamically in a streaming setting. Despite this advancement, existing SSBR methods face challenges in modeling complex inter-session dependencies and capturing the evolution of user interests. To overcome these limitations, we introduce the hierarchical information fusion–graph neural network (HIF-GNN) framework. This framework leverages item-level and session-level graph convolutional networks to capture users’ multilevel behavioral patterns. We also propose a hierarchical attention fusion module, which dynamically adjusts the importance of different information levels, and a diffusion sampling module that utilizes entropy-based sampling to adaptively capture users’ evolving interests and session behaviors. Extensive experiments on three real-world datasets show that HIF-GNN outperforms state-of-the-art methods, demonstrating its superior performance and suitability for dynamic streaming recommendation tasks.

title: TrashToTreasure: An Informative and Interactive Multi-View Classification Framework
authors: Guoqing Chao, Mingjie Zhang, Xiru Wang, Jie Wen, Weiping Ding, Dianhui Chu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: As a basic machine learning task, Multi-View Classification (MVC) has garnered considerable attention and achieved great success. However, the existing MVC methods, especially late fusion style ones still suffer from some problems: 1) hidden valuable information is not well exploited; 2) a lack of interaction before decision making. To address these problems, we propose a novel framework named ”TrashtoTreasure” that leverages mutual information to effectively exploit hidden valuable information. Specifically, the framework explicitly disentangles multi-view information into ”useful” components and ”trash” (noisy) components, and further extracts potentially valuable ”treasure” information from the ”trash”components of all views. Additionally, we design a tailored objective function that facilitates the effective separation of ”useful” and ”trash” components, as well as the synergistic extraction of ”treasure” information. This function guides model optimization through triple mutual information constraints. Experimental results on synthetic data and several real-world data sets verified the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method. The fresh perspective offered by this article may inspire more interesting exploration in this direction. The codes are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/jiezhang054/TrashToTreasure</uri>.

title: DADCUNet: Dual-Attention Deformable Convolution UNet for Building Extraction
authors: Bole Zhang, Wangshan Yang, Xian Zhang
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Our study proposes a new approach for building extraction by introducing the Deformable-Convolution Fusion Feature module, based on the Dual-Attention Network and deformable convolution. The core of the algorithm consists of three modules: Auxiliary Encoder, Main Encoder, and Decoder. The Auxiliary Encoder is responsible for linearly embedding and downsampling the input RGB images and utilizes multiple DA Blocks to learn image features, thereby achieving multi-scale feature extraction. Simultaneously, the Main Encoder performs multiple downsampling operations and integrates features from different sources through the DCFF module, facilitating feature complementarity and fusion. This integration ensures that the final output feature map contains comprehensive semantic information, providing strong support for subsequent tasks. The Decoder further processes the multi-scale features, modeling the spatial and channel dependencies effectively through position and channel attention mechanisms, thereby enhancing feature representation. To improve feature learning capability, the algorithm incorporates the improved R Block, replacing the traditional Transformer structure. This integration, combined with GateMLP and Dual Attention techniques, significantly enhances the model’s ability to learn complex patterns. The proposed algorithm was validated on publicly available urban building scene datasets and compared with other methods. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach achieves new state-of-the-art performance, with significant improvements over the previous SOTA method, TransUnet (with an average increase of +1.75% in accuracy, +1.8 in F1 Score, and +3.6 in mIoU). The source code is available at https://github.com/zbole/DADCUNet.

title: Rapid Design Optimization Method for a Novel Cascaded Brushless Power-Fed Permanent-Magnet Adjustable Speed Drive
authors: Xinlei Zheng, Heyun Lin, Chenggang Wang, Yibo li, Shuhua Fang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: In this article, a rapid design optimization method for a novel cascaded brushless power-fed permanent-magnet adjustable speed drive (CBPF-PMASD) is proposed to address the issues of parameter combination and high computational cost resulting from the cascaded structure. Initially, the topology and operating principle of the proposed drive are introduced. Subsequently, its initial design, including the pole-pair combination, slot combination and initial dimensions, is formulated based on the load characteristics. Then, a two-stage rapid optimization method is proposed to perform a comprehensive optimization for the drive, thereby achieving high efficiency, low cost, and low torque ripple simultaneously. The method independently optimizes the power and control sub systems, replacing the complex three-dimensional (3D) model with two simplified two-dimensional (2D) models. Additionally, an equivalent coupling model is developed based on a field-circuit coupling technique to predict the overall electromagnetic performance of the drive. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed design optimization method and the accuracy of the equivalent coupling model are validated through both simulated analysis and experimental tests.

title: Adaptive Lane Angle Prior and Feature Enhancement for Robust Lane Detection
authors: Xin Luo, Yingping Huang, Kui Zheng
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Lane detection is a crucial component of intelligent driving perception. Among the various lane detection methods, the line anchor-based approach has demonstrated state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance. However, this method still faces challenges when dealing with blurred or occluded lane markings. To enhance the quality of line anchors, we propose an Adaptive Lane Angle Prior, which enables localized refinement of curved lane geometries, thereby more accurately capturing the variations in lane lines. By dynamically adjusting the angle parameters based on angle variance threshold, our method significantly improves lane detection precision in scenarios with curve. To address the common occlusion issues in lane detection, we design a Feature Enhancement Module that integrates both Non-local Blocks and Large Kernel Attention mechanism. The Non-local module enhances the global consistency of features through global information interaction. Furthermore, the Large Kernel Attention mechanism captures large-scale contextual information through large-sized convolutional kernels. To more accurately capture lane line variations, we integrate an Angle Loss together with adaptive angle prior to enhance geometric constraints within the model. Specifically, our method achieves an F1 score of 97.89% on the TuSimple dataset and 80.27% on the CULane dataset. These experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on both benchmarks.

title: Variable-Axis Extended Back-EMF Oriented Sensorless Method Based on Auxiliary Reference Frame SMO for MAS-IPMSM
authors: Haitao Zhang, Guangyu Pu, Hanlin Zhan, Hui Yang, Yi Long, Dianguo Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: In this article, the extended back-electromotive force (EMF) is generalized to variable-axis, by orienting toward which, a universal auxiliary reference frame sliding-mode observer (ARF-SMO) based sensorless method is proposed for magnet-axis-shifted interior permanent magnet synchronous motor (MAS-IPMSM). First, the variable-axis extended (VAE) back-EMF is proposed, which characterizes the information of the asymmetrical back-EMF due to magnet-axis-shifting (MAS) effect and unifies the back-EMF model of different rotor saliencies and symmetries. Subsequently, a universal ARF-SMO based sensorless method coping with MAS effect is proposed, in which the proposed auxiliary reference frame can obtain the unbiased position information by orienting toward the VAE back-EMF. The proposed ARF-SMO is a generalization of conventional SMO and keeps the universality for both MAS-IPMSM and typical PMSM. Additionally, the stability of the proposed ARF-SMO is proved based on the lyapunov theory. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is verified with the experiments.

title: Automatic Pipelining for Arbitrary Circuits via Gate-level Forwarding and Customized Scheduling
authors: Chongxiao Li, Shuyao Cheng, Rui Zhang, Qicheng Wang, Pengwei Jin, Di Huang, Jiaming Guo, Zidong Du, Xing Hu, Qi Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Circuit pipelining is essential for improving the frequency and throughput of sequential circuits, yet it still largely relies on manual design at the behavioral level, which is effort-intensive, error-prone, and fails to exploit fine-grained gate-level opportunities. Existing automatic approaches analyze feedforward paths to insert registers but fall back to conservative stalling in the presence of feedback loops, sacrificing parallelism to preserve correctness. As a result, neither manual nor existing automatic methods simultaneously achieve fine-grained throughput optimization and correctness guarantees on arbitrary sequential circuits. We present an automatic pipelining framework that combines gate-level forwarding and customized scheduling. Our key insight is that gate-level analysis reveals shorter feedback loops, whose hazards can be resolved by forwarding paths spanning fewer pipeline stages, thus avoiding long multi-cycle dependencies that cause redundant stalls. Our method consists of two steps: the first focuses on fine-grained optimization beyond behavioral-level analysis, while the second ensures correctness for arbitrary circuits. Concretely, we (i) greedily assign pipeline stages at the gate level and create short forwarding paths for fine-grained pipeline optimization, and (ii) design a circuit-specific scheduler to ensure correctness. The scheduler analyzes state-conditioned reads/writes to predict future dependencies and apply only the necessary stalls, and is then synthesized into hardware using an improved Binary Speculative Diagram (BSD) algorithm with acceptable hardware overhead. Across diverse circuit datasets, our approach identifies 35.23% redundant pipeline stalls overlooked by behavioral-level manual designs and improves throughput by 9.46% for 3-stage and 23.07% for 4-stage pipelines over EDA-based automatic pipelining techniques, while guaranteeing functional correctness on arbitrary sequential circuits.

title: IEEE Industrial Electronics Society Members Elevated to IEEE Fellows [Society News]
authors: Óscar Lucía
publish: IEEE Industrial Electronics Magazine
abstract: Provides society information that may include news, reviews or technical notes that should be of interest to practitioners and researchers.

title: Recognize Excellence
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Internet Computing

title: A Novel DFT Architecture and Implementation for 2.5D Chiplets Based on Test-Bus
authors: Jingrui Hu, Ye Guo, Yan Feng, Xiaolin Tang, Sumin Fan, Guanfei Gong, Zhiqiang Li
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: With the slowdown of Moore's Law, 2.5D/3D Chiplets have become a key solution to break through the bottlenecks of traditional monolithic chips. However, their high integration density poses testing challenges such as pin constraints, cross-layer transmission difficulties, and bonding failure risks, making traditional 2D test architectures incompatible. To address this issue, this paper proposes a test bus-based Design for Testability (DFT) architecture for 2.5D Chiplets. By integrating an interposer-embedded test bus, Bus Network Hosts (BNH), and dedicated Mid-bonding Bypass Modules (MBB), the architecture facilitates comprehensive multi-chiplet testing across both the mid-bonding and post-bonding phases, while natively supporting dynamic assembly scenarios. A 2.5D experimental platform consisting of three chiplets is established for feasibility validation. Synthesis and simulation results demonstrate that this architecture maintains robust timing with minimal area and power overheads, and reduces total test time by 17.5% compared to the multi-component test solution defined by the IEEE 1687 standard. Ultimately, it provides a feasible, efficient, reliable, and cost-effective solution for 2.5D Chiplets testing.

title: Advanced Fault Diagnosis in Rotary Machines Using Optimized Transfer Learning
authors: Wasim Zaman, Muhammad Farooq Siddique, Shafi Ullah Khan, Jaeyoung Kim, Jong-Myon Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Rotary machine fault diagnosis is essential for maintaining system performance and ensuring operational safety. Deep data–driven models have demonstrated exceptional performance in fault diagnosis of rotary machines when sufficient training data is available. However, obtaining this data, particularly under faulty conditions, can be labor-intensive and time-consuming. The challenges posed by a data-driven model with insufficient training data have been addressed by transfer learning (TL) techniques when similar machine data is available. However, these conventional TL-based models struggle to perform effectively for a machine where gathering similar training data is difficult. This paper presents a deep TL approach with an optimized fine-tuning strategy, enabling the effective transfer of knowledge from a source domain with diverse machine data to a target domain with limited data from target machines. The proposed framework introduces a feature-space guided fine-tuning strategy in which the optimal retraining depth is determined through quantitative evaluation of target-domain feature separability across successive pretrained layers using silhouette score analysis. This transforms conventional heuristic layer-freezing into an objective data-driven adaptation process, enabling controlled transfer under domain shift and limited target data availability. Experimental results using data obtained from a real industrial testbeds involving milling cutting tool, grinding machine and public Paderborn dataset validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach and demonstrate its superiority over existing TL techniques in the similar domain.

title: Autonomous Firefighting Drone Using ROS, YOLOv8, and ArduPilot with Intelligent Fire-Extinguishing Ball Deployment in Simulated Environments
authors: Kalyani Ghuge, Swapnil Jadhav, Yadhni Kharkar, Prasad Koshatwar, Chaitanya Luniya, Harshada Ghanwat
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: This paper presents an autonomous firefighting UAV that integrates ROS2, AirSim, ArduPilot, and a YOLOv8n-based detector to identify fire, smoke, and human victims and deliver fire-extinguishing balls. We quantify system performance across simulation and real-world trials (30 missions): detection mAP@0.5 = 0.925 (avg), end-to-end response time = 58.3 ± 14.2 s, payload delivery accuracy = 0.35 ± 0.18 m, and overall mission success = 80%. Ablation experiments show YOLOv8n provides a favorable tradeoff between inference latency (3.5 ms/frame after TensorRT) and detection accuracy compared to baselines. We discuss failure modes (wind, heavy smoke), statistical significance of results, and practical considerations for field deployment.

title: Curved Microstrip Patch Antenna Designed From Transparent Conductive Films for Military and Defense Application
authors: Tamil Vani R, Kanna S, Melvin Joel T
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Transparent antennas has been seen as widespread adoption in sectors including satellite communication, medical devices, consumer electronics, and military and defense communication systems. Traditional flat MPA that utilize transparent conductive films (TCFs) tend to exhibit low radiative efficiency and gain, particularly at lower frequencies. To overcome this challenge, a novel design for a curved MPA has been created in the C band (4-8 GHz) range using transparent materials and different bending angles. This advanced design enhances radiative efficiency and gain while retaining the desired transparency and lightweight properties. The process involves depositing TCFs onto a polyethylene terephthalate (PET) film, which is subsequently mounted on a 3D-printed curved polyethylene terephthalate glycol (PETG) support frame. The CST Studio Suite 2021 is employed for simulating and assessing the antenna, concentrating on metrics such as radiation efficiency, S11, VSWR, energy, and gain. To verify its performance, the prototype of this transparent curved patch antenna undergoes both simulation and fabrication, evaluating its effectiveness based on these crucial parameters.

title: Simulation and Experimental Study on the Breakdown Rule of Busbar Insulating Sheath Under Typical Defects Network
authors: Feixiang Cao, Xuedong Zhao, Yuanpeng Liang, Bo Xu, Dian Jiao, Jinbang Meng, Jiajia Du, Wei Shi
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: In recent years, interphase short circuit failures caused by overlapping foreign matter on the surface of the busbar insulating sheath on the main transformer low-voltage side have frequently occurred. In order to reduce the trip rate caused by foreign matter on the busbar, it is necessary to study the flashover characteristics of the busbar sheath. In this regard, this paper builds a flashover test platform for grounding foreign objects between busbars with insulating sheaths and single-phase foreign objects between busbars. The flashover characteristics of busbars and insulating sheaths when different defect types and different types of foreign objects overlap are explored. The test results show that the breakdown voltage of the insulating sheath is significantly reduced when foreign objects overlap, and the average breakdown voltage value when single-phase wire is grounded is 16.4 kV. In addition, this paper established a multi-physical simulation calculation model for the insulating sheath of the busbar lap under foreign matter. The simulation results show that the insulating sheath of the foreign matter will cause local electric field distortion and temperature rise in the overlap point of the busbar sheath, and the degree is determined by the material of the foreign matter and has nothing to do with the type of defect. The electric field intensity at the most severe electric field distortion is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.4 \times 10^{\wedge} 7 \mathrm{V} / \mathrm{M}$</tex>, and the maximum temperature rise is 76° C. This study provides a certain reference for the insulation dimension design of insulating sheath.

title: An Improved Pattern Search Based Oscillation Suppression Method for Local Power Grids During Active Support of Wind-Storage Coupled System
authors: Zhiyuan Sun, Lichun Chen, Kun Zheng, Jinzhao Li, Boya Peng, Chang Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the continuous integration of wind-storage coupled system (WSCS) in the weak grid, the frequency and voltage active support of WSCS is important. However, during the active support, the local power grid is prone to weak damping events, causing power oscillations. The power system stabilizer (PSS) is an effective method to achieve oscillation suppression. Due to the power characteristics of WSCS, this paper proposes an improved pattern search based oscillation suppression method for local power grids during active support of WSCS. This method aims to suppress frequency and voltage oscillations in the local grid and enhance the support capability of WSCS. The main contributions of this paper are summarized as: (1) The active and reactive power output is quantified for frequency and voltage support of WSCS; (2) The PSS parameters are optimized using an improved pattern search algorithm to increase the damping ratio for oscillation suppression. Extensive test results have validated the effectiveness and performance of the proposed method for oscillation suppression during active support of WSCS.

title: A Method for Online Uninterrupted Replacement of Old and New Equipment in Comprehensive Power UPS System Upgrades
authors: Fangbo Xing, Zhongyu Feng, Zhiyuan Qiu, Tianwei Guo, Bin Li, Xin Li, Ya Lu, Chenyu Xie
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: In recent years, with the advancement of intelligentization, integration, and safety-enhanced applications in monitoring systems at all levels of power system substations, a novel online uninterrupted replacement method for old and new equipment during comprehensive UPS system upgrades has been proposed. This method utilizes self-developed uninterrupted replacement equipment for old and new cabinets and an uninterrupted switching device, ensuring continuous power supply for all loads throughout the entire process of power UPS system upgrades.

title: Hybrid Wind Power Prediction Model Combining Time–Frequency Analysis and Deep Learning
authors: Yuhao Zhao, Hang Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: To address the limitation that traditional single prediction models struggle to capture the multi-scale time–frequency characteristics of wind power sequences and lack targeted modeling capability for the latent features within data, this paper proposes a novel wind power prediction framework that integrates time-frequency analysis with deep learning. Specifically, the Stationary Wavelet Transform (SWT) is first employed to perform multi-scale decomposition of the raw wind power sequence, separating it into low-frequency components that describe the overall trend and mid- and high-frequency components that characterize local fluctuations. For the low-frequency subseries, a pattern recognition module is developed based on structural feature extraction and unsupervised clustering (K-Means), followed by targeted prediction using a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) network. Meanwhile, for the mid- and high-frequency components, Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) combined with Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) is applied to extract deep representations in the time-frequency domain, which are then fitted by a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP). Finally, the predicted results of all subcomponents are linearly superimposed to obtain the final wind power prediction. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed hybrid prediction model effectively exploits the potential multi-scale patterns embedded in wind power sequences, achieving a more comprehensive characterization of their time-frequency dynamics. Compared with conventional single models, the proposed approach exhibits superior predictive accuracy and robustness across multiple evaluation metrics.

title: Improved DNN-Based Smith Predictive Control for pH in Flue Gas Desulfurization Systems
authors: Jian Yue, Kaiyan Wu, Yeming Xu, Lihui Shan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: The control of slurry pH in the absorber tower of the wet flue gas desulfurization (WFGD) system is characterized by significant time delays and strong nonlinearity. To maintain stable operation under these conditions, traditional controllers must adopt conservative parameter settings, which lead to slower response speeds and diminished control accuracy. To address this limitation, this paper proposes an improved Smith predictor based on a deep neural network (referred to as DNN-Smith) to enhance the performance of traditional controllers in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{p H}$</tex> control. First, based on the existing neural network-based Smith predictor structure, the two internal neural network models are consolidated into a single one. Second, the dynamic characteristics of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{p H}$</tex> in the WFGD system are modeled using a deep neural network. Finally, the improved DNN-Smith is implemented for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{p H}$</tex> control in the WFGD system. The results demonstrate that the deep neural network-based pH prediction model reduces key prediction errors (RMSE and MaxAE) by 84.54 % and 87.04 %, respectively, compared to the traditional ARX model. The proposed DNN-Smith-PI controller outperforms the conventional PI controller, reducing the RMSE and IAE in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{p H}$</tex> control by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{8 7. 0 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{8 6. 4 \%}$</tex>, respectively. Furthermore, the improved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{p H}$</tex> control performance contributes to more stable control of the clean flue gas <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SO}_{2}$</tex> concentration, achieving an approximately 22 % reduction in RMSE compared to traditional PI control. Therefore, the DNN-Smith-PI controller presented in this paper provides an effective solution.

title: Design and Development of AI-Enabled Solar-Powered Seeder with Adaptive Depth and Spacing Control
authors: Kalluri Praveen, M. Vinayak
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The current precision farming requires energy-saving and smart systems to maximize crop production and use of resources. The design and development of an AI-enabled and solar-powered adaptive depth and spacing controlled seeder that allows it to carry out sowing operations automatically due to soil and crop needs is represented in this paper. The system also takes real-time data provided by sensors and runs it through a convolutional neural network (CNN)-based soil classification module to dynamically compensate sowing depth (10 to 50 mm range) and inter-seed spacing (100 to 250 mm). The seeder was driven by a 150W photovoltaic solar system and it was able to produce 92.4 percent energy efficiency during field trials against the diesel-driven systems. In addition to this, adaption depth control enhanced seed germination by 18.6 and the best spacing cut seed wastages by 21.3. The actuator system uses a microcontroller-based placement set up to perform the mechanical operation precisely, and path optimization is dependent on GPS. These findings are very crucial and point out the fact that the suggested solar-powered system (AI-augmented) is a positive side toward green and intelligent agricultural technologies, particularly in energy-deficient rural regions.

title: Resilient Distributed Data Systems: Enhancing Transactional Integrity for Continuous Uptime
authors: Shiva Kumar Bommakanti
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: This paper addresses the critical challenge of maintaining consistent and performant operations in large-scale distributed data environments. It introduces a novel transaction processing architecture, built upon an existing key-value storage framework, engineered to significantly enhance both responsiveness and data integrity. By optimizing concurrency control and ensuring robust state persistence, this system achieves superior operational reliability and throughput. The work presented demonstrates how an evolved protocol for distributed transactions contributes to the continuous availability and seamless user experience in high-demand data platforms, even under severe load conditions.

title: A Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Framework for Intelligent Topology Control to Facilitate Renewable Integration
authors: Wangjie Xu, Jing Zhang, Xiang Zheng, Xuanchen Xiang, Ruisheng Diao, Ke Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: High penetration of distributed energy resources (DERs) creates operational challenges like reverse power flows and line overloading risks, limiting the grid's renewable integration capacity. While topology control can mitigate these issues, existing methods struggle with scalability and enforcing critical safety constraints in real-time decision-making for largescale power networks. This paper presents a novel MARL framework that overcomes these barriers, employing k-medoids clustering to decompose the network into manageable regions, each controlled by a local agent. A dynamic action masking mechanism ensures operational safety by design, filtering out any actions that would violate critical constraints. Validated on a high-fidelity simulation of a real-world feeder, the proposed approach effectively mitigates reverse power flow overloads without violating operational limits, achieving a 99.47 % success rate on standard test cases. It significantly outperforms a less effective centralized controller, providing a scalable path to enhance the grid's DER integration and safety.

title: Wind Farm Control via Reinforcement Learning: A Sim-to-Real Case of Blyth Demonstration Project
authors: Zhilong Cao, Zhichuan Ma, Yunhe Wei, Jianhong Wang, Zhong Fan, Dawei Qiu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The integration of wind power into smart grids requires adaptive control strategies to handle its inherent variability and intermittency. While reinforcement learning (RL) offers a promising solution, transferring learned policies from simulation to real-world operation remains a critical challenge. This paper presents a comprehensive study on AI-driven cooperative control of wind farms, evaluating multi-agent RL under multiple wind scenarios and validating policies with real-world data of the UK offshore wind farm Blyth Demonstration Project. To enhance realism, we propose a modified training framework that incorporates time-varying wind sequences, thereby capturing more representative system dynamics. Our results show that policies trained under this framework achieve greater robustness and superior performance in real-world evaluations, significantly outperforming those trained in static or fully augmented environments. Overall, this work narrows the gap between simulated benchmarks and real-world deployment, providing practical insights for developing reliable and efficient AI-driven wind farm control systems.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Brain MRI-Based Autism Detection Techniques
authors: Reva S. Patil, Rashmi J. Deshmukh
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a complex neurodevelopmental condition characterized by varied symptoms and diagnostic challenges. This study presents a comprehensive review of current autism detection methods, with a focus on machine learning and deep learning approaches that utilize brain MRI data. By systematically analyzing conventional clinical tools, questionnaire-based screening, and AI-powered image analysis models, this work highlights the strengths and limitations of existing techniques. Special focus is given to neuroimaging modalities, particularly structural and functional MRI, which offer promising avenues for non-invasive, early diagnosis. Key requirements for model design, such as data quality, feature selection, interpretability, and generalizability, are identified and discussed. The study also outlines critical gaps, including dataset imbalance, lack of standardization in feature extraction, and insufficient inclusion of diverse populations. The insights derived aim to guide future research towards developing robust, ethically sound, and clinically viable AI models that enhance the accuracy and accessibility of ASD diagnosis.

title: Implementation of Adaptive Network Shielding Algorithm (ANSA) to Improve lifetime and Security in IoT Networks
authors: Vasanthakumar Muthukumaran, Sri Deepti Kalapala, L. Bhargavi, Arun M, G. V. S. Padma Rao, V. Gokula Krishnan
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Exponential growth in interconnected networks has been realized with the adoption of more Internet of Things (IoT) devices resulting in complex ecosystems that are highly susceptible to many cyber threats. Conventional security systems are inefficient in changing topology and heterogeneity of the people on the Internet of Things. In order to cope with this challenge, the paper suggests adopting an Adaptive Network Shielding Algorithm (ANSA) that will assist in strengthening the security and resilience of IoT networks. The proposed ANSA redacts malicious activities by putting into use node behavior analysis that is intelligent and adaptive threat response mechanisms so as to dynamically monitor and identify threats and diminish them. It uses lightweight cryptographic protocols, intrusion detection in real-time and based on anomaly detection, to minimize congestion overhead and provide high protection. The algorithm carries on working in a changing network environment and an evolving attack profile; it is appropriate in a resource-limited IoT device. Simulation findings prove to us that ANSA remarkably enhances the ratio of successful packet delivery, detection trueness, and network lifetime with least latency and false positives than the current strategies. In addition, ANSA maintains confidentiality of data transmission and increases trustiness of IoT nodes. Its efficiency in protecting itself against different types of attacks such as sinkhole, Sybil, and selective forwarding has been proven during the experimental evaluation. The offered solution introduces a scalable and smart architecture of real-time protection of IoT networks in our modern environment.

title: High-Precision Real-Time Detection of Marine Fish Species Using Fine-Tuned YOLO Models
authors: Bussa Surya, Harshitha Dandamudi, Lekshmi S. Nair, Hema P. Menon
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Since most underwater species are deceptively similar in appearance, it is often difficult to monitor underwater animals due to obstacles and poor lighting, which are key to marine biodiversity conservation. Hence, we need the establishment of an advanced detector using the pre-trained FishInv.pt model to further improve YOLOv9t and YOLO10n, wherein the proposed system involves a transformer-based detection head for long-range contextual information collection, Coordinate Attention modules for refining spatial attention, and a Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Network for effectively fusing multi-scale features for more reliable and accurate underwater species detection. The dataset consists of 16,145 labeled images of 25 species, divided into 70% training, 20% validation, and 10% testing. The models were trained for 30 epochs on a Tesla T4 GPU. The results indicate YOLOv9t runs at 344.83 FPS with mAP@50 of 0.995, while YOLO10n runs at 370.37 FPS with mAP@50 of 0.992. Weighted Box Fusion also increases robustness.

title: Faster and Smarter Budgeting in Higher Education: A Data-driven Approach to Strategic Financial Planning based on Design Science Research Methodology
