title: Intelligent Optimization Method for Project Management Based on Large Models
authors: Jiaqi Li, Tongping Lin, Yongshi Lu, Xiaoshi Deng, Yuhan Wen, Runhuan Lin, Lixiao Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the development of the digital economy and artificial intelligence, unstructured text in project management has surged, making it difficult for traditional methods to effectively extract knowledge and track trends. To address this issue, this paper proposes an intelligent optimization method for project management based on large models. By standardizing documents, generating summaries driven by prompt words, and time tag analysis, it achieves structured processing of heterogeneous texts. At the same time, by combining knowledge unit vectorization, incremental clustering, and hierarchical dimensionality reduction visualization, it realizes intelligent extraction and evolution tracking of topics and technical hotspots. The study shows that this system can efficiently reveal the evolution laws of technical topics and core study hotspots, and reflect the shift of project management focus from basic deployment to architecture optimization and complex system integration, providing a feasible new paradigm for knowledge mining, trend prediction and intelligent decision-making.

title: GAT-Enabled Cooperative Scheduling for Multi-Station EV Charging-Discharging
authors: Chengjie Dai, Hao Fu, Zhaoyi Yang, Chaofan Li
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the rapid expansion of electric vehicle adoption, coordinating the bidirectional charging and discharging processes among multiple stations and vehicles has emerged as a significant challenge for power grid operations. This paper presents a multi-agent deep reinforcement learning framework based on the Graph Attention Network-Value Decomposition Network (GAT-VDN), which facilitates real-time scheduling under the Centralized Training Decentralized Execution (CTDE) paradigm. In this approach, charging stations, vehicles, and photovoltaic arrays are abstracted as graph nodes. The spatial coupling relationships are captured through GAT, enabling simultaneous balancing of peak-valley differences in the power grid, user satisfaction levels, and renewable energy consumption rates via a multi-objective strategy optimization method. For scenarios with high penetration of photovoltaic systems, we introduce a collaborative reward model termed source-grid-load, aimed at enhancing the priority utilization of renewable electricity. Simulation results indicate that GAT-VDN achieves a 44.4% increase in cumulative rewards compared to a no-learning baseline. Furthermore, when compared to VDN alone, it reduces peak-valley differences by 44.45%, increases local photovoltaic consumption rates by 76.8%, and enhances average user satisfaction by 27.02%.

title: Realistic Water Leakage Synthesis in Indoor Electrical Power Environments: A Semantics-Guided Approach with Irregular Mask Generation and Controllable Diffusion
authors: Zhujiang Liu, Qiang Zhang, Ronghui Li
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Acquiring diverse and realistic datasets of water leakage in critical electrical power environments, such as substations, is a significant challenge, yet it is essential for training robust perception models for intelligent inspection. This paper proposes an automated pipeline for synthesizing realistic water leakage in indoor scenes by combining semantic guidance with controllable diffusion models. Our key innovation is a semanticsguided irregular mask generation algorithm that identifies plausible floor regions and creates complex puddle masks using aggregated elliptical primitives. These masks serve as input for a diffusion-based image-to-image translation framework using Stable Diffusion. We enhance realism through ControlNet to preserve structural details and soft inpainting for seamless blending. Our approach generates diverse synthetic images that respect scene semantics and geometry, effectively addressing data scarcity for downstream computer vision tasks and improving model robustness in complex environments.

title: Data-Driven Estimation Model for HEMP Effects on Power Plant Protection Equipment
authors: Shuai Dong, Sirui Ren, Mingyan Song, Fan Jian, Songhao Yang, Zhiguo Hao
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: High-altitude electromagnetic pulse (HEMP), generated by high-altitude nuclear explosions, is characterized by high amplitude and broad coverage. Its E1 component can cause extensive damage to power equipment and lead to system failures. As the intelligentization of power plant equipment continues to advance, the threat posed by HEMP is becoming increasingly severe. Therefore, it is necessary to investigate the effects of the E1 component on equipment. To address the high computational demands and long simulation time required for coupling simulation of the E1 component of HEMP, this study first supplements the dataset and then employs an artificial intelligence-based regression algorithm to predict the peak core voltage under arbitrary incident angle <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varphi$</tex>, azimuth angle <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\psi$</tex>, and polarization angle <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex>. Subsequently, a generalized extreme value (GEV) distribution is used to fit the damage threshold data of secondary equipment obtained from injection experiments. By integrating coupling analysis, regression prediction, and extreme value distribution, this research establishes a damage probability curve for secondary equipment, enabling the condition discrimination of intelligent relay protection devices. The study provides a reference for electromagnetic protection in power plants and enhances their operational reliability.

title: Transient Stability Support for Synchronous Generators Using Deloading of HVDC-Linked Offshore Wind Power Plants
authors: Maira Izammel Binti Zainoren, Kenichiro Sano
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The integration of large-scale offshore wind power plants (WPPs) via high-voltage DC (HVDC) transmission presents challenges to the transient stability of onshore AC grids. A strategy to prevent synchronous generators (SGs) from losing synchronism during a fault is to temporarily suppress the active power injected by the HVDC link. However, this action creates a secondary problem: a critical DC overvoltage that threatens the HVDC system itself. This paper proposes a coordinated control strategy that enables the primary goal of transient stability support while simultaneously solving the consequential DC voltage issue. The strategy involves two coordinated actions: first, an output suppression control on the onshore (receivingend) converter is activated at the moment of fault detection to stabilize the SG. Second, to prevent the resulting DC overvoltage, a deloading control is applied to the offshore (sending-end) converter to curtail power intake. Through simulations of a threephase line-to-ground (3 LG) fault, it is demonstrated that this coordinated approach successfully achieves the primary objective of maintaining the generator's stability, thereby allowing the entire system to ride through the severe disturbance securely.

title: Source-Grid-Load-Storage Coordinated Smart Microgrid for Remote Rural Areas: Practice and Operational Analysis of the Guangzhou Taolian Village Project
authors: Jun Zhao, Lifang He, Yue He, Yipeng Xiao, Zhi'an He, Longjun Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: To address the challenges of low power supply reliability, poor power quality, and difficulties in distributed energy resource (DER) integration caused by long feeder lines and weak grid infrastructure in remote rural distribution networks, this paper presents a practical case study of the whole-village smart microgrid cluster project in Taolian Village, Guangzhou City, Guangdong Province. A comprehensive solution integrating coordinated planning and intelligent control of “Source-Grid-Load-Storage” (SGLS) is proposed and implemented. The project constructs a microgrid system comprising small hydropower, distributed photovoltaics (PV), medium and low-voltage grid-forming energy storage systems, and low-voltage flexible interconnection devices, with coordinated regulation achieved via a cloud-edge collaboration architecture. This paper elaborates on the system architecture design, the energy storage capacity configuration method based on load and reliability requirements, and the multi-mode coordinated operation strategy encompassing “Normal GridConnected Operation,” “Fault-Induced Islanding Operation,” and “Grid Reconnection Recovery.” Operational results demonstrate that this microgrid has successfully achieved 100% local consumption of renewable energy, effectively mitigated issues such as low voltage, and significantly enhanced power supply resilience, providing a replicable technical pathway for constructing new rural distribution systems.

title: LatentTune: Efficient Tuning of High Dimensional Database Parameters via Latent Representation Learning
authors: Sein Kwon, Youngwan Jo, Seungyeon Choi, Jieun Lee, Huijun Jin, Sanghyun Park
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: As data volumes continue to grow, optimizing database performance has become increasingly critical, making the implementation of effective tuning methods essential. Among various approaches, database parameter tuning has proven to be a highly effective means of enhancing performance. Recent studies have shown that machine learning techniques can successfully optimize database parameters, leading to significant performance improvements. However, existing methods still face several limitations. First, they require substantial time to generate large training datasets. Second, to cope with the challenges of highdimensional optimization, they typically optimize only a subset of parameters rather than the full configuration space. Third, they often rely on information from similar workloads instead of directly leveraging information from the target workload. To address these limitations, we propose LatentTune, a novel approach that differs fundamentally from traditional methods. To reduce the time required for data generation, LatentTune incorporates a data augmentation strategy. Furthermore, it constructs a latent space that compresses information from all database parameters, enabling the optimization of the full configuration space. In addition, LatentTune integrates external metric information into the latent space, allowing for precise tuning tailored to the actual target workload. Experimental results demonstrate that LatentTune outperforms baseline models across four workloads on MySQL and RocksDB, achieving up to 1332 % improvement for RocksDB and 11.82 % throughput gain with 46.01 % latency reduction for MySQL.

title: Investigation on Improving the Ampacity of Cable in Duct through the Application of Heat Pipes
authors: Fuquan Li, Liangyou Shao, Cheng Chen, Peng Peng, Shilei Kou, Lei Cui, Guojun Gong, Gang Hong
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The duct laid cables are becoming more common in urban areas. However, the ampacity of the cable laid in duct will be severely restricted due to the existence of overheating issues. On the purpose of addressing the ampacity limitation problems, this paper proposed a novel heat dissipation strategy based on the application of gravity-assisted grooved wick heat pipes. At the beginning, a multiphysics simulation model for typical duct laid cable was constructed. Then, the overheating issues occurring in duct cable laying scenario were clarified, and the necessity of applying heat pipes was highlighted. Subsequently, a new gravityassisted grooved wick heat pipe was designed, and its layout strategy in duct laid cable was proposed. The 2D simulation model for cable laid in duct after the introduction of heat pipes was established based on the layout strategy. The ampacity improvement effect of heat pipes was analyzed by the established multiphysics simulation model. Finally, the layout of the heat pipes was further discussed and analyzed on the basis of considering the construction complexity and economic feasibility. By introducing heat pipe, the ampacity of duct laid cable could be significantly improved.

title: Research and Practice of Digital and Intelligent Technologies for UHVDC Projects Driven by Beidou Centimeter-Level Spatiotemporal Reference
authors: Zhihui Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Aiming at the core challenges of UHVDC (Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current) projects, such as the urgent need for precise management in complex geographical environments and prominent barriers in full-life-cycle data integration, this paper proposes and practices a digital twin technology system driven by Beidou centimeter-level spatiotemporal reference. With the goal of “full-element collaboration among physical power grid, digital power grid, and business systems”, the system constructs a “12345” technical architecture: relying on two pillars, namely the Beidou + 5G centimeter-level spatiotemporal reference network (with a positioning accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 5 ~\text{cm}$</tex> under strong electromagnetic interference) and the “BIM + GIS + SCADA” full-chain modeling technology (with a point cloud classification accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geqslant 98 \%$</tex>); a three-level verification system is established covering macro transmission corridors (error <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leqslant 0.3 ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>), meso-scale devices (error <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leqslant 8 ~\text{mm}$</tex>), and micro components (error <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leqslant 2 ~\text{cm}$</tex>). Focusing on the optimization of four-dimensional performance indicators (accuracy, efficiency, reliability, and scalability), and through key technologies such as air-ground collaborative 3D data acquisition, terrain-adaptive modeling engine, and streaming data governance mechanism (Kafka + Flink engine with a throughput of 100,000 points/second), it realizes high-precision air-space-ground integrated modeling (global error <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 5 ~\text{cm}$</tex>) and millisecond-level full-life-cycle data fusion (topological accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geqslant 99.97 \%$</tex>). Verified by the Kunliulong DC project, this technology has successfully compressed fault response time to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leqslant 30$</tex> minutes in five application scenarios, including active defense for personnel safety (zero near-electrical accident rate), intelligent equipment diagnosis (defect recognition rate 98.7%), and network-free emergency collaboration, significantly improving the intelligent operation and maintenance level and safety management capabilities of UHV projects.

title: Finite-Frequency Attack Detection for Power Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Chaowei Sun, Qingyu Su, Jian Li
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: In this paper, the problem of finite- frequency attack detection for power cyber-physical systems is investigated. Firstly, a multiobjective optimization problem is introduced to represent the decision process of the cyber layer. By solving the optimization problem, the optimal output power of generators can be determined. Then, a dynamic model of the power system is formulated, and a controller is designed to ensure that each generator operates in accordance with the decisions of cyber layer. In order to trigger an alarm in the presence of finitefrequency sensor attacks, an attack detection scheme based on dynamic observer is proposed. The LMI conditions required for solving the observer parameters with small-gain performance are formulated, simultaneously. Finally, the effectiveness of the attack detection scheme is validated through simulations on the IEEE 9-bus test system.

title: A Hardware-in-the-Loop-Based Digital Twin Cyber-Physical System for Microgrids
authors: Yifu Li, Xingchi Ma, Payman Dehghanian, Xiaohua Zhang, Ruotan Zhang, Benedict Vergara, Haoyu Wang, Jinshun Su
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With increasing DER penetration and operational complexity, reliable fault detection in distribution networks has become more challenging, particularly under transient and diverse fault conditions. This work develops a digital twin (DT) framework that combines hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) real-time simulation with a light-indicated urban distribution diorama, enabling dynamic fault emulation and high-resolution synchrophasor measurements acquisition. The generated time-series data are utilized to develop deep learning models for identifying fault types and locations. To improve scalability, graph convolutional networks (GCNs) encode network topology and enable transfer across reconfigured grid structures with minimal retraining. Case studies demonstrate that the proposed HIL-enabled DT with graph-based learning offers an effective and extensible approach for intelligent fault detection in urban microgrids.

title: A Cross-Validation Framework for Classifying Plant Diseases from Sensor Data
authors: Batta Saranya, V.Vasuki Rohinidevi, Ashlesha Kolarkar, Ravikiranreddy Kandadi, Juttu Suresh
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Plant diseases are a serious threat to world food security and cause millions worth of losses in crops each year. Traditional methods of detecting plant diseases rely on visual inspection or laboratory analysis; both are time-consuming and require a certain level of proxy professionalism. The current study is based on a machine learning process to classify plant diseases using soil and environmental sensor data. Sensor data in the study collects data on nitrogen (N), phosphorus (P), potassium (K), temperature, humidity, pH and rainfall. A feedforward neural network with dropout regularization was trained on a dataset with 140,000 samples for 14 disease classes under five crops (cherry, corn, grape, pepper bell, potato). Using 5 fold cross-validation, the model yielded an average accuracy of 93 percent overall, with crop accuracies ranging from 87% (cherry) - 98.57% (pepperbell). Overall the results demonstrate the utility of sensor based data as an accurate and non-invasive way to classify diseases, creating opportunities to intervene methodologically early in precision agriculture.

title: DFD-TL-GAN: Deepfake Detection Using Transfer Learning and GAN-Augmented Datasets
authors: Medha Wyawahare, Milind Rane, Tushar Bhandari, Pratham Bisen, Sandesh Bellale
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: In recent years, the proliferation of deepfake content has raised serious concerns regarding digital misinformation and identity fraud. This paper presents DFD-TL-GAN, a hybrid deepfake detection framework that synergizes transfer learning with Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to achieve superior detection performance. The model leverages the InceptionV3 architecture, pre-trained on ImageNet, as a feature extractor, fine-tuned for binary classification of real versus fake facial images. To augment training data diversity and improve generalization, we introduce synthetic fake images generated by StyleGAN2-ADA, enriching the representation of deepfake variants. The model is trained and evaluated on a composite dataset derived from Celeb-DF and DeepFakeTIMIT, achieving a test accuracy of 91% prior to GAN integration. Our proposed framework significantly enhances robustness to evolving deepfake manipulations and demonstrates scalable applicability in real-world detection scenarios. The combination of transfer learning and adversarial data augmentation establishes a forward-compatible baseline for next-generation deepfake forensics.

title: Power System Transient Stability Assessment and Prediction Based on Deep Learning
authors: Yibo Xia
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: Background: Modern power grids require stability assessment and confidence reporting within 100 milliseconds of a fault. Traditional energy functions and full-time simulations are hindered by speed and universal applicability. Methods: A physics-driven graph-time series deep learning model is developed. This model integrates grid topology and PMU time series data into a graph sequence representation. A GNN and Transformer fusion encoding is implemented, and physical constraints are constructed based on the swing equation residual. Temperature scaling and the Dirichlet method are integrated to perform probability calibration and early identification. Results: On a publicly available standardized dataset, the proposed algorithm outperforms baselines using SVM, LSTM/GRU, GNN, Transformer, and PINN-aided in terms of AUC, Brier, ECE, and number of early identification steps, while meeting the 95% performance level of P95 online latency. Inter-system testing demonstrates excellent generalization. Conclusion: The combination of physical priors and graph time series improves both accuracy and confidence, making it suitable for engineering implementation and forming a foundation for subsequent protection and control collaboration.

title: Lightweight Tower Crane Load Detection Algorithm Based on Improved YOLOv10n
authors: Xinyan Shi, Huimin Ouyang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: This paper addresses the issues of YOLO-based load recognition in the process of visually detecting the swing angle of tower cranes. In complex laboratory environments, it tends to blend into the background, resulting in low detection accuracy and insufficient lightweight design. We propose a tower crane load detection algorithm based on an improved YOLOv10n: DDP-YOLOv10n. This algorithm replaces the C2f module in the backbone network with a DynamicConv module, significantly reducing the model's parameter count and computational load, making the model more lightweight. It introduces a DySample module to enhance the model's integration of feature details and semantic information. Additionally, it replaces the CIoU loss function with the Powerful-IoU loss function, allowing the model to achieve faster convergence speed and higher accuracy. Experimental results show that compared to YOLOv10n, the improved model reduces the parameter count and computational load by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0. 2 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 4. 1 \%}$</tex> respectively on a self-built dataset. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> value reaches 95.5 %, and the FPS increases by 56.1 % to 243.9, laying an important foundation for subsequent deployment of load detection in three-dimensional coordinates and swing angle on depth cameras.

title: A Hybrid Method for WPT System Compensation Topology Development
authors: B. SomaShekar, Sunil Kumar A V, D. P Somashekar, Jisha L K, Ganapathy D Moger
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The advantages of a WPT (wireless power transfer) system include simplicity, safety, and dependability. Electrical adjustments should be made to energy transfer coils in applications to boost output power while decreasing input capacity. This article provides a hybrid approach for compensatory topologies in WPT system. The hybrid technique combines the Lyrebird Optimization Algorithm (LOA) and the Dual Attention Graph Convolutional Network (DAGCN), and it is known as the LOA-DAGCN technique. The proposed solution aims to improve power efficiency in the WPT system. The proposed LOA approach is to determine the best controller parameters and generate a set of control signals. The DAGCN predicts the power delivered to the load. The suggested technique's performance is evaluated using the MATLAB working environment and compared to other approaches currently in use. Current techniques include particle swarm optimization (PSO), recurrent neural networks (RNN), and artificial neural networks (ANN). According to the findings, the proposed technique is 97.0% efficient, while the existing ANN's is 87.0%, the RNN's is 77.0%, and the PSO's is 68.0%.

title: Federated Learning Framework for Privacy-Preserving Oral Cancer Detection Using Lightweight Vision Transformers and Edge AI Deployment
authors: Kavitha A, Archana V, Girisha J K
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Oral cancer is one of the most common types of cancers in the globe, and in this case, early diagnosis and prompt treatment is crucial to survival. The existing deep learning algorithms like convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have been successful in recognizing oral lesions but need to be centralized in data collection and cloud computing, bringing privacy and security issues and restricting their application in resource-restricted locations. This paper proposes a federated learning framework that combines ViTs (lightweight) and edge AI to detect oral cancer privately. Photos are taken with smartphones or handheld diagnostic machines, processed locally and ViT lightweight models are trained directly on the machines. All encrypted model updates are transmitted to a central server, and Federated Averaging (FedAvg) algorithm averages them to create a global model and sends it back to devices to customize it according to their local populations and imaging differences. Lesion classification (benign, potential malignant, malignant) and inference in real time are done entirely on edge devices, thus providing privacy, low latency and offline functionality. A differentiable feedback loop ensures a better performance without sensitive data being exposed. The suggested system offers scalable, privacy-preserving, and real-time oral cancer detection, which is an intermediation between high-performance AI diagnostics and real-life clinical use in a variety of healthcare settings.

title: Lung Nodule Classification Using Transformer - depending on Multi-View Radiomics Fusion
authors: Manish R Bhise, Ohmshankar S, Smitha Vas P, Suchithra B, Chaliki Archana, Keerthana N V
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The proposed approach offers a new methodology for lung nodule recognition utilizing transformer-based architectures combined with a multi-view radiomics fusion framework. This approach exploits the complementary characteristics of different radiological views to achieve better diagnostic accuracy and interpretability. Employing transformer models, the system efficiently identifies long-range dependencies and situational relationships within and between different imaging modalities. Radiomics features from various views are combined via an attention-based mechanism that intensifies the model's focus on the most informative areas. This fusion technique not only deepens the feature representation but also alleviates the problem of noise and inconsistency caused by different views. The entire structure is set up in a way that it can learn effectively from diverse radiological sources with an accuracy of 93.7%, thus enabling the exact classification of lung nodules into benign or malignant classes. The combination of deep learning and radiomics in a transformer-based pipeline is still far from unleashing its full capacity, but it already signifies that considerable progress can be made in clinical decision support for early lung cancer detection.

title: IES InterOp Plugfest 2025 [News in Industry Activities]
authors: Victor Huang
publish: IEEE Industrial Electronics Magazine
abstract: Provides society information that may include news, reviews or technical notes that should be of interest to practitioners and researchers.

title: IEEE IES Sri Lanka Chapter Hosts Two Events [Chapter News]
authors: Hani Vahedi
publish: IEEE Industrial Electronics Magazine
abstract: Provides society information that may include news, reviews or technical notes that should be of interest to practitioners and researchers.

title: Space Cloud Networks: Technologies, Architectures, and Key Enablers
authors: Wenxiao Ge, Juan A. Fraire, Xinxin Shen, Kanglian Zhao
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: The rapid growth of artificial intelligence models and global computing demands has placed an unsustainable burden on ground-based data centers, exacerbating energy consumption and environmental concerns. Simultaneously, emerging space-based infrastructures face stringent real-time processing requirements, while underserved regions experience persistent connectivity gaps. To address these challenges, we propose a space cloud network powered by space-based data centers interconnected through high-speed laser links. We present a three-layer architecture comprising cloud, access, and user layers and analyze the strengths and limitations of different orbits and configurations. We advocate for a multi-layer heterogeneous fusion network that leverages dawn-dusk and inclined orbits to balance performance, latency, and coverage. Additionally, we identify key enabling technologies, including space cloud operating systems, file systems, network management, and network security. This work presents a comprehensive framework for establishing the space cloud network, aiming to enhance global connectivity and computational efficiency.

title: Large Language Models for Wireless Communications: From Adaptation to Autonomy
authors: Le Liang, Hao Ye, Yucheng Sheng, Ouya Wang, Jiacheng Wang, Shi Jin, Geoffrey Ye Li
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: The emergence of large language models (LLMs) has revolutionized artificial intelligence, offering unprecedented capabilities in reasoning, generalization, and zero-shot learning. These strengths open new frontiers in wireless communications, where increasing complexity and dynamics demand intelligent and adaptive solutions. This article explores the role of LLMs in transforming wireless systems across three key directions: adapting pretrained LLMs for communication tasks, developing wireless-specific foundation models to balance versatility and efficiency, and enabling agentic LLMs with autonomous reasoning and coordination capabilities. We highlight recent advances, practical case studies, and the unique benefits of LLM-based approaches over traditional methods. Finally, we outline open challenges and research opportunities - including multimodal fusion, collaboration with lightweight models, and self-improving capabilities - charting a path toward intelligent, adaptive, and autonomous wireless networks.

title: Thermo-Optic Control of Barium Titanate Modulators on Silicon-on-Insulator Substrates
authors: Hee Gon Kim, A. Raju, L. Tian, D. Krueger, A. Posadas, A. A. Demkov, D. Wasserman
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: Efficient thermo-optic (TO) phase shifting in a barium titanate (BTO) Mach-Zehnder interferometer (MZI) with integrated micro-heaters is demonstrated. Devices are characterized spectrally, and MZI transmission is measured as a function of micro-heater power. We demonstrate repeatable π− phase shifts with only ∼108mW power, the lowest reported for TO phase shifters in BTO photonic devices. Modulation bandwidths of ∼34kHz are demonstrated. Combining finite element simulations with optical measurements enables the extraction of an effective BTO thermo-optic coefficient (TOC) of 8.87×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup>K<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup>. The power required for a π-phase shift, modulation bandwidth, and compact heater dimensions are all competitive with state-of-the-art lithium niobate-based thermo-optic devices. The large extracted TOC suggests the potential for hybrid electro- and thermo-optic control of BTO-on-Si devices for future low-power, compact photonic systems.

title: Renewable-Energy-aware Advance Reservation Service Scheduling in Optical Networks
authors: Yanran Xiao, Wei Wang, Kexin Gao, Yajie Li, Yongli Zhao, Jie Zhang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society

title: High-Frequency rTMS Facilitates Mental Fatigue Recovery in Healthy Young Adults
authors: Xinyi Xu, Mengru Xu, Yingqi Fu, Xiaoyu Li, Zhao Feng, Yeting Hu, Jie Zhou, Chuantao Li, Yu Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: In the contemporary society, mental fatigue is a major challenge to the efficiency of production and safety of daily life, highlighting the necessity of exploring effective intervention strategies. Here, 73 healthy adults (male/female = 36/37, age = 22.6 ± 2.2 yrs) were recruited to participate in a randomized, single-blind, sham-controlled study. Specifically, the high-frequency repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) was applied to the left dorsolateral prefrontal cortex (DLPFC) in-between a sustained attention task to explore, for the first time, the feasibility and efficacy of promoting fatigue recovery. EEG data obtained during task were then projected to cortical space through source imaging, followed by functional brain network construction and quantitative graph theoretical analysis. Compared to the sham group, the real rTMS group exhibited preserved reaction times and significantly alleviated subjective worry. These behavioral benefits were accompanied by an intervention-related reorganization of brain networks. Specifically, rTMS-related modulation of long-range fronto-temporal connectivity and shifts in nodal centrality within the right fronto-parietal network and the posterior cingulate cortex (PCC) was also revealed. Further inspection of global graphical properties showed that brain network developed toward a higher segregation state after the intervention, as indicated by the increased local efficiency and clustering coefficient. Our findings indicate that high-frequency rTMS mitigates mental fatigue not through passive rest, but by promoting a functional transition of large-scale brain networks toward a more efficient topological state. This study confirmed the feasibility of rTMS as an effective intervention for fatigue recovery, and contributed to the development of precise, neurobiologicallybased strategies for fatigue intervention.

title: Intelligent Fault Diagnosis for Industrial Processes Based on Deep Feature Extraction Networks
authors: Zihan Fen, Hua Ding, Ning Li, Xiaochun Sun
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Early fault detection of equipment can detect and deal with potential problems in a timely manner, reducing the risk of production disruptions. Although various machine learning methods have been proposed for early fault detection of devices, their detection capabilities have not achieved the expected results and cannot accurately detect early minor faults 3, 9 and 15 of Tennessee Eastman process (TEP). A new deep feature extraction network (DFENet) is proposed for this problem. First, the block-combination strategy is used to obtain the eigenmatrix of multi-source data. The singular value of the eigenmatrix is calculated by sliding window to form a singular value matrix. Secondly, the singular value matrix is input into the newly constructed Local-Global information Fusion (LGIF) algorithm to obtain T2 and Q statistics, which are recombined into a large eigenmatrix by column, and the fault detection index Dp is used to detect early faults in industrial processes. Finally, two case studies by TEP and actual shearer data collected of Shaanxi Shuanglong Coal Industry Development Co., Ltd. show that DFENet is significantly superior to DePCA and FENet in early fault detection, achieving feature extraction for early minor faults and having practical promotion and application value.

title: Pillar-SAN: A Pillar-based Spatiotemporal Attention Network for 3D Object Detection from 4D Radar Point Cloud
authors: Weibin Zhang, Xuzheng Gong, Shihao Zhu, Yang Li, Qi Wang, Wenjing Zhao
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: 3D object detection is a crucial component of the perception system in autonomous driving. With the advent of 4D millimeter-wave radar technology, 3D object detection with 4D radar introduces new challenges and opportunities. 4D radar is recognized as a state-of-the-art sensor owing to its superior all-weather performance and remarkable robustness in complex environmental conditions. However, compared to LiDAR, 4D radar point cloud is sparser and contains more noise. These characteristics hinder the effectiveness of existing LiDAR-based 3D object detection methods when applied to 4D radar, making them less suitable for accurate 3D object detection in this context. To cope with this issue, we propose a Pillar-based Spatiotemporal Attention Network (Pillar-SAN). The network builds a sequential perception attention module to establish intrinsic connections between point cloud across both time and space, thereby enhancing its capability to extract global and local features from sparse point cloud. In addition, the receptive field attention module is built to refine object feature details while effectively suppressing noise. Experimental results on the VOD dataset show our Pillar-SAN achieves significant improvements over previous state-of-the-art methods, with a 7.22pp increase in 3D mAP, a 7.8pp increase in BEV mAP, an 11.42pp increase in BBOX mAP, and a 12.94pp increase in AOS mAP.

title: Analysis of Superposition Exceeding Radiation in Time-Domain Tightly Coupled Arrays
authors: Zhiqiang Yi, Linyan Li, Jiyang Xie
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: Time-domain antenna arrays are critical for transient applications, yet their radiation mechanisms remain insufficiently studied. Inspired bytightly coupled array (TCA), this work studies Vivaldi and transverse electromagnetic mode (TEM) horn antennas in a tightly coupled configuration. A novel phenomenon, called superposition exceeding radiation, is discovered, where the TD field synthesis efficiency (FSE) exceeds 100%. Theoretical analysis reveals that the tightly coupled structure functions as a transient current modulator, temporally redistributing energy toward the waveform peak. This is the primary contribution and innovation of this work. Measurements on a fourelement Vivaldi TCA show a FSE of 104.3%, with simulated and measured results in good agreement. This work clarifies the time-domain radiation mechanism of TCAs, thereby paving the way for extending their applications to transient electromagnetic systems.

title: Error-Based Filtering and Adaptive Weighting for Real-Time DL-TDoA Localization
authors: Lei Ling, Maobin Tang, Jintao Wang, Weiqiang Tan, Chunguo Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Downlink time-difference-of-arrival (DL-TDoA) localization using ultra-wideband (UWB) is well suited for supporting large-scale tagging systems, but its accuracy degrades severely under non-line-of-sight (NLOS) conditions. Existing approaches often rely on high-dimensional channel impulse response (CIR) data for NLOS mitigation, yet the associated processing overhead and latency limit their practicality for real-time applications. This paper proposes an error-based filtering and adaptive weighting strategy for DL-TDoA localization that operates without CIR data. The filtering step identifies a subset of potentially erroneous TDoAs by examining the inconsistency between Chan- and LS-based estimates and the most recent valid position, refining the measurement set by selectively removing measurements and recomputing positions to confirm reliable TDoAs. The weighting step adjusts each measurement’s contribution based on the ToF-based ranging error between anchor pairs, as well as the RSSI of the received signal. Experimental evaluations in representative indoor NLOS scenarios demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms both the conventional Chan+Taylor and CIR-Stat algorithms in localization accuracy, while maintaining a relatively low computational overhead, making it suitable for real-time applications.

title: Edge-Enabled Big Data Pipelines for Low-Latency Predictive Intelligence
authors: Aravind Satyanarayanan, Raunaq Malik, Gayatri Tavva, Nithin Kakani, Adya Mishra, Chirag Mavani
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The immediate application of big data in fields like smart cities, health care, industry IoT, etc. demands real-time analytics through low-latency applications. The conventional cloud-based pipelines are usually unable to perform at these very high-performance requirements caused by bandwidth constraints and excessive overhead communication. This paper suggests Edge-Enabled Big Data Pipelines, which is a framework to combine edge computing with scalable data processing pipelines to execute predictive analytics nearer to data sources. Through the use of distributed edge nodes, adaptive stream processing, and lightweight machine learning models, the proposed solution reduces round-trip delays and achieves an optimal use of resources. Moreover, we create a dynamic orchestration system to balance the computational loads between edge and cloud environments to meet scalability, reliability and fault tolerance. The experimental assessments of real-world datasets show that it achieves a significant improvement in reduction of latency, throughput, and predictive accuracy over traditional cloud-only pipelines. The results point to how edge-enabled pipelines can enable real-time intelligence in data-intensive systems, which will form the basis of the next-generation applications in the critical areas demanding the rapid pit of information.

title: Maximizing Insights, Minimizing Data: I/O Time Prediction Using Transfer Learning
authors: Adrian Voß, Radita Liem, Julian Kunkel, Jay Lofstead, Philip Carns, Matthias Müller
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: Predicting application I/O time in large HPC clusters is a difficult task due to the shared nature of the resources involved and intermingled layers of the I/O stack. However, a more accurate prediction of application I/O time would improve resource scheduling and make it easier to identify scaling and portability bottlenecks. Currently, this type of prediction, which involves high-dimensional parameter spaces, is often performed by using machine learning, introducing another problem: data availability. Several related works require a large amount of data or access to the specific cluster's job scripts to perform the prediction, which can be a significant challenge for small and medium clusters or new systems without prior performance data. In this work, we use a transfer learning workflow and I/O modeling to create a targeted I/O time prediction tailored for a specific application. Our work demonstrates that we can utilize <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 1 \%$</tex> of the data required to create the base model prediction to achieve up to 87.25 % of the predictions that fall within the acceptable range of I/O time variability.

title:
Complete Dielectric Function and Optical Constants of AgBiS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Quantum Dots: A Foundational Dataset for Modeling Nanocrystal-Based Optoelectronic Devices
authors: Hamed Dehdashti Jahromi, Hussein Thary Khamees
publish: IEEE Journal of Quantum Electronics
abstract: This work presents a complete, self-consistent determination of the size-dependent complex refractive index and dielectric function of AgBiS2 nanocrystal films (4.2 nm, 6.2 nm, and 28 nm). Using multi-Gaussian fitting combined with Kramers-Kronig analysis validated by the f-sum rule, we derive a full suite of optical constants. AgBiS2 is a high-index material (n ≈ 2:7 for 28 nm films). Size-dependent optical behavior is quantitatively explained by Bruggeman effective medium theory, yielding volume fractions of fA = 0:65 (4.2 nm) and fA = 0:75 (6.2 nm). The real dielectric function ε1 exhibits a reversal in size dependence near 600 nm, arising from competition between packing density and quantum confinement effects. Tauc analysis reveals an indirect bandgap blue-shifting from 0.95 eV (28 nm) to 1.10 eV (4.2 nm) under quantum confinement, while high absorption suggests pseudo-direct behavior from cation disorder. Urbach energies range from 62.7-93.9 meV, with discrepancies from prior reports explained by methodological differences. Optical conductivity shows a broad joint density of states with absorption peaking > 1:2 eV above the band edge. Calculated Fresnel reflectivity exceeds 25% at the air-film interface, establishing anti-reflection coatings as essential for efficient devices.

title: Enhancing Measurement Accuracy: The Impact of Missing Data on Parameter Estimation in Mass-Spring-Damper Systems
authors: Michkath Omanda Bouraima, Steven Thompson, Maciej Zawodniok
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In sensor-driven dynamic systems, missing data can severely degrade parameter estimation accuracy. This paper investigates the impact of missing data on phase estimation in a mass–spring–damper system using an information-theoretic framework based on the Cramér–Rao Lower Bound (CRLB). Closed-form CRLB expressions are derived for four scenarios: complete data, Missing Completely At Random (MCAR) deletion, MCAR-based imputation, and Missing At Random (MAR) missingness via a selection-weighted formulation. These bounds are used as theoretical benchmarks to evaluate classical imputation methods (Last Observation Carried Forward (LOCF), linear interpolation) and advanced approaches (Kalman filtering, Rauch-Tung-Striebel (RTS) smoothing, Bayesian inference, and transformer-based imputation) through Monte Carlo simulations. Empirical results show that classical imputation introduces substantial bias and variance, while advanced methods improve stability but do not outperform direct estimation from available samples nor approach the complete-data or MCAR CRLBs, even under realistic model-parameter mismatch. Normality tests confirm approximately Gaussian estimator behavior, supporting the validity of the CRLB-based analysis. Under MAR conditions, estimation from observed samples consistently yields the lowest variance, highlighting the importance of explicitly accounting for the missingness mechanism in parameter estimation.

title: Progressive Orthodontic Motion Planning based on Hierarchical Diffusion Transformer
authors: Yeying Fan, Yuanfeng Zhou, Weijie Liu, Guangshun Wei, Zhiming Cui, Yiran Shen, Yong-Jin Liu, Wenping Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
abstract: Orthodontic motion planning plays a crucial role in digital orthodontics by predicting tooth motion sequences to assist dentists in formulating treatment plans efficiently. Most prior work generates the entire intermediate tooth motion sequence given the initial and target tooth alignments. In practice, only the initial alignment of the patient is obtained. However, no existing method can predict the complete motion sequence using only the initial tooth alignment. To address this gap, we propose OrthoDiff, a novel target-free framework that uses only initial tooth alignment through a progressive generation strategy. This strategy generates tooth motion sequences by decomposing the entire motion sequence into multi-level motions, progressively constraining the inference space and reducing the complexity of target-free planning from coarse to fine. Moreover, we design a hierarchical diffusion transformer as the backbone of OrthoDiff, which treats tooth alignment as a sequence of tooth tokens and fully leverages the topological prior knowledge of the dental model. Through extensive evaluations, we demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art techniques in target-free tooth motion generation. Ablation studies further confirm the efficacy of key components in our network design. Meanwhile, we also achieve state-of-the-art results in tooth target alignment prediction, benefiting from our framework. The code and data will be publicly available at https://github.com/Intelligent-Orthodontics/OrthoDiff.github.io.

title: MARRS: Masked Autoregressive Unit-based Reaction Synthesis
authors: Yabiao Wang, Shuo Wang, Jiangning Zhang, Jiafu Wu, Qingdong He, Yong Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: This work aims at a challenging task: human action reaction synthesis, i.e., generating human reactions conditioned on the action sequence of another person. Currently, autoregressive modeling approaches with vector quantization (VQ) have achieved remarkable performance in motion generation tasks. However, VQ has inherent disadvantages, including quantization information loss, low codebook utilization, etc. In addition, while dividing the body into separate units can be beneficial, the computational complexity needs to be considered. Also, the importance of mutual perception among units is often neglected. In this work, we propose MARRS, a novel framework designed to generate coordinated and fine-grained reaction motions us ing continuous representations. Initially, we present the Unit distinguished Motion Variational AutoEncoder (UD-VAE), which segments the entire body into distinct body and hand units, encoding each independently. Subsequently, we propose Action Conditioned Fusion (ACF), which involves randomly masking a subset of reactive tokens and extracting specific information about the body and hands from the active tokens. Furthermore, we introduce Mutual Unit Modulation (MUM) to facilitate interaction between body and hand units by using the information from one unit to adaptively modulate the other. Finally, for the diffusion model, we employ a compact MLP as a noise predictor for each distinct body unit and incorporate the diffusion loss to model the probability distribution of each token. Both quantitative and qualitative results demonstrate that our method achieves superior performance. The code will be released upon acceptance.

title: A Systematic Evaluation of Dragging Interaction Using Raycasting in Virtual Reality
authors: Jiachang Zhang, Baoni Xing, Huawei Tu, Huiyue Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Dragging is a fundamental interaction technique in human-computer interaction. Prior research on dragging has generally been concerned with 2D objects on 2D interactive surfaces, with limited attention to 3D interactive spaces. We performed three experiments to systematically investigate dragging using raycasting in virtual reality (VR), a representative 3D interactive environment. The first experiment examined dragging 2D targets on 2D surfaces placed in VR. The second experiment investigated dragging 3D targets positioned at identical depths in VR. Both experiments examined the effects of target depth, angular target amplitude, and angular target on dragging performance. The third experiment investigated dragging 3D targets with varying depths, focusing on the effects of target layout, target amplitude, and target width. Findings revealed the different impacts of the above factors on dragging interaction. We also proposed a new 3D Fitts' law model tailored for dragging using raycasting in VR, which fits well with our data. Finally, we discuss our results and provide design recommendations for dragging (i.e., translational positioning) tasks using raycasting under the tested experimental conditions in VR.

title: Secrecy Performance Analysis for Near-field MISO Systems
authors: Zhiqing Tang, Haoran Yin, Wanming Hao, Gangtao Han, Shouyi Yang
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Secrecy performance in the near-field (NF) presents a compelling topic for sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks. In this paper, we investigate the physical layer security (PLS) of NF downlink networks serving a single legitimate user in the presence of an eavesdropper. To characterize the performance of the network, through the central limit theorem (CLT), the expected value of the eavesdropping channel statistics is derived for the downlink link in the case of Rayleigh fading. Furthermore, the performance of the proposed network is evaluated in terms of the secrecy outage probability (SOP). The closed-form expressions of the SOP and the floor of SOP under high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions are derived. We also contrast the impact of the number of antennas as well as the target secrecy rate on the overall secrecy performance of the considered network, and analyze the impact of the eavesdropper on the SOP of the considered network. We finally show that: The SOP of the system decreases with the increase of the transmit power, and finally converges to a fixed value. Our Monte Carlo simulations perfectly illustrate the analytical results.

title: UAV Trajectory Planning with Network Age of Information Minimization
authors: Yifan Luo, Jin Xu, Junting Chen, Jianwei Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) have emerged as powerful tools for real-time data collection across Points of Interest (PoIs) in critical domains such as environmental monitoring, disaster response, and intelligent transportation systems. In this paper, we tackle the challenging problem of UAV trajectory planning to minimize Age of Information (AoI)—a key metric that captures data freshness—within networks modeled as graphs. By establishing fundamental lower bounds on achievable AoI under any routing policy, we reveal that these bounds become tight specifically when the graph contains a Hamiltonian cycle, while proving the general AoI minimization problem is NP-hard. To overcome this complexity barrier, we introduce two innovative approximation algorithms: HPA, which achieves a 4 3 -approximation ratio for Hamiltonian paths, and TSA, which guarantees a 2-approximation ratio for general connected graphs. For disconnected graphs, we propose algorithms to construct the connected graphs by adding additional nodes, and then apply HPA and TSA on the connected graphs. Numerical studies further show that these algorithms outperform both conventional heuristic and deep reinforcement learning approaches and maintain robust performance across diverse graph granularities, confirming their real-world applicability and effectiveness.

title: KoopShield: A Koopman based Online Data-Driven Safety Framework for Truck Platoons Resilient to Communication Delays
authors: Aijing Kong, Xian Wu, Chao Huang, Peng Hang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: High-speed autonomous truck platooning with short inter-vehicle spacing is essential for improving freight efficiency. However, in mixed traffic environments, safety and stability are strongly affected by sudden human-driven disturbances and stochastic communication delays. This study proposes a two-layer Koopman-based framework that combines open-loop and closed-loop data-driven predictors with delay-aware compensation and safety assurance for distributed platoon control. In the first layer, an open-loop Koopman predictor is integrated with a robust control barrier function based safety filter formulated as a constrained quadratic optimization. This formulation ensures that nonlinear vehicle dynamics and model uncertainty are handled with explicit safety margins. In the second layer, a closed-loop Koopman predictor performs forward state estimation to compensate delayed predecessor information and reduce delay-induced observation bias in the control loop. The method is evaluated in three representative scenario classes, with random communication delays up to 500 ms and platoon sizes from 3 to 10 vehicles. Under uniformly distributed 300 ms delay, the proposed strategy limits the maximum absolute spacing error to 0.8 m (versus 5.5 m for baseline MPC and 1.94 m for robust H∞), while preserving string-stable car-following behavior. In emergency sudden-braking scenarios, it increases the minimum following distance to 5.9 m, compared with 1.3 m without a safety filter and 2.4 m with a linear control barrier function, demonstrating effective safety correction under critical disturbances.

title: Optimization Model for Multi-Unit Commitment of Coastal Renewable Energy Bases Considering Frequency Security
authors: Xun Lu, Jinye Cao, Ruisheng Diao, Ying Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: To address frequency stability issues in coastal renewable energy bases (CREB) with the integration of large-scale renewable energy resources, this paper proposes a multi-unit commitment optimization model considering frequency security. Firstly, a frequency response model is constructed to characterize transient frequency regulation, fitting controllable units into firstorder inertial models via least squares. Then, key frequency security indicators, including maximum frequency deviation, rate of change of frequency, and steady-state frequency deviation, are derived to quantify and evaluate the operational stability of CREB. Under the constraints of frequency security indicators, the multiunit commitment model comprehensively considers the operating costs of thermal/hydraulic units and the penalties for wind/solar power curtailment, and formulates the optimal power scheduling plan. Finally, several case studies are conducted on the IEEE 39bus system to verify the feasibility of the proposed model. The results show that the proposed model can effectively maintain system inertia within secure operational thresholds through optimized unit commitment scheduling. By harnessing the fast frequency response capabilities of energy storage systems, an effective balance between economic efficiency and stability can be achieved for CREB.

title: Impedance Modeling and Characteristic Analysis of A Grid-forming VSC-HVDC System
authors: Yuexi Yang, Yanying Sun, Hongbo Hao, Xiao Zhou, Shan Liu, Yuhao Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: To deal with the unstable issues of grid-following VSC-HVDC stations connecting to weak grids, a grid-forming method for the VSC-HVDC is put forward in this paper. Firstly, the output impedance model is built, and its analytical expression is given. Then, the frequency modal analysis strategy is adopted to investigate interactive stability. Analysis outcomes reveal that the grid-forming VSC-HVDC station shows characteristics of a voltage source as the synchronous generator. Comparing with the grid-following VSC-HVDC, grid-forming VSC-HVDC stations are able to maintain steady operation when connecting to the weak or stiff grid, and have strong adaptability to the grid. At last, for further verification, simulations are conducted on the PSCAD/EMTDC.

title: Self-Fire Voltage Setting Method of Series Compensation Spark Gap Based on PSCAD Simulation Model
authors: Mingwei Qin, Chengxi Huang, Yan Zhao, Wenpeng Zhang, Yuanyuan Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Aiming at the self-triggering event of the spark gap in series compensation system of the 500 kV transmission line in the substation, combining with the principle of spark gap and the parameters of the series compensation system, a two-terminal system model of series compensation based on PSCAD, which is carried out under the condition of internal fault and external fault of series compensation system is established in the paper,. According to the simulation results, the changes of current and voltage analog quantities of series capacitor, metal oxide arrester (MOV) and spark gap under different faults are obtained. It is concluded that the reason for the self-triggering event of the spark gap is that the self-breakdown voltage setting value of the spark gap is too low. Finally, considered the relationship between the spark gap's self-triggering setting value and the relay protection, a method about the spark gap self-breakdown voltage setting value of the series compensation system is proposed.

title: A Triple-Fused Blockchain-Enabled Framework for Robust Credential Intrusion Detection in Hybrid Active Directory Environments
authors: Nisha Rajpal, Dinesh Rai, Alpana Jijja
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Credential intrusion detection in hybrid Active Directory (AD) environments refers to the identification of unauthorized access attempts that exploit stolen or compromised credentials across both on-premise and cloud-integrated AD systems. Existing detection models face significant challenges, including high false positives due to noisy enterprise login data, limited ability to capture sequential and behavioral patterns in user activity, and lack of transparent, tamper-proof auditing mechanisms for forensic accountability. To overcome these limitations, this research proposes the TriFuse Blockchain-based IDNet model, a novel multi-layered framework that integrates advanced AI-driven anomaly detection with blockchain-enabled auditing. The model employs a Triple-Fused AI Detection Engine comprising Isolation Forest for statistical outlier detection, BiGRU for sequential pattern modeling, and an Attention-Based Autoencoder for feature-level anomaly recognition whose outputs are combined through a weighted fusion strategy to enhance accuracy and resilience. Detected anomalies are immutably recorded in a Hyperledger Fabric blockchain with smart contracts enforcing real-time security policies, while IPFS integration enables decentralized sharing of suspicious activity. This contribution ensures robust intrusion detection, secure auditability, and accountable threat intelligence, providing a scalable and enterprise-ready solution for safeguarding hybrid AD systems. Numerical results demonstrate the model's superior performance, achieving accuracy of 97.86 %, precision 96.21 %, recall 95.87 %, F1-score 96.04 %, and AUC 0.982, outperforming baseline methods.

title: Investigation on Optimizing Cable Heat Dissipation in Comprehensive Cable Trench Through the Application of Loop Heat Pipes
authors: Liangyou Shao, Fuquan Li, Wenpei Li, Lei Cui, Cheng Chen, Peng Peng, Shilei Kou, Gang Hong
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Owing to the limitation of underground land resources, comprehensive cable trench laying method has become a popular cable laying way. Nevertheless, the dense laying of cables in the comprehensive cable trench will lead to overheating problems. As an efficient heat conduction equipment, the introduction of loop heat pipe could solve this issue. Therefore, a new heat dissipation method based on the loop heat pipe was investigated and designed. First of all, the overheating issue was verified and analyzed through simulation, in which the necessity of applying loop heat pipe could be highlighted. Then, a new heat dissipation method through introducing the loop heat pipe was proposed. The working principle of the loop heat pipe and its layout method in the comprehensive cable trench were introduced in detail. Finally, the excellent heat dissipation effect was also verified through simulation. The research in this paper shows that the utilization of loop heat pipe can be a highly effective method to improve the ampacity of comprehensive cable trench laid cables.

title: Dynamic Resource Management in HPC Systems Using Dynamic Processes with PSets
authors: Dominik Huber, Keerthi Gaddameedi, Tobias Neckel, Hans-Joachim Bungartz, Martin Schulz, Pierre-François Dutot, Olivier Richard, Martin Schreiber, Sergio Iserte, Antonio Pena
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: With the increasing scale of High-Performance Computing (HPC) systems and a new awareness of the environmental impact of HPC, new strategies are required to improve the efficiency of resource usage on these systems. One such strategy is Dynamic Resource Management (DRM), which allows changing the resources assigned to a job dynamically during its execution. This increased flexibility in resource allocation and job scheduling can lead to improvements in several system efficiency metrics. Despite these benefits, DRM has not yet been established as a ready-to-use technology for production HPC systems. This is caused by the significant changes required in all the layers of the HPC system software stack, which are only achievable with an extensive and holistic co-design process between resource management software and applications. In this work, we demonstrate the applicability of a recently introduced, generic design approach for dynamic resources called Dynamic Processes with PSets (DPP), to enable DRM in realworld systems. To this end, we developed an exemplary, dynamic system software stack implementation following the DPP design principles throughout all layers. Based on this, we assess the applicability and performance of our approach using both synthetic benchmarks and job mixes consisting of several dynamic, real-world applications. On up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0}$</tex> nodes, we measure moderate overheads for process reconfiguration in applications while significantly improving the system throughput and average job turnaround time compared to static scheduling in crowded system scenarios.

title:
K
                    <sup>4</sup>
                    : Online Log Anomaly Detection Via Unsupervised Typicality Learning
authors: Weicong Chen, Vikash Singh, Zahra Rahmani, Debargha Ganguly, Mohsen Hariri, Vipin Chaudhary
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: Log anomaly detection (LogAD) is crucial for identifying failures and threats in large-scale computing and cyber-infrastructure systems. However, most existing LogAD approaches suffer from key limitations: they depend on slow and error-prone log parsing, employ tightly coupled end-to-end pipelines, often require supervision for improved detection performance, and rely on flawed single-pass evaluation protocols that fail to reflect the temporal dynamics of real-world online detection. These issues significantly hinder scalability, adaptability, and the practical deployment of solutions. To address these limitations, we introduce <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{K}^{\mathbf{4}}$</tex> (Knowing the Unknown by Knowing only the Known), a fully unsupervised, parser-independent, and representation-agnostic LogAD framework designed for high-performance online detection. At its core, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K^{4}$</tex> is grounded in a novel formulation based on representation-level typicality estimation, which transforms arbitrary log embeddings into compact and interpretable four-dimensional descriptors: Precision, Recall, Density, and Coverage (PRDC), which are swiftly computed via GPU-acceleration into geometric <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex>-nearest neighbor statistics. These descriptors inform lightweight, modular detectors, including KDE, GMM, OCSVM, and a new adaptation of DeepSVDD, which enables efficient and accurate anomaly scoring without relying on structured formats or log representation retraining. To support realistic deployment scenarios, we also propose a principled streaming-faithful evaluation protocol that partitions datasets into fixed-size chunks and applies sliding-window sampling with strides to emulate online log ingestion, alleviating the performance overestimation and dataset undercoverage issues of prior single-pass evaluations and enabling reproducible benchmarking across datasets with varying anomaly densities. Using this setup, we conduct over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 5, 0 0 0}$</tex> experiments across three real-world datasets (HDFS, BGL, Thunderbird), six pretrained embedding models, four detectors, and multiple training and log sampling configurations. Compared to six representative baseline methods spanning supervised, semi-supervised, self-supervised, and unsupervised paradigms, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K^{4}$</tex> consistently sets new state-of-the-art results (AUROC: 0.995-0.999, F1: 0.989-0.992) and outperforms all baselines by large margins, while keeping detector training under 4 seconds and per-sample inference latency as low as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \mu ~\mathrm{s}$</tex>, which are orders of magnitude faster than the most competitive alternatives.

title: Low-Level Leakage Current Detection Based on Adaptive Kalman Filtering
authors: Yan Dai, Lan Wu, Zhen Dai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: In low-voltage distribution systems, it is difficult to promptly detect small-magnitude leakage currents. Conventional residual-current devices (RCDs) often do not trip for leakage below 30 mA, and prolonged micro-leakage can cause insulation degradation and elevate fire risk. This paper proposes a weakleakage detection method based on adaptive Kalman filtering(AKF), capable of accurately estimating milliampere-level leakage current under strong noise. By adaptively updating the filter's process and measurement noise covariances (Q and R) in real time, the method achieves higher estimation accuracy and noise immunity in dynamic environments. Simulation results show that even when the leakage current is far below the conventional threshold, the method still enables reliable detection, with the estimation mean-squared error maintained at the milliampere level, thereby providing an effective solution for intelligent monitoring of low-current leakage.

title: A Hybrid CNN-SVM Approach For Enhanced Sentiment Analysis and Trend Monitoring on Social Media
authors: Rahul Joshi, Krishna Pandey, Suman Kumari, Ankita Bharti, Mitali Mohanty, Deepak Upadhyay
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid deep learning approach for sentiment classification, utilizing convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for feature extraction and support vector machines (SVMs) for final classification. A synthetic Twitter-like dataset was generated with controlled noise, slang, emojis, and temporal event shifts to mimic realistic conditions. Experiments demonstrate that the CNN–SVM model achieves higher accuracy, macro F1 score, and AUC compared to the CNN with softmax or traditional SVM. The framework also shows strong robustness against noisy input and captures sentiment trend changes over time with relatively low detection delay. Scalability tests confirm that training time increases but remains manageable with a larger dataset. While some misclassification between neutral and negative still occurs, the approach provides a practical and effective solution for sentiment analysis.

title: Automated Forensic Image Inspection: Identifying Alterations with SVM and CNN
authors: Shashikala S, Karthik B U
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The authenticity and integrity of images are indispensable in forensic law and investigative processes in digital forensics field. The study under consideration introduces a forensic image inspection system based on the use of a supervisor learning and smart-DL system for detection about tampering. The proposed system, which encompasses Support Vector Machines (SVM), has excellent feature extraction and classification functions, therefore, guaranteeing the reliability of the manipulated image recognition process. In addition, Convolutional type of Neural structure (CNN) are used to analyze deep graphics and the structural inconsistency, thus, the small inconspicuous image modifications can still be detected. These methodologies are combined by the system to the extent that it can judge the authenticity or the originality of an image and also, thus, augment the robustness of forensic analysis. The integration of SVM with CNN makes the process more effective and comprehensive, thereby leading to better performance in the detection of image manipulations such as splicing, retouching, and cloning. On the other hand, this study aids in the advancement of forensic image analysis by delivering an automatic framework, which has the capability of dealing with more complicated manipulations with more accuracy and adaptability.

title: IRN-LB: A Lightweight Hybrid Deep Learning Model using LSTM, BERT and RNN for Sarcasm and Irony Detection in Twitter (X) Data
authors: Sravan Kumar Devulapalli, Suresh Kumar Mandala, Satyanarayana Vollala
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Detecting irony and sarcasm in tweets is a complex task due to the implicit, figurative, and often contradictory nature of such expressions. In this study, we introduce IRONY-DETECTOR (IRN-LB), a hybrid deep learning framework that synergizes the semantic power of a distilled BERT encoder with the sequential modeling capabilities of LSTM and RNN layers. Unlike conventional transformer-based approaches, our model achieves high accuracy while maintaining a lightweight footprint, making it suitable for real-time and resource-constrained environments. IRN-LB utilizes DistilBERT to extract contextualized embeddings, significantly reducing computational load while preserving linguistic richness. These embeddings are fed into a two-stream LSTM-RNN architecture designed to capture both long-term dependencies and local linguistic patterns associated with sarcasm, irony, and figurative language. We evaluate our model on two benchmark datasets: the TweetEval Irony Subtask and the Ghosh & Veale Sarcasm Dataset. IRN-LB achieves a macro-F1 score of 91.4% on TweetEval and 93.2% on the Ghosh & Veale dataset, outperforming traditional deep learning models and approaching the accuracy of full-scale transformers with a 46% reduction in model size and 38% faster inference. Our results demonstrate that IRN-LB offers a robust and efficient solution for irony detection in noisy, short-text environments like Twitter.

title: A distributed consensus controller with indirect state interaction for multi-agent systems in the port-Hamiltonian framework
authors: Jingyi Zhao, Yongxin Wu, Weijun Zhou, Yuhu Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal and Information Processing over Networks
abstract: This paper investigates the consensus problem in a class of multi-agent systems with port-Hamiltonian dynamics, where agents update their states through information exchange with their neighbours to achieve consensus. However, the communication process poses a risk of leaking an agent's exact state to other agents or external eavesdroppers, resulting in a potential privacy breach. To address this issue, we propose a distributed consensus controller designed with privacy protection. With the proposed controller, the interaction among multiple agents only depends on their own estimation to the average output of the whole, thus avoiding explicit state sharing. Additionally, the multi-agent systems maintain the passive characteristic, making the Hamiltonian function a natural choice as the Lyapunov function candidate. Furthermore, we prove that the multi-agent systems converge exponentially to the equilibrium where the outputs of the multi-agent systems achieve consensus, and provide the corresponding convergence rate. Finally, a simulation example is presented to illustrate the effectiveness of the proposed controller.

title: Sample-Efficient Learning of a Robotic Tube Insertion Policy_supp1-3675942.mp4
authors: Asheer Bachoo
publish: N/A
abstract: Reinforcement learning for manipulation with real robots is complicated when faced with object variations and contact dynamics. Further, achieving support across a category of objects for manipulation is in itself a challenging task. A recent work that addresses training inefficiencies on a real robot is the Contrastive Pre-training and Data Augmentation for Efficient Robotic Learning (CoDER) framework. It uses a combination of contrastive-pretraining, data augmentation, expert demonstrations and reinforcement learning to achieve sample-efficiency. To improve sample-efficiency further, we used force/torque inputs, pre-trained image encoders, layer normalization, and we warmed up our actor-critic networks before training. In this work, we propose these improvements to the original CoDER for stable and efficient training of a real robot for fine manipulation - we insert medical tubes into a rack. Our policy is trained on a single tube and it is able to support different tubes in the test set. The experimental results show an average insertion success rate of 98.1% across several different types of medical tubes. We achieve this robustness with a policy trained in only 800 episodes over approximately 6 hours using sparse rewards.
