title: Insight Into Neighborhood Information: A Novel Multimodal Sentiment Analysis Approach With Joint Alignment, Fusion, and Denoising
authors: Dongtao Cao, Jiale Zhang, Zheyi Chen, Hongju Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Multimodal sentiment analysis (MSA) is an important research topic for understanding human behavior by fusing information across modalities. Attention mechanisms in multimodal fusion highlight key information and improve recognition performance. However, conventional attention-based fusion methods effectively capture salient sentiment information while potentially overlooking subtle cues. Moreover, noise may arise both before and after multimodal fusion, which can readily disrupt downstream tasks. In this article, we leverage neighborhood information to analyze individual samples, extract contextual cues within neighborhoods, and detect residual noise after multimodal fusion. Accordingly, we propose N-AFD, a method that jointly performs alignment, fusion, and denoising. First, we present a cross-modal alignment method that constructs high-purity neighborhoods in the text representation space and aligns other modalities to text. Then, we employ a cross-modal fusion method based on neighborhood attention to capture subtle signals while preserving dominant semantics, and further extend it to a multispace and multiscale variant to enhance representational capacity. Next, we adopt an outlier-aware denoising method that masks isolated samples to reduce their interference with subsequent learning processes. Finally, extensive experiments on two benchmark datasets validate the effectiveness of the proposed N-AFD for the MSA task and demonstrate its competitive performance against other baseline methods.

title: CEINMS-RT: An Open-Source Framework for the Continuous Neuro-Mechanical Model-Based Control of Wearable Robots_supp1-3643986.pdf
authors: Massimo Sartori
publish: N/A

title: Accuracy and Computational Cost of Machine Learning Models for Electromagnetic Object Classification
authors: Ergun Simsek
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Classification of randomly oriented objects from electromagnetic data is important in applications such as remote sensing and security screening, where robustness to simulation/measurement configuration and data variability is critical. While prior work has shown that attention mechanisms can significantly improve classification performance for long, highly structured electromagnetic scattering sequences, their benefit in simpler data regimes remains unclear. In this numerical work, we study classification using plane-wave excitation and electromagnetic field data recorded by a limited number of receiver antennae, resulting in short input sequences. We show that under these conditions, a simple one-dimensional convolutional neural network achieves nearly the same accuracy as attention-based models. We further compare neural networks with classical machine learning methods, including support vector machines (SVMs) and extreme gradient boosting (XGB), and demonstrate that for large datasets these methods perform comparably to neural networks, whereas neural networks offer a clear advantage in the small-data regime. However, neural networks’ tuning and training times can be substantially higher than those of SVMs and XGB. These results provide practical guidance on selecting model complexity for electromagnetic classification based on dataset size and structure, computational resources, and time.

title: Causality-based Modality&Platform-invariant Representation Learning for Dynamic RGBT Tracking and A Benchmark
authors: Zhaodong Ding, Chenglong Li, Shengqing Miao, Jin Tang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Each sequence in existing RGBT tracking datasets is typically captured from a single platform equipped with both RGB (visible light) and TIR (thermal infrared) sensors. In real-world applications, tracking some objects requires cross-platform collaboration and these platforms might be equipped with different sensors. However, changes in modalities and platforms may cause significant variations in target appearance and abrupt position shifts, which existing RGBT trackers struggle to handle. To address these challenges, we define a new task, termed dynamic RGBT tracking, focusing on cross-platform and modality-variant scenarios. Considering the dynamic changes of modalities and platforms, we investigate dynamic RGBT tracking from a causal perspective, and assume that images consist of causal factors (target-relevant information) and non-causal factors (target-irrelevant information, i.e., modality/platform information), where only the former is conducive to stable tracking. Based on this assumption, we propose a novel causality-based modality&platform-invariant representation learning approach to capture robust invariant representations for dynamic RGBT tracking. In particular, to mitigate the challenges posed by modality variations, we design a causal consistency encoder that introduces an intervener to model feature uncertainty and simulate modal variations, compelling the model to focus on modality-invariant features to improve tracking robustness. To overcome the issue of abrupt view change and position shift, we design a platform-independent global searcher to relocalize the target whenever a platform switch occurs, which leverages an intervener to simulate the interference of platform changes on features, encouraging the searcher to learn platforminvariant representations for improved localization accuracy. In addition, to promote the research and development of dynamic RGBT tracking, we construct a dataset named DRGBT603, which consists of 603 sequences with a total of 1.49 M frame pairs. Extensive experiments on DRGBT603 dataset validate the effectiveness of the proposed method against other state-of-the-art methods. Our code and data are now available: https://github.com/dongdong2061/DRGBT.

title: Harnessing Topological Semantics for Network Topology Diagram Retrieval_supp1-3675542.pdf
authors: Chen Gong
publish: N/A
abstract: Network topology diagram retrieval aims to retrieve the topology diagrams with both visual and topological similarities to a query diagram within the domain of network science and engineering, which can be viewed as a specific image retrieval task. However, current image retrieval approaches are mostly designed for natural images which typically identify the similar candidates by measuring the similarity of visual features. Therefore, these approaches are inherently limited in retrieving network topology diagrams, as they struggle to effectively capture the crucial topological semantics (i.e., the semantics characterized by device roles, connection types, and network structures) embedded in network topology diagrams. To address this issue, we propose the Network Topology-aware Retrieval Framework (NTRF) by incorporating network domain knowledge, which emphasizes the functionality conveyed by the topological semantics to enhance retrieval performance. To be specific, beyond the traditional visual retrieval module, we design a Network Topology Encoder (NTE) to capture the topological semantics by simultaneously representing the device roles, connection types, and network structures. Furthermore, we introduce a SubGraph-aware Re-ranking Module (SGRM) to refine the ranking of candidates, which pays attention to the core diagram regions with significant topological semantics. Intensive experimental results on the collected dataset based on Huawei’s Product Documentation demonstrate that our NTRF outperforms state-of-the-art image retrieval methods by 2.75%, 6.19%, and 9.97% in terms of Recall@1, Recall@5, and Recall@10, respectively.

title: Convergence Analysis and Implementation of Linear Multistep Methods for Solving Ordinary Differential Equations
authors: Rakesh Reddy Thalakanti
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive analysis and implementation of Linear Multistep Methods (LMMs) for solving ordinary differential equations (ODEs). The study delves into the essential criteria for the convergence of LMMs, emphasizing the importance of both zero stability and consistency. An algorithmic approach for verifying convergence is outlined, detailing steps to assess zero stability through characteristic polynomial analysis and consistency through coefficient evaluation. The pa-per also introduces a detailed algorithm for calculating the order of convergence and error constants, providing a foundation for evaluating the accuracy of LMMs. Additionally, the paper covers the practical application of explicit and implicit LMMs in solving ODEs. For explicit methods, a step-by-step algorithm is provided, including the use of the fourth-order Runge-Kutta method for generating initial values. For implicit methods, the PECE (Prediction-Evaluation-Correction-Evaluation) algorithm is discussed, highlighting its effectiveness in enhancing solution accuracy through iterative prediction and correction steps. The outlined algorithms serve as practical guides for implementing LMMs in computational environments, offering robust frame-works for tackling a variety of ODE problems with precision and efficiency.

title: Improved Feature Extraction with Optimization for Offline Signature Verification Using Pretrained ResNet Ensemble and Dimensionality Reduction
authors: Ritika, Dalip
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Offline handwritten signature verification is a difficult biometric task due to a significant degree of variation in authentic signatures. Success of any verification system rely on the quality of feature extraction. Feature extraction from static images of a person’s handwritten signature is a challenging problem, particularly due to character and style differences. Moreover, there is a possibility of sophisticated counterfeit signatures. In this work, an enhanced and optimized framework for feature extraction has been proposed. This approach combines pretrained ResNet 18 and ResNet 34 models in an ensemble setting. Further, dimensionality reduction and classification with SVM are done. Hyperparameter optimization is incorporated to enhance classifier performance. By utilizing deep pretrained networks for comprehensive representation learning and minimizing redundancy, the framework enhances both accuracy and robustness. Experiments conducted on multilingual datasets, including BHsig-260 (Hindi and Bengali) and CEDAR, show that the proposed work has outperformed the state-of-the-art models. The results highlight the effectiveness of integrating improved feature extraction, ensemble learning, and dimensionality reduction for reliable offline signature verification.

title: AI-Driven Predictive Maintenance in Industry 5.0
authors: Harshit Kohli, Bala Krishna Rao Guntupalli, Sonali Suri, Rahul P. Mahajan, Mohamed Affiq
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Industry 5.0 is a paradigm shift that promotes collaboration between humans and machines, sustainable production, and resilient industrial ecosystems. In this context, predictive maintenance (PdM) has become a pillar towards operational efficiency, minimized downtime, and resource maximization. Conventional condition-based monitoring systems are generally not as scalable and adaptable as needed in extremely dynamic systems. In this paper, we present an AI-based predictive maintenance solution that combines machine learning, deep learning, and real-time data analytics with cyber-physical systems and Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) architectures. The framework utilizes sensor fusion, digital twin technology, and explainable AI models to identify anomalies, predict equipment failures, and prescribe proactive anti-equipment intervention with a high accuracy. Moreover, the system is focused on sustainability by reducing the amount of wasted energy and increasing the lifespan of equipment, which is also in line with Industry 5.0 that emphasizes the production process as eco-sustainable and human-friendly. Experimental analyses show better detection of faults, fewer false positives, and better decision support than traditional approaches. The conclusions suggest that AI-enabled predictive maintenance can become a powerful facilitator of intelligent, resilient, and sustainable Industry 5.0 ecosystems.

title: Existence of AC Power Flow Solutions via the Multidimensional Intermediate Value Theorem
authors: Chenxi Hu, Naichao Chang, Qiang Ding, Yunhe Hou
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Reliable initialization of alternating-current (AC) power flow analysis commonly relies on direct-current (DC) approximations; however, whether a feasible DC operating point always admits a corresponding AC solution in its vicinity remains an open theoretical question. This question is critical for guaranteeing the convergence, stability, and physical validity of large-scale grid computations. The difficulty stems from the nonlinear, trigonometric coupling in the AC equations, which renders standard iterative solvers effective but devoid of formal existence guarantees. This paper establishes a rigorous analytical framework for certifying the existence of AC power flow solutions corresponding to DC operating points. A power-difference mapping is formulated between the AC and DC models, whose Taylor expansion around the DC solution enables explicit characterization of the local angle correction. The multidimensional intermediate value theorem is employed to derive verifiable sufficient conditions for existence and uniqueness under realistic transmission parameters. Analytical bounds on higher-order terms further quantify the accuracy of the first-order correction. Numerical results on a representative transmission system confirm the theoretical predictions and demonstrate the engineering relevance of the proposed method. The proposed framework provides verifiable existence conditions for AC power flow solutions, offering a rigorous foundation for reliable initialization and secure operation in large-scale grid analysis.

title: Performance Testing and Improvement Methods for Fault Ride-Through Capability of Megawatt-Scale Hydrogen Fuel Cell Grid-Connected Systems
authors: Tingwang Wang, Yongji Li, Libin Zhao, Xiaopei Li, Yuxuan Li, Junlei Long
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: To enhance the fault ride-through capability of megawatt-scale hydrogen fuel cell grid-connected systems, this paper proposes a collaborative optimization scheme for energy storage system and grid-side inverter control. The scheme first constructs a fuel cell-battery hybrid power generation unit, with the energy storage system stabilizing power fluctuations, allowing the fuel cell to operate stably in an efficient constant power mode, ensuring power balance from the “source” side. Then, addressing the key issues during grid faults, a collaborative control strategy for the grid-side inverter is designed: the Lagrange multiplier method is used to solve for the optimal distribution ratio of positive and negative sequence currents, to maximize support and suppression of the grid-connected point voltage. The active fluctuation constraints of the inverter itself are strictly considered to ensure equipment safety. Simulation results show that in the event of an asymmetric grid fault, this collaborative strategy can effectively enhance the grid-connected point voltage level, and limit power fluctuations and current surges during the fault to a safe range, significantly enhancing the system's fault ride-through stability.

title: Cognitive Cyber Deception: An Agentic AI Framework for Dynamically Adaptive Collaborative Honeypots
authors: Jyoti Kanjalkar, Hitanshi Sahni, Ishan Agrawal, Sahishnu G, Hitanshi Meshram, Viraj Divekar, Pramod Kanjalkar
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The escalating sophistication of cyber threats necessitates a paradigm shift from static, signature-based defenses to proactive, intelligent, and adaptive security measures. Honeypots, a foundational tool in cyber deception, have traditionally been limited by their static nature, rendering them susceptible to fingerprinting and evasion by skilled adversaries. This paper presents a novel framework for a cognitive, adaptive honeypot system that leverages an agentic Generative AI framework to create a dynamic and evolving deception environment. Our system architecture integrates distributed medium-interaction Cowrie honeypots with a periodic data pipeline using the MQTT protocol, which transfers attacker activity logs to a central analysis module for deep semantic analysis. This module, whose logic is partitioned into several functional roles, interprets attacker intent, identifies complex Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures (TTPs), and autonomously generates tailored bash scripts to dynamically reconfigure the honeypot’s environment, thereby enhancing its realism and prolonging attacker engagement. A Pinecone vector database provides the system with long-term contextual memory, enabling the recognition of recurring attack patterns and facilitating more sophisticated, stateful deception strategies. Comparative analysis demonstrates that our adaptive honeypot detains attackers over 400% longer than a static counterpart, captures a significantly richer dataset of malicious activities, and successfully elicits more advanced attack tools and payloads. This research pioneers a closed-loop, AI-driven deception-feedback mechanism, laying the groundwork for next-generation autonomous cyber defense systems.

title: Second-order Robust Iterative Pose Optimization for Fine-grained Cross-View Localization
authors: Mingtao Feng, Xin Li, Jianqiao Luo, Weisheng Dong, Yaonan Wang, Ajmal Mian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Fine-grained cross-view localization seeks to estimate precise camera poses by matching ground images with GPS-tagged aerial imagery. Existing methods typically employ first-order iterative optimization to progressively update the camera pose based on cross-view feature correspondences. However, they rely on local features and neglect global and complementary contextual information, making them prone to local optima and slow convergence under large initial errors or strong disturbances. To overcome these limitations, we propose a second-order robust iterative pose estimation framework for fine-grained cross-view localization. Firstly, we devise a second-order deep iterative optimization module to capture complementary forward and backward motion cues, leading to a bidirectional correlation volume. A motion aggregator uses the volume to approximate the dynamics of second-order iterators, substantially facilitating convergence and robustness. In addition, a bidirectional motion-aware robust regularization module mitigates geometric distortions and outlier interference by leveraging bidirectional motion cues to generate fine-grained confidence maps, adaptively suppressing unreliable regions and enhancing the stability of iterative optimization and pose estimation accuracy. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves faster convergence and higher pose estimation accuracy than state-of-the-art methods, particularly under large initial errors and challenging conditions.

title: Dynamic Wireless Control Networks based on Cell-Free Massive MIMO
authors: Xinhua Wang, Chao Zhai, Zheng Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: This paper investigates a dynamic wireless control network leveraging cell-free massive MIMO, targeting future 6G vertical application scenarios such as indoor Internet of Things (IoT) deployments. To accommodate more user equipments (UEs), the non-orthogonal random pilots are adopted for channel estimation, which poses challenges for subsequent performance analysis and optimization.We first investigate the statistical properties of channel estimate of different UEs, and derive the closed-form expression of achievable rates. Considering the randomness of data arrivals, we then formulate a stochastic optimization problem aimed at maximizing the minimum long-term successful transmission probability, where both the scheduling policy and power allocation strategy are jointly optimized. Using Lyapunov optimization theory, we transform the max-min time average problem into a series of mixed-integer optimization problems for each time slot, which can be solved using our proposed second-order cone programming based shrinking (SOCPS) algorithm. Finally, simulation results show that the joint scheduling and power allocation strategy can boost the transmission reliability under latency constrains.

title: Modeling and Experimental Investigation of Time-Varying Joint Impedance in a Human–Machine Coupled Shoulder–Elbow System
authors: Jianbin Liu, Yechao He, Haitao Liu, Weidong Liu
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Existing studies on time-varying joint impedance identification are mostly confined to single-joint scenarios, while multi-joint coordinated motion involving human-machine coupling lacks effective solutions. To address this challenge, this paper focuses on the upper limb shoulder-elbow dual-joint system and proposes an integrated solution covering modeling, trajectory design, identification, and experimental verification. A dynamic model of the human-machine coupled system is established via the Newton-Euler method, incorporating elastic strap constraints, friction characteristics, and human time-varying impedance. A Fourier series excitation trajectory improved by quintic polynomials is designed to balance system observability and human motion safety. An optimized "short-time linear time-varying" identification method combined with QR decomposition is proposed to solve the rank deficiency problem, enabling simultaneous estimation of constant parameters and time-varying impedance coefficients for the multi-input multi-output (MIMO) system. A wearable upper limb exoskeleton platform is developed to realize real-time data acquisition of joint kinematics and dynamics. Simulation and physical experiments (with 3 subjects) verify the method’s effectiveness: the root mean square error (RMSE) of joint torque is as low as 4.23×10⁻⁶Nm and 1.84×10⁻⁶Nm for the two joints in noise-free simulations, in simulations with Gaussian white noise (average amplitude 0.1), the RMSE of the proposed method is 1.636Nm and 1.274Nm, reducing errors by 25.3% and 74.6% compared to the reference method. In physical experiments, the RMSE ranges from 1.67 to 2.84 Nm.

title: HPE-CogVLM: Advancing Vision Language Models with a Head Pose Grounding Task
authors: Yu Tian, Tianqi Shao, Tsukasa Demizu, Xuyang Wu, Hsin-Tai Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Head pose estimation (HPE) requires a sophisticated understanding of 3D spatial relationships to generate precise yaw, pitch, and roll angles. Previous HPE models, primarily CNN-based, rely on cropped close-up human head images as inputs and often lack robustness in real-world scenario. Vision Language Models (VLMs) can analyze entire images while focusing on specific objects through their attention mechanisms. In this paper, we propose a novel framework to improve the HPE accuracy by leveraging the object detection grounding capability of a VLM, referred to as CogVLM. We empirically find that directly LoRA fine-tuning of this VLM for the HPE task fails to achieve desirable HPE accuracy, while some model merging methods can improve accuracy but frequently produce blended invalid response formats, struggling to handle both object detection and HPE tasks simultaneously. To integrate HPE capability into CogVLM effectively, we develop a novel LoRA layer-based model merging method. This merging approach applies a high cosine similarity threshold and a “winner-takes-all” layer selection strategy, aligning attention to the HPE task while preserving original object detection knowledge. It successfully resolves issues with blended invalid response formats and improves accuracy. Results show that our HPE-CogVLM achieves a 31.5% reduction in Mean Absolute Error over the current state-of-the-art CNN model, 6DRepNet, in cross-dataset evaluation. Furthermore, HPE-CogVLM outperforms both directly LoRA fine-tuned and task arithmetic-based merged VLMs across all HPE metrics.

title: Generative AI-Empowered Signal Processing for Collaborative Embodied Agents: A Survey on Agentic Security
authors: Gaofeng Pan, Yi Tao, Ziyi Yang, Junnan Li, Yuanyuan Peng, Xuemin Bao, Yiyang Zhang, Zizheng Hua, Shuai Wang, Rui Zhang, Changhao Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: The transition from the Internet of Things (IoT) to the Internet of Agents (IoA) necessitates a fundamental paradigm shift in physical layer security, moving from static, rule-based defenses toward autonomous, agentic resilience. However, current embodied nodes often lack the cognitive capability required to handle adaptive, intelligent threats. We propose employing Generative AI (GAI) as the cognitive engine—rather than merely a signal processing tool—to empower agents to construct an internal world model of the physical environment. In this work, we present a holistic agentic security framework that instills autonomy across two key dimensions. First, Endogenous Self-Evolution allows agents to utilize GAI for predictive environmental modeling and autonomous resource decision-making, thereby self-calibrating against hardware impairments. Second, Exogenous Active Defense implements a generative observeorient-decide-act (OODA) loop. This loop enables agents to autonomously execute intelligent threat perception, confrontation planning, and a strategic duality of behavioral concealment and identity camouflage. Finally, we discuss the path toward general-purpose security agents via neuro-symbolic architectures. This survey bridges the gap between signal generation and agentic reasoning, defining a new era where security becomes an innate, evolving property of collaborative embodied intelligence.

title: A Dual-Function Chipless RFID System for IoT Soil Moisture Sensing and Tag Identification Using Complex Natural Resonance
authors: Sirisak Nongpromma, Akkarat Boonpoonga, Kamol Keamarungsi, Prayoot Akkaraekthalin
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: This paper proposes a dual-function chipless RFID system that simultaneously performs soil moisture sensing and tag identification using complex natural resonances (CNRs) enhanced by a machine-learning approach, essential for scalable Internet of Things (IoT) applications in smart agriculture. The system leverages CNRs, extracted from a chipless RFID tag’s response using the short-time matrix pencil method (STMPM), and processes them via a machine-learning framework. For soil moisture sensing, the highest-order CNRs are utilized with a linear regression model. For tag identification, the remaining CNRs, combined with the estimated moisture, enable robust tag identification using a support vector machine (SVM) to construct a 3D decision boundary. Experimental evaluations on soil samples with interdigital capacitor (IDC)-based chipless RFID tags for sensing varying moisture levels validate the system’s efficacy. The results show that CNRs vary with soil moisture, yielding superior sensing accuracy compared with natural frequency-only methods. The CNR-SVM approach achieved tag identification accuracy up to 92.19%, substantially outperforming conventional frequency-based or polynomial fitting methods. This improvement results from the inherent ability of CNRs to expand the feature space, enhancing data separability and decision boundary definition for both sensing and identification, which is valuable for scalable smart agricultural.

title: MARS: A Multi-Agent Collaborative Reasoning Framework for Service Recommendation
authors: Mingyi Liu, Zijie Yin, Congcong Tian, Shuang Yu, Taotao Cai, Zhihui Xu, Zhongjie Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Services Computing
abstract: Service recommendation for mashup development faces critical challenges due to the sparse usage history of newly introduced mashups and APIs, as well as the difficulty of inferring genuine API dependencies from mashup compositions, where APIs are often co-used in a noisy and implicit manner. Traditional collaborative filtering, content-based methods, and standalone LLM-based approaches have limitations in jointly addressing these challenges within a unified framework. We propose MARS, a multi-agent collaborative recommendation framework that systematically integrates semantic alignment, structure-aware retrieval, and validation-based recommendation under a constrained candidate space. MARS incorporates multiple algorithmic components to improve different stages of the service recommendation process. Specifically, agent-driven semantic enrichment substantially mitigates cross-representation semantic mismatch between mashups and APIs, reducing the average Jensen-Shannon distance from 0.7333 to 0.6333, while baseline methods exhibit negligible changes. Structure-aware fine-tuning captures API compositional patterns beyond surface-level semantics, and data-driven weight optimization learns the fusion weights in the hybrid retrieval stage, replacing static retrieval parameters with empirically calibrated strategies. Finally, multi-agent collaborative reasoning enhances robustness by combining diverse proposal generation with validation-based selection. Experiments on ProgrammableWeb dataset demonstrate that MARS consistently outperforms representative baselines, achieving 63.31% Recall@5 compared to 58.28% for Native RAG and 43.35% for the best traditional method (ServeNet). The results indicate that MARS provides an effective and extensible framework for improving mashup-oriented service recommendation. Our code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/banirabbit/mars</uri>.

title: Toward Cognitive Autonomy in UAV Networks: Empowering Intelligence with Multi-Modal Large Language Models
authors: Baolin Yin, Xuming Fang, Xianbin Wang
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: As unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) networks become increasingly critical for diverse applications such as disaster response and commercial services, the demand for situation-aware UAV network operation continues to rise. Traditional UAV network operations are typically context- unaware, resulting in limited adaptability to rapidly changing environments. To cope with this challenge, we propose a cognitively autonomous framework for UAV networks empowered by multi-modal large language models (MMLLMs). The proposed framework integrates environmental cognition, intelligent decision- making, feedback optimization, and domain knowledge comprehension enhanced through retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). By leveraging heterogeneous onboard sensors, UAVs collect multi-modal data, enabling real-time environment cognition and achieving semantic- and intent-level comprehension via MMLLMs. Furthermore, advanced deep reinforcement learning (DRL) techniques are incorporated, facilitating adaptive decision-making and dynamic network control under varying conditions. The embedded RAG capability allows seamless integration of the latest domain-specific knowledge, including recent research findings, technical standards, and environmental prior knowledge. Feedback optimization continually refines network policies based on real-time evaluations, ensuring adaptability to evolving network dynamics. Simulation results show that our proposed framework substantially enhances UAV network performance and autonomy.

title: AI-Empowered Channel Generation for IoV Semantic Communications in Dynamic Conditions
authors: Hao Liu, Bo Yang, Zhiwen Yu, Xuelin Cao, George C. Alexandropoulos, Yan Zhang, Chau Yuen
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: The Internet of Vehicles (IoV) is transforming the transportation ecosystem, enabling pervasive connectivity and data-driven approaches. Deep learning and generative artificial intelligence (GAI) have the potential to significantly enhance IoV application performance by enabling efficient decision- making and predictive capabilities, including intelligent navigation, vehicle safety monitoring, and intelligent traffic management. Nevertheless, efficiently transmitting and processing the massive volumes of data generated by the IoV in real time remains a significant challenge, particularly in dynamic, unpredictable wireless channel conditions. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a semantic communication (SC) framework based on a CNN-Transformer architecture that provides Channel estimation via Diffusion (CTCD) to improve the accuracy and efficiency of data transmission. The SC model extracts and compresses the information to be transmitted. The wireless channel is then estimated using a generative diffusion model to predict dynamic channel states, thereby improving the quality of IoV services. In dynamic scenarios, however, channel estimation performance may degrade when substantially new scenarios occur, adversely affecting the user experience. To mitigate this limitation, we fine-tune the channel generation model to enhance its adaptability across varying scenarios. The performance and reliability of the proposed framework are evaluated on two public image datasets, and the framework outperforms the comparative methods in image reconstruction metrics.

title: Probabilistic Fusion Framework Based on Fully Convolutional Networks and Graphical Models for Burnt Area Detection from Multiresolution Satellite and UAV Imagery
authors: Martina Pastorino, Gabriele Moser, Fabien Guerra, Sebastiano B. Serpico, Josiane Zerubia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Mapping areas affected by wildfires is an important task for fire disaster management. When dealing with these applications, it is common to have access to images of the same scene collected by very different acquisition systems. The development of processing methods capable to benefit from these multimodal datasets is promising for wildfire management but is generally challenging. This paper addresses the semantic segmentation of zones affected by forest fires from input multimodal imagery collected by both unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) and satellite platforms. The multiresolution fusion task is especially challenging in this case because the difference between the involved spatial resolutions is very large – a situation that is normally not addressed by traditional multiresolution schemes. Two novel multiresolution fusion approaches, based on deep learning, Bayesian and probabilistic graphical fusion models are proposed. The first method performs the multiresolution fusion of the multimodal imagery via a probabilistic decision fusion framework, after computing posteriors on the multiresolution data separately with deep neural networks or decision tree ensembles. The optimization of the parameters of the model is fully automated through an approximate formulation of the expectation maximization (EM) algorithm. The second proposed method performs the multiresolution fusion of the multimodal data through a probabilistic graphical model defined on a pyramidal tree structure, where the imagery can be inserted, modeled, and analyzed at their native resolutions. The experimental validation was conducted with three real datasets consisting of UAV and satellite data collected over the South of France over areas affected by wildfires.

title: Optimistic Synchronization-Based Server Allocation with Preventive Start-Time Optimization Under Server Failure in Delay-Sensitive Applications
authors: Masaki Oda, Imran Ahmed, Akio Kawabata, Eiji Oki
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Real-time applications require low latency and strict event ordering to ensure seamless operation. Distributed server processing is effective for this purpose, and there are two synchronization algorithms: a conservative synchronization algorithm (CSA) and an optimistic synchronization algorithm (OSA). OSA improves delay performance compared to CSA. While prior studies have considered OSA, they have not incorporated the impact of server failures. This paper proposes an OSA-based server allocation model for delay-sensitive applications with preventive start-time optimization (PreSO) under single-server failures (OSA-PreSO). The proposed OSA-PreSO model minimizes the largest total delay across all failure scenarios while satisfying constraints in OSA with PreSO under single-server failures. We formulate the proposed model as an integer linear programming (ILP) problem. In OSA-PreSO, the objective is to minimize the largest total delay across all failure scenarios, without giving special consideration to the total delay in the no-failure scenario. As a result, a penalty arises in the form of an increased total delay in the no-failure scenario. To reduce the penalty, we develop an improved OSA-PreSO model, OSA-PreSO-LP (low-penalty), which reduces the total delay in the nofailure scenario while maintaining the same delay characteristics in failure scenarios. We prove that the decision version of OSA-PreSO is NP-complete. We introduce heuristic algorithms to handle large-scale problems. Numerical results show that the proposed OSA-PreSO model reduces the delay compared to the conventional CSA-based model by effectively utilizing server memory resources. We observe that the proposed model achieves a lower largest total delay than start-time optimization and provides greater stability by preventing unnecessary user reassignments compared to run-time optimization. Numerical results also show that OSA-PreSO-LP reduces the penalty at most by 83%, while maintaining the same delay characteristics in failure scenarios.

title: Fine-Grained Uncertainty Quantification via Collisions
authors: Jesse Friedbaum, Sudarshan Adiga, Ravi Tandon
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: We propose a new and intuitive metric for aleatoric uncertainty quantification (UQ), the prevalence of class collisions defined as the same input being observed in different classes. We use the rate of class collisions to define the collision matrix, a novel and uniquely fine-grained measure of uncertainty. For a classification problem involving K classes, the K × K collision matrix S measures the inherent difficulty in distinguishing between each pair of classes. We discuss several applications of the collision matrix, establish its fundamental mathematical properties, and show its relationship with existing UQ methods, including the Bayes error rate (BER). We also address the new problem of estimating the collision matrix using one-hot labeled data by proposing a series of innovative techniques to estimate S. First, we learn a pair-wise contrastive model which accepts two inputs and determines if they belong to the same class. We then show that this contrastive model (which is PAC learnable) can be used to estimate the row Gramian matrix of S, defined as G = SST . Finally, we show that under reasonable assumptions, G can be used to uniquely recover S, a new result on non-negative matrices which could be of independent interest. With a method to estimate S established, we demonstrate how this estimate of S, in conjunction with the contrastive model, can be used to estimate the posterior class probability distribution of any point. Experimental results are also presented to validate our methods of estimating the collision matrix and class posterior distributions on several datasets.

title: Robust Moving Horizon Estimation with Diverse Prior Predictions for Nonlinear Systems
authors: H. Arezki, A. Zemouche, A. Alessandri
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: This paper addresses robust stability analysis for moving horizon estimation (MHE) in nonlinear systems and proposes new conditions for tuning the MHE cost function parameters. These conditions are directly linked to the MHE window size and the incremental exponential input/output-to-state stability (i-EIOSS) coefficients of the system. Our approach begins by introducing a general constraint equation within the MHE optimization program, combined with a filtering prior prediction technique. This equation incorporates an auxiliary dynamical system assumed to satisfy the i-EIOSS property. To further enhance the robustness of MHE stability conditions while reducing the required window size, we integrate this auxiliary system with advanced prediction techniques, marking a significant advancement in MHE design. Finally, comparative discussions and numerical evaluations are included to clarify the differences and connections between our proposed method and existing approaches in the literature.

title: Partitioning and Observability in Linear Systems via Submodular Optimization
authors: Mohamad H. Kazm, Ahmad F. Taha
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: Network partitioning has gained recent attention as a pathway to enable decentralized operation and control in large-scale systems. This paper addresses the interplay between partitioning, observability, and sensor placement (SP) in dynamic networks. The problem, being computationally intractable at scale, is a largely unexplored, open problem in the literature. To that end, the paper's objective is designing scalable partitioning of linear systems while maximizing observability metrics of the subsystems. We show that the partitioning problem can be posed as a submodular maximization problem—and the SP problem can subsequently be solved over the partitioned network. Consequently, theoretical bounds are derived to compare observability metrics of the original network with those of the resulting partitions, highlighting the impact of partitioning on system observability. Case studies on networks of varying sizes corroborate the derived theoretical bounds.

title: The Application of Artificial Intelligence in the Teaching of Ideological and Political Theory Courses
authors: Chunyan Zhang, Ziping Wei
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence
abstract: None

title: Power System Balance Capabilities Analysis Considering Flexiramp Potential From Dvfs-Controlled Data Center Clusters
authors: Qian Ma, Shuchen Kang, Jialu Li, Yihang Cao, Fangneng Huang, Aoyu Lei, Zhenyuan Zhang, Meiying Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Data center clusters (DCCs) are increasingly recognized as the main demand consumers for AI device development and deployment. To alleviate the risk of lack-of-flexiramp risk during the entire scheduling and marketing process, the potential of DCCs to reallocate workloads across multiple DCCs through workload balancing is investigated. The process of workload reallocation across multiple DCCs, facilitated through dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) control schemes, offers substantial flexibility potential and can support the secure operation of power systems. Through DVFS-based control hosted in an individual DCC device, the workload allocations process for each DCC is mathematically modeled and subsequently reshaped as a group of linearized constraints through the McCormick envelope technique. On this basis, a novel power system balance capabilities analysis method is constructed to capture the flexiramp potential from multiple DCCs. The resulting models are evaluated on an IEEE 6-bus test system to demonstrate the validity and scalability of the proposed method.

title: Digit-Grid: An Open-Source Framework for AI-Assisted Digital Power Systems
authors: Zhichuan Ma, Zhilong Cao, Zhanhua Pan, Zhong Fan, Dawei Qiu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The decarbonization of energy and the rapid expansion of wind, solar, and electrified demand are fundamentally reshaping the Great Britain (GB) power system, bringing significant uncertainty and complexity. Conventional studies often depend on simplified test networks or proprietary tools, which restrict realism and limit transparency. To address this, we develop an open-source, AI-assisted digital twin of the GB grid that integrates real transmission, generation, and demand data with a DC optimal power flow (DCOPF) model to jointly optimize energy cost and carbon intensity. In contrast to autonomous black-box tools, large language models (LLMs) serve as structured intermediaries, querying optimization models and datasets to generate verifiable results. This framework connects real data, optimization, and AI interaction, enhancing transparency, accessibility, and reproducibility, while offering a practical platform for carbon-aware power system analysis and policy support.

title: Adaptive Microgrid Energy Management Under Power Flow Uncertainty Using Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Niphon Kaewdornhan, Kenichi Kawabe, Rongrit Chatthaworn
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Nowadays, a low-voltage power distribution system is being developed as a more self-reliant power system by installing solar PhotoVoltaic (PV) systems and using Electric Vehicles (EVs), defined as a MicroGrid (MG). However, due to the uncertain behaviors of PV generation and EV charging, the power flow within the MG is difficult to estimate, which can lead to violations of the operational constraints of the MG. To control the power flow as desired, a Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) controlled by an Energy Management System (EMS) is installed. The EMS must have adaptive capabilities to control the BESS when the power flow changes. To this end, this paper proposes improving EMS performance by using Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) to provide a robust EMS with excellent adaptive capability when power flow uncertainty occurs. The power flow uncertainty is generated by adding noise from a Gaussian distribution to the original load profile of the MG for training and testing the EMS. Moreover, the performance of the proposed EMS is compared with state-of-the-art EMS approaches, such as rule-based EMS and metaheuristic EMS. The simulation results show that the proposed EMS outperforms state-of-the-art EMS approaches by providing more effective BESS control, achieving lower daily total related costs, and preventing transformer overloading and BESS operation violations under power flow uncertainty.

title: Refined Sequential Operation Simulations Considering Power Mapping Interactions Between Clustered and Individual Units
authors: Qian Ma, Yihang Cao, He Huang, Chenyao Pang, Yuechao Jin, Jalu Li, Zhenyuan Zhang, Xiaosheng Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Conventional temporal operation simulation methods adopt a technical framework of embedded time series and rolling optimization to characterize the spatio-temporal complementary characteristics of power sources and multi-scale power and energy balance capabilities under the background of the new-type power system. Nevertheless, the temporal operation simulation and optimization model with individual units as scheduling units features a highly complex structure and a highly difficult solution process. To address this issue, this paper proposes a clustering method for aggregated units adapted to the temporal operation simulation of the new-type power system, achieving model structure dimensionality reduction by constructing a mapping relationship from individual units to aggregated units. This paper analyzes the corresponding relationship between the constraint set of aggregated units and the constraints of individual units, and constructs additional constraint conditions reflecting the consistency of ramping capacity from aggregated units to individual units on the premise of avoiding the simultaneous on/off of aggregated units. Tests are conducted on the IEEE 30-bus test system, verifying the feasibility and superiority of the proposed method.

title: An Pearson Verification Algorithm of User-Transformer Connectivity in a Low Voltage Distribution Network
authors: Huiun Hong, Yiru Yang, Ying Chen, Xiangwen Zhao, Jiaying Hu, Huashi Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Current methods used to verify topological structures of low voltage distribution networks recorded in databases are expensive and do not deliver results in real time. To address this problem, this paper presents an online verification algorithm based on the Pearson correlation coefficient for user-transformer connectivity in a low-voltage distribution network. Firstly, the similarity of the voltage data of the low-voltage user is analyzed. Secondly, based on the voltage data from low-voltage user meters, the proposed Pearson Algorithm validates user-transformer connectivity within a neighborhood of users through an iterative process, identifying users incorrectly associated with a transformer in the database.

title: From Classroom to Entrepreneurship: An Analysis of Entrepreneurial Intention Among Engineering Students at an Ecuadorian University
authors: Mayra D'Armas Regnault, Pedro Noboa Romero, Luis Castillo Salvatiera, Agustín Mejías
publish: 2025 World Engineering Education Forum - Global Engineering Deans Council (WEEF-GEDC)
abstract: This study analyzes the factors influencing entrepreneurial intention among engineering students at an Ecuadorian university, applying Ajzen's Theory of Planned Behavior. Through a non-experimental quantitative approach, 254 students were surveyed using validated scales that assessed personal attitude, subjective norms, perceived behavioral control, entrepreneurial intention, and eight entrepreneurial competencies. The results reveal a favorable entrepreneurial profile characterized by positive attitudes and strong social support, but limited by low perception of practical self-efficacy. Perceived behavioral control obtained the lowest score, identified as the main barrier to materializing business initiatives. In entrepreneurial competencies, interpersonal skills predominate over technical-business skills. Significantly, no gender differences were found in entrepreneurial intention, challenging traditional stereotypes. The analysis revealed a statistically significant interaction effect between work experience and knowledge of entrepreneurs, demonstrating that these factors operate synergistically. The findings suggest the need to strengthen technical-business competencies and develop comprehensive educational strategies that combine practical experiences with exposure to successful entrepreneurial models to maximize entrepreneurial potential in engineering students.

title: Reforming the Curriculum and Assessment System of the HVDC Courses through Engineering Practice-Oriented Diversification
authors: YingXue, Yanpeng Hao, Huang Qing Xiao, Chunguang Zhou, Yan Wang, Xin He, Siqi Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the accelerating transition of the energy structure toward low-carbon development, the field of DC power transmission places increasingly high demands on the cultivation of high-quality and innovative talent. The curriculum system plays a critical role in ensuring the direction and quality of talent development. However, traditional high-voltage direct current (HVDC) courses are typically limited to classroom-based instruction, with assessment processes dominated by theoretical examinations. This leads to inefficient knowledge transfer, a disconnect from engineering practice, and a lack of alignment with technological frontiers. To address these issues, the teaching team of Advanced HVDC Transmission Technology and Intelligent Control (TTA) has adopted engineering practice as its core orientation. Through close collaboration with leading enterprises in the industry, the TTA has systematically reconstructed the course structure and developed a four-part integrated curriculum framework that includes fundamental theory, engineering case studies, simulation-based practice, and innovative design. On this basis, a diversified assessment system has been established, integrating process-oriented assessment, competency-based evaluation, and multi-stakeholder participation, forming a “four-module, three-orientation, two-entity” model. This marks a shift from single-outcome assessment to comprehensive, process-based evaluation of student competencies. The reform has significantly enhanced the engineering relevance, frontier alignment, and overall educational effectiveness of the course, providing a replicable and scalable model for the integrated innovation of curriculum design and evaluation mechanisms in HVDC-related education.

title: Middleware-Based Solutions for Challenges in Distributed Database Systems: A Comprehensive Review and Proposed Strategies
authors: Nishi Tadamalla
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Distributed Database Systems (DDBS) are essential for scalable, fault-tolerant data management, yet face significant challenges in concurrency control, replication consistency, deadlock management, and resource allocation. This paper provides a comprehensive review of these issues and proposes a middleware-based synchronization model that combines master-slave and peer-to-peer paradigms. Our approach introduces a distributed conflict-resolution queue synchronized via a mutex mechanism, decoupling read-write operations to improve concurrency. Theoretical analysis and discrete-event simulations indicate up to 27% improved throughput over traditional Two-Phase Locking (2PL) protocols, demonstrating the viability of middleware-enhanced DDBS architectures for scalable industrial deployment.

title: Automated Lung Cancer Detection from CT Images Using Hybrid GLCM-CNN Features and SVM Classification with ROI Localization
authors: Rakshitha Kanneboina, Muntha Raju, Bhoomeshwar Bala, Arathi Chitla, K. Nagi Reddy, Raja Shekar Kadurka
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Lung cancer is a major problem of cancer-related deaths, and its survival rate is closely tied to early and accurate diagnosis. Computed Tomography (CT) imaging is mostly used for lung cancer detection, but manual interpretation is consumes more time, subjective, and prone to mistakes in early-stage nodules. To address these limitations, this study presents an automated detection framework that combines handcrafted and deep learning features with robust classification. Texture features are extracted using the Gray-Level Co-occurrence Matrix (GLCM), while high-level semantic features are learned through a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). An automated Region of Interest (ROI) localization technique is used to accurately identify candidate nodule regions, eliminating the need for manual annotation and improving reproducibility. The fused GLCM-CNN feature set is then categorized with the help of Support Vector Machine (SVM), chosen for its efficiency to maintain high-dimensional medical data. Experiments were conducted on the IQOTH/NCCD dataset, which includes malignant, benign, and normal lung CT cases. The proposed system achieved 99% accuracy, outperforming conventional single-feature approaches. These results show that combining handcrafted and deep features with automated ROI localization provides a reliable and clinically relevant tool for supporting radiologists in early lung cancer detection.

title: Metaheuristic-Optimized Deep Learning Framework for Multimodal Imaging in Cancer Prognosis and Survival Prediction
authors: PradeepKumar G.M, Murthy S V N
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Proper prognosis and survival prediction is still one of the main challenges in cancer treatment since multimodal medical imaging data are heterogeneous. The traditional deep learning models have demonstrated impressive relationships with the medical image analysis, but their performance frequently becomes limited by the problems of the poor choice of features, low generalization, and extreme sensitivity to the parameters. In order to overcome those shortcomings, in this paper The Metaheuristic-Optimized Deep Learning Framework of multimodal imaging in cancer prognosis and survival prediction will be proposed. The framework combines state-of-the-art deep learning designs with population-based metaheuristic optimization algorithms, which allow efficient hyperparameter optimization, feature combination, and robust training with a wide range of imaging modalities, such as CT, MRI, PET, and histopathology. The proposed framework, with the help of multimodal fusion strategies, would increase the level of predictive accuracy and reliability and decrease model overfitting. Early trainings on standardized cancer imaging datasets indicate a higher performance than traditional deep learning baselines, and as significant as an increase in the accuracy of survival predictions and increased concordance index scores. This paper points out the opportunities of integrating metaheuristic optimization with deep learning to develop adaptive, generalizable, and clinically relevant models to be applied to oncology.

title: Predictive Execution of Workflows in a HPC+Cloud Environment
authors: Subhendu Behera, Jae-Seung Yeom, Daniel Milroy, Marc Niethammer, Frank Mueller
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: Meeting deadlines for data-intensive workflows on HPC systems is challenging as jobs experience varying wait times before resources become available. This impact is significant in hybrid HPC+Cloud scheduling, which can lead to resource idleness, deadline violations, and higher costs. To address these issues, we propose scheduling data-intensive workflows over a combined HPC+Cloud hybrid environment in a deterministic manner by scavenging unused HPC resources. We predict resource availability (RA) of HPC systems, and exploit this prediction to dynamically split resource allocation between HPC's unused and Cloud's on-demand resources to complete a workflow by a given deadline. The deterministic resource allocation allows for preloading input data for workflow tasks, avoiding execution delays. Further, we develop an adaptive scaling algorithm that effectively backs up the targeted HPC allocation on Cloud facilities to avoid workflow execution delays in the event of incorrect RA estimation. Experiments show that our scheduling technique imposes minimal impact on HPC production jobs, saves cost for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>75 \%$</tex> workflow runs, suggests accurate budgets with a mean 7.11 % to 14.75 % cost estimation error, and finishes a mean 98 % to 99.4 % of tasks before deadlines.

title: Adaptive Load Shedding Using Deep Reinforcement Learning for High Renewable Penetration Grids
authors: Tian Wang, Feng Hua, Mierzati Dilimulati, Boyuan Pan, Ruilin Yang, Hailiang Zhang, Xiaolei Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: With rapid wind power integration worldwide, power systems face unprecedented frequency stability challenges due to reduced system inertia and increased uncertainty. This paper develops adaptive underfrequency load shedding strategies for high wind penetration grids using multi-objective deep reinforcement learning optimization. An intelligent algorithm incorporating real-time inertia identification and uncertainty quantification handles wind power variability and dynamic operating conditions. Probabilistic methods model wind power stochastic characteristics, while multi-time scale frequency response modeling captures system dynamics. By optimizing load shedding parameters through real-time estimation and risk-based decision making, the strategy enhances frequency stability while minimizing unnecessary load curtailment. Case analysis demonstrates significant improvements: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 6. 8 \%}$</tex> average reduction in total load shedding, minimum frequency enhancement of 0.47 Hz, and 26.6 % faster recovery time compared to traditional schemes, providing a robust solution for frequency control in wind-dominated systems.

title: FedGraphCollab: A Secure Federated Knowledge Graph Framework for Privacy-Preserving Multi-Agent Collaboration
authors: Blaise Joseph, B. Lavaraju, S.Thangamayan, R Roshini
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: In the era of distributed intelligence, ensuring secure and privacy-preserving collaboration among autonomous agents has become a critical challenge. The paper offers FedGraphCollab, a new Federated Knowledge Graph (KG) system that allows processing multi-agent systems to jointly learn, reason, and perform the task without exchanging raw data. Individual agents create local knowledge graphs according to domain-specific observation and encode these as latent vectors using GraphSAGE after which Relational Graph Convolutional Networks (R-GCN) is used to model semantic relations. The encoded models are then averaged using a Federated Averaging (FedAvg) process, to construct the collective semantic cognition, although it is kept Privacy-Preserving through Dollar privacy (DP) at the client side. We examine FedGraphCollab under a variety of use cases such as healthcare tracking, industrial automation and smart city platforms. Experimental findings prove that FedGraphCollab has the KG inference accuracy of 92.1% on average, which is higher than Fed-GNN (86.5%), Centralized KG models (89.0%), and isolated local models (77.4%). As well, it minimizes convergence rounds by up to 29%, communications overhead by 14% over traditional federated models. There are reduced risks of privacy leakage of 1.1%, which proves its potent data protection. The framework exerts an optimal balance between learning performance, privacy and scalability. FedGraphCollab presents a new standard in federal semantic learning and provides a solid infrastructure of future collaborative AI platforms. It offers the avenue through which intelligent agents can exchange abstract knowledge, reason about heterogeneous environments and adjust to the changing tasks, yet retain data sovereignty.

title: Phishing URL Detection Using NLP and Weighted Ensemble Model
authors: Shikhar Bansal, Abhinav Kumar, Nidhi Mishra
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Phishing remains a major cybersecurity threat, exploiting users’ trust by mimicking legitimate websites to steal sensitive information. Existing defenses often fail to detect novel or obfuscated attacks, underscoring the need for more robust solutions. In this work, we propose a phishing URL detection system that combines natural language processing (NLP)-based feature extraction with a weighted ensemble of machine learning models. Our dataset includes approximately 500,000 URLs collected from multiple public sources to curate a list of 6,000 phishing-related keywords, and another 22,720 URLs were analyzed, and more than 80 lexical and content-based features were extracted with a balanced mix of phishing and legitimate samples. To evaluate performance and generalization, we trained and tested seven classifiers individually and in ensemble, using both single-dataset and cross-dataset validation. Results show that the Random Forest model achieved the highest accuracy (97.01%), while the weighted ensemble achieved competitive performance (96.81%) with improved robustness. These findings highlight the effectiveness of ensemble learning for resilient phishing detection and provide a foundation for cost-efficient deployment in real-world systems.

title: Hybrid XGBoost-SVM classifier for Assessing Media Accountability
authors: C.Jayamala, Thilagavathi.K, Beula Jayarani.B, K. Gayathri Devi, Swaastik.S, G Anjali
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Media plays a critical role in shaping public perception. However, misinformation, sensationalism, and biased reporting can influence public opinion leading to ethical concerns. Media-Net is a hybrid AI-driven framework designed to assess media. By integrating LegalBERT for legal text analysis, Explainable AI (XAI) for transparency, and a hybrid XGBoost-SVM classifier for bias detection, the model provides an automated and scalable approach to evaluating media narratives. The system analyzes news articles, social media posts, and televised transcripts for factual accuracy, sensationalism, and legal compliance. Experimental results demonstrate that MediaJustice-Net achieves an accuracy of 98.21%, significantly outperforming baseline models such as LegalBERT + Random Forest (88.2%) and BERT + XGBoost (86.4%). Sentiment and accountability analyses reveal that mainstream print media has the highest factual alignment (65.2%), while social media exhibits the highest misinformation rate (24.7%). The study highlights the necessity of automated legal fact-checking and bias detection mechanisms to ensure responsible journalism. This framework can be a valuable tool for journalists, legal analysts, and media watchdog organizations, aiding in the ethical reporting of sub-judice cases.

title: Design and Experimental Characterization of a HTS REBCO Current Lead for Fusion Applications
authors: Fuda Wang, Qianjun Zhang, Run Wu, Tengfei Shi, Lele Jiang, Zhaosheng Zhu, Zhengxin Yin, Wenqing Yi, Lang Qin, Binbin Wang, Shouzhi Wang, Rui Chen, Qing Li, Yi Tan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: In order to meet the current transport requirements of fusion magnets, we have designed and fabricated a high stability, low thermal loss HTS current lead. The focus of this work is on the HTS module, a key section linking the warm end and cold end of the current lead. The HTS module includes 24 stacks of REBCO tapes, in which each stack contains 3 tapes. The HTS module is designed to operate within a temperature range from 20 K to 77 K. The operating current (Iop) is designed to be 13 kA, and the heat leakage to the magnet end is limited to a maximum of 4 W. Finite element method (FEM) simulations were conducted to estimate the resistance, the critical current and the safety margin in loss of flow accident. The critical current (Ic) and joint resistance of REBCO stacked tapes on current lead were examined experimentally at 77 K in a liquid nitrogen bath, under self-field conditions.
