title: Large-Signal Stability Analysis and Enhancement Scheme for GFL-MMC
authors: Jianxiang Xie, Jiaqi Wu, Lidong Lin, Jingyi Li, Zhu Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: This paper analyzes the transient stability of Grid-Following Modular Multilevel Converter (GFL-MMC) from the large-signal perspective. First, without adopting any linearization methods, an accurate large-signal model for analyzing the transient synchronization stability of GFL-MMC is established. Next, based on the dynamic characteristics of the phase-locked loop (PLL), the transient synchronization stability mechanism of GFL-MMC under large disturbances is analyzed. Subsequently, using the large-signal model, this paper investigates the influence of PI parameters on transient stability and derives a current reference setting method that guarantees the existence of equilibrium points. To enhance the transient stability of GFL-MMC, a novel control strategy is proposed. This method comprises two components: control parameter optimization and improved adaptive switching of current reference values. It enables GFL-MMC to operate with preset parameters during normal operation or non-severe faults while adaptively switching during large disturbances. Simulation results of PSCAD/EMTDC assess the practical effectiveness of the theoretical findings and the proposed approach.

title: Constrained Policy Learning for Voltage Control and Peak Power Mitigation in EV Fast Charging Stations
authors: Liangliang Hao, Wei Liu, Qisheng Huang, Jiangliang Jin
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: We study the charging scheduling problem for EV fast charging stations while ensuring distribution network safety under stochastic EV arrivals and renewable generation uncertainties. We propose a novel safe reinforcement learning-based control approach that introduces a state-wise safety value function based on Control Barrier Functions (CBF) to quantify constraint violation risks, coupled with a state-wise Lagrangian network that dynamically adjusts penalty terms according to the safety value function. The proposed architecture enforces strict safety compliance while maximizing the long-term expected charging revenue of the charging station. Numerical results demonstrate that our method reduces peak power demand by 7.3-17.5 % while achieving zero voltage safety violations, validating its effectiveness in balancing network safety with operational efficiency.

title: Research on Improving Reliability Management of Medium-Voltage Power Supply Lines Based on Fault Tree Analysis Method
authors: Wenqiang Zou, Yieng Liu, Xuann Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the people's yearning for a better life and the gradual increase in requirements for power supply reliability, the reliability of medium-voltage power supply lines has become a key factor affecting the stable operation of the power grid and the reliable power consumption of the people. Aiming at the current situation of frequent faults in medium-voltage distribution network lines, this paper introduces the Fault Tree Analysis (FTA) method, uses the power reliability model to analyze the medium-voltage line data of a certain power supply bureau in the past year, compares and analyzes the results of fault tree analysis with the actual occurrence, identifies key fault factors, and proposes comprehensive improvement measures of technical optimization, management improvement and data-driven. The effectiveness of the method is verified through practical cases, and a set of targeted reliability management improvement measures are proposed to form a reliability management improvement system for power supply lines. The research results show that the fault tree analysis method can significantly improve the pertinence and scientificity of power supply line reliability management, provide theoretical support and effective prediction for the intelligent upgrading of the future development of the distribution network, and accurately improve the reliability management level.

title: Flexibility Evaluation Method of Power Systems Considering Security Regulation Capability
authors: Weigang Jin, Xuantong Hu, Hongkun Chen, Yilin Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Existing flexibility indices primarily focus on system-level supply-demand balance, neglecting physical network constraints. This can lead to situations where a system with sufficient flexibility margins is unable to resolve localized security issues due to network congestion. This paper proposes a comprehensive flexibility evaluation method that incorporates the concept of Security Regulating Capability (SRC). First, flexibility indices are established based on two key dimensions: flexibility margin and SRC. Subsequently, a comprehensive evaluation method that combines Principal Component Analysis and Fuzzy Comprehensive Evaluation is developed to integrate these multi-dimensional indices. Finally, case studies conducted on the FTS-213 test system demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively distinguish between a system's overall flexibility adequacy and its effectiveness in addressing security issues, offering valuable guidance for future flexibility resource planning.

title: Digital Twin Modeling and Simulation of Hydrogen Production Equipment for Alkaline Electrolyzers
authors: Zhexuan Shuai, Wei Cong, Deliang Chen, Tingting Zhang, Fangyuan Wang, Qingsong Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: To support the operation and early warning of photovoltaic electrolytic water hydrogen production systems under the “dual carbon” goals, this paper constructs a digital twin model of alkaline electrolyzers in view of the engineering limitations of traditional modeling. By analyzing the mechanism of hydrogen production through alkaline electrolysis of water, three sub-models - temperature characteristic model, electrochemical characteristic model, and material flow model were built, and finally the digital twin modeling was completed through Simulink. Simulation verification shows that the model can describe the volt-ampere characteristics of the electrolyzer well within a certain temperature range; At the same time, the error between the steady-state hydrogen production rate output by the model and the actual electrolyzer is only 2.3%, which can simulate the actual situation of the electrolyzer well.

title: ZEUS: An Efficient GPU Optimization Method Integrating PSO, BFGS, and Automatic Differentiation
authors: Dominik Soós, Marc Paterno, Desh Ranjan, Mohammad Zubair
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: We introduce a novel, efficient computational method, ZEUS, for numerical optimization, and provide an open-source implementation. It has four key ingredients: (1) particle swarm optimization (PSO), (2) the use of the Broyden-Fletcher-Goldfarb-Shanno (BFGS) method, (3) automatic differentiation (AD), and (4) GPUs. Our approach addresses the computational challenges inherent in high-dimensional, non-convex optimization problems. In the first phase of the algorithm, we get a potentially good set of starting points using PSO. Thereafter, we run BFGS independently in parallel from these starting points. BFGS is one of the bestperforming algorithms for numerical optimization. However, it requires the gradient of the function being optimized. ZEUS integrates automatic differentiation into BFGS thus avoiding the need for the user to calculate derivatives explicitly. The use of GPUs allows ZEUS to speed up the calculations substantially. We carry out systematic studies to explore the trade-offs between the number of PSO iterations taken, starting points, and BFGS iteration depth. We show that a handful of iterations of PSO can improve global convergence when combined with BFGS. We also present performance studies using common test functions. The source code can be found at https://github.com/fnal-numerics/global-optimizer-gpu.

title: Transfer Learning with ResNet-50 for Multi-Label Chest X-Ray Classification on the NIH ChestX-Ray14 Dataset
authors: Sumangala Gejje, Anjana Joshi, Jaspreet Kaur, N. Divyaa, E Sweety Bakyarani, M.Baritha Begum
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Early and correct diagnosis of several lung diseases based on chest X-rays is crucial to the proper management of patients. The current methods are more likely to miss comorbidities, are linked to unbalanced data problems, and cannot be understood clinically. This paper introduces a transfer learning system on ResNet-50 to classify multiple labels with the NIH ChestX-ray14 dataset. It is proposed to use preprocessing, data augmentation, and residual block feature extraction followed by thresholded multi-label prediction to achieve good results. The experimentally obtained results are more accurate (94.6%), recollective (91.1%), F1-score (0.917) and AUC (0.97), whereas Inference times are less; this attributes to its efficiency and suitability. The framework is a feasible and scalable approach to automatic lung disease diagnosis.

title: Securing Interconnected Infrastructures: Control-Centric Cyber Resilience
authors: Shiva Kumar Bommakanti
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: This paper delves into the critical challenge of safeguarding complex cyber-physical networks (CPNs) against evolving digital threats. As modern critical infrastructures be-come increasingly interconnected, their inherent interdependencies create new vulnerabilities for coordinated malicious attacks. We present an examination of fundamental security concerns and advanced control methodologies essential for designing resilient CPNs. The focus is on strategies for real-time threat detection, robust secure estimation, and the development of fault-tolerant and secure control mechanisms that maintain operational integrity despite sophisticated cyber intrusions. This work provides a consolidated perspective on building proactive cyber resilience in highly integrated critical systems.

title: A Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Dynamic Reactive Power and Voltage Control Strategy for Power Systems
authors: Kai Li, Jian Chen, Chenxi Wu, Shunnan Liu, Yeyan Xu, Zhilan Tao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: With the large-scale integration of renewable energy into power grids, traditional reactive power and voltage control methods exhibit limitations such as slow response and poor adaptability under complex operating conditions. This paper proposes a dynamic reactive power and voltage control strategy for power systems based on the Deep Q-Network (DQN) approach. The control problem is formulated as a Markov decision process, and a multi-agent coordinated control framework is designed to enable real-time intelligent voltage regulation through deep reinforcement learning. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared with conventional control strategies, the proposed method achieves significant improvements in voltage deviation, network losses, and response speed, with a 15 % increase in voltage compliance rate, an 8.3 % reduction in network losses, and more than a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{6 0 \%}$</tex> decrease in response time.

title: AI and Machine Learning for Cloud Security: A Comprehensive Survey of IDS and Threat Detection Methods
authors: Anil Kumar Devarapalem
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: In today digital infrastructure, cloud computing now takes center stage and ensures strong security is a priority, especially against advanced cyberattacks that operate on dynamic and multi-tenant infrastructures. Established intrusion detection systems (IDS) cannot satisfy the needs of cloud ecosystems because of the scalability and lack of flexibility. The paper provides a high-level overview of AI and ML by conducting a thorough survey of their applications in enhancing IDS for cloud security. This paper discusses ways to extend intrusion detection capabilities through the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML), and presents research into a cloud-based IDS transformation. The ability to detect zero-day threats, adapt to trends in real-time data, and improve detection accuracy are three key features shared by supervised, unsupervised, and hybrid learning models, as validated by a comprehensive analysis. The research compares the results of the application of the demanding models of IDS XGBoost, MLP, LSTM, and CNN+LSTM applied to various datasets, demonstrating the highest rates of precision, recalls, and F1-score. Besides, emerging topics such as federated learning, explainable AI, and adversarial resilience are discussed in a literature review; the main gaps in research are found. The results support the necessity of scalable, explainable, and privacy-preserving frameworks of IDS in cloud and IoT systems. Smart, flexible, and reliable security solutions for today's cloud infrastructures are a hot topic, and this study adds to the existing literature on the subject.

title: Feedback-Driven Differential Evolution Model for Device Sizing Optimization in Analog Circuit Design
authors: Trang Hoang, Huy Duong Gia
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The device sizing of analog integrated circuits remains one of the most critical challenges in modern design due to the highly nonlinear interdependencies among device parameters and the often conflicting performance specifications, such as gain, bandwidth, power consumption, and stability. Such coupling makes the sizing flow strongly simulation-driven; under typical population-based settings, a single run can require thousands of SPICE-level evaluations. To address these challenges, this study proposes a novel Differential Evolution (DE) model with a feedback-driven online parameter controller that incorporates a lightweight success-based selection feedback mechanism into the DE loop to adapt control parameters dynamically during optimization, improving solution quality and robustness while reducing the number of simulation calls. Specifically, each individual maintains its own differential weight and crossover rate, which are updated by multiplicative rules driven by selection feedback, while two population-level rates (Frate, CRrate) and a stagnation-triggered penalty regulate the adaptation. The proposed model is evaluated on: (i) device sizing of an analog circuit, and (ii) a set of 12 benchmark functions. The results show that the proposed model achieves higher solution quality and improved robustness than conventional DE, SADE, SHADE and several representative metaheuristic baselines on the analog sizing task, while remaining competitive across diverse benchmark landscapes.

title: Joint Routing and Model Pruning for Decentralized Federated Learning in Bandwidth-Constrained Multi-Hop Wireless Networks
authors: Xiaoyu He, Weicai Li, Tiejun Lv, Xi Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Decentralized federated learning (D-FL) enables privacy-preserving training without a central server, but multi-hop model exchanges and aggregation are often bottlenecked by communication resource constraints. To address this issue, we propose a joint routing-and-pruning framework that optimizes routing paths and pruning rates to keep communication latency within prescribed limits. We analyze how the sum of model biases across all clients affects the convergence bound of DFL, and formulate an optimization that maximizes the model retention rate to reduce these biases under communication constraints. Further analysis shows that each client’s model retention rate is path-dependent, which reduces the original problem to a routing optimization. Leveraging this insight, we develop a routing algorithm that selects latency-efficient transmission paths, allowing more parameters to be delivered within the time budget and thereby improving D-FL convergence. Simulations show that, compared with unpruned systems, the proposed framework reduces average transmission latency by 27.8% and improves testing accuracy by about 12%; relative to standard benchmark routing algorithms, the proposed routing method further improves accuracy by roughly 8%.

title: Transient Stability of GFL Converters Subjected to Switching of Droop-Controlled GFM Converters
authors: Bingfang Li, Songhao Yang, Pu Cheng, Zhiguo Hao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion
abstract: Integrating grid-forming converters (GFMCs) into grid-following converter (GFLC)-dominated power systems enhances the grid strength, but GFMCs' current-limiting characteristic triggers dynamic mode switching between constant voltage control (CVC) and current limit control (CLC). This switching feature poses critical transient stability risks to GFLCs, requiring urgent investigation. This paper first develops a mathematical model for this switched system. Then, it derives mode switching conditions for droop-controlled GFMCs, which are separately GFMC angle-dependent and GFLC angle-dependent. On this basis, the stability boundaries of GFLC within each subsystem are analyzed, and the impact of GFMC mode switching arising from GFLC angle oscillation is investigated. The findings reveal that the switched system's stability boundary coincides with that of the CLC subsystem. To enhance GFLC's transient stability and ensure GFMC converges to the CVC mode, this paper introduces a virtual fixed d-axis control (VFDC) strategy. Compared with existing methods, this method achieves decoupling and self-stabilization using only local state variables from individual converters. The conclusions are validated through simulations and Controller Hardware-in-the-Loop tests.

title: EFGCL: Learning Dynamic Motion through Spotting-Inspired External Force Guided Curriculum Learning_supp1-3675955.mp4
authors: Keita Yoneda
publish: N/A
abstract: Learning dynamic whole-body motions for legged robots through reinforcement learning (RL) remains challenging due to the high risk of failure, which makes efficient exploration difficult and often leads to unstable learning. In this paper, we propose External Force Guided Curriculum Learning (EFGCL), a guided RL approach based on the principle of physical guidance, in which external assistive forces are introduced during training. Inspired by spotting in artistic gymnastics, EFGCL enables agents to physically experience successful motion executions without relying on task-specific reward shaping or reference trajectories. Experiments on a quadrupedal robot performing Jump, Backflip, and Lateral-Flip tasks demonstrate that EFGCL accelerates learning of the Jump task by approximately a factor of two and enables the acquisition of complex whole-body motions that conventional RL methods fail to learn. We further show that the learned policies can be deployed on a real robot, reproducing motions consistent with those observed in simulation. These results indicate that physically guided exploration, which allows agents to experience success early in training, is an effective and general strategy for improving learning efficiency in dynamic whole-body motion tasks.

title: A Strategic Framework for Supplier Performance Assessment and Anomaly Detection in High-Tech Supply Chains: Integrating Shared-Resource DEA with Machine Learning
authors: Sheng-Wei Lin, Wen-Min Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: Evaluating supplier performance in high-tech supply chains is challenging because suppliers must allocate shared resources across operational and sustainability-related activities while maintaining resilience under uncertainty. This study develops an integrated decision-support framework that combines two-stage shared-resource data envelopment analysis (DEA) with machine learning for supplier assessment, anomaly detection, and efficiency prediction. Using NVIDIA's semiconductor supply chain as the empirical setting, the framework explicitly models resource allocation between operational and ESG objectives, thereby addressing the measurement distortion that arises when conventional DEA assumes independent resource use across stages. The proposed approach further incorporates multimodel anomaly detection to identify suppliers with abnormal performance patterns and employs predictive analytics to support forward-looking monitoring. Research rigor is strengthened through transparent data construction, comparative model benchmarking, time ordered validation, and multi-method analytical triangulation. The findings show that the proposed framework produces more reliable and more discriminating supplier assessments than conventional efficiency-evaluation approaches, while also offering earlier identification of performance risks. For engineering management, the framework provides a practical basis for balancing operational and ESG priorities, prioritizing supplier development efforts, and allocating managerial attention to suppliers requiring early intervention.

title: A Bounded Nonlinear Controller for a Class of Step-Up DC/DC Converters: Application to Renewable Energy Sources
authors: Jefferson S. Costa, Nabil Benyahia, Vincent Reinbold, Frédéric Mazenc, Alfeu J. Sguarezi Filho, Alessio Iovine
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article presents a novel time derivative-free bounded nonlinear controller (BNLC), including a refined integral action, for a class of boost DC/DC converters. The proposed strategy avoids the complexity of optimization-based methods through an explicit analytical form and mitigates potential noise issues associated with derivative-based controllers. The proposed controller guarantees boundedness via explicit duty cycle limits, with a complete closed-loop stability analysis provided. Validation on an experimental testbed compares the method against advanced proportional integral (PI) and sliding mode plus PI (SMPI) controllers. Results demonstrate superior and consistent transient response across the entire operational range, particularly in highly nonlinear low-current regions where it is 72% faster than robust benchmarks. In addition, it shows significantly higher noise immunity [12–20 dB higher signal-to-noise ratio (SNR)] and robustness under PV shading and large parametric variations.

title: Surface estimation of translucent materials: an application to fabric digitization
authors: Diego Sagredo, Javier Fabre, Jorge Lopez-Moreno
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Estimating the surface of translucent objects from photometric data poses significant challenges due to complex internal light scattering. We introduce a novel method that computes a depth map from single-viewpoint photographs of a material sample, captured under multiple illuminations. Our approach leverages inverse rendering to derive a volumetric representation, including density, albedo, and phase function, from which a surface mesh is reconstructed. Beyond validation with synthetic and 3D-printed physical models, we illustrate our technique's power by successfully applying it to the digitization of fabrics, a notoriously difficult material due to its intricate translucent structure. This work advances the state-of-the-art texture stack acquisition via enhanced surface reconstruction.

title: AI-Driven Horizons: A Wide-Ranging Review of Deep Learning Approaches for Heart Disease Risk Prediction
authors: Rowthu Neelima, Santosh Kumar Henge
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Heart disease remains one of the leading causes of mortality worldwide, underscoring the urgent need for accurate and early risk prediction. The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence (AI), particularly deep learning (DL), has transformed predictive healthcare by enabling scalable, data-driven solutions. This review systematically examines the evolution, methodologies, and clinical applications of DL models for heart disease risk prediction. We highlight state-of-the-art architectures, including convolutional neural networks (CNNs), recurrent neural networks (RNNs), autoencoders, and hybrid models, and discuss their ability to process diverse data sources such as electronic health records (EHRs), medical imaging, and electrocardiograms (ECGs). Key challenges—such as interpretability, generalizability, data imbalance, and multimodal integration—are critically analyzed. In addition, we evaluate benchmarking datasets and performance metrics to provide a standardized framework for model comparison. Ethical considerations, patient privacy, and regulatory requirements are also addressed to ensure safe clinical translation. By consolidating prior advances and identifying unresolved gaps, this review offers a roadmap for enhancing diagnostic accuracy, reducing healthcare costs, and improving cardiovascular patient outcomes.

title: Cross-Domain Transfer Learning and Self-Distillation in Histopathological Image Analysis for Automated Colorectal Cancer Grading
authors: Kavitha A, Naveen Kumar S, Mayilan Y
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: CRC is one of the most prevalent forms of malignancy throughout the whole world, accompanied by both high morbidity and mortality rates. Optimal grading of histopathological images is certain to improve patient outcomes. The automated deep learning for the diagnosis of CRC is proposed to minimize the use of human supervision. It includes cross-domain transfer learning, domain adaptation, and self-distillation for the enhancement of classification performance in the framework. Since histopathological images are not homogeneous since they are stained, and data there commonly lacks any labeling, preprocessing such as stain normalization and picture improvement are used. Convolutional Neural Networks e.g. ResNet-50, Xception, and EfficientNet are optimized on large histopathological data and then on cancer samples on CRC against progressive layer unfreezing.In order to further increase the extent of generalization and reduce the influence of label noise, The model has been adopted a strategy of self-distillation, in which the model has been trained student models on soft targets generated by teacher networks.Domain adaptation is done using the Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD) method, and adversarial adolescent learning with Gradient Reversal Layers (GRL), thus reducing the gap between source and target domain distribution features. Ulti-level attention model, which is Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) and Shuffle Attention (SA), is utilized to lock into key regions of tumors and core structures. The final classification point uses dense layers with focal loss and ReLU activation, which therefore deals with the problem of class imbalance effectively. The dataset is 1,186 samples, including 514 controls and 672 case samples, though, there were significant results involving people who had been diagnosed with CRC at the age below 50. The laboratory findings exhibit that the suggested model complements feature extraction, resolves generalization, and assists in making clinical decisions. The model has got a lot of potential in its capability to identify early-stage colorectal cancer and make individualized treatment choices.

title: Cognitive Digital Twins for Predictive AI-Driven Operations
authors: Abdul Hadi, Prashanthi Pala, Sangeetha Govindarajan, Naga Venkata P Janapareddy, Adya Mishra
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Cognitive Digital Twins (CDTs) are the latest form of digital twin technologies combining advanced artificial intelligence, machine learning, and real-time analytics to allow it to perform predictive, adaptive, and self-optimizing processes. In contrast to the conventional digital twins, which are mostly mere virtual representations of material objects, CDTs have cognitive functions to think of them in a particular context, identify anomalies, and provide decision support. The following paper proposes a new model of Cognitive Digital Twins to predictive AI-driven processes in the fields of manufacturing, health care, and critical infrastructure. It will be recommended to leverage the real-time sensing, multimodal data fusion, and edge-cloud orchestration with reinforcement learning and explainable AI models to guarantee proactive fault detection, performance optimization, and resilience improvement. The experimental validation shows that CDTs save time, improve situational awareness, and allow making autonomous decisions under dynamic conditions. The framework builds a basis of scalable, reliable and people-centered predictive activities by connecting the physical and digital ecologies by means of continuous learning.

title: Personalized Healthcare Revolutionized Through Deep Federated Residual Learning Model for Chronic Disease Prediction
authors: S.Thangamayan, Ganesh Balaji. S, Sabareeson. S, Arvinth Raj. A.P, Subash Krishnan. K, Sudharsan. A
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The healthcare sector employs artificial intelligence (AI) as an innovative tool to detect chronic diseases early combined with their management strategies. A Deep Federated Residual Learning Model introduces new technology to predict chronic diseases using decentralized learning methods that preserve patient privacy integrity. The model receives diverse healthcare data from multiple institutions across wearable devices medical documentation and laboratory tests and medical imaging while protecting all sensitive information using federated learning methods. The research model produced superior prediction capabilities resulting in 93.5% accuracy and 95% ROC-AUC value together with outstanding results for detecting Diabetes as well as Cardiovascular Disease and Hypertension. The system achieved better outcomes than traditional centralized learning methods which helped sustain stronger privacy while enabling scale expansion. The custom predictions designed for each patient allow the model to identify chronic diseases accurately. Federated learning successfully addresses privacy needs in medicine by demonstrating its practical application in healthcare according to experimental findings. The model achieves both high diagnostic accuracy and smooth integration with present healthcare systems. AI-driven personalized healthcare reveals significant promise to forecast and handle diseases effectively and promptly.

title: Contactless Respiratory Rate Variability Using Smartphone Acoustics
authors: Kounkou Vincent, Xiaozhi Qi, Lin Wang, Tao Gong
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Monitoring respiratory rate variability (RRV) via smartphone microphones represents a significant opportunity for accessible digital health. However, extracting clean respiratory signals from acoustic recordings in noisy real-world environments remains a major obstacle. This paper presents a Frame-bin Adaptive Stochastic Modeling (FASM) framework with an Attention-based Time-Frequency Masking Network (ATFRespNet) for robust RRV estimation using commodity smartphones. FASM adaptively partitions the acoustic field into localized frame-bins and introduces a coefficient-based stochastic modeling strategy that precisely distinguishes physiological reflections and separates RRV from motion artifacts through a stochastic instantaneous frequency (SIF) method. ATFRespNet further enhances the respiration-related spectral regions through an attention-driven masking mechanism, while an autoencoder (AE) refines the waveform morphology for accurate rate estimation. We evaluated our model on a diverse dataset of real-world recordings containing various environmental noises. The results demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art accuracy in estimating respiratory rate.

title: A Population-Based Variable Neighborhood Search for Multi-AGV Scheduling with Sudden Failures of Production Cells in Intelligent Workshops
authors: Linhu Li, Baoxian Jia, Biao Zhang, Leilei Meng, Wenqiang Zou
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: With the application and popularization of intelligentworkshops, the sudden failure has become one of the key factors affecting production efficiency and stability. However, little attention has been paid to the scheduling of automatic guided vehicle (AGV) in the event of sudden failures. This paper investigates a multi-AGV scheduling problem with the sudden failure of production cells in a matrix manufacturing workshop, aiming to maximize the task delivery rate of AGVs. To address the problem, a mixed integer linear programming model and an algorithm which combines Q-learning withVariable Neighborhood Search based on population initialization (PQVNS) are proposed. Specifically, three strategies and two acceleration theorems are proposed in the Shaking stage, and four strategies in Variable Neighborhood Descent as well as Q-learning are designed to enhance the exploration and exploitation of the algorithm, and two rescheduling strategies are presented to deal with the reassignment of multiple AGVs at the time of sudden failure. Finally, 80 instances of real workshop collected are evaluated and compared with five existing algorithms in the related literature. The experimental results show that the proposed strategies are very effective and the proposed algorithm is better than the existing algorithms in solving the multi-AGV problem.

title: MR_Go: A Magnetorheological Quadruped Robot for Energy-Efficient, High-Payload, and Impact-Tolerant Planetary Exploration_supp1-3675964.mp4
authors: Shuaishuai Sun
publish: N/A
abstract: Planetary exploration missions increasingly rely on agile robotic platforms capable of traversing unstructured and unpredictable terrain. While quadruped robots offer superior mobility compared to wheeled rovers, their deployment in long-duration and shock-load missions remains limited, due to the limitations of high power consumption, low payload-to-weight ratio, and poor resilience to impact and overloads with the conventional actuator. To address these challenges, we present the MR_Go, a magnetorheological (MR)-based quadruped robot featuring real-time joint damping modulation to enhance energy efficiency, impact mitigation, and load-bearing capability. Built upon the Unitree Go1 platform, MR_Go achieves a 400% increase in payload-to-mass ratio with only 40 W of MR activation power and can sustain 20 kg payloads without continuous motor engagement. Experiments conducted in lunar-analog terrain demonstrate that MR_Go extends mission battery life by 18%, reduces peak joint torque by 10% during an 80 cm cliff drop, and maintains superior thermal stability under prolonged loading. These results highlight the potential of MR-based hybrid actuation for enabling durable and energy-efficient quadruped systems suited for extended planetary operations.

title: Dominant Transient Stability of the Co-Located PLL-Based Grid-Following Renewable Plant and Synchronous Condenser Systems
authors: Bingfang Li, Songhao Yang, Qinglan Wang, Xu Zhang, Huan Xie, Chuan Qin, Zhiguo Hao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: Deploying synchronous condensers (SynCons) near grid-following renewable energy sources (GFLRs) is an effective and increasingly adopted strategy for grid support. However, the potential transient instability risks in such configurations remain an open research question. This study investigates the mechanism of dominant synchronization instability source transition upon SynCon integration and proposes a straightforward approach to enhance system stability by leveraging their interactive characteristics. Firstly, a dual-timescale decoupling model is established, partitioning the system into a fast subsystem representing phase-locked loop (PLL) dynamics and a slow subsystem characterizing SynCon rotor dynamics. The study then examines the influence of SynCons on the transient stability of nearby PLLs and their own inherent stability. The study shows that SynCon's voltage-source characteristics and its time-scale separation from PLL dynamics can significantly enhance the PLL's stability boundary and mitigate non-coherent coupling effects among multiple GFLRs. However, the dominant instability source shifts from the fast-time-scale PLL to the slow-time-scale SynCon after SynCon integration. Crucially, this paper demonstrates that the damping effect of PLL control can also be transferred from the fast to the slow time scale, allowing well-tuned PLL damping to suppress SynCon rotor acceleration. Consequently, by utilizing SynCon's inherent support capability and a simple PLL damping loop, the transient stability of the co-located system can be significantly enhanced. These conclusions are validated using a converter controller-based Hardware-in-the-Loop (CHIL) platform.

title: SPIoT: An Adaptive Federated Sparse Framework for Intrusion Detection in IoT
authors: Huijia Liang, Jin Yang, Haonan Deng, Dongqing Jia, Yulin Lei

title: FedAIM: Graph-Driven Scheduling for Concurrent Asynchronous Federated Learning in Computing Power Networks
authors: Guoming Yang, Shaoyong Guo, Yitao Xiao, Xinran Mao, Sai Huang, Feng Qi, Jiaka Hao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Asynchronous federated learning (AFL) faces multi-job scheduling challenges in computational power networks (CPNs). In large-scale CPN environments, the decision space is prohibitively large, and it is challenging to extract critical scheduling information. This leads to asynchronous jobs contending for computing and communication resources, which affects the staleness of local models and results in degraded training efficiency.To address these issues, we propose FedAIM, a graph-driven scheduling framework for multi-job AFL in CPNs. FedAIM encodes the system state into computation, communication, and staleness views to capture the interdependence between jobs and computing nodes in resource scheduling and model optimization, and leverages reinforcement learning for job scheduling. It combines two key components: a Mixture-of-Views Router (MoV-R), which applies context-aware gating across multiple graphs to generate lightweight representations that filter redundancy while preserving interference-critical signals; and an asymmetric Actor–Critic scheduler, where the Actor exploits MoV-R features for low-latency job–device allocation and the Critic integrates a global view for long-term evaluation. Experiments on large-scale simulations and a 50-node Raspberry Pi testbed show that FedAIM improves accuracy by 13–19%, reduces communication by 43%, and lowers computation cost by 45%, achieving a favorable trade-off between learning quality and system efficiency.

title: Newton-BT-FDTD Method for Wave Propagation in Time-Varying Magnetized Plasma
authors: Yun Fang, Peng Wan, Dandan Wang, Hongjun Yang, Wanchun Yang, Dingfang Zhang, Xue Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: A novel finite-difference time-domain (FDTD) method based on Newton’s second law of motion and bilinear transform (BT) technique is presented for modeling electromagnetic (EM) wave propagation in time-varying magnetized plasma. The procedure precisely expresses the time-varying electron density through its temporal derivative, enabling a more accurate capture of the rich dynamic characteristics of time-varying magnetized plasma. The numerical stability of the scheme is further examined through a von Neumann analysis. Numerical simulations indicate that the proposed BT approach has higher accuracy and computational efficiency for plasmas with strong electron-density variations compared with both the Newton-ADE-FDTD and the traditional ADE-FDTD methods. In addition, the proposed Newton-BT-FDTD scheme provides the improved accuracy relative to the conventional BT-FDTD method, especially in the case of time-varying magnetized plasma with electron-density drastic changes. Finally, the propagation characteristics of EM waves in a time-varying magnetized plasma are analyzed.

title: Compression in 3D Gaussian Splatting: A Survey of Methods, Trends, and Future Directions
authors: Muhammad Salman Ali, Chaoning Zhang, Marco Cagnazzo, Giuseppe Valenzise, Enzo Tartaglione, Sung-Ho Bae
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has recently emerged as a pioneering approach in explicit scene rendering and computer graphics. Unlike traditional neural radiance field (NeRF) methods, which typically rely on implicit, coordinate-based models to map spatial coordinates to pixel values, 3DGS utilizes millions of learnable 3D Gaussians. Its differentiable rendering technique and inherent capability for explicit scene representation and manipulation positions 3DGS as a potential game-changer for the next generation of 3D reconstruction and representation technologies. This enables 3DGS to deliver real-time rendering speeds while offering unparalleled editability levels. However, despite its advantages, 3DGS suffers from substantial memory and storage requirements, posing challenges for deployment on resource-constrained devices. In this survey, we provide a comprehensive overview focusing on the scalability and compression of 3DGS. We begin with a detailed background overview of 3DGS, followed by a structured taxonomy of existing compression methods. Additionally, we analyze and compare current methods from the topological perspective, evaluating their strengths and limitations in terms of fidelity, compression ratios, and computational efficiency. Furthermore, we explore how advancements in efficient NeRF representations can inspire future developments in 3DGS optimization. Finally, we conclude with current research challenges and highlight key directions for future exploration.

title: Sound Source Localization Based on Deconvolution and Variable-Scale Search

title: Single-Phase-to Ground Fault Location in Generator-Motor Stator Windings via Fundamental and Third Harmonic Components
authors: Chijian Jiang, Liguo Wang, Wei Hu, Zixiong Wan, Zhenfei Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: Existing methods for stator ground fault detection in generator are unable to perform online measurement of winding ground parameters, accurately identify the nature of ground faults, or achieve precise fault localization. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a real-time measurement method for three-phase asymmetric ground parameters. This method is based on the fundamental and third harmonic components of the neutral point voltage, explicitly considering the inherent asymmetry of the stator winding's three-phase ground capacitance and conductance. This paper introduces a method for determining the fault phase and calculating the ground resistance. By constructing an evaluation equation for single-phase ground fault location in the stator winding, reliable positioning with a resolution down to the specific stator slot is achieved. Finally, the nature of the ground fault is accurately distinguished by performing a secondary measurement of the ground resistance value. PSCAD/EMTDC simulation results validate the reliability of the proposed methodology.

title: Boundary Input-Output Finite-Time Stabilization of Distributed Parameter Systems
authors: Manna Li, Weijie Mao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: In this study, attention is focused on the consideration of the boundary input-output finite-time stabilization of distributed parameter systems. A Neumann boundary controller is constructed based on the distributed state, and subsequently, adequate criteria for boundary input-output finite-time stabilization of the closed loop system are established via the Lyapunov-like functional. Subsequently, the boundary input-output finite-time stabilization problem for the system is thereby reduced to solving linear matrix inequalities(LMIs), with the method's efficacy corroborated by numerical simulations.

title: AI-Based Language Translation with Attention Mechanisms: German to English to Marathi
authors: Ricky Raina, Archana Jadhav, Aryan Londhe, Shreya Salve, Aditi Dhole
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Translation is supposed to break all barriers in communication, and thus, make communication avenues more efficient between countries. This is a very good paper on that particular space in NMT which operates from hub to hub, that is, German to English and then, Marathi, using transformer models capable of attention mechanisms. In general, all underlying syntactic structures and related contexts are too complex in nature to improve upon rule-based and statistical machine translation methods. Therefore, for our case, we wanted to leverage transformer-based encoders and decoders with multi-head self-attention layers to maintain fine word relationships towards enhancing accurateness in translation. The system introduced in this paper uses parallel training and test datasets available for German-to-English and English-to-Marathi language pairs, which serve as a reference for estimating the system's BLEU score. These improvement metrics indicate improved translation qualities over the baseline models. The opening advantage of this project would realistically be its wide development towards a multilingual translation system, and especially so on a really resource poor language such as Marathi which can be augmented by a solid translation system to Indian regional languages.

title: Investigation of AI-based Deepfake Evidence: A Digital Forensics Approach
authors: Bapi Saha, Ajoy Kumar Khan
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Deepfake technology, which is driven by artificial intelligence (AI), has become a significant concern worldwide in recent years due to its potential for misuse and the growing challenges of verifying the authenticity of digital content. In India, there has been an increase in incidents related to deepfakes, leading to urgent action from forensic experts and investigators. This research presents a forensic analysis of a deepfake case sent to the Forensic Science Laboratory (FSL) for digital forensic analysis. The process began with the recovery of data from digital devices, including the potential deepfake image exhibits, while maintaining its integrity, followed by source analysis to track the origin of files by classification. A comprehensive AI-based link analysis of the chats of suspected applications was conducted, which revealed the intention of the perpetrators to do such an action. For further confirmation, Side-by-side visual analysis and RGB Colour Histogram analysis, along with structural analysis using Magnet Verify, are also performed to check whether AI is used to manipulate this data or not. The combination of traditional forensic techniques along with AI-driven tools has enabled a thorough assessment of the evidence, leading to a reliable conclusion that the potential deepfake exhibit showed signs of manipulation by an AI tool and was confirmed as a deepfake. This research underlines the urgent need to adopt advanced analytical techniques and develop a forensic approach for analyzing deepfake photos, videos, and related digital content.

title: Optimized Cardiovascular Disease Prediction Using Stochastic L1 Regularization and SHAP-Based Interpretability
authors: Irfan Khan, Pinaki Ghosh
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease remains a leading cause of death worldwide. Thus, there is a need for predictive models with adequate accuracy for early diagnosis and intervention of the disease. Artificial neural networks have shown potential in this domain because they can handle complicated and nonlinear relationships in medical data. However, ANNs tend to overfit on high-dimensional datasets with small sample sizes, which can impact their ability to generalize. In this study, we present a novel regularization technique named Stochastic L1 regularization with layer-wise probabilities. This approach applies the L1 penalty randomly to weights, with each layer having its own probability, promoting adaptive sparsity. It enhances generalization and supports more effective feature selection. Furthermore, SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) utilizes the contribution of each feature in the prediction, thereby promoting transparency and clinical use of any given model. On a common CVD dataset, the stochastic L1-regularized ANN attained an accuracy of 97%, a precision of 98%, a recall of 96%, and an F1 score of 97%.

title: Dynamic Voltage Support Using LCC-HVDC Systems
authors: Siqi Lin, Meng Chen, Conghuan Yang, Ying Xue, Zhenyu Ma, Shirui Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the increasing integration of renewable energy sources, global power grids are witnessing the large-scale transmission of gigawatt-level power through LCC-HVDC links. This paper examines the capability of LCC-HVDC systems to provide dynamic voltage support in weak grids, with the overarching goal of enhancing system voltage stability. The core contribution lies in the formulation of a linearized reactive powervoltage relationship, which quantitatively characterizes the correlation between AC voltage variations and the reactive power adjustments required from the LCC-HVDC system. Building upon this linearized model, a droop-based dynamic voltage support (DVS) strategy is proposed to fully exploit the reactive power regulation potential of LCC-HVDC, thereby improving voltage support, particularly in mitigating overvoltage issues prevalent in weak grid scenarios. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy, a test system is simulated in PSCAD. The results confirm that the proposed DVS approach significantly enhances voltage stability in weak grids and demonstrates a high degree of effectiveness in suppressing overvoltage.

title: Distributed Metadata Querying on HPC Systems
authors: Suben Kumer Saha, Houjan Tang, Wei Zhang, Suren Byna
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: Efficient execution of range queries and exact queries on metadata is critical for many scientific workflows on large-scale high-performance computing (HPC) systems. Range queries in a distributed setting pose a challenge, as typical partitioning methods such as hash-based, list-based, and range-based partitioning do not work efficiently. There are also challenges in balancing the workload using locality-sensitive partitioning methods, which are capable of routing range queries to a subset of nodes to achieve efficiency. In this paper, we study prefix-hash tree and range-hash partitioning methods that fit the requirements of efficient range queries in distributed HPC systems. We also proposed a novel load-balancing method inspired by the Gossip protocol, which has similar complexity for most operations while keeping the coefficient of variation (CV) less than 7% across diverse metadata workload distributions, i.e., normal, uniform, and exponential. Our proposed methods outperform the commonly used Key-Value store, RocksDB, for exact queries by up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15 \times$</tex> and for range queries by up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$120,000 \times$</tex>.

title: HOPPS: Hardware-Aware Optimal Phase Polynomial Synthesis with Blockwise Optimization for Quantum Circuits
authors: Xinpeng Li, Ji Liu, Shuai Xu, Paul Hovland, Vipin Chaudhary
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: Blocks composed of {CNOT, R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">z</inf>} are ubiquitous in modern quantum applications, notably in circuits such as QAOA ansatzes and quantum adders. After compilation, many of them exhibit large CNOT counts or depths, which lowers fidelity. Therefore, we introduce HOPPS: a SAT-based hardware-aware optimal phase polynomial synthesis algorithm that could generate {CNOT, R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">z</inf>} blocks with CNOT count or depth optimality. Sometime {CNOT, R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">z</inf>} blocks are large, such as in QAOA ansatzes, HOPPS's pursuit of optimality limits its scalability. To address this issue, we introduce an iterative blockwise optimization strategy: large circuits are partitioned into smaller blocks, each block is optimally refined, and the process is repeated for several iterations. Empirical results show that HOPPS is more efficient comparing with existing near-optimal synthesis tools. Used as a peephole optimizer, HOPPS reduces the CNOT count by up to 50.0 % and the CNOT depth by up to 57.1 % under OLSQ. For large QAOA circuit, after mapping by Qiskit, circuit can be reduced CNOT count and depth by up to 44.4 % and 42.4 % by our iterative blockwise optimization.

title: Enhanced MPI Intra-Node Communication Framework: A Hybrid Approach with Cooperative DMA Channel-Based Data Transfer
authors: Shulei Xu, Tu Tran, Dhabaleswar K. DK Panda
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: In modern multi-/many-core HPC systems, the increasing demands for data transfers and memory I/O workloads have emerged as a predominant challenge for parallel programming models like MPI. Specifically, memory copy performance within the node has emerged as a significant bottleneck of the existing state-of-the-art MPI intra-node communication scheme. This paper introduces a hybrid approach to offload memory copy tasks partly from CPU, utilizing the Linux DMA Engine API with support for various CPU architectures, including I/OAT and PTDMA. We characterize the performance of DMA channel-based copy design for further optimizations. Subsequently, we integrate the DMA memory copy design into the existing MPI intra-node communication framework, thereby empowering the MPI intra-node point-to-point communication to simultaneously leverage DMA-based schemes and existing CPU-driven schemes in a cooperative manner. To demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed design, we conducted systematic experiments on two emerging architectures. Our experimental results demonstrate up to 26 % lower communication latency in microbenchmarks, up to 23 % faster execution time in the 3D-stencil application.

title: Adaptive ADMM for Distributed Peer-To-Peer Energy Trading
authors: Laith Alkhawaldeh, Claudio A. Cañizares, Kankar Bhattacharya
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: This paper proposes an adaptive Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) framework for distributed energy management in Peer-to-Peer (P2P) energy trading within Active Distribution Networks (ADNs). The model enables decentralized coordination of houses equipped with Photovoltaic (PV) generation and Battery Energy Storage System (BESS), while preserving user privacy. Centralized optimization, Dual Decomposition (DD), and ADMM-based approaches are compared against a baseline scenario of individual household optimization without P2P trading. A heuristic scaling-based adaptive ADMM strategy was proposed and compared with the residual balancing strategy, which reduced the convergence iterations compared to the standard ADMM and DD, while achieving comparable solutions. A comprehensive analysis involving four houses with varying energy resources demonstrates the significant benefits of P2P trading, including a reduction in energy costs and a significant decrease in grid exchanges. Both Time-Of-Use (TOU) pricing and Feed-In-Tariffs (FITs) are considered, with results showing that higher FITs lead to increased P2P energy transactions.

title: DRL-Based Intelligent Control of Phase-Shifting Transformer for Power Flow Optimization
authors: Anqi Wang, Ruisheng Diao, Jinru Cai, Wentao Sun, Chao Cai, Jun Han
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Uneven power flow distribution in modern power systems constrains transmission capacity and renewable energy integration, posing increasing challenges for grid operation. This paper presents a deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based framework for optimizing phase-shifting transformer (PST) tap positions to regulate loading rates of Transformers Connected to PSTs (TCPSTs), mitigate overloads in TCPSTs and adjacent 220 kV transformers, and minimize network losses. A multi-objective control model for PST tap positions, designed to adapt to complex power system operating scenarios while incorporating PST operational principles, is proposed, and an effective DRL-driven control strategy is adopted to solve this model. Through iterative learning, the well-trained DRL agent, validated on 500 Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)-generated test scenarios, achieves 100 % overload prevention for TCPSTs, with 69.2 % of scenarios exhibiting no heavy load conditions. Comparative evaluation of power flow distributions with and without DRL-controlled PSTs substantiates the framework's efficacy in enhancing load balancing, minimizing network losses, and bolstering grid reliability.

title: A Multi-Domain Digital Twin Model Layer for Accurate Transient Simulation of High Voltage Circuit Breakers in Smart Grids
authors: Bin Dong, Xiaolu Lin, Wenhu Tang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: This paper presents a new approach to building a digital twin model layer for high voltage circuit breaker (HVCB). With a high percentage of renewable energy sources and power electronics connected to the power system, HVCBs face more complex operating conditions, which puts new demands on electrical equipment condition testing techniques in the smart grid. Existing models suffer from deficiencies in transient processes, which may lead to errors when monitoring new energy application scenarios. This study combines mechanical dynamics, electromagnetic accompanying signals, and action transient processes. Firstly, a multi-body dynamics model was constructed to represent the complex motion mechanisms. The objective is achieved by considering the role of buffers and mechanical constraints. Secondly, a four-level segmentation model is established based on the coupling relationship of coil current-core motionarc discharge. Moreover, the transient model is established by combining the transient characteristics of HVCB and grid equipment. Finally, the multi-domain model coupling mechanism is analyzed, and a mechanical-electromagnetic-transient cosimulation framework is established on the MATLAB platform. The framework solves the deficiencies of the existing digital twin models of transient processes.

title: Two-Stage Integrated Planning for Transition to Lowcarbon Regional-Level Power System Considering Multi-Departmental Coupling
authors: Xuan Zhang, Qin Wang, Mengyao Xu, Zhaoxia Jing
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The decarbonization transformation of the power system is the main approach to achieving a carbon-neutral energy system. However, the influence of other departments (such as the industrial sector, transportation sector, construction sector, etc.) that have a strong coupling relationship with the power sector is not adequately considered in the existing low-carbon planning models for the power system, especially in the macro planning issues of large-scale power systems at the regional or provincial level. To solve this problem, this paper proposes a two-stage macro integrated planning model for the low-carbon transition problem of the regional-level power system, considering the influence of other important energy consumption departments. The proposed macro planning model consists of an energy system planning model and a power system dispatch model, which can integrate the macro energy development goals with the micro system operation characteristics to achieve overall optimization. Case studies show the effectiveness of the proposed planning model and methods.

title: A Review of Distribution Network Reconfiguration Methods Enabled by Flexible Interconnection
authors: Nuo Xu, Jiabin Gao, Yan He, Xiaokang Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the rising penetration of distributed energy resources and the emergence of flexible interconnection technologies, Distribution Network Reconfiguration (DNR) has evolved into a key approach for realising adaptive and efficient operation of modern distribution systems. This paper reviews the research progress on DNR methodologies based on flexible interconnection, focusing on their algorithmic evolution and enabling technologies. The fundamental principles of DNR and the roles of flexible interconnection devices (FIDs) are first introduced. Then, representative solution methods, including mathematical optimization, heuristic, metaheuristic, hybrid, and machine learning-based approaches, are systematically discussed and compared. Finally, major challenges and future research directions are identified, emphasizing the need for scalable, real-time, and uncertainty-aware methodologies to realize truly flexible and autonomous distribution networks.

title: Design of Reliability Unit Commitment Mechanism Based on Information Gap Theory
authors: Jisong Zhu, Jiang Dai, Yuhui Song, Shijin Tian, Nianjie Tian, Zhaoxia Jing
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the gradual increase in renewable energy penetration, a deterministic reliability unit commitment (RUC) approach becomes less effective in maintaining sufficient intraday available capacity when renewable output fluctuates significantly. To address this challenge, this paper improves the existing RUC clearing model using information gap decision theory (IGDT) and proposes a complete clearing framework and model. Simulation results on the IEEE 118-bus system demonstrate that the IGDT-based RUC mechanism effectively achieves market clearing by trading off a small degree of economic optimality to enhance computational tractability. The proposed model exhibits strong applicability and high computational efficiency, making it well-suited for large-scale systems with high renewable integration.

title: Crop Recommendation and Smart Irrigation Prediction System
authors: P. Sri Satvik, P. Vijay Kumar Reddy, P. Anil, A. Raja Shekar, M.A. Jabbar
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Agriculture is still a vital food security and economic stability sector, particularly in areas where agriculture is the main source of livelihood. Despite that, productivity is usually hindered by factors such as unpredictable weather patterns, inappropriate crop selection, and ineffective water use. To overcome these constraints, the proposed paper involves a Crop Recommendation with Smart Irrigation Prediction System that combines machine learning and IoT technologies to aid sustainable agriculture. The system is designed to suggest the appropriate crops as per soil parameters (pH, nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium levels), temperature, humidity, and rainfall. The system processes this information through a trained machine learning algorithm to estimate best crop types for a given region or land. At the same time, an intelligent irrigation module estimates the crops’ water requirements on the basis of real-time sensor data and weather predictions, scheduling irrigation precisely and efficiently. Not only does this save water, but crop health and yield are also increased. The system is made available through a simple web or mobile application, allowing farmers to make decision-supporting information-driven choices. Using data-driven information, the solution reduces wastage of resources, supports soil health, and enhances farm productivity overall. Overall, the paper helps achieve precision agriculture through a double solution of smart crop suggestion and adaptive irrigation recommendations and has 97.5% accuracy. The paper is viable for large-scale application in rural and semi-urban agriculture communities, promoting sustainable agriculture and enhancing climate variability resilience.

title: A Hybrid YOLO-DenseNet Framework for Skin Lesion Classification
authors: Varshini S, Resma Sre P
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Skin cancer is a growing global health concern, especially for people with long sun exposure or lighter skin. It develops when abnormal skin cells multiply uncontrollably and often goes unnoticed in the early stages. Detecting it early is vital to reduce deaths, particularly in areas with limited access to dermatologists. Deep learning models can quickly and accurately identify skin lesions from dermoscopic images without needing medical specialists. Often called a silent threat, skin cancer can mimic harmless moles or minor skin changes, making it easy to miss. Among image processing methods, YOLO offers fast real-time detection of suspicious lesions, while DenseNet121 improves classification with strong feature extraction. This study introduces a hybrid YOLOv8– DenseNet121 model trained on the HAM10000 dataset to identify and classify skin lesions, supporting early treatment and better outcomes in diverse healthcare settings.The proposed hybrid YOLOv8–DenseNet121 model got an overall accuracy of approximately 97% on the HAM10000 dataset, demonstrating its effectiveness in correctly identifying and classifying skin lesions.

title: Intelligent Speech Summarization Using Lightweight AI Models for Enhanced Audio Analysis
authors: Mahesh Kini, Karthik N, Keerthana M, Nisarga S, Puttaraju D P
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Automatic summarization of spoken audio is increasingly important for education, business, and accessibility. However, typical production pipelines depend on cloud services for ASR and summarization, raising privacy, latency, and connectivity concerns. We present a complete offline pipeline suitable for mobile devices that converts wav2vec2 acoustic encoders from PyTorch to ONNX and then to quantized TensorFlow Lite, and couples them with compact TFLite summarizers (distilled T5 / MiniLM / Pegasus-small variants). We detail training, conversion, representative calibration for int8 quantization, and Flutter integration with NNAPI/CoreML delegates. Experiments on LibriSpeech, AMI, and curated podcast sets and on-device tests (Pixel 6, Samsung A52) show WER ≈ 6.8%, ROUGE-1 ≈ 0.72, and end-to-end latency ≤0.95 s for typical segments. We publish conversion commands, calibration recipes, and reproducible evaluation to help others reproduce on-device summarization results.

title: AI-Enhanced Phishing Attack Detection with Visual-Semantic Analysis
authors: Sai Susmitha Gollapudi, Chaitanya Jammalamadaka, Sravan Kumar Reddy Bolla, Krishna Mula, Viraj Soni
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Phishing scams are no longer based on the text messages, but users are increasingly being duped using in their visual representation, as in webpage layout, logos and multimedia content. These multimodal cues are often not detected by conventional detection systems which places a serious gap in defence strategies. The current paper introduces a phishing detection system with AI-supported functionality, which combines visual-semantic analysis to recognize the intent of some malicious behaviour with increased precision. The suggested system utilizes the deep convolutional neural networks to identify discriminative visual features on webpage snapshots and transformer-based language models to identify semantic contradictions in written materials. These representations can be reconciled by a multimodal fusion layer, which allows identification of minor associations between misleading visual images and distorting language representations. Experimental testing on benchmark phishing datasets indicates that our method is much more precise and recalls more accurately than the state-of-the-art baselines, with fewer false positives on legitimate websites and a high amount of successfully identified visually convincing phishing websites. The results emphasize the opportunities of integrating the visual and semantic intelligence in real-time anti-phishing systems, providing a flexible and scalable defence mechanism against the new phishing schemes.
