title: A Learning Platform for Dyslexic Children using Tracing and Speech Recognition
authors: Neeraj Barhate, Snehal Bawanthade, Anushka Bhalerao, Vedant Bijjargi, Piyush Gawali
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Dyslexia is one of the most common learning disabilities. It affects the person’s skills to read, write and pronounce words. Those with dyslexia usually struggle to recognize letters, spell words, and articulate sounds. It can lead to bad academic performance and can create problems in doing day to day activities as well. Traditional learning methods can be stressful for many. They do not necessarily provide the proper assistance that dyslexic people require. To tackle this problem, the research introduces a web platform that includes handwriting practice and speech recognition into a single leaning system. The system applies a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) that is trained over handwritten glyphs to evaluate user input, validate correctness, and provide real-time feedback to refine writing proficiency. In addition to that, they incorporate a speech recognition component to test pronunciation, highlight errors, and suggest corrections. It supports users in developing verbal communication proficiency. It builds up an interesting, interactive environment encouraging users to practice and learn by themselves. The application is lightweight, economical, and browser-accessible, making it appropriate for use at home and in the classroom. By linking technology to inclusive learning, the solution supports dyslexics to overcome fundamental literacy hurdles. It is also beneficial for teachers and guardians to analyze progress and provide target-based support.

title: Modeling Contextual and Behavioral Aspects of Human AI Interaction Using Deep MLP
authors: Subir Gupta, Tejaskumar Laljibhai Tank, Aditya Rath, Utpal Chandra De, Bibhuti Bhusan Dash, Sudhansu Shekhar Patra
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: This study is significant as it addresses the increasing utilization of AI in making critical decisions. This means we need to find a way to make computers work well and people be able to make decisions at the same time. The issue stems from the lack of cohesive models that successfully integrate human behavioral aspects with algorithmic efficiency, hence facilitating reliable and context-sensitive collaboration. This research is innovative since it utilizes a survey-based dataset to elucidate the sociological, behavioral, and functional aspects of human–AI interactions. Then, it looks into the data using a Deep MLP (Wide & Deep) architecture. This design shows how models work together in both straight and curved lines. The recommended approach implies preparing the dataset for machine utilization, training a large and complex model using adaptive optimization, and carefully evaluating its performance. The results section does various performance measures such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, ROC AUC, reliability indices, and confusion matrix analysis, to show the model’s behaviour.

title: Sequential Modeling of Huntington’s Disease Progression Using Bidirectional LSTM Networks
authors: Bhanumathi M, R.Hemalatha, S. Murugan, Shofia Priyadharshini. D, B Rajasekaran, Ishwarya B
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Huntington's Disease (HD) is a progressive neurological condition that affects motor, cognitive, and behavioral functioning. Precise modelling of disease development is crucial for early intervention and personalized therapeutic care. This paper presents a Bi-directional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) model for predicting the progressive phases of HD using temporal clinical and genetic data. The model is trained and assessed using the publicly accessible Huntington Disease Dataset from Kaggle, including over 48,536 patient observations that include various variables such as age, motor symptoms, cognitive decline, HTT gene expression, and modifier categories. The Bi-LSTM design effectively models illness trajectories by capturing temporal relationships in both forward and backward directions. The proposed Bi-LSTM model had a classification accuracy of 99.57%, indicating its dependability in stage prediction. This result underscores the model's capability in facilitating early diagnosis and individualized treatment planning. The work confirms the efficacy of deep temporal models for monitoring neurological disorders and establishes a basis for future improvements in illness progression analysis.

title: FarmInsight: Optimising Crop Recommendation and Disease Diagnosis Using Ensemble Learning and CNN
authors: Vooradi Harshith, K. Sugamya, Bakkapolla Prashanth Reddy, Ankuri Mounika
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Precision agriculture has become an essential component in meeting the global demand for food security amidst rapidly changing climatic and environmental conditions. This paper presents FarmInsight, a comprehensive decision-support system that integrates two critical functionalities: crop recommendation and plant disease diagnosis. The crop recommendation module leverages an ensemble of ten machine learning algorithms, trained on agro-climatic and soil datasets, to provide data-driven crop suggestions. The disease diagnosis module uses a deep convolutional neural network (CNN) trained on plant leaf imagery to identify 38 classes of crop diseases with high accuracy. Additionally, FarmInsight includes a diagnosis recommendation layer powered by the Plant.id API, offering treatment suggestions and agricultural best practices based on the detected disease. Extensive evaluations reveal that the system achieves 99.3percent accuracy in crop recommendation and 99.1 percent validation accuracy in disease detection. Designed for deployment on edge devices and scalable to large agricultural areas, FarmInsight offers a holistic solution for modern farmers, agronomists, and policymakers.

title: Exploring Fine-Tuned EfficientNetB1 Performance Dynamics and Their Impact on Dermatological AI Systems
authors: Angelina George, Sai Rishi R, K Dinesh Kumar
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Skin disease classification presents significant challenges due to the vast diversity in visual characteristics caused by differences in skin tone, lighting conditions, and anatomical locations, compounded by image quality variability, artifacts, class imbalance, and overlapping disease features. This study evaluates the performance dynamics of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for multi-class skin disease classification using the original DermNet dataset from Kaggle, containing approximately 19,000 images across 23 categories. The dataset is employed in its entirety, without image removal or manual cleaning, thereby preserving real-world challenges such as class imbalance, visual diversity, and image noise. Six lightweight CNN architectures: MobileNetV3-Small, MobileNetV3-Large, RegNetY-400MF, ShuffleNetV2, EfficientNetB0 and EfficientNetB1, were trained and evaluated on Kaggle’s GPU environment. Detailed epoch profiling across metrics such as accuracy, loss, and Area Under the Curve (AUC) was conducted to analyze performance trends over training. Among these, a fine-tuned EfficientNetB1 consistently demonstrated superior generalization, achieving the highest test accuracy (66.97% at the 29th epoch), the most stable train–validation–test performance, the highest validation AUC (80.51%), and the highest validation accuracy (66.74% at the 36th epoch with early stopping). These findings highlight EfficientNetB1’s capability to handle the complexity of uncurated dermatological datasets, while epoch-level profiling offers deeper insight into model behavior under realistic, noisy conditions.

title: Paddy Plant Disease Detection Using Deep Convolutional Neural Network and Quantum Neural Network
authors: Pradeep B, Naidila Sadashiv, Nagamani N Purohit
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Rice is a globally cultivated crop and constitutes a primary staple food, sustaining nearly half of the world’s population. Paddy plant diseases have long presented serious problems for farmers and agricultural specialists, resulting in lower yields and making it harder to fulfill the expanding world population's need for food. It is essential to automatically detect these illnesses in order to increase agricultural output. Although a number of deep learning-based techniques have been put forward to identify paddy plant disease detection, many were ineffective because of the size and complexity of the model. But as an advancement we introduced a lightweight deep Convolutional Network(dCNN) that outperforms several state-of-art models in detecting paddy plant diseases and also proposed architecture for Quantum Neural Network (QNN), this hybrid approach leverages the expensive power of quantum computing to reduce model complexity. Experimental results were achieved about 99.3% for dCNN, with high precession, accuracy and f1-score, whereas for QNN achieved around 56.7%, for real world application in agricultural disease management.

title: Advanced Methodological Approaches to Multienergy Virtual Power Plant Operations: Integrating Behavioral Dynamics, Habit Formation, and Robust Splitting Algorithms
authors: Hao Chen, Shanke Mou, Nan Yang, Yingbei Yao, Yiqing Xu, Xiangwen Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: The increasing complexity of modern energy systems necessitates innovative frameworks for optimizing the operation and management of Multi-Energy Virtual Power Plants (MEVPPs). These systems integrate electricity, gas, and heat networks while incorporating renewable energy sources (RES) and energy storage systems (ESS) to achieve sustainability and resilience. This paper proposes a novel behavior-driven optimization framework that integrates Habit Formation Theory with Distributionally Robust Optimization (DRO) to address the multifaceted challenges of MEVPP operations. The framework uniquely models user behavior to capture the long-term effects of incentives on energy consumption, fostering sustainable demand response through dynamic adjustments. A multi-objective optimization model is developed to balance competing goals, including cost minimization, carbon emissions reduction, renewable energy utilization maximization, and grid stability. The model incorporates practical constraints, such as energy balance, thermal and gas network flows, and cybersecurity requirements. To solve the high-dimensional problem efficiently, a splitting algorithm tailored for DRO is proposed, enabling the decomposition of the optimization problem into computationally manageable subproblems. The methodology ensures scalability and robustness under uncertainties in renewable generation, market participation, and user behavior.

title: Design and Characteristic Analysis of a Hybrid Excitation Reverse Salient Pole Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor
authors: Xiao Yang, Zhangqi Liu, Xiping Liu, Tianqi Jin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel hybrid excited reverse salient pole motor (HE-RSPM) for electric vehicles, characterized by low torque ripple and a wide speed regulation range. Its innovation lies in introducing an H -shaped stator for winding the excitation winding to control the air gap flux, while adding magnetic barriers on the rotor side <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$q$</tex>-axis to achieve reverse salient pole characteristics. The paper details the HE-RSPM's topology and operating principles, comparing its performance against conventional Internal Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (IPMSM) and Surface-Mounted Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (SPMSM). Finite element analysis investigates the electromagnetic characteristics of HE-RSPM. Results validate the motor's design rationality, demonstrating that the HE-RSPM exhibits superior flux regulation capability and a broader speed regulation range.

title: Machine Learning Driven Auto-Tuning for Non-Uniform All-to-All Collectives
authors: Kunting Qi, Ke Fan, Jens Domke, Seydou Ba, Venkatram Vishwanath, Michael E. Papka, Sidharth Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: Non-uniform all-to-all communication patterns present optimization challenges in parallel computing due to their irregular data distribution and dynamic behavior. While MPI_Alltoallv provides the standard interface for such exchanges, achieving optimal performance requires careful selection among multiple implementation variants and tuning of algorithm-specific parameters. This paper presents a data-driven autotuning framework that combines machine learning-based runtime prediction with a lookup-table mechanism for fast configuration selection. The ML model estimates the communication time of each algorithm configuration under a given system setup, allowing the framework to identify the optimal implementation and parameter set based on predicted performance. We validate our approach through comprehensive benchmarking of MPI_Alltoallv and two specialized algorithms across varying process counts, message sizes, and tunable parameters. Applied to a real MPI-based transitive closure application on the Fugaku supercomputer, our framework achieves up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.03 \times$</tex> reduction in communication time over the vendor implementation, providing a detailed understanding of non-uniform collective communication behavior and a practical framework for automatic performance optimization in HPC applications.

title: ATGLNets: Adaptive Temporal Gated Liquid Neural Networks for Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring
authors: Tisha Hui Zi Liu, Chee Kiat Seow, Kenneth Gek Chuah Goh
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring (NILM) is a key technology for understanding energy consumption at the appliance level without per-device instrumentation. Traditional deep learning approaches for NILM face challenges in capturing multiscale temporal dynamics, generalising across diverse appliance types, and maintaining robustness to non-stationary household patterns. This paper proposes an Adaptive Temporal Gated Liquid Neural Networks (ATGLNets) to address these limitations. LNNs formulate neural networks as continuous-time differential equations, enabling adaptive temporal processing that naturally aligns with the diverse timescales of electrical signals. A comprehensive comparative analysis against established architectures (LSTM, GRU, TCN, ResNet, and Transformer) was carried out with the UK-DALE dataset. Results show that our proposed ATGLNets implementation outperforms traditional approaches by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$24-34 \%$</tex> in Mean Absolute Error across all appliance types and households, with particularly significant improvements for complex multi-scale appliances. The continuous-time formulation and adaptive time constants of LNNs provide superior capabilities for capturing electrical signatures' temporal dynamics, thus offering a promising direction for more effective energy disaggregation techniques.

title: A Fault Location Method for DC Transmission Lines Based on Fourier Transform and Composite Neural Network
authors: Junjie Lan, Ning Yu, Jinlin Wei, Han Zhang, Shikai Li, Yu Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Quickly determining the location of faults in high-voltage direct current transmission lines is of great significance for quickly resolving faults and delivering electricity. This article proposes a fault location method for DC transmission lines based on the combination of Fourier transform and composite neural network, which addresses the existing problems in current fault location methods. The method extracts voltage and current signal features at different fault distances based on Fourier transform, constructs a composite vector of transformed voltage and current fault features, and trains and tests the feature vectors at different fault locations based on the composite neural network model to optimize the weight parameters of the fault location model. Finally, the method was tested and the results showed that it has higher fault localization accuracy compared to traditional methods, and can effectively meet the requirements of on-site applications.

authors: Mikko Salminen, Satu Jumisko-Pyykkö, Eveliina Toivonen, Brian Scotcher, Wilho Rönkä, Gert Hattingh, Roosa Lundström, Antti-Tuomas Pulkka

title: Socially-Aware Collaborative Learning Framework for Robust and Adaptive V2V Communication in CDSs
authors: Bingyi Liu, Haoxiang Zhao, Weizhen Han, Mengwei Li, Shihong Cui, Xun Shao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: A group of connected and autonomous vehicles (CAVs) can drive cooperatively (so-called cooperative driving), and cooperative driving systems (CDSs) are recognized as an effective approach for enhancing traffic safety by periodically broadcasting beacon messages. However, current research often overlooks the complexity of hybrid transportation scenarios and intricate wireless environments, in which vehicles with heterogeneous communication patterns coexist. This coexistence easily leads to severe packet collisions, thereby degrading communication quality and undermining cooperative driving stability. Worse yet, each vehicle’s limited perception range in real-world deployments makes it non-trivial to learn globally coordinated strategies for resource usage. To address these challenges, we formulate time-slot acquisition for cooperative driving as a Markov game. We further propose a novel Cooperative HIerarchical Consensus LEarning (CHICLE) framework to solve this game. In particular, the hierarchical design of CHICLE mitigates the delayed reward issues arising from transmission collisions, enabling more foresighted decision-making. Additionally, at each level of the hierarchy, we integrate graph attention networks to capture multi-agent interactions among vehicles, thus better modeling hidden- and exposed-terminal effects. We also incorporate a consensus generator that helps unify local observations into consistent embeddings, fostering more effective agent coordination under distributed execution. Experimental results reveal that CHICLE attains performance comparable to centralized algorithms while requiring only 19% of their model capacity. Under harsh interference scenarios, CHICLE significantly surpasses all baselines by improving beacon reception by at least 10% and reducing communication latency by 30% or more.

title: Machine Learning for THz-RIS enabled 6G: Survey and Key Challenges
authors: Karamot Kehinde Biliaminu, Joaquim Bastos, Jonathan Rodriguez, Felipe Gil-Castiñeira
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The integration of Machine Learning (ML) with terahertz (THz) communication and Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) is set to transform the sixth-generation (6G) wireless network landscape. THz frequencies provide ultra-high bandwidth, enabling support for data-intensive applications, whereas RIS offers intelligent control of the wireless environment through passive beam manipulation. However, the integration of these technologies presents significant challenges, including substantial path loss, heightened susceptibility to blockages, and increased system complexity. ML techniques, which are known for their ability to optimize systems in an adaptive and data-driven manner, have emerged as promising solutions to address these issues. This paper presents a thorough overview of the role of ML in enhancing THz-RIS-enabled 6G networks, specifically, its application towards optimised beamforming, resource allocation, channel estimation, and mobility management. Additionally, this paper identifies critical challenges, including data scarcity, model interpretability, scalability, and real-time deployment, which must be addressed to enable the effective use of ML in future 6G systems. Finally, potential research directions to steer the development of intelligent and high-capacity 6G communication systems are outlined.

title: SLIM: Stable Latent Integration for Robust Watermark in Diffusion Model
authors: Xiaoxi Kong, Pengdi Chen, Bin Li, Jieyu Yuan, Zhanchuan Cai, Hao Wu, Lifeng Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Embedding watermarks in the diffusion latent space improves robustness but often alters visual content due to the distribution shift between watermarked and clean latent variables. To address this issue, stable latent integration watermark (SLIM) is proposed in this paper, in which watermarks are integrated into the features output by the noise prediction network of a diffusion model, while ensuring that the perturbation introduced in the diffusion latent space remains negligible. Specifically, a watermark encoder–decoder is first trained to convert binary watermark sequences into watermark latent variables that are dimensionally compatible with the diffusion latent variables, enabling flexible and reliable embedding and extraction. The watermark latent variables are processed through the first down-sampling block of the denoising U-Net, and the resulting watermark features are fused with the block output to minimize interference with image semantics. To counteract the perturbations in diffusion features induced by watermark embedding and to ensure accurate watermark extraction, the denoising U-Net is efficiently fine-tuned using a low-rank adaptation module. Experimental results demonstrate that SLIM achieves superior generation quality while exhibiting exceptional robustness against diverse attacks compared with baseline methods. Code will be available at https://github.com/XiaoxiKong/SLIM.

title: A Compact Dual-band Metantenna on PDMS Substrate for WBAN Applications
authors: Zhenzhen Jiang, Zijian Dong, Pengfei Song, Mark Leach, Yi Huang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A compact dual-band metantenna for wireless body area network (WBAN) applications is presented. The design is realized using conductive fabrics on a polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) substrate, ensuring flexibility. The dual-band periodic metasurface exhibits polarization-insensitive behavior from 0° to 90° under normal incidence and maintains stable performance over a wide range of oblique incidence angles. Measured impedance bandwidths of 840 MHz and 1120 MHz are achieved at 2.45 GHz and 5.8 GHz. Anechoic-chamber measurements confirm stable S-parameters and radiation characteristics under bending conditions. The metasurface enhances the realized gain by 115.7% at 2.45 GHz and 103.7% at 5.8 GHz. On-body SAR evaluations demonstrate compliance with safety standards. The results indicate that the proposed antenna is suitable for wearable WBAN applications.

title: XGB-AirSched: A Machine Learning–Guided Multi-objective Optimization Framework for Airport Slot Allocation
authors: Saad Alharbi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The airport slot allocation problem poses a complex challenge in balancing operational efficiency, fairness, and delay minimization at congested airports. Researchers are turning to optimization-based approaches, and more recently machine learning (ML), to overcome the limitations of traditional slot allocation methods. However, ML and optimization have been applied only in sequence or separately, and conflict prediction has rarely been integrated into the evolutionary optimization process. Moreover, optimization frameworks have not been evaluated in settings that mimic real-time operations. This study introduces XGB-AirSched, a hybrid framework that integrates ML-based conflict prediction into a multiobjective evolutionary optimization process for intelligent slot assign-ment. The framework bridges computational optimization and air-traffic scheduling by combining discrete-event simulation with predictive optimization. By integrating an XGBoost classifier with the nondominated sorting ge-netic algorithm II, the search process avoids infeasible slot combinations while optimizing for minimal delay and equitable allocation. The resulting schedules were validated through discrete-event simulations to assess real-world feasibility. On real and synthetic datasets, XGB-AirSched achieved conflict-free allocations with improved con-vergence behavior, outperforming traditional optimization methods. In simulations on real-world-scale datasets, XGB-AirSched demonstrated practical readiness for deployment within airport scheduling systems.

title: Homotopy-Guided Potential Games for Congestion-Aware Navigation_supp1-3675907.mp4
authors: Mohammed Irshadh Ismaaeel Sathyamangalam Imran
publish: N/A
abstract: We address the multi-agent motion planning problem where interactions, collisions, and congestion co-exist. Conventional game-theoretic planners capture interactions among agents but often converge to conservative, congested equilibria. Homotopy planners, on the other hand, can explore topologically distinct paths, but lack mechanisms to account for the interdependence of agents' future actions. We propose a unified framework that leverages homotopy classes as structured strategy sets within a receding-horizon setup. At each planning stage, a deterministic homotopy planner generates topologically distinct paths for each agent, conditioned on the joint configuration. To avoid intractable growth of candidate paths, we propose a simple heuristic filtering step that selects a top-K subset of the most suitable congestion-free joint strategies to ensure computational tractability. These serve as initializations for a potential game that enforces homotopy-consistent constraints and yields a generalized open-loop Nash equilibrium (OLNE), with penalties discouraging abrupt strategy shifts in a receding-horizon setting. Simulations with three agents demonstrate improved efficiency (faster completion) and enhanced safety (greater inter-agent clearance, leading to reduced congestion) compared to a local baseline and NH ORCA that do not reason about homotopies. Hardware trials with two robots and one human demonstrate robustness to irrational behaviors, where our method adapts by switching to alternative feasible equilibria while the baseline game fails.

title: Hyperspectral Band Selection with Dynamic Graph Enhancement and Hierarchical Feature Fusion
authors: Jiahua Zhang, Jingyuan Wang, Dejie Li, Jun Zhang, Xudong Sun, Xiaodi Shang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Graph convolutional networks (GCNs) have been widely used in hyperspectral band selection (BS) and exhibits great potential. However, the substantial redundant information inherent in hyperspectral imagery and the fixed graph structure hinder the ability of GCNs to effectively capture the complex inter-band relationships. Moreover, most existing methods rely on a single criterion during the BS phase, often leading to biased results. To address these issues, this paper proposes a dynamic graph enhancement and hierarchical feature fusion (DEHF) model for efficient and robust BS. Specifically, the model first utilizes an autoencoder (AE) to extract key latent features of ground objects, thereby enhancing the contribution of high-quality representation in the BS process. To overcome the limitations of conventional graph construction, we introduce a two-stage graph optimization strategy comprising initial graph optimization and dynamic enhanced GCN (DEGCN). This strategy adaptively updates both the graph structure and node representations, enabling more accurate modeling of the complex local-global relationships among spectral bands. In order to further enhance feature integration of the model, a hierarchical fusion mechanism is designed to fuse multi-source features extracted by the AE and DEGCN across different spatial scales, thereby emphasizing the responses of salient bands. Finally, to comprehensively evaluate band importance, a multi-dimensional priority criterion is developed by integrating the information entropy and class discriminability, which guides spectral clustering to yield a more informative and representative band subset. Experimental results on three real hyperspectral datasets demonstrate the superior performance of DEHF compared to state-of-the-art methods. The source-code is available at https://github.com/ShangXD1994/DEHF-for-hyperspectral-band-selection.

title: Enhancing Material Characterization: Multi-Fidelity Deep Neural Network for Stress-Strain Curve Prediction from Small Punch Test Data
authors: Zhengni Yang, Rui Yang, Weijian Han, Wenyuan Kang, Xiaohan Chen, Ruihan Dong, Jingyi Zhang, Chao Tong, Jingyu Kong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The accurate determination of a material’s true stress-strain relationship is crucial for predicting its performance in engineering applications. However, traditional methods for determining material behavior often depend on extensive experiments. Although these methods are essential, they are inherently complex, time-consuming, and often suffer from limited accuracy and reliability when small sample sizes are involved. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a multi-fidelity deep neural network architecture that utilizes both low-fidelity and high-fidelity datasets to predict true stress-strain curves from small punch test data. The low-fidelity data are calculated using an empirical function based on the Swift model, providing an initial understanding of material behavior. In contrast, the finite element method provides high-fidelity data, enhancing the model’s ability to capture complex material characteristics. This framework merges low-fidelity and high-fidelity networks to estimate mechanical properties with low-fidelity and high-fidelity data. The effectiveness of the proposed approach has been demonstrated through experimental validation, highlighting its exceptional ability to generalize across material thicknesses and properties beyond the training set. The proposed approach outperformed traditional empirical methods and maintained strong performance even when applied to real-world materials.

title: Advanced State Feedback Control of DC-DC Converters for Renewable Energy Systems
authors: Y. Mo, G. Mirzaeva, E. E. Espinosa
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: DC microgrid is an increasingly popular architecture for integration of renewable energy sources, many of which are DC by nature. Voltage controlled DC-DC converters play a crucial role in the modern energy systems by providing high-quality control of the microgrid DC-bus voltage. The current paper proposed a novel design methodology for three-phase interleaved non-isolated DC-DC converters, based on principles of state feedback control and extended Kalman Observer. It presents practical observer models, detailed design of gains and parameters, and develops control algorithms comparable in computational efficiency to PI control. Advantages of the proposed control over the traditional methods include faster dynamic performance and superior quality of the output DC voltage and load current. This is demonstrated by simulation and experiment, in comparison to the conventional double-loop PI control in the context of DC microgrid operation.

title: Short-Term Wind Power Forecasting Supporting Time-Scale Adaptive Update and Fusion of Multivariable Spatio-Temporal Information
authors: Xin Li, Chang Liu, Shijie Guan, Lin Ye, Yingying Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Short-term power forecasting for single wind farms mainly focuses on analyzing the temporal relationships in historical power generation data, often neglecting the spatial relationships between multiple meteorological variables in wind power generation systems. Moreover, the spatial coupling relationships between meteorological variables, such as wind speed and air pressure, exhibit dynamic changes across different time scales, which are challenging for machine learning models to capture. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel short-term wind power forecasting method from a graph-based perspective, which supports time-scale adaptive updates and integrates multi-variable spatiotemporal information. First, multi-scale dynamic identification is performed using Fourier transform. Then, a multi-scale adaptive graph convolutional network is used to model wind farms and meteorological variables as graph structures, thereby capturing the spatial relationships of variables across multiple scales. Simultaneously, Mamba attention, combined with Transformers, is introduced to explore temporal dependencies, resulting in improved wind power output forecasting. Finally, an NRBO-STL trend error compensation module is designed to correct the forecasting results. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method effectively leverages the spatio-temporal coupling characteristics between wind power output and meteorological data, achieving higher forecasting accuracy.

title: FedGA: Genetic Algorithm-Guided Federated Learning for Medical Image Segmentation with Non-IID Features
authors: Faisal Ahmed, Rodrigo Moreno, David Sánchez, Zouhair Haddi, Josep Domingo-Ferrer
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enhances data privacy and compliance with data regulations by enabling multiple decentralized parties to collaboratively train machine learning models without sharing their data. This makes it an ideal paradigm for the healthcare domain. Despite its inherent privacy-by-design, FL faces performance and convergence challenges when dealing with non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) data. Although previous studies have primarily addressed the challenges of skewed label distribution across clients, which are more effective for classification, we focus in this paper on the much less explored challenge of multi-domain FL in medical image segmentation tasks, where client data originate from different domains with varying feature distributions. To address this problem, we propose FedGA, which performs heuristic, gradient-free optimization on the server side after local model aggregation in order to optimize the global model by using a genetic algorithm. Empirical results on breast lesion segmentation from ultrasound images and prostate segmentation from T2w MRI images show the potential of FedGA in federated learning schemes. Specifically, FedGA improves the segmentation precision in critical boundary regions compared to existing approaches, accelerates global model convergence, reduces the total number of communication rounds required to achieve optimal performance, and offers better overall efficiency.

title: Multi-Satellite Coordinated Beam Hopping for Interference Mitigation under Tilted Beam Effects: A Graph-Theoretic Approach
authors: Zijun Liu, Yafei Wang, Wenjin Wang, Yi Sun, Hong Yan, Zhili Sun
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: This letter presents a low-complexity multi-satellite coordinated beam hopping (BH) algorithm based on graph theory to achieve efficient resource allocation and interference mitigation (IM) in large-scale low earth orbit (LEO) satellite communication systems. We first reveal that the severe interference impact of tilted beams necessitates the joint optimization of satellite-cell association (SCA) and BH slot assignment (BHSA). To address the coupling between SCA and BHSA, we innovatively reformulate this problem as a specialized dynamic graph coloring problem, where both the graph topology and the vertex coloring are optimization variables for this problem. Consequently, we propose a two-stage algorithm integrating the minimum-cost maximum flow (MCMF), tabu search (TS), and graph coloring (GC). The MCMF algorithm efficiently generates a high quality initial SCA scheme under the limitation of satellite beam resource. The TS algorithm further performs joint optimization of SCA and BHSA, where the former is explored through neighboring solution generation, and the latter is obtained through GC. The simulation results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed algorithm, which can achieve high-quality seamless coverage with low complexity in mega-constellation and dense cell scenarios.

title: Task-Prediction-Augmented Multi-Agent Collaborative Offloading in Space-Air-Ground Integrated Network
authors: Linghao Zhang, Bo Xu, Haitao Zhao, Suyao Huang, Qin Wang, Hongbo Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: The space-air-ground integrated network (SAGIN) can provide a promising architecture for computation-intensive mobile applications through the complementary advantages of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) and low Earth orbit (LEO) satellites. However, efficient task offloading remains challenging due to the strong coupling among UAV trajectories, user association, and resource allocation, especially with dynamic and uncertain task workloads. In this paper, we aim to minimize the average task completion delay by enabling UAVs and satellites to cooperatively assist users in executing complex computational tasks. We formulate the joint optimization problem as a decentralized partially observable Markov decision process (DEC-POMDP), where task workload prediction is incorporated to enhance foresight and coordination among agents. Then, a task-prediction-augmented multi-agent collaborative offloading (TAMACO) framework is proposed, which integrates task-prediction-augmented multiagent proximal policy optimization (TA-MAPPO) for UAV trajectory control. Meanwhile, a prediction-augmented coalition formation game (PA-CFG) algorithm embedded into TA-MAPPO is proposed to solve the joint problem of user association and computing resource allocation. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed TAMACO framework achieves up to 19.3% reduction in average task completion delay compared to nonpredictive baselines, validating its effectiveness for real-time task offloading in dynamic SAGIN.

title: A Static, Soft Error Tolerant, TSPC Flip-flop for High Reliability Applications
authors: Yugal Kishore Maheshwari, Manoj Sachdev
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: Radiation induced soft errors can upset data stored in registers. In this paper, we propose a radiation hardened by design, static, contention-free, true single-phase clock flip-flop, referred to as TSPC-DICE. This proposed flip-flop is based on the primary-secondary configuration and uses radiation hardened DICE cell in both primary and secondary latches. The post-layout simulation results in CMOS 65 nm technology show that TSPC-DICE is power and energy-efficient amongst all considered radiation hardened flip-flops. Test-chip measurement results show that at a voltage of 1 V, a clock of 5 MHz, and data activity of 6.25%, scan-chain of TSPC-DICE is 11% more power-efficient than the scan-chain of the regular primary-secondary flip-flop, MS-DFF. Accelerated neutron radiation testing results indicate that the proposed flip-flop exhibits a lower FIT rate compared to the traditional flip-flop.

title: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management

title: Wheat moisture content detection method based on data-driven microwave multi-frequency optimization under multiparameter influence
authors: Dong Dai, Yehong Liu, Xu Mao, Shujin Guo, Du Chen, Astanakulov Dullievich Komil, Shumao Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Non-destructive measurement of wheat moisture content is of paramount importance for minimizing grain loss and guaranteeing quality, yet its accuracy is easily compromised by various external environmental factors. To compensate for this, this research develops a microwave detection platform for wheat MC, and explores the impacts of external parameters on MC detection by adjusting the antenna distances, sample thicknesses and frequency. Through machine learning, this research established a wheat MC prediction model using the RF algorithm, and proposed a dual feature parameter optimization method based on data-driven methods that combines clustering analysis with swarm intelligence algorithms, achieving frequency subset screening applicable to different sample thicknesses. Based on the data characteristics, an optimal four-frequency subset with the highest sensitivity was identified, which effectively improved the MC detection accuracy (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.9905, RMSE=0.9722, MSE=0.9452). The methodology provides an innovative solution for implementing microwave technology in grain product inspection, effectively balancing measurement accuracy with operational efficiency.

title: Ultrasound Video Segmentation of Uterine Niche Using Temporal Graph Enhancement
authors: Jimin Joung, Manhua Liu, Jian Zhang, Yang Wang, Yuan Tian
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: The uterine niche is a localized defect formed at the site of a cesarean scar and is clinically associated with chronic pelvic pain and abnormal bleeding. Accurate segmentation is essential for reliable diagnosis, yet remains challenging due to intrinsic signal artifacts, severe speckle noise, and sparse medical annotations. To address these challenges, we propose a robust ultrasound video segmentation framework that integrates three complementary components. First, an entropy-based progressive complexity curriculum regulates information complexity during training, stabilizing optimization and improving robustness under noisy and data-scarce conditions. Second, a graph Fourier temporal enhancement module injects informative adjacent-frame features and multi-scale temporal context into feature representations, clarifying blurred boundaries and strengthening the delineation of fine anatomical structures. Third, a FlickerSteady loss explicitly constrains inter-frame prediction variations, suppressing temporal flickering and ensuring smooth and continuous segmentation over time. Extensive experiments on clinical ultrasound datasets demonstrate that the proposed framework significantly outperforms existing methods in both spatial accuracy and temporal stability. The source code will be released at https://github.com/Joungjimin/US-Video-MedSAM2.

title: Time-Varying Sub-Synchronous Oscillation Mitigation in DFIG Wind Farms via Dual-Layer Adaptive Band-Pass Filtering
authors: Chuang Liu, Rui Xu, Cong Sun, Heling Yang, Rakhmonov Ikromjon Usmonovich
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: This paper proposes a sub-synchronous oscillation (SSO) suppression strategy based on a Dual-layer Adaptive Band-Pass Filter (DL-ABPF). In the proposed scheme, the outer-layer filter (ABPFout) is employed for modal separation and monitoring, while the inner-layer filter (ABPFin) is activated once dual discriminant criteria are satisfied, thereby enabling real-time targeted suppression. Operating under a wide-area monitoring framework, the method utilizes secondary dynamic data storage combined with the ESPRIT algorithm to achieve online identification of the target modal frequency and damping factor. When the criteria are met, the center frequency of ABPFin is adaptively updated, and its output is fed back into the converter controller through a damping channel, forming a closed-loop process of “monitoring-discrimination-suppression.” Case studies on both SSCI and SSR scenarios, conducted in MATLAB/Simulink, demonstrate that the proposed strategy maintains real-time tracking and effective mitigation of target modes under frequency drifts and varying operating conditions, thereby significantly enhancing the robustness and universality of oscillation suppression. Compared with conventional singlelayer filtering approaches, this work, for the first time, unifies monitoring and suppression within a dual-layer structure, providing an innovative framework for the dynamic prevention and control of sub-synchronous oscillations.

title: Layer-Wise Relevance Propagation (LRP)-Guided Clustering-Based Compression Framework for Deploying Deep Neural Network Models on Memory-Constrained Devices
authors: Debajyoti Sahoo, Nithyashree Ravi, Divij Ghose, Sashikumaar Ganesan
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: The deployment of deep neural networks on edge devices remains challenging due to their computational demands and memory requirements. We introduce a novel compression framework that integrates Layer-wise Relevance Propagation (LRP) with high-dimensional clustering to simultaneously identify and eliminate both unimportant and redundant neurons. Unlike conventional magnitude or gradient-based pruning methods, our approach computes LRP scores across all network layers to capture functional dependencies, performs global k-means clustering to group functionally similar neurons, and implements parameter sharing through representative neurons or synthetic centroids. We provide empirical analysis demonstrating how relevance conservation principles yield more reliable importance estimates than traditional methods. Extensive experiments on MNIST and CIFAR-10 datasets using fully connected networks, ResNet-9, and ResNet-18 architectures demonstrate compression ratios up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16 \times$</tex> with minimal accuracy degradation (less than 2% in most cases), outperforming pruning methods including SNIP, GraSP, and Fisher pruning. Edge deployment on platforms ranging from NVIDIA Jetson to Raspberry Pi shows consistent performance and reduced inference latency, validating our method's practical applicability for resource-constrained environments.

title: SFCC: A Scalable and Flexible RDMA Congestion Control Algorithm
authors: Yi Liao, Anran Xu, Wenming Zheng, Biyao Che, Jian Tang, Yonghang Zhang, Xiaoping Fan, Luren Liu, Ying Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: The rapid evolution of cloud computing, big data, and Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies has created an urgent demand for ultra-high bandwidth and ultra-low latency in modern data centers. Although Remote Direct Memory Access (RDMA) technology has been widely adopted, traditional congestion control algorithms such as Priority-based Flow Control (PFC) and Data Center Quantized Congestion Notification (DCQCN) face significant challenges in scalability, deployment flexibility, and tail latency management in complex network environments. To address these limitations, we propose SFCC, a sender-driven rate control solution based on Round-Trip Time (RTT) that requires no switch configuration. SFCC incorporates an adaptive Additive Increase Multiplicative Decrease (AIMD) control loop for enhanced scalability and latency reduction, an RTT re-estimation mechanism to correct deviations, dynamic RTT calculation for optimized queue management, and Negative Acknowledgment (NAK) signal utilization for fast convergence during packet loss. Implemented on commercial RDMA Network Interface Cards (NICs) using a Programmable Congestion Control (PCC) platform, SFCC has been evaluated through small-scale testbeds, storage network testbeds, and NS-3 simulations of ultra-large-scale incast scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that SFCC significantly improves throughput while reducing switch queue lengths, decreases the 99th percentile tail latency by 55.2 %, maintains high reliability under packet loss conditions, and achieves up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5. 4 \%}$</tex> reduction in flow completion time for small flows compared to DCQCN, along with faster convergence speed.

title: FDIA Detection Using Image Encoding and Residual Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Kaiyang Liu, Peng Cao, Bo Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: To enhance the resilience of power system equipment against False Data Injection Attacks (FDIA), this paper introduces the GADF-ResNet framework for FDIA detection in power systems. The framework transforms raw data from SCADA and PMU into 2D image data through Max-Min normalization and Gramian Angular Difference Field (GADF), thereby preserving spatial features for computer vision-based detection. The integration of ResNet's shortcut connections effectively mitigates the issue of gradient vanishing, leading to improved feature extraction. Evaluations conducted on the IEEE 14-bus system and IEEE 118-bus system show that the proposed framework outperforms traditional methods such as SVM, LightGBM, and MLP in terms of F1-score, elementaccuracy (Eacc), and sample-accuracy (Sacc) across different attack intensities. By combining advanced data representation techniques with architectural optimization, the GADF-ResNet framework achieves topology-independent FDIA detection while enhancing interpretability through the application of computer vision methodologies.

title: Performance Comparison of A Dual Element Micro Strip Patch Antenna Array With and Without Metamaterial Superstrate
authors: N. Durga Naga Lakshmi, Talam Navya Sai Sri, Ramayanam Suresh, Polavarapu Jeevan Teja, Pokuri Jaswanth Kumar, Vipparthi Sajeev Abhishek
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The Meta-material Superstrate Antenna plays a major role for improving the gain of the antenna. The foremost objective of this work is to enhance the gain and directivity of the Metamaterial Microstrip Patch Antenna used for Wireless Point to Point Communication applications. An unloaded antenna array reverberates at IEEE 802.16a 6.3GHz Wi-MAX band with gain of 3.4 dBi. However, when the similar arrangement is encumbered with a meta-material superstrate, comprising of a pair of Split Ring Resonators (SRR), there is instantaneous improvement of gain to 4.0 dBi, which corresponds to gain improvement by 17.5%. The fabrication of this proposed antenna array is carried out, and its simulated and practical results are compared. When the metamaterial superstrate loaded antenna was related to the traditional unloaded antenna, it was revealed that metamaterials have a lot of potential for improving antenna performance.

title: Scene Text Detection and Recognition Using EasyOCR: Performance Analysis and Evaluation
authors: Suman, Champa H N
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The extraction of text in natural environments is challenging, particularly because of the variance in complex scenes combined with diverse patterns and styles of text. A detailed method for the detection and recognition of scene text using EasyOCR and OpenCV is proposed. Leveraging EasyOCR’s deep learning capabilities, a custom evaluation pipeline is developed for system performance analysis. Experiments on real-world images achieved precision, recall, and F-score values of 1.00, 0.89, and 0.94 respectively. The system provides bounding box visualization and recognizes text of different orientations and styles. This work contributes to real-time automated text detection from natural images using standard information retrieval metrics.

title: Fabric Defect Detection Using CNNs for Enhanced Textile Production Efficiency
authors: R. Rajagopal, M. Abirami, Hari Krishna Kalidindi, P. K. Sheela Shantha Kumari, C Srinivasan, Jagan P
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Identifying fabric defects is essential for maintaining quality and reducing losses in textile manufacturing. Conventional hand inspection techniques are laborious, subjective, and frequently inadequate in identifying minor problems. This study created a model based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to automate fabric flaw identification with excellent accuracy and efficiency. The publicly accessible TILDA Fabric Defect Dataset, comprising 12,000 labelled fabric images across six defect categories, enabled the proposed CNN to achieve an overall accuracy of 98.60% in defect classification. The system processed inspection images at a rate of 30 frames per second (FPS), facilitating real-time implementation on production lines. The CNN-based method decreased fault detection time by 65% and enhanced flaw identification consistency by 22% compared to conventional manual inspection. These findings highlight the capacity of CNNs to markedly improve textile manufacturing efficiency, reduce operational expenses, and provide superior quality assurance. Future work will concentrate on broadening the defect classifications and refining the model for implementation on edge devices in smart textile manufacturing settings.

title: Graph Learning for Cooperative Cell-Free ISAC Systems: From Optimization to Estimation
authors: Peng Jiang, Ming Li, Rang Liu, Qian Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Cell-free integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems have emerged as a promising paradigm for sixth-generation (6G) networks, enabling simultaneous high-rate data transmission and high-precision radar sensing through cooperative distributed access points (APs). Fully exploiting these capabilities requires a unified design that bridges system-level optimization with multi-target parameter estimation. This paper proposes an end-to-end graph learning approach to close this gap, modeling the entire cell-free ISAC network as a heterogeneous graph to jointly design the AP mode selection, user association, precoding, and echo signal processing for multi-target position and velocity estimation. In particular, we propose two novel heterogeneous graph learning frameworks: a dynamic graph learning framework and a lightweight mirror-based graph attention network (mirror-GAT) framework. The dynamic graph learning framework employs structural and temporal attention mechanisms integrated with a three-dimensional convolutional neural network (3D-CNN), enabling superior performance and robustness in cell-free ISAC environments. Conversely, the mirror-GAT framework significantly reduces computational complexity and signaling overhead through a bi-level iterative structure with shared adjacency. Simulation results validate that both proposed graph-learning-based frameworks achieve significant improvements in multi-target position and velocity estimation accuracy compared to conventional heuristic and optimization-based designs. Particularly, the mirror-GAT framework demonstrates substantial reductions in computational time and signaling overhead, underscoring its suitability for practical deployments.

title: Scalable Machine Learning with Apache Spark MLlib: A Fuzzy Rough Feature Selection Framework for Big Data Dimensionality Reduction
authors: Rajkumar Sharma, Dr.Devdas Saraswat
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Massive datasets in fields like cybersecurity and high-energy physics are not only a challenge to process a large amount of data but also to take into consideration uncertainties and noise inherent in these fields. Sequential feature selection algorithms or those that are rooted in the theoretically sound Fuzzy Rough Set Theory (FRST) do not tend to scale effectively because of the computational cost of determining similarity relations, which normally demands computations of instances. The framework presented in this paper, the Scalable Fuzzy Rough Feature Selection (SFRFS) framework, is a new, distributed architecture that can be seamlessly integrated into the Apache Spark MLlib environment. SFRFS incurs horizontal partitioning and parallel dependency computation to overcome the quadratic complexity barrier, and scale to datasets of more than 10 million instances with efficient preprocessing. Three large-scale benchmark datasets, KDD Cup 1999, HIGGS, and HEPMASS are used to validate the framework. A comparative study of four different Spark MLlib classifiers, including Logistic Regression (LR), Random Forest (RF), Gradient Boosted Trees (GBT), and Naive Bayes (NB), show that SFRFS attains a high level of dimensionality reduction (up to 70 percent) and provides a large factor in improving training time (up to 7.8 times faster than Gradient Boosted Trees (GBT)) with comparable or slightly worse classification accuracy (Area Under the ROC Curve, AUC).

title: Scalable Deep Learning Architectures for Lane Tracking, Road Segmentation, and Collision Alerts in ADAS
authors: Sreepal Reddy Bolla, Revanth Reddy Bandaru, Krishnam Raju Narsepalle, Balakrishna Aitha
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Road traffic collisions remain a major challenge to public health, prompting the development of solutions like computer vision-based Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS). Therefore, this research introduces a vision-powered ADAS that utilizes deep learning and computer vision techniques to enhance driving safety by detecting and interpreting critical visual cues. The framework consists of three core components: lane detection, road segmentation, and Forward Collision Warning (FCW). Lane detection leverages Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to identify lane markings and assist in avoiding unintended lane shifts. Road segmentation is performed with a U-Net-based model to categorize different parts of the road, aiding comprehensive scene interpretation. The FCW component utilizes segmented outputs to predict potential collisions and issues drivers’ visual alerts. To evaluate the system, the TuSimple dataset supported lane detection, while the BDD100K dataset was applied for road segmentation. The U-Net architecture outperformed the FCN in detection benchmarks. The segmentation system achieved an accuracy of 85.79%, a mean IoU of 36.67%, and a mAP of 51.52%, even when operating with limited hardware capacity.

title: A Comprehensive Study on Edge-AI Resource Recommendation Techniques for 5G/6G Channel
authors: Ashwini Sampath, Syed Thouheed Ahmed
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: This study presents a multi-dimensional investigative framework aimed at optimizing resource allocation in 5G/6G cloud services through predictive analytics and adaptive scheduling techniques. The proposed framework integrates an open-stack model to enhance resource pooling and scheduling efficiency in dynamic cloud environments, minimizing latency and operational costs. Additionally, a robust cross-cloud resource sharing framework is developed to facilitate seamless data exchange among cloud clusters, addressing interoperability challenges across diverse network slices. By employing intelligent orchestration and predictive algorithms, the framework ensures optimal allocation of computing, storage, and network resources, promoting scalability and high-performance service delivery. This interconnected approach effectively resolves resource management complexities, contributing to reliable cloud operations in next-generation 5G/6G communication channels

title: Temporal-based Intelligent Machine Health Monitoring by Probabilistic MultiModal Data Fusion using Ensemble GBM and LSTM Model
authors: Lakshmaiya N
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Smart machine monitoring systems are prone to a high amount of data collection that is of a multimodal data type. It is common to collect large amounts of information for predicting machine condition to facilitate maintenance purposes. Handling the enormous amount of data and classifying it is a tedious task. Data fusion is a method that combines information from multiple modalities to provide an integrated output. In practical scenarios, data fusion based on the temporal dependency of information from monitoring systems is essential for taking timely action. Therefore, the proposed work utilises probabilistic data fusion using the Ensemble Gradient Boosting model and the LSTM to provide temporal dependency for the generated sequential fused data. The proposed work is simulated using the smart manufacturing-based IoT monitoring dataset and the spectrogram-based vibrational analysis dataset from Kaggle, which achieved 95.2% accuracy and 96.5% specificity in probabilistic fused multimodal data using LSTM for prediction purposes.
