title: Postprocessing Time Alignment Method of Power Angular Delay Profile for Automated mmWave WPAN Channel Sounding
authors: Hiroaki Endo, Yusuke Koda, Norichika Ohmi, Hiroshi Harada
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Accurate propagation channel measurements and modeling––encompassing both angular and delay characteristics–– are fundamental to the design of millimeter wave (mmWave) communication systems. Accordingly, steerable directional antennas and strict time-synchronization are commonly employed to capture power angle delay profiles (PADPs) that share synchronized standard time axes. However, time synchronization requires labor-intensive management to ensure clock synchronization during or after measurements, which hinders the construction of massive propagation channel databases. Thus, this study aimed to develop a streamlined channel sounding system by fully automating the PADP acquisition flow. First, an automated data-driven postprocessing method is proposed to reconstruct a time-synchronized PADP from an unsynchronized PADP, thereby discarding the requirement for managing clock synchronization. This approach is well-suited for simple channel sounding setups in mmWave wireless personal area network (WPAN) scenarios. The proposed method was evaluated through raytracing simulations tailored to WPAN systems with a comprehensive analysis of the impact of various measurement parameters. Simulation results demonstrated that the method achieved a time alignment error of less than 1 ns in over 99% of cases across various receiver (Rx) locations and Rx antenna rotation angles in noise-free environments. Moreover, a real-world experimental evaluation revealed that the time-aligned PADP obtained using the proposed method reached an alignment Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) below 1 ns. These findings strongly demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed method for channel sounding in mmWave WPAN systems.

title: Trust and Security in Critical Digital Infrastructures: A Conditional Analysis of Acceptance and Intention to Change in Electronic Voting Systems
authors: Celio Castro Wermelinger, Ari Melo Mariano, João Mello Da Silva
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Electronic voting systems have become central components of contemporary democratic infrastructures, aiming to increase operational efficiency, reduce fraud, and enhance electoral integrity. However, their legitimacy depends not only on technical robustness but also on citizens’ perceptions of security, transparency, and institutional trust, particularly in polarized sociopolitical environments. The objective of this study is to analyze the heterogeneity of the mechanisms underlying both the acceptance and the contestation of electronic voting machines, examining how trust, performance expectancy, effort expectancy, and social influence vary as a function of individual characteristics. An exploratory and descriptive study with a quantitative approach was conducted using structural equation modeling (PLS-SEM), based on a sample of 1,851 Brazilian voters familiar with electronic voting. A hierarchical second-order construct was specified to model Overall Trust by integrating Perceived Security and Trust in Technology. The model exhibited high explanatory power for Behavioral Intention (R² = 0.848) and moderate explanatory power for Intention to Change (R² = 0.588). Overall Trust emerged as the dominant predictor, positively influencing behavioral intention and strongly and negatively influencing intention to change. Performance Expectancy and Social Influence positively affected Behavioral Intention, whereas Effort Expectancy was not significant. For Intention to Change, Performance Expectancy and Effort Expectancy showed positive but smaller effects, while Social Influence was not significant. Moderation analyses revealed significant effects of Gender, Age, Education Level, and Political Orientation, indicating heterogeneous behavioral patterns across population segments. These findings indicate that acceptance and contestation of electronic voting systems are governed by dynamic sociotechnical interactions between perceived technical safeguards, institutional trust, and individual characteristics, with implications for electoral governance and trust-oriented public policies.

title: TDEMSNet:Remote Sensing Image Spatiotemporal Fusion Network Based on Temporal Difference Enhancement and Multi-Stage Attention Fusion
authors: Weiquan Kong, Yurong Qian, Lu Bai, Yuanxu Wang, Yingying Fan, Sen Luo
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: In remote sensing image spatiotemporal fusion, temporal difference features and multi-scale features play a crucial role in the fusion performance. However, insufficient exploration of temporal difference features in existing methods often leads to noise introduction, making it difficult to make accurate predictions in areas with land cover changes, such as floods, phenological shifts and land type changes. Moreover, insufficient utilization of multi-scale features limits the ability to perceive temporal changes. To address these issues, we propose a temporal difference enhancement and multi-stage attention fusion network for remote sensing image spatiotemporal fusion (TDEMSNet). Specifically, a multi-scale convolution-mixed attention block is designed to replace Transformer, aiming to extract global temporal information at different scales of remote sensing images and enhance temporal change perception. This module models long-range temporal dependencies with a large receptive field, while preserving the image's spatial structure and fully considering multi-scale features. Next, a temporal difference enhancement network is constructed to extract and enhance multiscale temporal difference features, suppress noise interference and improve the model's sensitivity to areas with land cover changes. Finally, a multi-stage attention fusion network integrates multi-scale spatiotemporal information. The experimental results validate the superiority of our method over nine state-of-theart (SOTA) methods in terms of reconstruction quality, spatial structure and spectral retention on CIA, Wuhan and custommade SW datasets. The SW dataset and source code are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/remoteSTF/STF</uri>.

title: Accelerated Reinforcement Learning With Verifiable Excitation for Cubic Convergence
authors: Jiacheng Wu, Changyun Wen, Yang Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: This article proposes and analyzes an accelerated reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm for discrete-time linear systems with unknown dynamics. The method achieves cubic convergence, improving upon the quadratic rates of existing policy iteration (PI)-based RL algorithms, and it does not rely on persistency of excitation (PE). The value function matrix is computed through a midpoint-centered Lyapunov equation, which provides a third-order Newton-type update and ensures fast convergence. To enable data-driven implementation without PE, we introduce a verifiable excitation condition based on filter outputs. This condition is easily testable online and guarantees that the collected data are sufficiently informative for solving the required least-squares equations. Within this framework, we further propose a data-driven procedure to compute an initial admissible control gain, where a bisection rule is used to determine the maximal feasible discount factor. Theoretical analysis establishes cubic convergence of the accelerated algorithm and proves the effectiveness of the designed excitation condition. Finally, we verify the proposed method through Monte Carlo experiments and comparative simulations with existing methods.

title: Reducing Complexity on Intra Mode Decision in Video Coding: A Review
authors: Ei Ei Tun, Supavadee Aramvith
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This article surveys low-complexity techniques for intra mode selection in modern video coding standards. The objective is to consolidate knowledge of intra prediction methods and clarify their computational impact in High Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC) and its successor, Versatile Video Coding (VVC). The survey reviews the evolution of intra coding frameworks, outlines the growing complexity of prediction modes and partitioning structures, and analyzes representative solutions. These include statistical, optimization-based, learning-driven, and hardware-oriented methods, each designed to reduce encoding time while maintaining rate–distortion performance. Trade-offs between complexity reduction and coding efficiency are highlighted, along with the scalability challenges of applying fast algorithms at the coding tree unit level. The article concludes by identifying open challenges and proposing future research directions, providing a consolidated reference for advancing efficient intra coding in HEVC and VVC.

title: Synergizing Smoke and Hotspot: A Visible-Infrared Co-Learning Framework with Dataset for Large-Scale Wildfire Detection
authors: Ziyang Kong, Qizhi Xu, Youshi Ye, Jianhua Li, Wei Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Visible and infrared spectra are critical data sources for wildfire detection, yet their complementary capabilities have not been fully exploited. While visible sensors capture early smoke plumes with high resolution, they suffer from cloud interference. Conversely, thermal infrared sensors reliably identifies hotspots but can be easily effected by other heat sources. To bridge these limitations, we synergized smoke and hotspot to obtain a comprehensive set of fire features. Specifically: (i) A feature grouping method was proposed to inject thermal information into the R-channel, thereby enhancing the saliency of fire-related features. (ii) A visible-infrared co-learning strategy was designed to enhance the smoke-hotspot representations for accurate fire detection. (iii) The VIF-FireDataset - the first publicly available dataset for wide-area, multi-modal wildfire detection was proposed. It contains 630 co-registered visible-infrared-fused image pairs, having an ultra-high resolution of 10240 × 10240 pixels. Our work not only introduces a new approach for wildfire detection, but also contributes a publicly available dataset to the research community, enabling further progress in satellite-based wildfire monitoring. Source codes and proposed dataset will be available at https://github.com/KTqizhi/KTqizhi.github.io.

title: Dielectric barrier discharge treatment for improving the efficiency of tribo-electrostatic separation of polymer mixtures containing brominated flame retardants
authors: Siham Labiod, Thami Zeghloul, Mohamed Sofiane Bendilmi, Zehira Ziari, Lucian Dascalescu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: The aim of this paper is to evaluate the effect of dielectric barrier discharge (DBD) plasma treatment on the tribo-electrostatic separation of brominated and non-brominated polymers contained in granular plastic wastes. The experiments were carried out on mixtures consisting of equal quantities of mm-size brominated and non-brominated polyethylene (PE) particles. Two triboelectric charging devices were used: a rotating multi-cylinder tribocharger, consisting of an assembly of six cylinders made of polyvinyl chloride (PVC), and a vibrating tribocharger (a container made of acrylonitrile butadiene styrene (ABS) and fixed on a vibrating table). The selective sorting of the granular mixtures was carried out using a roll-type electrostatic separator. Exposing the plastic mixture to a DBD plasma was used to increase the triboelectric charge of the particles and improve the efficiency of the tribo-electrostatic separation. The best results were obtained with the vibrating tribocharger: the recovery of almost 90% of the non-brominated PE particles in a product containing less than 1% of bromine.

title: Graph Condensation via Homophily Node Refining and Fine-Grained Distribution Matching
authors: Ruiwen Yuan, Yongqiang Tang, Wensheng Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: The remarkable success of GNNs has provoked the challenge of high computational and memory overhead when training with large-scale graphs. As a promising solution, graph condensation is committed to constructing synthetic graphs with significantly smaller size, which are expected to preserve the essential characteristics of the original ones. During this process, a core problem is how to accurately portray and align the data distribution structures between the original graph space and the synthetic graph space. A mainstream idea in existing research is matching the class distributions between the two spaces. Unfortunately, they generally overlook two key issues: 1) heterophilic nodes in original graphs may render the chaotic class distribution patterns; 2) coarse-grained matching of the overall class centroid between original and synthetic spaces is insufficient for data with complex subcategory distributions. In this paper, we propose a novel Graph Condensation method via homophily node Refinement and fine-grained class Distribution matching (GCRD). Given the original large-scale graph, we first distinguish the nodes into advantageous homophilic nodes and detrimental heterophilic nodes, followed by adaptively assigning node weights to refine the generated class distribution patterns of the original graphs. Furthermore, with the refined class distribution patterns, we propose a fine-grained distribution matching objective to more delicately align the local distribution structure of subclasses within each class. The rigorous theoretical analysis confirms the effectiveness of our proposal in precisely learning the class information. Extensive experiments demonstrate our state-of-the-art classification and cross-architecture generalization performance against various baselines.

title: High-Performance 15-Level Multilevel Inverter for Renewable and Smart Grid Applications
authors: P. Vinod Kumar, Challa Silpi Priyanka, C. Valli, Sudhakar C, Arunraja A, M. Lokanadham
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Multilevel inverters have emerged as a promising solution for improving power quality, reducing switching stress, and enhancing conversion efficiency in renewable energy and smart grid applications. Conventional two-level topologies struggle with high Total Harmonic Distortion (THD), electromagnetic interference, and bulky filters, limiting their suitability for high-power systems. To address these challenges, advanced multilevel architectures have been designed to deliver multiple voltage steps, thereby approximating a sinusoidal waveform with greater precision. This paper investigates a high-performance multilevel inverter with an optimized 15-level configuration that achieves superior harmonic reduction, enhanced voltage boosting, and reduced switching device count compared to conventional alternatives. A detailed switching sequence and operational modes are provided to demonstrate the generation of fifteen distinct output voltage levels. Simulation and analytical results validate the performance in terms of THD minimization, voltage stress reduction, and waveform quality. The proposed configuration is suitable for integration with photovoltaic systems, wind generation, and smart grid applications where reliability, efficiency, and grid compliance are essential. Furthermore, the design demonstrates modularity, scalability, and compatibility with wide bandgap semiconductors, reinforcing its role as a practical solution for modern energy systems.

title: A Hippopotamus Algorithm enhanced Extreme Learning Machine for Solar Power Forecasting
authors: Reddi Ganesh, Tapas Kumar Saha, M L S Sai Kumar
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: To ensure the uninterrupted operation of the grid, it is imperative to establish a PV power prediction. Climate conditions, such as temperature and irradiance, significantly influence PV power. Conventional intelligent techniques fail to predict PV power accurately due to the nonlinear time series data. Thus, machine learning algorithms are used for prediction. However, these techniques require large computational power and vast data sets to make accurate predictions. But extreme learning machines are simple and do not require large amounts of such data. Therefore, this paper employs an Extreme Learning Machine (ELM) whose initial weights are optimized using the hippopotamus algorithm to forecast the real-time PV power. Performance indicators, including MAE, MSE, MAPE, RMSE, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> are computed and compared with regular ELM and AOA ELM.

title: Healthcare Insurance Fraud Detection Using a Blockchain–Deep Neural Network–AdaBoost Hybrid Model
authors: Sujit Kumar Sadhukhan, R. Jaishankar, Balaji Adusupalli, V.M. Revathi, S. Kaliappan, Muruganantham Ponnusamy
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Healthcare insurance fraud is a substantial danger to the financial viability of healthcare systems, resulting in yearly losses up to billions. Conventional fraud detection systems face challenges related to data manipulation, delayed recognition, and insufficient openness. The proposed study presents a hybrid AI-driven architecture that integrates Blockchain technology, Deep Neural Networks (DNNs), and AdaBoost ensemble learning to improve detection accuracy, data security, and real-time responsiveness. Blockchain guarantees data immutability and traceability through smart contracts and cryptographic hash functions, whereas the DNN identifies complex fraud patterns in insurance claims. AdaBoost enhances categorisation by boosting weak learners trained on DNN outputs. The suggested system was evaluated using curated healthcare fraud datasets, attaining 98.4% accuracy, 97.8% precision, 98.0% recall, and 99.3% AUC-ROC, outperforming conventional models such as Logistic Regression, SVM, and Random Forest. This hybrid method offers a scalable and secure system for effectively identifying fraudulent claims while maintaining trust among all stakeholders. The findings indicate substantial potential for implementation in extensive insurance systems, providing considerable improvements in fraud prevention, operational efficiency, and audit security. Future enhancements may involve federated architectures and real-time mobile integration for cross-institutional fraud detection systems.

title: A Homothetic Tube-Based Distributed Economic Model Predictive Control Approach to Building Temperature Regulation
authors: Zihang Dong, Cheng Hu, Dawei Qiu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: This paper proposes a homothetic tube-based distributed economic model predictive control (HT-DEMPC) framework for robust and energy-efficient temperature regulation in buildings. A multi-zone thermal dynamics model is first formulated to capture inter-zone heat exchange and ambient disturbances. To mitigate model uncertainties, a homothetic tube-based approach is developed that decouples the nominal and error dynamics, while a feedback policy ensures robust constraint satisfaction. By optimising the tube scaling factor, the proposed method adaptively tightens the reachable set, thereby reducing the conservatism in conventional rigid tube-based MPC. To enable scalable coordination among thermal zones, an alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM) is integrated into the EMPC optimisation, achieving distributed computation and preserving information privacy. Simulation studies on both single-and multi-zone building cases demonstrate that the proposed HT-DEMPC enhances temperature regulation accuracy, improves robustness against disturbances, and reduces overall energy consumption compared with rigid tube-based DEMPC strategies.

title: Performance Analysis of Partial-NOMA in Integrated Satellite–Terrestrial Networks With Co-Channel Interference
authors: Chenrui Shi, Xiaozheng Gao, Minwei Shi, Zeyu Zhang, Jiacheng Wang, Dusit Niyato, Zhanxin Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Integrated satellite-terrestrial networks (ISTNs) have played a crucial role in next-generation wireless systems. In this paper, we introduce partial non-orthogonal multiple access (p-NOMA) into ISTNs for downlink transmission, focusing on the system performance of satellite user equipments (UEs) and terrestrial UEs with co-channel interference and imperfect successive interference cancellation. We employ the Poisson point process to model the spatial distribution of the ground base stations, with Shadowed-Rician fading for satellite-terrestrial links and Rayleigh fading for terrestrial links. The closed-form expressions for the outage probability and average achievable rate of both satellite UEs and terrestrial UEs are derived. Additionally, we analyze the performance of the p-NOMA scheme and present the comparisons with non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) and orthogonal multiple access schemes. We validate the analytical results through simulations, confirming that p-NOMA outperforms NOMA in terms of both outage probability and average achievable rate. Moreover, we investigate the impact of p-NOMA parameters on the average achievable rate in the terrestrial network, providing insights for optimizing system performance.

title: Fuzzy Logic-SVM Integration for Energy-Efficient Intrusion Detection in Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Anshad A. S, Preeti Nitin Bhatt, Arun Raj S. R., M. Karthikkumar, G. Deepa, K. Sathish
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are susceptible to many security risks, such as sinkhole, Sybil, and denial-of-service attacks, which compromise data integrity and network durability. Conventional intrusion detection systems frequently encounter compromises between precision and energy efficiency, hence constraining their efficiency in resource-limited settings. The proposed study introduces an innovative hybrid intrusion detection framework that integrates fuzzy logic's uncertainty management with Support Vector Machine (SVM) classification to achieve energy-efficient and high-accuracy detection. The fuzzy logic module pre-filters network input to selectively activate the SVM classifier, thereby reducing computational effort and energy expenditure. Experimental assessment of Kaggle-sourced WSN datasets reveals exceptional performance, achieving 96.8% accuracy, 95.6% precision, 97.2% recall, and a false positive rate of 2.8%, surpassing both SVM-only and fuzzy-only benchmarks. The suggested system provides an excellent equilibrium between detection effectiveness and energy efficiency, making it a viable alternative for safeguarding extensive wireless sensor network implementations.

title: Enhancing Network Lifetime in WSNs using PSO-Optimized TEEN Clustering Algorithm
authors: Lakshita Rai, Kanika Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Optimizing energy consumption is crucial in Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) to prolong the overall network lifespan and maintain reliable data communication. In this study, a hybrid clustering approach combining Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) with the TEEN protocol is presented. PSO optimally selects cluster heads by minimizing communication distance and balancing node energy, while TEEN reduces data transmissions using hard and soft thresholds. The proposed PSO-TEEN method significantly conserves energy and outperforms traditional protocols in residual energy, node survival, and network lifespan, making it ideal for time-critical, energy-constrained WSNs.

title: Analysis of Sub-Synchronous Oscillation Characteristics in a Wind-Solar-Thermal Integrated System Under Multiple Operating Modes
authors: Yanhui Xu, Xiaobing Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of wind and photovoltaic (PV) power in modern power systems, the integrated transmission mode of wind-solar-thermal generation has gradually become an important approach for large-scale renewable energy delivery. However, the high share of windsolar integration and the application of series compensation devices in transmission lines may trigger sub-synchronous oscillations (SSO). This paper systematically analyzes the influence mechanisms of wind-solar penetration level, additional reactance between thermal power units and the grid, and the degree of series compensation in transmission lines on the SSO characteristics of wind-solar-thermal integrated systems. A small-signal mathematical model is developed to derive the relationships between these key parameters and the system's electrical stiffness and damping characteristics, thereby revealing the variation patterns of SSO under different operating conditions. On the basis of theoretical analysis, a PSCAD/EMTDC simulation model is established, and dynamic simulations are conducted under typical scenarios. The results demonstrate that increasing the wind-solar penetration level and series compensation ratio aggravates the negative damping effect and amplifies oscillation magnitude, while increasing additional reactance effectively suppresses SSO. Finally, an analysis of variance (ANOVA) method is employed to quantitatively evaluate the contribution of different factors to SSO.

title: Energy-Efficient 200 GHz Power Amplifier Design for 5G-IoT Smart-Grid Edge Communication
authors: Ziteng Liu, Zhou Na, Zhengliang Fu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the rapid development of fifth-generation mobile communication (5G) and the Internet of Things (IoT), the communication demands of smart grids are evolving toward higher frequencies, broader bandwidths, and greater energy efficiency. Future smart grid nodes require not only stable power control but also high-speed, low-latency data interaction in distributed environments. Consequently, radio frequency frontend circuits operating in the millimeter-wave and sub-terahertz (sub THz) frequency bands have emerged as a critical technology. However, existing 200 GHz power amplifiers (PAs) in CMOS and SiGe technologies typically achieve only 10-15 dB gain with PAE below 5%, limiting their use in energy-constrained smartgrid edge nodes. Therefore, a design that combines broadband gain and high energy efficiency under compact integration is required. This paper designs and simulates a broadband power amplifier operating at 200 GHz to support edge communication in 5G and IoT driven smart grids. The amplifier achieves a gain exceeding 20 dB, a 3 dB bandwidth of 40 GHz, and input output matching better than −10 dB. By adopting a dual parallel structure and Wilkinson power combiner, the amplifier delivers approximately 6 dB of power gain, achieving 10 mW output power and over 10% power added efficiency (PAE) under a 2 V supply voltage. Simulation results demonstrate stable performance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K>1, \mu>1$</tex>) across the 1 GHz-400 GHz frequency range, validating its potential application in future 5G smart grids and IoT communication nodes.

title: A Comparative Study on the Key Properties of Commercial Silicone Rubber Insulation Materials for High Voltage Cable Accessories
authors: Runge Lu, Bangwen Yan, Siyuan Li, Tao Xu, Baojun Hui, Zhuozhan Han, Jiasheng Huang, Hanru Li
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: This study presents a comparative evaluation of three commercial imported silicone rubber insulation materials (A1, A2, and A3) for high-voltage cable accessories. Key electrical properties, including dielectric breakdown strength and tracking and erosion resistance, along with mechanical properties like tensile strength and elongation at break, were systematically tested. The results revealed a significant performance trade-off: materials A1 and A2 showed superior insulation reliability but poor surface degradation resistance, whereas A3 exhibited excellent tracking and erosion resistance at the cost of lower insulation reliability. Mechanically, the materials displayed distinct profiles, with A1 being highstrength and A2 being high-toughness. These findings demonstrate that no single material is universally optimal, highlighting the necessity of a multi-dimensional evaluation to align material selection with specific application requirements, thereby ensuring the long-term reliability of cable accessories.

title: Oscillation Suppression Approach for GFM Direct-Drive Wind Power Converter Adapted to Wide Grid-Strength
authors: Shuo Zhang, Kai Wu, Qiqi Miu, Lixia Sun, Tao Zhang, Miao Tian
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Due to factors such as line switching, faults, load fluctuations, and series-compensated transmission lines, grid-forming controlled wind power converters often operate in grid-connected environments with wide variations in grid strength, making them prone to interactions with the grid that may trigger broadband oscillations. To mitigate the risk of broadband oscillations in grid-forming wind power converters under wide gridstrength variations, this paper employs a harmonic linearization method to establish a sequence impedance model of the wind power converter, incorporating factors such as the power control loop, voltage control loop, and current sampling delay. The mechanism underlying broadband oscillations is analyzed based on this model. Furthermore, an impedance reshaper based on an improved flower pollination algorithm is designed to reshape the positive-sequence impedance of the converter, ensuring real-time matching between the output impedance of the grid-forming converter and the grid strength. This approach suppresses adverse convertergrid interactions, thereby reducing the risk of broadband oscillations. Simulation results validate the accuracy of the impedance model and the effectiveness of the proposed impedance reshaping method.

title: Data Approximation Recovery and Validation Based Detection for False Data Injection Attacks in Power Systems
authors: Haofeng Zheng, Yufei Song, Kaiyang Liu, Xuan Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The traditional anomaly detection methods fail to detect false data injection attacks (FDIA) with a high probability. To address this issue, this paper proposes a method for detecting FDIA in power systems by utilizing data approximation recovery and validation techniques. Based on FDIA's local characteristics, the data approximation recovery algorithm employs Euclidean distance and state estimation residuals to recover the suspected attack data from historical samples, yielding the approximated data that resembles the normal one used by attackers. Subsequently, the data validation algorithm is employed to verify the variation between the suspected attack data and the approximated data based on two rules: Barrier Constraints and Maximum Deviation. If both rules are satisfied, the suspected attack data is confirmed to be attack data. The numerical analyses conducted on the IEEE 118-bus system demonstrate that the proposed method can accurately detect FDIA with stealth and local characteristics.

title: Optimal Planning and Operation of Soft Open Points in Distribution Networks: A Review
authors: Yan He, Nuo Xu, Jiabin Gao, Xiaokang Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The integration of high-penetration renewable energy sources into distribution networks has introduced pressing challenges such as voltage violations, power flow fluctuations, and reliability concerns. Soft Open Points (SOPs), which are power electronic devices that replace traditional tie switches, have emerged as a pivotal technology to enhance network flexibility and controllability through their four-quadrant active and reactive power control capabilities. This paper presents a systematic review of the SOP research landscape, focusing on three core domains: optimal configuration, operational optimization, and coordinated planning. It meticulously analyzes the evolution from single-objective to multi-objective and uncertainty-aware planning models. Furthermore, it dissects advanced control strategies, including those based on optimization and artificial intelligence, and elaborates on coordination mechanisms between SOPs and other grid assets like reactive power compensators, distributed energy resources, and energy storage systems. Finally, the paper synthesizes current research challenges pertaining to model fidelity, standardization, and system stability, while charting a course for future research directions aimed at realizing the full potential of SOPs in future power systems.

title: Daptive Distributed Photovoltaic Power Distribution Line Protection Setting Strategy
authors: Wenli Yang, Jixiu Bao, Yan Li, Jianlong Liang, Ningxia Wang, Qiyun Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Under the “dual carbon” goal, a high proportion of distributed photovoltaics are connected to the distribution network, making its operation more complex. The intermittent and random output of distributed photovoltaics can cause traditional line protection to reject and override actions. This article proposes an adaptive setting strategy based on existing protection devices and communication systems. On the basis of traditional multi-level protection, combined with photovoltaic output data, considering the effects of boosting and draining, the current protection setting is dynamically adjusted. Based on PSCAD/EMTDC, an IEEE-33 node model was built for simulation verification, and the results showed that this strategy can effectively avoid refusal and misoperation, and improve protection reliability.

title: Outage-Prone Neural Networks Analysis for Short-Term Electrical Demand Forecasting
authors: Muhammad Javaid Aslam, Wing Yee Chung, Yik Chuen Lau, Boris Wong, C. Patrick Yue
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Short-term load forecasting (STLF) is crucial for efficient power distribution, generation, and utilization, particularly in developing countries like Pakistan, where demandsupply gaps are substantial. Summer electricity load patterns exhibit high nonlinearity due to frequent, intense, and unplanned power outages, resulting in discontinuities in consumption and complicating forecasting and planning. This study explored the incorporation of power outage data as an explanatory variable in forecasting models. Initially, a statistical regression model was tested for its simplicity and robustness, but it showed limited accuracy. Various artificial neural network (ANN) models were trained and tested to capture complex nonlinear relationships more effectively. A comparative analysis between the regression and ANN models used several error metrics. The results show that outage-informed Feedforward Backpropagation (FFBP) Neural Networks achieved the lowest Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 3 \%}$</tex> and Mean Square Error (MSE) of 7 %, reducing errors by up to 98 % compared to traditional forecasting and other ANN architectures. This study highlights the significant benefits of outage-aware neural networks in improving short-term electrical load forecasting in outage-prone environments.

title: A Closed-Loop Scheduling-Control Coordination Strategy for Flexible HVDC-Assisted AC Grids
authors: Shanke Mou, Nan Yang, Hao Chen, Ziqiu Liu, Shujing Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The increasing penetration of renewable energy introduces pronounced variability and cross-regional power imbalances in modern AC transmission systems. Flexible high-voltage direct current (HVDC) links offer rapid and controllable power modulation, but the traditional decoupled scheduling and control processes hinder their coordinated utilization. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a closed-loop scheduling-control coordination framework for flexible HVDC-assisted AC grids. The framework integrates an upper-level scheduling model and a lower-level model predictive control (MPC) layer through bidirectional information feedback. At the scheduling level, an economic dispatch model determines generator outputs, reserves, and HVDC power references under network and operational constraints. At the control level, converter dynamics and AC voltage states are regulated in real time, and deviation signals are fed back to adapt scheduling decisions. The resulting formulation achieves consistent multi-timescale decision-making while preserving computational tractability through convex decomposition. Case studies on an extended IEEE 118-bus hybrid AC-DC system demonstrate that the proposed closed-loop strategy effectively mitigates tie-line oscillations, enhances voltage stability, and increases renewable utilization, achieving near-centralized performance with substantially reduced computation time.

title: Improving Transient Stability of Grid-Following Converter-Integrated Power Systems Through DOA Optimization Considering Current Control Loops
authors: Lu Gao, Rui Xu, Yuqin Gao, Haijun Tang, Tong Li, Xin Qi
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy, analyzing the impact of grid-following (GFL) converters has become critical for power system transient stability. However, traditional transient stability analyses mainly consider phaselocked loop (PLL) dynamics, leading to conservative or inaccurate stability assessments. This paper develops a detailed dynamic model of GFL converters that includes both inner AC current control loops and PLL dynamics, and introduces the dynamics of interactions in multi-machine systems. A domain of attraction (DOA) estimation method based on sum of squares programming (SOSP) is adopted to evaluate system stability with this model. To reduce conservativeness and improve transient stability of GFL integrated power systems, a parameter optimization algorithm is introduced into the SOSP framework, enabling adaptive tuning of the converters' proportional-integral (PI) controllers. The proposed method is validated in a case of renewable power sending, in which a GFL converter-based renewable power plant operates in a parallel manner with a synchronous generator. The tested scenario models the bundled operation of renewable power plants and thermal power plants. Simulation results demonstrate that the optimized converter parameters significantly enlarge the estimated DOA while maintaining conservativeness, thus enhancing transient stability analysis of power systems.

title: BlkMaC: A Blockchain-based Multi-Agents System with LLM Communication Framework
authors: Ruolin Yang, Te Hou, Erfu Zhang, Minfeng Qi
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence

authors: Ahmed Munieb Sheikh, Rehan Khan

title: Compact Memristor Emulator with SDDP for Integrated Neuromorphic Temporal Learning
authors: Sreeram Bediga, Tarun Kumar Sharma, Arun Kalakola, Vedant Upadhyay, Rajeev Kumar Ranjan, Sung-Mo Kang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Artificial Intelligence
abstract: A dynamic-threshold MOSFET (DTMOS) based circuit with a back-gate biasing is introduced as a new memristor emulator. The memristor emulator uses only two MOSFETs and a capacitor, which allows for large-scale integration and high-frequency operation as high as 200 MHz. This memristor emulator can be used in both grounded and floating configurations, which simplifies the circuit structure for monolithic integration. The emulator’s performance, validated through comprehensive parametric evaluations, shows consistent pinched hysteresis loops (PHLs) at various frequencies, process corners and enables neuromorphic system designs capable of training and detection, supporting spike density-dependent plasticity (SDDP). To achieve SDDP, the memristor emulator is integrated with a fundamental switching circuitry. Additionally, pre and post-layout validations are conducted using computer aided design tool (i.e., Cadence Virtuoso) with 180-nm process design kits (PDKs) from the SCL to further demonstrate the circuit’s efficacy in neuromorphic designs. The proposed memristor emulator was implemented and tested on hardware using IC HCF4007UBE, demonstrating its memristive characteristics. The proposed architecture shows promise for low-power and compact design features, occupying an area of 0.569 mm2 and consuming only 1.041 mW of power for a single-column 64-bit crossbar array at an SCL 180 nm technology node.

title: Game-Theoretic Adaptive Routing and Integrated Security Framework for Multi-Hop LoRaWAN Networks
authors: Abdullah Alajmi, Abdulrahman Ghandoura, Abdelwahed Motwakel, Ghada Abdelhady
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Multi-hop Long Range Wide Area Network (LoRaWAN) deployments supporting mission-critical applications face serious limitations when static routing protocols are unable to adapt to changing network conditions or routing-layer security threats. Our analysis indicates that static routing protocols can experience up to 43% degradation in packet delivery ratio under selective forwarding attacks compared to normal operating conditions. Current approaches offer limited capability to autonomously respond to topology changes, device failures, or varying traffic loads. This paper introduces a game-theoretic adaptive routing framework combined with reinforcement learning to dynamically optimize path selection in multi-hop LoRaWAN networks. The proposed framework operates entirely on the network server, while end devices perform only lightweight state reporting, ensuring compatibility with Class A LoRaWAN devices. Trust information derived from security monitoring is incorporated to balance performance objectives with security-aware routing decisions. The approach is evaluated through large-scale simulations with network sizes ranging from 100 to 2000 nodes. The results show consistent improvements in packet delivery ratio and latency compared to static routing across the evaluated scenarios, while maintaining stable performance under attack conditions. Attack detection achieves high precision (92.0%) with a low false positive rate (0.65%), with an average detection time of 26.6±6.8 seconds. Integration overhead remains limited, resulting in minimal additional energy consumption.

title: MambaCapsule: Toward Transparent Cardiac Disease Diagnosis With Electrocardiography Using Mamba Capsule Network
authors: Yinlong Xu, Zitai Kong, Yixuan Wu, Yue Wang, Xiaoqiang Liu, Yingzhou Lu, Jian Wu, Hongxia Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Cardiac arrhythmia, a condition characterized by irregular heartbeats, often serves as an early indication of various heart ailments. With the advent of deep learning, numerous innovative models have been introduced for diagnosing arrhythmias using electrocardiogram (ECG) signals. However, recent studies solely focus on the performance of models, neglecting the interpretation of their results. This leads to a considerable lack of transparency, posing a significant risk in the actual diagnostic process. To solve this problem, this article introduces MambaCapsule, a deep neural network for ECG arrhythmias classification, which increases the explainability of the model while enhancing the accuracy. Our model utilizes Mamba for feature extraction and Capsule networks for prediction, providing not only a confidence score but also signal features. Akin to the processing mechanism of human brain, the model learns signal features and their relationship between them by reconstructing ECG signals in the predicted selection. The model evaluation was conducted on Massachusetts Institute of Technology - Beth Israel Hospital (MIT-BIH) and Physikalisch-Technische Bundesanstalt Diagnostic ECG Database (PTB) datasets, following the AAMI standard. MambaCapsule has achieved a total accuracy of 99.54% and 99.59% on the test sets, respectively. These results demonstrate the promising performance under the standard test protocol.

title: Sensory Feedback for Upper-limb Amputees by Noninvasive Electrical Stimulation and Its Performance Assessment
authors: Qiyun Tan, Yuxiang Zhang, Haotian Meng, Guanglin Li, Peng Fang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Haptics
abstract: Most commercial prostheses lack a function of natural and intuitive sensory feedback, which is one of the reasons for their high rate of abandonment. Transcutaneous Electrical Nerve Stimulation (TENS) has been proved as an effective approach to evoke sensations for limb amputees. This paper aims to explore the impact of TENS parameters on sensation evoking and evaluate its performance based on behavioral response and EEG data, involving three transradial amputees and seven able-bodied subjects. Experimental results show that the sensation thresholds were predominantly influenced by stimulus amplitude and width, and the sensation intensity increased with the increase of either amplitude or width. Variation of stimulus frequency caused transitions between sensation types, where stimulating at 10 and 100 Hz could achieve stable vibration and pressure sensations for all subjects, respectively. A stimulation encoding strategy was thereupon proposed, where a pressure sensation was to simulate grasp force and the sensation intensity to encode force amplitude. The amputees could achieve a high accuracy rate above 94.4 and 77.8% for sensation type discrimination and intensity grading, respectively, with slightly longer response time than the able-bodied. The obvious cortical activation and clear ERP components demonstrated the reliability of TENS-based sensory feedback, where the N1 component could distinguish different sensation types and intensities (p≤0.05), and the amputees had slower discriminatory responses and weaker activation in sensorimotor cortices than the able-bodied (p≤0.05). This study promisingly confirmed TENS for restoring sensory feedback in limb-amputees, providing a support for closed-loop interactions in amputee-prosthesis systems and even bionic robots.

title: Climate-driven glacier retreat and biocrust colonization dynamics in the Quelccaya Ice Cap, Peru
authors: Eliana Lima da Fonseca, Tiago Pereira Bettervide, Jefferson Cardia Simões
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The spatiotemporal dynamics of glacier cover and biocrust surrounding the Quelccaya Ice Cap in the Peruvian Andes from 2020 to 2024 were analyzed. Using Sentinel-2 satellite imagery, the Adjusted Normalized Difference Snow Index (ANDSI) and Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) were employed with the K-means machine learning algorithm to generate land cover time-series maps. TerraClimate dataset provided monthly estimates of climate and water balance variables, which were analyzed to identify the drivers of seasonal land cover transitions. The results indicate that the climate variables influenced the seasonal patterns of land cover. A reduction of 4.74 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{km}^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> in the ice cover area and 7.48 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{km}^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> in the snow cover area was observed between June 2020 and June 2024, primarily influenced by high incoming shortwave radiation, which increased air temperatures, evaporation, and the sublimation process. Biocrust occurs predominantly in the north and west of the ice cap, particularly in flat relief areas, while wetlands occur at lower altitudes. Both vegetation types are highly sensitive to environmental shifts, showing seasonal growth associated with higher temperatures and increased water availability. The top-of-atmosphere imagery can be used in a clustering process to generate land cover maps. The combination of ANDSI and NDVI proved effective in mapping ice, snow, biocrust, and wetlands using Sentinel-2 top-of-atmosphere reflectance images, thereby avoiding the relief shadow effects typically observed in the clustering process.

title: Hierarchical Attention-Driven Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Resource Allocation in Cell-Free Massive MIMO with Integrated Sensing and Communication
authors: Ming Jiang, Erwu Liu, Wei Ni, Rui Wang, Shuyan Hu, Lingzhi Xia, Yuanzhe Geng, Dusit Niyato, Abbas Jamalipour
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Cell-Free massive MIMO (CF mMIMO), as a cornerstone for future wireless communication networks, can significantly enhance spectral efficiency, reduce energy consumption, and achieve seamless coverage. This paper investigates dynamic resource allocation in CF mMIMO systems provisioning integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) and involving multiple users and heterogeneous services. To address the rapidly varying channels, high complexity, task-dependent relevance and insufficient information sharing among access points (APs) arising from ISAC requirements, we propose a novel Hierarchical Attention-Driven Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (HADMARL) framework. A higher-layer “Commander Controller” outputs the AP-user pairing matrix, which is passed to a lowerlayer “Worker Controller” that leverages the central attention mechanism to perform fine-grained resource allocation. Simulations demonstrate that HAD-MARL achieves about a 10% improvement in system utility score compared to benchmarks, with the performance gains becoming more pronounced as the number of APs and users increases. We empirically demonstrate and visualize the effect of the central attention mechanism on the formation of efficient collaboration among APs. The source code is available at: https://github.com/Andyyy2000/HAD.git.

title: Observer-based Hierarchical Formation Control for Multi-agent System Subject to External Disturbance
authors: Lan Li, Cao Chen, Zilin Gao, Min You
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper designs an observer-based hierarchical formation controller for Euler-Lagrange multi-agent system (MAS) under external disturbances. The proposed approach can achieve MAS formation under symmetric and non-symmetric communication topologies without dependency on global information. It features an upper-layer finite-time distributed observer that reconstructs the leader’s states, and a lower-layer chattering-free sliding-mode controller that achieves robust tracking without tracking accuracy degradation. In particular, a fully distributed observer is developed to observe the leader’s state. Once the leader’s state is reconstructed, a sliding-mode controller (SMC) is designed to track the trajectory of the leader. To suppress chattering and ensure robust trajectory tracking, a SMC with a second-order sliding-mode variable is designed. Theoretical analysis proves stability of the developed control schemes for the MAS. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the developed scheme.

title: Equivalent and Compact Representations of Neural Network Controllers With Decision Trees
authors: Kevin Chang, Nathan Dahlin, Rahul Jain, Pierluigi Nuzzo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: Over the past decade, neural network (NN)-based controllers have demonstrated remarkable efficacy in a variety of decision-making tasks. However, their black-box nature and the risk of unexpected behaviors pose a challenge to their deployment in real-world systems requiring strong guarantees of correctness and safety. We address these limitations by investigating the transformation of NN-based controllers into equivalent soft decision tree (SDT)-based controllers and its impact on verifiability. In contrast to existing work, we focus on discrete-output NN controllers including rectified linear unit (ReLU) activation functions as well as argmax operations. We then devise an exact yet efficient transformation algorithm which automatically prunes redundant branches. We evaluate our approach and demonstrate the practical efficacy of the transformation algorithm using three benchmarks from the OpenAI Gym environment, including an autonomous driving NN controller. Our results indicate that the SDT transformation can benefit formal verification, showing runtime improvements of up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$20\times$</tex-math></inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2\times$</tex-math></inline-formula>, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$20\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> for the MountainCar-v0, CartPole-v1, and CarRacing-v2 environments, respectively.

title: Steering-Invariant Beamwidth Control Using Hybrid Vector-Sensor Nested Arrays in Circular-Linear Fusion
authors: Peilin Cao, Xiangyang Zeng, Mingmin Zeng, Menghui Lei, Qing Huang, Zihan Na
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This paper proposes a broadband beamforming method for underwater target radiated-noise measurement using a hybrid vector–pressure array that combines a vector nested line subarray with a pressure uniform circular array. A hybrid measurement model is established, and a unified steering vector is derived by jointly accounting for subarray geometries and sensor responses. Simulations are conducted using a plane-wave far-field baseline and two three-path waveguide environments with varying multipath strengths. Performance is quantified by continuous-spectrum reconstruction errors over 250–2000 Hz, excluding tonal components. Results demonstrate that the vector-assisted scheme substantially mitigates ambiguity-induced leakage and yields markedly lower reconstruction errors in waveguide conditions, maintaining overall MAE/RMSE at the 5–7 dB level, lower than that of conventional and CBW about 12-15dB. This demonstrates improved robustness to multipath propagation and enhanced port–starboard ambiguity suppression for wideband radiated-noise reconstruction.

title: Novel Single-Stage Isolated AC/DC Rectifier with Power Factor Correction and Tri-Mode Power Decoupling Modulation
authors: Huijie Cheng, Jiawei Zhao, Jingbo Dong
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel single-stage isolated PFC topology. By sharing power devices, the proposed structure integrates the front-stage diode rectifier circuit and the flyback circuit, allowing the input voltage of the flyback circuit to be controlled as an independent variable, thereby enabling simultaneous power factor correction (PFC) and power decoupling. Furthermore, the control strategy and steady-state operating principles of the proposed circuit are analyzed, the passive components within the topology are designed, and experimental results validate the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed circuit.

title: A Review of Lambert $W$ Function Utilization in Nanoelectronics Modelling
authors: Adelmo Ortiz Conde, Francisco. J. García-Sánchez
publish: 2025 IEEE 34th International Conference on Microelectronics (MIEL)
abstract: We present an overview of recent uses in nano-electronic device modeling of the special mathematical function known as the Lambert <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$W$</tex> function. First, we present the Lambert W function's definition and properties, and discuss some useful related functions. Next, we also examine some applications of this special function to modeling of PN junctions and to bipolar junction transistors. Particular attention is given to the description of applications to surface potential- and inversion charge-based models for MOSFETs, including nanometric devices. Finally, some illustrative examples of applications to the modelling of other related types of nanoelectronic devices, are briefly presented.

title: Advancements in Cardiovascular Disease Prediction: Integrating Deep Learning and Blockchain for Secure and Accurate Healthcare Solutions
authors: Boya Hussain, Pulamolu Kiran Kumar
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The development of new diagnostic and predictive methods remains crucial because CVDs continue to cause most global mortality. Deep learning technology in AI allows modern artificial intelligence to provide precise, high-accuracy diagnosis and prediction of CVDs. Healthcare organizations apply ensemble approaches and deep neural networks to combine the analysis of ECG signals with clinical patient information and medical images for better disease diagnosis and treatment individualization. Wearable systems equipped with AI technology provide continuous heart tracking for healthcare providers to supply quick, appropriate interventions that lead to better patient outcomes. Block chain technology provides healthcare institutions with a security system that helps resolve their data management issues. The block chain infrastructure provides protected cardiovascular patient health data storage along with unalterable and secure data storage capabilities. Through its secure data exchange system, medical practitioners can provide safe data transfers to enhance teamwork and monitor patient care. The research continues to thrive because researchers dealt with multiple barriers related to requiring diverse data sources and managing data security issues in transactions. The research needs to progress toward developing combined systems of federated learning with generalization techniques that enable performance tests for AI medical solutions throughout patient care procedures. The fusion of deep learning technology components with block chain and Internet of Things creates secure, innovative heart care that healthcare professionals can access.

title: Eigenstate Transition Model for Successful Tests on Pre-Earthquake Prediction Through Magnetic Field Signals
authors: G. Ramkumar, Ahmad Abdelhafiz Ali Samhan, Swathi B, Ravi Gangadharolla
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: To reduce the damage and human death it becomes very essential to predict the earthquakes at the earlier stage. In the traditional research time series is mainly concentrated for this prediction process. But we focus on the concept of magnetic field signals for the prediction of earthquakes. In this article, an eigenstate transition model on pre-earthquake prediction using magnetic field signals (ETPPMF) system is developed which includes the sub sections like data acquisition, multi-channel analysis and magnetic field signal analysis. The simulation of this model is constructed in the python tool and the parameters which are calculated for the performance analysis are sampling points, onset time error detection and input signals vs features analysis. This measure results can help to predict the earthquake at the earlier stage which is able to provide effective damage control.

title: What is True Technical Barrier to Renewables Hosting Capacity of Unbalanced Distribution Network? - A Comprehensive Assessment Approach
authors: Yunqing Zhang, Cuo Zhang, Renjie Dai
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The growing integration of renewable energy, such as photovoltaics (PVs), into distribution networks brings significant environmental and economic benefits, but also technical issues including voltage rise, overloading, and voltage unbalance, which limit hosting capacity (HC) enhancement. This paper proposes a comprehensive assessment approach to identify the true technical barrier to renewables HC of unbalanced distribution networks. First, a current injection model with a backward-forward sweep (BFS) algorithm is applied to estimate network operating states and compute a voltage unbalance factor (VUF). Then, three technical barriers are defined for overvoltage, overloading and voltage unbalance respectively, and two assessment cases are designed, one adding PV in fixed increments to determine the thresholds of PV capacity and the other adopting Monte Carlo sampling to model uncertainty of PV sitings and sizes. The proposed method is tested on the IEEE 123-bus system, to determine how technical barriers limit PV HC of an unbalanced distribution network. The simulation results validate the proposed comprehensive assessment approach and provide insights into the enhancement of renewables HC.

title: Simple and Precise Extraction of Inertia and Damping Parameters in Synchronous Generators and Inverter-Based Sources
authors: Zhaohan Zhu, Kenichiro Sano
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: To evaluate the frequency support capability of individual generation sources, this paper proposes two methods for simultaneously extracting two key parameters: inertia constant and damping coefficient for synchronous generators and inverterbased sources with virtual inertia control. The first method is a simple approach that enables fast and practical evaluation in field measurements, offering moderate accuracy. However, it tolerates a bias error in inertia estimation caused by a constant deviation in angular velocity disturbance transmission, which was typically overlooked in previous research. To address this, the second method provides a high-precision evaluation by incorporating impedance-based compensation for the constant deviation, reducing inertia estimation error. These two methods offer flexible measurement options from simple and fast field evaluations to laboratory-level high precision depending on application needs. Simulation results validate their accuracy: the high-precision method achieves parameter extraction with errors within 0.5%, while the simple method still maintains acceptable errors around 5%.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning and Ensemble Approach for Detecting Fake Reviews on E-Commerce Platforms
authors: C. Madan Kumar, P. Poornima, Srividhya S, Arul Mary Rexy V, Sobiyaa P, Priyadarshini S
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: False reviews on e-commerce platforms weaken consumer confidence and skew market dynamics, presenting a significant risk to the integrity of online retail. The proposed study presents a hybrid architecture that integrates deep learning with ensemble methods, using BERT, CNN, and LSTM for complete textual feature extraction, in conjunction with the integration of metadata and behavioural data. The model employs Random Forest and XGBoost classifiers within a stacking ensemble to improve detection accuracy and resilience. This strategy, unlike existing systems that typically depend on single-modal features or conventional classifiers, effectively captures both linguistic nuances and user behaviours simultaneously, resulting in enhanced performance. Experimental findings on benchmark datasets indicate an F1-Score of 95.7%, an accuracy of 95.8%, and a ROC-AUC of 98.4%, exceeding baseline methods such as Logistic Regression and SVM. This method provides a scalable, interpretable, and highly efficient alternative to safeguard customers and e-commerce platforms from false reviews.

title: Analysis and Verification of Single-Point Grounding Faults in Tripping Circuit of Substation Circuit Breaker Considering Transition Resistance
authors: Jianming Liu, Dahan Yan, Shaohong Zheng, Lin Cai, Zheyuan Xu, Luliang Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Single-point grounding faults in the DC system trip circuits of substations easily lead to circuit breaker maloperation. Existing researches has not fully considered the influence of transition resistance between the ground fault point and the earth on fault current and maloperation risk, which makes it difficult to support field management. This paper takes the circuit breaker trip circuit of a 110 kV substation as the research object to examine the effect of transition resistance on fault characteristics. First, the critical components of the circuit and the causes of faults are identified. Then a second-order RLC equivalent circuit model incorporating transition resistance is established. Based on Kirchhoff's voltage law, the differential equation for the fault current is derived. The analysis further explores the evolution of fault current under underdamped, critically damped and overdamped states. A simulation model is built in MATLAB/Simulink to verify the theoretical analysis. The results show that the transition resistance governs the affects the damping ratio of the circuit, which thereby affects both the peak current magnitude and its attenuation characteristics. The value of this resistance ultimately determines whether a maloperation risk arises in the trip circuit. This study fills the gap in the analysis of the effects of transition resistance. It provides technical guidance for equipment selection, operating current setting, and maloperation prevention of grounding faults in substation trip circuits, which is significant for ensuring the safe and stable operation of power systems.

title: Seismic Modeling and Simulation Analysis of Filter Current Transformer Structures in Power Systems
authors: Mingxiao Li, Zixuan Li, Yu Mao, Decai Zhou, Gaoyang Shi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: This paper aims to investigate the seismic response characteristics of filter current transformers (FCT) installed at different support heights in power systems, evaluate their seismic performance, and provide guidance for engineering design. A refined finite element model (FEM) of the equipment was developed using ABAQUS, taking into account the structural characteristics of the electrical equipment, post insulators, and steel supports. Frequency analysis and dynamic response analysis were performed to compare the modal properties, acceleration, displacement, and stress responses of the equipment under different support heights. A field hammer test was conducted to verify the accuracy of the FEM. Results show that taller supports reduce the natural frequency of the overall structure and amplify the acceleration and displacement responses to seismic excitations, especially when the input spectrum matches the fundamental frequency. The displacement amplification factor under the Xinsong ground motion reached up to 2.3. Stress response analysis indicates that while taller supports may reduce acceleration at the equipment top, the support structure itself becomes a weak point. The study demonstrates that support height significantly influences the seismic performance of filter current transformers, and it is recommended that support height and stiffness be carefully controlled in design to ensure the safe operation of the power grid system.
