title: Distribution-level Hyperspectral Target Detection Under Mixture of Gaussian
authors: Yubo Ma, Yinuo Zhao, Yiyun Gou, Qingke Zou, Siyu Cai
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Statistical inference-based hyperspectral target detection, which provides a distribution-driven perspective for distinguishing the target from background, has recently attracted much attention. Most of the existing statistical detectors concentrate on modeling hyperspectral image (HSI) with an appropriate distribution or designing a better hypothesis test. However, the intrinsic relations of pixel–distribution and distribution–distribution have been ignored. Aiming to address this problem, a novel distribution-level target detection (DLTD) method for HSI is proposed, which incorporates distribution-level detection into pixel-level one and possesses statistical interpretability. Specifically, an HSI is statistically modeled as a mixture of Gaussian, where each Gaussian component characterizes an endmember. To explore detection at distribution level, the distance between indeterminate distribution and target one is measured by Kullback-Leibler divergence. Similarity based on this divergence is the detection value of the distribution. Then, the detection output is a weighted average of distribution-level detection values, where the weight is posterior probability. Experiments on four HSIs demonstrate superiority of the proposed DLTD method over comparative target detectors.

title: Compact and Low-Profile Dual-Polarized Phased Filtenna Array based on Micro-Mode SIW
authors: Ning Liu, Shi-Wei Qu, Hang Wong
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this paper, a compact dual-polarized phased filtenna array is presented and characterized by an ultra-low profile and a simple structure. It is realized by introducing the novel micro-mode SIW (MMSIW) into the feeding network of a patch antenna, which not only provides a filtering response but also enhances the operating bandwidth. The size of the proposed MMSIW cavity is significantly reduced compared with that of conventional eighth-mode SIW (EMSIW) cavity, thereby ensuring the overall compactness of the array. The profile of the array is only 0.04 λc, demonstrating a notable improvement over state-of-the-art designs. The simplistic structure ensures the ease of fabrication. The center frequency of the phased filtenna array is 5GHz, with a fractional bandwidth of 20% and a 25dB stopband suppression. An 8×8 array prototype has been successfully fabricated and measured, which exhibits a stable filtering function across scanning angles ranging from 0 to ±45°. The effectiveness of the design is validated by the measured results.

title: Study on Mitigation of OCR Malfunction due to SFCL in Power Distribution System with DER
authors: Yoo-Jung Cho, Sung-Hun Lim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: The superconducting fault current limiter (SFCL) is an effective device to mitigate the fault currents with minimal power loss and negligible impact on the power distribution system. However, the SFCL can cause the malfunctions in existing overcurrent relays (OCRs) and disrupt the protection coordination. This paper presents an impedance-based compensation method for OCRs in a power distribution system with distributed energy resource (DER). DER is simply modeled as ideal three-phase voltage sources behind internal impedances. DER affects OCR operation by supplying additional fault current and the operating characteristics of OCRs located near DER is altered by the SFCL. The impedance-based compensation method compensates OCR malfunctions and restores the protection coordination between OCRs. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, fault simulations involving unbalanced ground fault were performed using PSCAD/EMTDC.

title: MAADRR: Multi-Agent Adaptive Dynamic Round Robin for Cloud Scheduling Using Real Cloud Workload Traces
authors: Zafar Iqbal Khan, Muzafar Khan, Syed Nasir Mehmood Shah
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Task scheduling is required to optimize the use of resources and minimize the waiting time in cloud computing systems. However, the traditional fixed scheduling techniques cannot address the variability, heterogeneity and dynamic behavior of cloud workloads. Current timesharing schedulers use fixed CPU time quantum and do not have any dynamic mechanisms to adapt to the workload, and SLA-driven priorities, resulting in poor performance of cloud infrastructures. To overcome these drawbacks, this paper proposes a novel Multi-Agent Adaptive Dynamic Round Robin (MAADRR) scheduling model for dynamic and heterogeneous cloud systems. Compared with traditional single-layer schedulers, MAADRR has hierarchical agent-based coordination. This allows it to balance workloads across the infrastructure and have local flexibility simultaneously. The framework is implemented and evaluated on the CloudSim Plus simulator and enables realistic modelling of the multi-level cloud execution environment. The VM-level agents Adaptive Round Robin are dynamically changed by real-time quantum due to real-time workload feedback. A global coordination agent, which exists at the infrastructure layer, integrates the previously set scheduling strategies, such as Dynamic Max-Min, DRALBA, PSSLB, PSSELB, and DeRALBA, to coordinate the distribution of different tasks among the heterogeneous resources. This multi-agent design is cooperative and provides autonomous local optimization, stability, and fairness of the system. The experiments were conducted on workloads of varying sizes. The experimental results obtained from Google-Cluster-like Job (GoCJ) traces demonstrate that MAADRR is a more energy-efficient solution and outperforms the baseline methods in terms of Energy Consumption, Throughput, Average Waiting Time (AWT), Average Turnaround Time (ATAT), and Total Completion Time (TCT). The findings confirm that MAADRR is a scalable scheduling framework that is suitable for cloud environments.

title: A Rod-Driven Minimally Invasive Bioprinting Continuum Robotic System for Repairing Knee Joint Cartilage Defects_supp1-3670164.pdf
authors: Qian Tang
publish: N/A
abstract: Robot-assisted minimally invasive bioprinting for knee cartilage repair remains a significant clinical challenge. Ensuring bioprinting quality requires the robotic tip to stay perpendicular to the working surface. Rigid robots are limited in controlling tip orientation in the joint cavity, whereas continuum robots, with enhanced flexibility, provide promising solutions. In this article, a minimally invasive bioprinting continuum robot (BCR) system (BCRS) is proposed. First, a novel four-rod-driven double-layer rigid–flexible-coupled BCR is designed and fabricated, featuring enhanced dexterity to accommodate the spatial characteristics of the knee joint cavity. Second, a Lagrangian-based kinetostatic model of the BCR is derived using the geometrically nonlinear Euler–Bernoulli beam theory. Based on this model, an Eulerian analytical moment–equilibrium formulation is established to verify structural safety, and an open-loop feedforward controller is developed to precisely regulate the BCR tip pose. Finally, path-tracking and bioprinting experiments are performed, with results showing a root-mean-square error of 1.89 mm in position and 2.50<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula> in orientation. Minimally invasive perpendicular bioprinting within the knee joint cavity is achieved using the proposed BCRS, thereby reducing undesirable molding effects caused by bioink fluidity and providing an effective solution for cartilage repair.

title: Beamforming Design for Beyond Diagonal STAR-RIS-Assisted Near-Field ISAC Systems: A Power Minimization Approach
authors: Rahul Prakash Singh, Keshav Singh, Sandeep Kumar Singh, Yatindra Nath Singh, Fan-Shuo Tseng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: This paper investigates the performance of a Beyond Diagonal Simultaneously Transmitting and Reflecting Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (BD-STAR-RIS)-aided near-field Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) system, where a Uniform Planar Array (UPA) equipped Base Station (BS) simultaneously communicates with multiple users and senses multiple targets spread across both reflection and transmission regions. With the prime objective of efficient ISAC transmission design, we formulate a power minimization problem that jointly optimizes transmit beamforming precoders, phase beamforming matrix, and receive beamforming filters while ensuring strict Signal-to-Interference-Noise Ratio (SINR) or Cramér-Rao Bound (CRB) sensing metric, minimum Quality-of-Service (QoS), and BD-STAR-RIS phase constraints. Alternatively, we also address the maximization of Energy Efficiency (EE) while satisfying the constraints using the Dinkelbach–Quadratic Transform fractional programming approach reformulated as a convex Quadratically Constrained Quadratic Program (QCQP). We propose an alternating optimization-based algorithm that tackles the non-convex nature of the problem and provides an optimal solution using tools such as Quadratically Semi-definite Program, Semidefinite Relaxation (SDR), Rayleigh quotient method, and Manifold Optimization (MO) technique. We validate the proposed algorithm’s convergence, accuracy, and effectiveness through Monte Carlo simulations, demonstrating the advantages of BD-STAR-RIS in near-field ISAC systems. We graphically analyze the impact of key system parameters and demonstrate that BD-STAR-RIS outperforms benchmark technologies, even with limited resources, and that the near-field model yields superior signal quality with lower power consumption. Additionally, we also show that incorporating a large number of BD-STAR-RIS elements with a UPA antenna enhances the beampattern, significantly improving system performance.

title: Constraint-Driven Neural Network Architecture Adaptation for Image Localisation on a Miniature Mobile Robot
authors: Jan Pleterski, Jernej Puc, Rok Vrabič, Primož Podržaj, Gašper Škulj
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Deploying deep learning models on low-power devices is an increasingly important research challenge. Microcontrollers, in particular, have strict limitations due to the limited processing power and memory. Specifically, object localisation on such devices remains in the early stages of research, showing the need for architectural designs tailored to resource-constrained environments. In this work, we implement and evaluate different architectural configurations of deep learning models on a miniature mobile robot operating in a granular, sand-like terrain, where both robots and rocks must be localised as a prerequisite for robust navigation. The localisation models are based on the MobileNetV2 architecture utilising the FOMO (Faster Objects, More Objects) algorithm with dynamic convolutions. The proposed system consists of an HM01B0 greyscale camera integrated with a low-power RP2040 microcontroller. The object detection models are trained on a custom dataset consisting of 10,000 greyscale images (160×160 px) featuring robots and rocks. Our findings indicate that the best trade-offs between accuracy and memory are achieved by truncating the middle layers of the base model, balancing output resolution with retained pre-trained features and classifier complexity. Additionally, the results indicate that increasing the input image resolution produces a greater improvement in accuracy compared to expanding the model’s classifier head, relative to the increase in inference time. This study presents the first large-scale variation of FOMO truncations for the purpose of object localisation on low-power microcontrollers and offers practical guidance for selecting neural network architectures.

title: Dynamic Alignment Learning with Multi-Level Perceptual Stimuli for No-Reference Point Cloud Quality Assessment
authors: Biqi Wu, Xiwu Shang, Xiaoli Zhao, Yifan Zuo, Xiaoshui Huang, Liquan Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Point cloud quality assessment (PCQA) aims to establish an objective and unified standard for point cloud evaluation. With the rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs), their application in PCQA has recently gained attention. Existing LLM-based PCQA methods typically adopt static quality description and perceptual information alignment to judge the relevance of each quality level. This overlooks the dynamic nature of human perception, which is progressively refined through multi-level visual stimuli. And the strategy of single-level alignment constrains the variability of the quality description process, thereby resulting in bias. To address this, we propose a dynamic alignment learning framework that simulates progressive quality perception via hierarchical perceptual stimuli. Specifically, to provide a more comprehensive visual information, we capture visual information from two perspectives: from 2D to 3D and from static to dynamic. In addition, a multi-perception learner is utilized to enhance the understanding of these visual information. Subsequently, we employ learnable weights to simulate perceptual stimuli at different levels and embed them into the quality description. During this process, we apply masking to certain perceptual stimuli to illustrate the impact of partial perceptual loss on quality description. Finally, the contrastive learning is introduced to further refine the aligned information under different perceptual stimuli, thereby improving the accuracy of quality assessment. We conduct comprehensive experiments on the public databases, and the results demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance. Further analysis confirms the robustness of our approach across various scenarios.

title: Physics-Informed Depth-Conditional Neural Network for Chlorophyll-a Profile Reconstruction and Uncertainty Quantification
authors: Yaqiong Yu, Dongfang Zhang, Tingzi Chi, Kaizhe Feng, Chengjun Li, Yi Yan, Yifan Zhao, Junnan Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate reconstruction of vertical chlorophyll-a (Chla) profiles is essential for understanding phytoplankton distribution, evaluating primary productivity, and constraining the marine carbon cycle. However, sparse in situ observations and the complexity of physical–biological interactions make it difficult to infer full-depth Chla structures from surface data. Existing observation-, model-, and hybrid-driven approaches trade off between physical interpretability and adaptability, while purely data-driven models often lack physical consistency and perform poorly under domain shifts. To overcome these limitations, this study proposes a zero-shot cross-domain Deep Conditional Neural Function Generator (DCNFG). The model explicitly incorporates depth as a continuous conditioning variable, applies Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM) to couple surface environmental factors with depth-dependent features, and integrates heteroscedastic and quantile regression for comprehensive uncertainty quantification. Physical regularizations, including smoothness, unimodality, and curvature constraints, are introduced to ensure ecological and optical consistency. Extending the evaluation beyond the Mediterranean and Black Seas, cross-domain experiments in the Subpolar North Atlantic and the Labrador Sea further confirm that DCNFG consistently outperforms baseline models in terms of Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Mean Squared Error (MSE), and profile-wise correlation (R2). The model maintains high structural fidelity and stable uncertainty coverage under distribution shifts. The results indicate that DCNFG achieves a unified balance between physical consistency, interpretability, and adaptability, providing a robust framework for reliable and generalizable reconstruction of oceanic Chla profiles.

title: An Intensity-Similarity Coupling Framework for Extracting Motor Unit Twitch Area From Ultrafast Ultrasound Imaging
authors: Yiming Kang, Chen Chen, Zongtian Yin, Jianjun Meng, Xiangyang Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Objective: Accurate and non-invasive mapping of motor unit (MU) territories is essential for linking motoneuron activity with muscle contraction. Ultrafast ultrasound (UUS) enables high-resolution mechanical imaging of MUs; however, existing methods show limited spatiotemporal consistency of MU territories and lack sufficient validation. This study aims to develop a UUS-based approach to extract MU twitch areas with high spatiotemporal precision. Methods: We propose P2P-R2: an automated two-step framework that integrates global intensity and temporal similarity features of muscular twitches to extract and refine MU twitch areas from spike-triggered averaged (STA) UUS data. To generate the STA data and provide validation, dual-probe UUS images and intramuscular EMG signals were concurrently recorded. To benchmark the proposed framework, multiple feature extraction strategies, including intensity-based, similarity-based, and previously published methods, were implemented and compared using spatial and temporal evaluation metrics. Results: P2P-R2 significantly outperformed all single-feature and existing methods, achieving higher within-region twitch consistency ($R^{2}_{s}$ = 0.96 $\pm$ 0.01) and between-probe twitch agreement ($R$ = 0.88 $\pm$ 0.26) than Naive STA ($R^{2}_{s}$ = 0.84 $\pm$ 0.19, $R$= -0.03 $\pm$ 0.62). It also reduced centroid-to-electrode distance (10.36mm $\pm$ 6.35mm) and improved spatial agreement (RoA = 0.09 $\pm$ 0.10). Furthermore, P2P-R2 captured complex MU activity patterns, including twisting, splits, and asynchronous motion. Conclusion and Significance: P2P-R2 enables precise and robust MU twitch area extraction across both spatial and temporal domains. Its fully automated, source-agnostic design supports transition to fully non-invasive applications in neuromuscular diagnostics, motor unit tracking, and human–machine interfaces.

title: Fish Species Classification Using Machine Learning
authors: M. Soundarya, M. Jayameena, B. Yalini
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Fish are the central element of nature and are an important component of mankind. They regulate the structure of the ecosystem, act as a source of food, and are economic and cultural pillars. Taxonomy of fish is defining their species and categorizing them according to their form and action. It is one of the key steps in preserving records of biodiversity, preserving animals that are unique, and in assisting scientists with their work. Our research interest in utilizing Resnet with feature pyramid Network for classification of fishes with images. The architecture captures the finest details and the whole body of the fish as an entirety by a sliding-window and layered framework. Because of that, it performs better, even on near indistinguishable species. Previous fish classification methods were sluggish and were followed by a litany of failures. They included hand tuned feature extraction, provided with little finesse for complicated images, and were unable to distinguish easily between similar organisms. They could not also be scaled for real time utilization, and therefore, they failed without the utilization of deep learning ideas. Our answer addresses these problems by promptly and correctly recognizing fish species from photos. It has the ability to be incorporated in real time use within fisheries, marine research, aquaculture, and conservation work, for improving the overall efficiency and reliability of the process.

title: Six Port Multifunctional MIMO antenna for Integrated Cognitive Radio System
authors: Jenitha A, Deeksha B, Deepika J, Manasa Aralumallige S
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Six-port multiple input, multiple output (MIMO) antenna designed and demonstrated for cognitive radio (CR) applications. The proposed geometry is made of a single-layer dielectric substrate, where metallic patterns and electronic components are articulately used to exhibit both types of CR techniques (interweave and underlay) through three different working states. In the first state, the antenna radiate over a wide bandwidth from 2.26 to 7.00 GHz for sensing applications, while in the second mode, the geometry has a frequency tunability between 4.80 to 6.20 GHz to be used for interweave CR communication. In the third mode, a fixed band-notch response is obtained at 4.20 GHz (which can also be made tunable from 3.10 to 4.80 GHz), thereby fulfilling the underlay operation. The novelties of the proposed work are the realization of an innovative antenna topology exhibiting all types of CR operations (102.38% in the wideband state, 14.4% in the tunable narrowband state, and 27.21% in the tunable bandnotch state), at the expense of using the lowest number of diodes components

title: Selection of Supervised Learning-Based Sparse Matrix Reordering Algorithms
authors: Tao Tang, Youfu Jiang, Yingbo Cui, Jianbin Fang, Peng Zhang, Lin Peng, Chun Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: Sparse matrix reordering is a key optimization strategy commonly used in large-scale sparse matrix solvers. By permuting the rows and columns of a matrix, it aims to reduce the matrix bandwidth or minimize fill-in during factorization, thereby improving computational efficiency. Traditional algorithm selection typically relies on brute-force search or empirical heuristics, which cannot adapt to the structural variety of different sparse matrices. To address this, we propose a supervised-learning-based model for selecting sparse matrix reordering algorithms. By learning the mapping between various matrix features and popular reordering methods, our model automates and intelligently chooses the most suitable algorithm. Experiments on the SuiteSparse matrix collection demonstrate that our model achieves high prediction accuracy for the optimal reordering method of each matrix. Compared to AMD, the predominant ordering algorithm in practice, our model achieves a 55.37% reduction in solution time with an average speedup ratio of 1.45.

title: Real-Time Lung Cancer Detection: A Multi-Objective Deep Learning Framework for LDCT Enhancement and Clinical Reliability
authors: Madderla Achyutha Sree, Rakshitha Kanneboina, Bhoomeshwar Bala, Arathi Chitla, Raja Shekar Kadurka, Mada Prasad
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The most common cause of cancer death globally is lung cancer, and early detection is critical to enhance survival rates. Low-Dose Computed Tomography (LDCT) has emerged as a standard screening procedure due to its capacity to identify lung nodules at early stages of development using negligible radiation doses. The effectiveness of LDCT is, however, hampered by the poor image quality, leading to difficulties in making precise diagnoses. Deep learning has proved potential for automating lung cancer detection, but current systems still encounter a number of hurdles for clinical adoption. These are suboptimal LDCT resolution, dependency on computergenerated enhancements, class imbalance, low data diversity, and inadequate interpretability for clinical use in real-world settings. This article presents a multi-objective deep learning framework whose aim is to improve real-time lung cancer detection by an integrated sequence of processes. First, a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) is employed to denoise and enhance LDCT images without compromising their essential diagnostic characteristics. Second, the system applies sophisticated resampling and cost-sensitive methods to handle class imbalance during the training data. Third, the research includes a heterogeneous, multi-institutional dataset to enhance generalizability across patient groups. Fourth, a light-weight Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is built to enable realtime classification with efficiency. Finally, model explainability is boosted through explainable AI methods like Grad-CAM so that the clinicians can visualize and verify the predictions. The paper thoroughly discusses every aspect, from preprocessing and image augmentation to classification and clinical verification. Experimental findings show enhanced accuracy, fewer false negatives, and real-time performance. The work seeks to close the gap between algorithmic breakthrough and real-world clinical application for lung cancer diagnosis.

title: A Transformer-Based Hybrid Model for Early Lung Cancer Detection Using CT Imaging and Clinical Metadata Fusion
authors: Kandula Sravani, Sneha G, Muntha Raju, Arathi Chitla, Raja Shekar Kadurka, Mada Prasad
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The early lung cancer identification is crucial for increasing patient survival, the majority of artificial intelligence (AI) models ignore important clinical context, including patient history, symptoms, and lifestyle factors, in favour of relying only on imaging data. In order to bridge this gap, system presents LungDetect-ViT, a hybrid deep learning architecture that combines TabTransformer for structured clinical metadata with Vision Transformers (ViT) for CT scan processing. Using a cross-attention fusion method, the system is built to jointly reason over tabular and visual inputs, allowing for more precise and customized predictions. The ViT branch embeds and processes CT images through multi-head self-attention layers after preprocessing them into 224×224 pixel inputs and dividing them into 16×16 patches. In order to capture feature dependencies, TabTransformer is used to simultaneously encode clinical metadata, such as age, gender, smoking history, symptoms and family history. The model can learn how clinical factors affect image interpretation by fusing the outputs from both branches via cross-attention. Cancer presence, stage, and a confidence score are predicted by the final classification head. The model was trained using ordinal regression loss functions and binary cross-entropy on a carefully selected dataset of 3,000 patients. It outperformed CNN-based baselines with 99.1% accuracy, 0.995 AUC, 98.9% precision, and 98.5% sensitivity. Grad-CAM visuals and attention weight analysis improve interpretability, which qualifies LungDetect-ViT for clinical use. This hybrid strategy shows how multimodal fusion can improve the diagnosis of early-stage lung cancer.

title: Hybrid YOLOv8-Transformer Approach for Accurate Leukemia Classification in Blood Smear Images
authors: N. Nagarani, Mirnal Jeslyn J, Oviya R, Umasankari S
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: In Leukemia, more so Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (ALL), is the most common blood cancer found in children in the age group of 2 to 5 years. Timely and proper diagnosis is needed for proper treatment and better survival rates. Conventional techniques like microscopy take a lot of time, are subject to human error, and are based mostly on specialized interpretation. Toflush out such challenges, this paper suggests a hybrid deep learning architecture merging YOLOv8 and Transformer models for leukemia classification from peripheral blood smear images using automated means. The suggested model embeds the local feature extraction ability of YOLOv8 with the global attention mechanism of Transformer models, allowing accurate identification of healthy cells and leukemic cells with classification into pre-cancer, benign, early, and pro-cancer stages. The approach is designed to help doctors by providing high accuracy, less diagnosis time, and better explainability. Experimental results show that the hybrid model outperforms the standard CNN-based approach with better performance, which is a strong AI-aided tool for pediatric leukemia classification and stage determination.

title: An Accessible Explainable AI Extension for Fake News Detection
authors: Pooja Gavhane, Aditya K. Vasipalli, Adreesh Zeminder, Aditya Kachwaha, Darshan D. Admane, Aditya S. Chimurkar
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Misinformation is an increasing challenge, yet existing detectors often lack the accessibility and interpretability for typical users, functioning as "black boxes" that erode trust. This paper presents an accessible, explainable fake news detector as a Chrome browser extension, prioritizing user trust and transparency. An initial study (60 participants) using a baseline Logistic Regression model confirmed this trust issue, with 25% rating the "black box" model's trust as low. The final BERT-based system, trained on 140,000+ samples, provides real-time SHAP explanations and uses a sliding window for long articles. A second study (68 participants) validated this approach: 95.6% found the extension credible, with a 4.62/5.0 user experience rating. Despite generalization challenges revealed in cross-dataset evaluation, the system is a production-ready, open-source tool bridging research and practical application by prioritizing usability and transparency.

title: Research on Joint Planning Method for Distributed Energy Resources and Electric Vehicle Charging Stations Based on NSGA-II Algorithm
authors: Fan Gui, Zheng Guo, Xinrui Shan, Yizhen Zhao, Xiongyi Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: Taking the minimum active power loss in the distribution network, the maximum grid-connected capacity of distributed generation (DG) integrated, and the highest traffic satisfaction reflecting user convenience in charging (with the minimum extra mileage driven by the user for charging) as optimization objectives, a joint planning and optimization model for the distribution network incorporating DG and electric vehicle charging stations (EVCS) is constructed. A joint planning method for DG and EVCS based on the NSGA-II algorithm is proposed to solve multi-objective problems. Through case analysis, it is demonstrated that appropriately increasing the penetration rate of DG is conducive to better coordination between charging load and DG output, and to the economic operation of the distribution network. This also indicates, to a certain extent, the accuracy, scientificity, and rationality of this algorithm.

title: Transient Frequency Support of Wind Turbines for Frequency Trajectory Optimization Considering Signal Delay
authors: Shuai Dong, Muyao Peng, Mingyan Song, Yubo Zhang, Songhao Yang, Zhiguo Hao
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The optimal-frequency-trajectory-oriented frequency support methods provide a system view for the wind turbines (WTs) frequency regulation. However, the power deficit information is required for the implementation of this frequency regulation strategy. The signal delay caused by the power deficit estimation and transmission will threaten the effectiveness of its implementation. To eliminate the influence of inevitable signal delay, this paper designs a zero power deficit preset strategy. By presetting power deficit parameter as 0 during the initial stage of disturbance, the proposed method successfully eliminates the system frequency overfall risk caused by lack of the necessary system power deficit information. Finally, simulation results in a modified IEEE 39-bus system verify the performance of the proposed method on delivering preemptive grid frequency support provided by the controllable wind turbine generator systems.

title: A Multi-Dimensional Quantitative Evaluation of Doubly-Fed Wind Turbine Frequency Regulation Strategy Based on Entropy Weight Method
authors: Limin Yin, Yuze Yang, Jiawei Man, Heling Yang, Cong Sun, Rakhmonov Ikromjon Usmonovich
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Aiming at the lack of objectivity and universality in the comprehensive performance evaluation of FM control strategy in new energy high penetration power system, this paper proposes a multi-indicator quantitative evaluation system based on entropy weight method. Firstly, an evaluation system containing five types of indicators, such as active power dynamic response, frequency pole shift, and double-fed turbine output ratio, is constructed from the dimensions of FM effect and power support; secondly, entropy weight theory is introduced to dynamically allocate weights according to the degree of dispersion of the indicator data distribution, so as to avoid subjective weighting bias; lastly, based on the threemachine, nine-node simulation system, no-attachment control, traditional VSG control, grid-type active support control, and fuzzy power control system are comparatively analyzed. Finally, based on a three-machine and nine-node simulation system, the comprehensive performance of four strategies, namely, no-attachment control, traditional VSG control, network-configuration active support control and fuzzy adaptive control, is compared and analyzed under the scenario of load surge/decrease. The results show the effectiveness and adaptability of the entropy weight method in quantitative assessment, which provides a theoretical basis for the optimization of wide-bandwidth domain FM strategies.

title: Maximizing Domain of Attraction of Grid-Forming Converters of Wind Power Integrated Power Systems Using SOS Programming
authors: Yuexi Yang, Liwen Zheng, Fangyuan Li, Xinyue Zhang, Junjie Lin, Haibin Sun, Guobin Fu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: This paper puts forward a configuration optimization method for grid-forming converters (GFMCs) in full-scale wind power generators, which focuses on domain of attraction (DA) maximization via sum-of-squares (SOS) programming for coordinated operational control. An expanding interior algorithm is designed for DA estimation and optimization. By adopting the expanding interior algorithm on the GFMC-based wind power integration system, the DA of the initial parameter model can be estimated by adopting an optimal level set of the Lyapunov function. The proposed algorithm maximizes the DA of GFMC-based wind power integration systems by optimizing the controller parameters, with verification through time-domain simulation in a 4-converter 11-bus test system.

title: An Intelligent Key Point Recognition Method for Transmission Tower Structures Integrating RTMDet and RTMPose
authors: Wenping Xie, Yijie Mao, Ming Nie, Wei Liu, Jingjing Zhang, Jiangshun Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: With the rapid development of smart grids and transmission systems, transmission towers (critical power transmission components) require increasingly important monitoring of structural safety and operational status. Traditional inspections, relying on manual work and image processing, have limited efficiency and accuracy in complex environments. To improve inspection intelligence, this paper proposes an intelligent key point recognition method for transmission towers by fusing RTMDet (efficient object detection algorithm) and RTMPose (pose estimation algorithm). Based on existing transmission tower point cloud data, a progressive key point extraction method integrating the two models is established: RTMDet locates the tower's target region, and RTMPose performs efficient key point extraction. Results show the relative error of key point extraction is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{2 \%}$</tex>; in tilt angle deviation tests, errors for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5}^{\boldsymbol{\circ}}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}^{\boldsymbol{\circ}}$</tex> scenarios are 0.22° and 0.3° respectively. Experiments demonstrate the method is more accurate and robust than traditional ones, stably identifying tower key components and estimating their poses under various environments, thus significantly improving inspection efficiency and reliability.

title: Unsupervised Detection of False Data Injection Attacks in Power Systems Using Deep Adversarial Autoencoders
authors: Haofeng Zheng, Yufei Song, Kaiyang Liu, Feng Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: This paper introduces an unsupervised detection scheme built on deep adversarial autoencoders (DAA) for identifying false data injection attacks (FDIA) in power systems. First, a deep autoencoder (DAE) is used to learn features from the original data. An adversarial training is used to enhance the DAE's robustness. Subsequently, cosine similarity and Chebyshev distance are employed to assess the similarity between the input data and their reconstructions produced by the DAE. Ultimately, a detector detects FDIA based on this similarity. Our method eliminates the need for manually labeled training data and learns normal and attack patterns from the mixed data rather than normal data only. The experimental results demonstrate that our approach achieves better results than competing unsupervised detection techniques on several metrics, which substantiates its effectiveness.

title: Uniform Loss vs. Specialized Optimization: A Comparative Analysis in Multi-Task Learning
authors: Gabriel S. Gama, Valdir Grassi Junior
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Specialized Multi-Task Optimizers (SMTOs) balance task learning in Multi-Task Learning by addressing issues like conflicting gradients and differing gradient norms, which hinder equal-weighted task training. However, recent critiques suggest that equally weighted tasks can achieve competitive results compared to SMTOs, arguing that previous SMTO results were influenced by poor hyperparameter optimization and lack of regularization. In this work, we evaluate these claims through an extensive empirical evaluation of SMTOs, including some of the latest methods, on more complex multi-task problems to clarify this behavior. More specifically, we start our analysis by evaluating all SMTOs on a simple MNIST problem to identify the promising optimizers and evaluate then further on progressively more complex Multi-Task problems. Our findings indicate that SMTOs perform well compared to uniform loss and that fixed weights can achieve competitive performance compared to SMTOs. Furthermore, we demonstrate why uniform loss perform similarly to SMTOs in some instances. The source code is available at https://github.com/Gabriel-SGama/UnitScal_vs_SMTOs.

title: Quantum Safe Networks: A Case Study on Securing QKD Interfaces using PQC
authors: Sayan Das, Aarav Varshney, Prasanna Ravi, Anupam Chattopadhyay
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Quantum-Safe Networks (QSNs) use quantum key distribution (QKD) to deliver keys generated using a Quantum Random Number Generator (QRNG) to consumers at scale. In practice, prevailing Quantum-Key-as-a-Service (QKaaS) offerings still assume that each consumer site deploys on-premise QKD hardware, creating cost and feasibility barriers that limit adoption. Although ETSI GS QKD 014 standardizes APIs for key retrieval between Secure Application Entities (SAEs) and Key Management Entities (KMEs), it does not address key delivery across untrusted classical networks to consumers without local QKD devices. We close this gap with a gateway architecture that transparently secures ETSI-compliant QKD APIs using Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC)—applying PQC signatures and key-encapsulation mechanisms end-to-end—without requiring changes to existing devices or software. The design supports cryptographic agility, enabling runtime selection and rotation among standardized PQC schemes. Evaluations on both simulated QKD environments and production-grade hardware show minimal overhead (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\approx 1.3\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> –1.5× versus baseline) and robust scaling across concurrent connections and multiple consumer sites, extending the reach of QSNs beyond premises equipped with QKD while preserving end-to-end quantum-safe guarantees. Overall, this paper equips practitioners with a systematized view of PQC–QKD integration in QSNs, with an actionable gateway blueprint for extending QSNs without the need for on-premise QKD deployments.

title: Demonstration of 6.5-kV/15-A Split-Gate SiC MOSFET for Fast-Switching Pulsed Power Applications
authors: Zhengxiang Liao, Juntao Li, Xingliang Xu, Xiangyu Liu, Lin Zhang, Lianghui Li, Yingjiang He, Lingfeng Li, Zhiqiang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: This article presents the design and fabrication of a 6.5-kV/15-A silicon carbide (SiC) split-gate (SG) MOSFET for fast-switching pulsed power systems. Through simulation-optimized SG structure and high-voltage termination design, the device significantly reduces gate-to-drain capacitance (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${C} _{\text {GD}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) while maintaining a blocking voltage (BV) exceeding 7.7 kV. Pulsed discharge tests at 5 kV, conducted under identical gate drive conditions, demonstrate that the proposed high-voltage SG MOSFET achieves a voltage fall time of 25.4 ns and a current rise time of 21.1 ns, corresponding to a 13.0% and a 14.2% reduction compared to the conventional planar-gate MOSFET, respectively. In a system-level comparison against a conventional approach using two series-connected 3.6-kV silicon-based insulated gate bipolar transistors (Si IGBTs), the single SG SiC MOSFET achieves comparable performance under the same pulsed discharge conditions with a greatly simplified gate drive circuit, which facilitates system miniaturization. This work provides systematic experimental validation of the SG ultrahigh-voltage SiC MOSFET as an enabling technology for next-generation compact and high-performance pulsed power supplies.

title: Transmission-Line Anti-Reflection Coating for High-Frequency Ultrasound IC Inspection
authors: Jun Chen, Xin Wang, Jianguo Ma, Yi Quan, Chunlong Fei, Yintang Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: High-frequency ultrasound imaging, due to its penetrability and micron-level resolution, is a critical modality for non-destructive testing (NDT) of integrated circuit (IC) packaging. However, the substantial acoustic impedance mismatch between the coupling medium (water) and the silicon substrate creates a severe measurement bottleneck: strong surface reflections drastically reduce the energy transmitted into the chip, degrading the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and resolution of internal interconnects. To overcome this physical limitation and enhance the measurement capability of scanning acoustic microscopy (SAM), we have designed a precise anti-reflection coating based on the theory of the evolution from electromagnetic transmission lines to acoustic transmission lines. By modeling the acoustic propagation path as an equivalent circuit, we designed and fabricated a three-layer composite structure (Parylene C/Au/Parylene C) specifically optimized for the particular frequency (70 MHz in this study). Experimental validation on commercial IC chips demonstrates that this "instrumentation conditioning" approach significantly improves signal integrity. Specifically, the coating yielded a 52% increase in the amplitude of internal echoes (from 142 mV to 216 mV). Furthermore, C-scan imaging revealed a 93% average improvement in the mean gradient of internal metal wires across multiple layers, enabling high-fidelity 3D reconstruction of interconnection structures that were previously indistinguishable from noise. This method offers a robust, low-cost solution to the intrinsic attenuation problem in high-frequency acoustic measurement, significantly extending the detection depth and clarity for IC reliability analysis.

title: ReflectGAN: Modeling Vegetation Effects for Soil Carbon Estimation from Satellite Imagery
authors: Dristi Datta, Manoranjan Paul, Manzur Murshed, Shyh Wei Teng, Leigh M. Schmidtke
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Soil organic carbon (SOC) is a critical indicator of soil health, but its accurate estimation from satellite imagery is hindered in vegetated regions due to spectral contamination from plant cover, which obscures soil reflectance and reduces model reliability. This study proposes the Reflectance Transformation Generative Adversarial Network (ReflectGAN), a novel paired GAN-based framework designed to reconstruct accurate bare soil reflectance from vegetated soil satellite observations. By learning the spectral transformation between vegetated and bare soil reflectance, ReflectGAN facilitates more precise SOC estimation under mixed land cover conditions. Using the LUCAS 2018 dataset and corresponding Landsat 8 imagery, we trained multiple learning-based models on both original and ReflectGAN-reconstructed reflectance inputs. Models trained on ReflectGAN outputs consistently outperformed those using existing vegetation correction methods. For example, the best-performing model (RF) achieved an R2 of 0.54, RMSE of 3.95, and RPD (Residual Prediction Deviation) of 2.07 when applied to the ReflectGAN-generated signals, representing a 35% increase in R2, a 43% reduction in RMSE, and a 43% improvement in RPD compared to the best existing method (PMM-SU). The performance of the models with ReflectGAN is also better compared to their counterparts when applied to another dataset, i.e., Sentinel-2 imagery. These findings demonstrate the potential of ReflectGAN to improve SOC estimation accuracy in vegetated landscapes, supporting more reliable soil monitoring.

title: Loss Optimization and Experimental Verification of MW-Level Medium-Frequency Transformer Using Grain-Oriented Silicon Steel
authors: Jialiang Hu, Biao Zhao, Bin Cui, Yiqing Ma, Xueteng Tang, Zhe Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: MW-level medium-frequency transformer (MFT) is crucial for the efficient transmission of large-scale renewable energy. But under the combined constraints of MW-level power, high current, and several-hundred Hz operation, traditional design approaches cause elevated losses, which restricts overall efficiency. To enhance the energy transfer efficiency of MFT, this study proposed a loss-optimized design methodology tailored for MW-level MFT. The approach combines material selection and structural optimization across three main subsystems: magnetic core, winding, and cooling system. A silicon steel core joint structure with a diagonally staggered lamination is designed to homogenize the magnetic density at the corner and reduce the local core loss. Copper foil windings are employed to handle the kA-level current, with the thickness and layer count optimized to suppress eddy current. Based on the leakage magnetic field distribution, the structural dimensions and layout positions of the cooling plate is optimized to reduce additional eddy current losses. Based on the proposed methodology, a 1.5 MVA / 600 Hz MFT prototype was developed using ultra-thin grain-oriented silicon steel and copper foil winding. Experimental results demonstrate a full-load efficiency of 99.57% and a hotspot temperature below 91 °C.

title: Hot Press Sintering of Copper Particles: An Effective Approach for Low-Resistivity Repair of Open Defects in Precision Circuits
authors: Changlong Ye, Dongchang Ma, Wenzhe Wang, Huitao Liu, Kang Wan, Junyi Lu, Guannan Yang, Yu Zhang, Guanghan Huang, Chengqiang Cui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: In this work, we present a repair method of open circuit defects in precision circuitry using hot press sintering of copper particles followed by laser ablation. First, the open circuit defect position is filled with copper particle paste. Then, the copper particles are hot-press sintered to form conductive pathways. Finally, a high-power-density laser with a micron-sized beam spot is used to remove residual excess copper. With this method, open circuit defects with a length of 100 μm, a width of 100 μm, a line spacing of 80 μm, and a thickness of 45 μm can be successfully repaired. In contrast to the traditional circuit forming route that uses laser sintering of copper particles, the hot press sintering step promotes the closure of internal voids. As a result, a more compact sintered structure is obtained, and the formable circuit thickness, porosity, and electrical properties can be significantly improved. The as-repaired circuit region exhibits a low porosity of 14% and a low excess electrical resistance of 0.014 Ω, which corresponds to an electrical resistivity as low as 3.04 μΩ·cm. These results are significantly better than the circuit defects repaired by the laser sintering process, which results in an overall porosity of 45% and an electrical resistivity of 316.8 μΩ·cm. The circuit repair method presented here provides a promising technique for yield improvement and cost control for the manufacturing of precision circuits.

title: Towards Higher Resolution Arctic Winter Sea Ice Concentration: Addressing Sentinel-1 Misclassification With AMSR2 Data Fusion
authors: Jozef Rusin, Anthony P. Doulgeris, Thomas Lavergne, K. Andrea. Scott, Frode Dinessen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate and high-resolution sea ice concentration (SIC) products are needed for operational forecasting and climate monitoring. Passive microwave sensors, like the Advanced Microwave Scanning Radiometer 2 (AMSR2), provide daily SIC observations at 5 km resolution. However, this coarse spatial resolution constrains their capabilities. Whereas, Sentinel-1 Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) offers high-resolution ice-water discrimination but is prone to classification errors in wind-roughened open water and land-fast ice. This study aims to obtain the strengths of both sensors to produce a robust km-scale SIC for winter Arctic conditions by applying a SAR–AMSR2 data fusion framework using a U-Net model trained on high-resolution pixel-level labels. The U-Net uses SAR features (HH, HV, incidence angle) and AMSR2 inputs, with thirteen feature combinations across two fusion strategies being evaluated: direct data-level fusion of AMSR2 brightness temperatures or preprocessed data-level fusion of AMSR2 SIC. The findings show that fusing AMSR2 SIC with SAR improves classification with mean intersection over union scores of 97.93% and 92.47% compared with SAR- and Landsat-derived validation data, with significant improvements in previously identified troublesome land-fast ice and open water regions. The preservation of spatial resolution in the fused SIC is also evaluated by using power spectral variance analysis, which confirms that the fusion does not degrade the effective spatial resolution of the final output. Overall, the approach presented in this study supports accurate, high-resolution winter SIC retrieval suitable for future operational forecasting and data assimilation, like the Barents-2.5 km model, by preserving fine-scale features and reducing misclassification.

title: Two-Timescale Antenna Movement for UAV-Mounted Movable Antenna-Enabled Integrated Sensing and Communication
authors: Qingwen Long, Jingze Ding, Ngai Cheong, Mingjing Huang, Bingli Jiao
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: This letter proposes a two-timescale antenna movement framework for an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-mounted movable antenna (MA)-enabled integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system. To reduce the overhead of channel state information (CSI) acquisition in practical applications, the UAV moves at the large timescale based on statistical CSI to mitigate large-scale path loss, while the MA moves at the small timescale based on instantaneous CSI to effectively exploit small-scale channel fading caused by the random superposition of multipath components. To jointly optimize the UAV and MA positions as well as the transmit beamforming vector, a double-loop algorithm is developed. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed antenna movement scheme effectively enhances ISAC performance over conventional schemes and approaches the ideal upper bound.

title: APCA-Net: Adaptive Patch-Correlation Attention Network for Vision-Based Human Hand Acupoint Detection in Acupuncture_supp1-3670428.pdf
authors: HE Zhaoshui
publish: N/A
abstract: Acupuncture plays an important role in the treatment of diseases, prevention, and health care. However, it is difficult to detect acupoints from the hand surface due to the complicated tasks: first, the acupoint regions are similar to the surrounding areas on the hand surface; second, the skin tone on the hand varies in depth. To address these challenges, an adaptive patch-correlation attention network (APCA-Net) is proposed for vision-based human hand acupoint detection in acupuncture, where the adaptive patch aware attention module is designed to distinguish acupoint from the surrounding skin tissue by capturing fine-grained features from small patches, while the adaptive correlation channel attention module is devised to detect acupoints of different skin tones by modeling channel correlation. Experiments on benchmarks show that the proposed APCA-Net can achieve the acupoint detection rate of 76.11% and 88.32% on the 11 K Hands Subset and Depth Hand datasets, respectively, outperforming other state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Jointly Optimal Coding and Control Policies for a Controlled Markovian System over a Communication Channel
authors: Evelyn Hubbard, Liam Cregg, Serdar Yüksel
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: We study the problem of joint optimization involving coding and control policies for a controlled Markovian system over a finite-rate noiseless communication channel. While structural results on the optimal encoding and control have been obtained in the literature, their implementation has been prohibitive in general, except for linear models. To this end, we first develop existence, regularity, and structural properties on optimal policies, followed by rigorous approximations and reinforcement learning results. Notably, we establish near optimality of finite model approximations obtained via predictor (conditional probability on the next state realization given the information at the controller) quantization as well as sliding finite window approximations, and the convergence of a reinforcement learning algorithm to near optimality. A detailed comparison of the approximation schemes and their performance is presented. To our knowledge, this is the first rigorous reinforcement learning study on jointly optimal coding and networked control with finite rate channels.

title: Whitening-Kernel-Adaptive Structured Pursuit Via AIC Guidance for Robust Active Sonar Detection
authors: Yuqi Zheng, Lerong Hong, Zetong Fan, Hongzhuang Cao, Rui Li
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: In active sonar systems, detecting weak targets obscured by strong, colored reverberation remains a persistent challenge, particularly when the Signal-to-Reverberation Ratio (SRR) is critically low. Conventional greedy algorithms, which assume isotropic noise, often suffer from severe performance degradation due to spectral mismatch in shallow-water environments. To address this, we propose a Kernel-Based Structured Pursuit (K-BSP) framework. By integrating an AIC-Guided Spectral Estimation mechanism, the algorithm constructs a whitening kernel to accurately model the reverberation spectrum. Furthermore, the solver employs a tensor-based architecture to enforce multidimensional signal consistency, effectively enhancing the target energy concentration. Validation using sea trial data collected in the South China Sea demonstrates the framework's superior capability in suppressing colored clutter. Results show that K-BSP successfully recovers weak targets that are otherwise completely masked by residual reverberation energy using standard methods, achieving near-ideal detection probability at low false alarm rates. The proposed method significantly outperforms conventional subspace and greedy baselines in low-SRR regimes.

title: Wideband Reflectionless Filtering Phase Shifter With Low Phase Deviation
authors: Shujiang Zeng, Lianwen Deng, Yi Deng, Ye Liu, Shengxiang Huang
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: One novel filtering phase shifter is designed to present concurrently reflectionless characteristics, wideband operation, and minimal phase deviation. The design incorporates wideband filtering structures, absorbing stubs, and dual delay lines to realize precise phase control while effectively suppressing stopband reflection. Bandwidth is enhanced through strategically positioned stubs that introduce additional transmission poles. Theoretical analysis based on even-odd mode decomposition validates the operating principles. For experimental verification, 45° and 90° prototypes were fabricated, and the measured results demonstrated strong agreement with theoretical predictions.

title: Robust Multivariate Time Series Imputation Model Based on Missing Position and Heterogeneous Variable Coding
authors: Rui Meng, HaiBo Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Ignoring the heterogeneity between different variables in multivariate time series, the accuracy of missing data imputation model based on traditional methods is low. This paper proposes a framework named Robust multivariate time series imputation model based on missing position and variable heterogeneous coding (RIMVC). Based on the SAITS framework, a learnable missing position coding module is introduced to provide the model with explicit semantic information about the data missing mode. Variable heterogeneous coding module is added to learn its unique identity representation for each time series variable, and rotation position coding is used to more accurately model the relative order and dependence between time points. Finally, in order to enhance the ability of the model to resist outliers, the robust mixed loss function combined with the average absolute error MAE and Huber loss is used to optimize, which significantly improves the stability of the training process. Extensive experimental results on several real power system public datasets show that the imputation accuracy of RIMVC is significantly better than many state-of-the-art baseline models under different missing rates.

title: Dynamic Boundary-Constrained Power System Operation: A Multi-Time-Scale Rolling Optimization Framework
authors: He Huang, Qian Ma, Jinbing Liang, Jin Zou, Meiying Huang, Yiping Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The operation of modern power systems requires balancing long-term planning consistency with short-term adaptability, particularly under rising penetration of variable renewable energy. Traditional approaches treat monthly scheduling, daily dispatch, and real-time balancing as largely decoupled processes, often resulting in boundary violations, inflated reserve costs, and excessive renewable curtailment. This paper proposes a unified multi-time-scale rolling optimization framework that explicitly integrates monthly, daily, and rolling horizons into a boundary-aware operational model. The formulation minimizes system-wide costs by simultaneously accounting for generation expenditures, reserve deployment, renewable curtailment penalties, and intertemporal boundary adjustment costs. A comprehensive case study is conducted on a test system reflecting characteristics of the IEEE 118-bus network with a peak demand of 6.5 GW and renewable penetration above 35 percent. Data inputs include monthly fuel allocations, daily boundary conditions, and high-resolution renewable and load profiles with forecast error standard deviations ranging from 0.05 to 0.30 p.u. Results demonstrate that the proposed rolling approach reduces renewable curtailment by approximately 33 percent, lowers boundary violation frequency by nearly 70 percent, and cuts system-wide operation costs by 8 – 12 percent compared to baseline scheduling. By demonstrating that consistent monthly-to-real-time integration can simultaneously enhance economic efficiency, renewable utilization, and system reliability, the paper advances both the theory and practice of multi-scale operational planning.

title: Analysis of Influencing Factors and Fault Control Strategies of Flexible DC Short-Circuit Current in High-Load-Density Power Grid
authors: Zuohong Li, Guoduan Zhong, Zhaobin Du, Zhi He, Xinyu Chang, Runpeng Weng, Haiia Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: In areas with high load density, short-circuit current overload has always been endangering the safety of the power grid. Although the short-circuit current provided by VSCHVDC is more controllable than that of synchronous power sources, it is still necessary to determine the contribution of VSC-HVDC to the total short-circuit current in multi power sources grid with different control objectives and its potential for limiting short-circuit current through control strategies. This paper conducts a comprehensive analysis of the short-circuit current characteristics of VSC-HVDC during AC-side faults by constructing an electromagnetic transient model of VSC-HVDC and considering various influencing factors and their relationship with system transient voltage. Moreover, a riskbenefit analysis framework on the control strategies of VSCHVDC for limiting short-circuit current in the receiving-end power grid is put forward considering voltage supporting needs. Thus, a reasonable decision strategy could be made based on the balance between voltage and short-circuit current control during transient.

title: Scenario Generation for Wind and Solar Power Output Based on an Improved Conditional Diffusion Model
authors: Songyan Lan, Zhewen Niu, Chaohui Wu, Xiaoqing Han, Xiaorui Cui
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Under the background of continuous increase in the proportion of new energy power generation, high-accuracy generation of wind-solar power scenarios is of critical importance for day-ahead scheduling decisions in power systems. This paper proposes a wind-solar power scenario generation method based on a Conditional Improved Diffusion Model (CIDM). Firstly, the method integrates the theoretical frameworks of Markov chains and variational inference. Historical data are progressively diffused into Gaussian noise and then reconstructed through a reverse denoising process, in which conditional information is incorporated to ensure that the generated scenarios are consistent with the target distribution. Secondly, a cosine noise scheduling strategy is adopted to alleviate premature feature loss, and a conditional denoising network with multi-scale temporal features is developed to enhance temporal modeling. Finally, experiments based on actual wind and solar power data demonstrate that the method not only effectively generates scenarios with strong physical consistency but also achieves superior performance over baseline models in key metrics such as CWC and MMD, providing a highly credible data foundation for power system optimization and scheduling.

title: Analysis of Electrostatic Induction Power Harvesting from Ground Wires of Overhead Transmission Lines and Selection of Device Parameters
authors: Peng Yang, Kepeng Chen, An Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: In order to provide stable power for online monitoring equipment on transmission lines, this paper investigates an electrostatic induction-based ground wire energy harvesting method. By establishing an equivalent circuit model accounting for conductor and ground wire sag, the open-circuit voltage and equivalent capacitance at the harvesting port were accurately calculated. Their variation patterns under different line structures were analyzed, revealing parameter centralization at the same voltage level. Based on these findings, this paper proposes a power harvesting device design utilizing transformers for impedance matching. Simulation verification demonstrates that this approach can achieve stable power output of several hundred watts, confirming the feasibility of designing a universal power harvesting device for the same voltage level.

title: A Hierarchical Predictive Control Framework for Operationally-Feasible Dispatch of Uncertain Microgrids
authors: Xu Lin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: To address operational challenges in microgrids with high renewable energy integration, this study proposes a multi-timescale dispatch framework combining day-ahead robust optimisation with intraday model predictive control(MPC). At the day-ahead stage, scenario-based robust optimisation formulates unit start-stop schedules that balance economic efficiency with resilience against risks. During the intraday phase, rolling optimisation via MPC dynamically adjusts dispatch plans in real time. Case study results demonstrate that this approach effectively mitigates generation and load uncertainties at an acceptable ̔robustness cost̓, significantly enhancing the system's operational safety margin.

title: Pitch Optimization Analysis of Bench Dragon Based on Bisection Method
authors: Meifeng Chen, Siyan Jin, Ye Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: The pitch of the bench dragon governs its progression along the helical path. A smaller pitch precipitates earlier collision between the head and the body, thereby reducing the radial distance from the collision point to the boundary of the turning envelope. Since the collision point is constrained to the outer edge of the front handle and must lie within the turning envelope, we first define the helix equation as a function of pitch and identify the coordinates of potential collision points. The actual coordinates of the collision point are then calculated to determine its polar radius. Finally, a pitch optimization model is formulated with the objective of minimizing the radial distance between the collision point and the boundary of the turning envelope, subject to the constraint that the polar radius of the collision point is less than the radius of the turning envelope itself. Using the bisection method, the minimum permissible pitch is determined to be 0.4917m.

title: Models Multimodal Emotion Recognition Using Audio–Visual Fusion with Emotion Shift and Conflict Detection
authors: Udhaya P, Vigneshwaran S, Viknesh S, G. Saravanan
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: A deep learning framework is introduced for multimodal emotion analysis that synergizes audio and visual information, augmented by two novel modules: emotion shift detection and modality conflict resolution. The proposed method adapts dynamically to temporal emotion changes and reconciles contradictory cues across modalities—for example, when facial expression and vocal tone diverge. Evaluated on IEMOCAP and CMU MOSEI, the model achieves strong performance (~ 88% Weighted Accuracy, ~75% F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf>-score), surpassing conventional cross-modal fusion techniques. Key contributions: (i) a dedicated emotion-shift detector, (ii) a conflict mitigation component, (iii) an attention-guided adaptive fusion strategy.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning and SHAP Approach for Explainable Anxiety Profiling and Risk Assessment
authors: Tofayel Ahmed, Rehana Parvin, Rafayet Rahman Ridoy, Tahira Mahbub
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Anxiety significantly impacts global mental health, highlighting the need for early detection and risk prediction. This study explores the application of machine learning (ML) in identifying anxiety and predicting its risks, utilizing supervised models such as Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), and Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), alongside unsupervised K-means clustering. Analysis of the Social Anxiety Dataset reveals three distinct anxiety subtypes, enabling more personalized risk estimation. Among the models, XGBoost consistently outperforms others in both regression and classification tasks. To enhance model interpretability, SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) are employed, highlighting key risk factors such as caffeine intake, stress exposure, sleep duration, and therapy session frequency. These findings demonstrate that ML can serve as a robust tool for early detection and personalized intervention in anxiety management, offering interpretable and actionable insights to support mental health research and practice.

title: Redefining Processing Power: The Shift to Neuromorphic Computing in Artificial Intelligence
authors: Hemalatha Raavi, Doondi Kumar Janapala
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: In today’s fast-paced digital world, the pursuit of advanced artificial intelligence is more critical than ever. This proposed research delves into neuromorphic computing, a transformative approach that mimics the human brain’s neural structure, offering a compelling alternative to traditional hardware. Conventional processors, like graphical processing units, often struggle with the escalating demands of Artificial Intelligence, facing inefficiencies and speed limitations that hinder progress. Neuromorphic architectures provide the opportunity for rapid parallel processing and significantly reduced energy consumption, paving the way for enhanced AI capabilities.Current methodologies predominantly rely on traditional computing, which proves inadequate for addressing the complexities of modern intelligent systems. This study demonstrates that neuromorphic computing not only overcomes these challenges but also introduces innovative features and improved adaptability. The implications are vast, with potential applications spanning robotics, natural language processing, and real-time analytics. By harnessing neuromorphic technology, stakeholders—including researchers and industry leaders—can unlock new tools that facilitate faster and more efficient AI solutions. This research underscores the importance of exploring neuromorphic computing to elevate the future of artificial intelligence beyond the limits of conventional hardware.

title: Deep Learning based Horticulture Crop Area Estimation Technique Using Satellite Imagery Datasets
authors: Sowmya Sundari L K, Mallikarjun M Kodabagi
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Horticulture supports food production, environmental conservation, and quality of life, making accurate crop estimates essential for effective export planning. This study proposes Area-Net, a deep learning model for estimating horticultural crop areas using satellite imagery. Input images are pre-processed with a dynamic histogram equalization and bright contrast (DEBC) filter to enhance quality. Segmentation is performed in two stages: farmland detection using bounding boxes and crop-specific segmentation for mango, coconut, and areca using Attention ResX V-Net. The model significantly improves Dice and Jaccard indices over SegNet, U-Net, and V-Net. It also achieves higher overall accuracy than RCF, CNN, and VGG-16 models. These precise estimates support better agricultural planning and resource management.

title: A novel Soil Moisture Retrieval Method Using the First Stokes Parameter: A Case Study with SMOS data
authors: Zhuoying Liang, Quan Chen, Binqi Guo, Jiangyuan Zeng, Tianjie Zhao
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Soil moisture is a crucial variable in the climate system. This letter undertakes the retrieval of soil moisture using data from SMOS, which is equipped with an L-band microwave synthetic aperture radiometer. The multi-angle brightness temperature (TB) of the synthetic aperture radiometer is affected, to varying degrees, by factors such as the aliasing effects, ionospheric disturbance, and radio frequency interference. This, in turn, impacts the subsequent inversion of land surface parameters like soil moisture. Departing from conventional approaches that rely on H- and V-pol ground-frame TB, we propose an effective alternative approach that directly utilizes the arithmetic mean of X- and Y-pol antenna-frame TB, corresponding to the first Stokes parameter, for soil moisture inversion. This strategy mitigates errors associated with ionospheric variability and geometric mis-registration, thereby improving the robustness of the soil moisture retrieval. Validation against in situ soil moisture observations from 97 ISMN stations demonstrates that the antenna-frame first Stokes parameter–based approach yields consistently improved performance compared with conventional soil moisture retrievals based on single- and dual-polarization H- and V-pol TBs. These improvements demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method for generating upcoming Chinese HY-4A soil moisture product and its associated applications.

title: Complex Electromagnetic Waveform Generation in Time-Domain
authors: Amedeo Capozzoli, Eros Ciccarelli, Claudio Curcio, Angelo Liseno
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: The generation of complex wavefronts in the Near-Field (NF) of a source is relevant whenever the generation of simple plane waves is not sufficient or does not match the application task. In the testing of wireless power transfer and electromagnetic imaging, the aim is to simulate specific devices or to generate specific illuminating patterns. In radar automotive applications, the interest is to design surrogates of potential targets as pedestrians, bycicles or cars. Here, an approach for the generation of complex waveforms in time-domain is introduced. The approach exploits mild a priori information on the geometry of the problem by aid of the Truncated Singular Value Decomposition approach and on the time-duration and essential band-limitation of the time-varying waveforms by use of Prolate Spheroidal Wave Functions representations. Numerical and experimental results for a two-dimensional geometry are presented. The reached accuracy is well below the tolerances typically considered for the applications of interest. In particular, for the experimental test, amplitude errors against the reference field have been confined to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm 0.09$</tex-math></inline-formula> dB, while phase errors to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm 1.28^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Furthermore, the Percentage Root Mean Square Error has been 0.08%.
