title: A Fast-Response Flexible Power Point Tracking Algorithm in a Single-Stage Photovoltaic System During Asymmetrical Voltage Drops
authors: Sepideh Shabani, Mehdi Gholipour, Mehdi Niroomand
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The grid-connected photovoltaic (GCPV) systems are required to comply with specific grid code standards, including low voltage ride through (LVRT). During voltage drops, particularly asymmetrical ones, GCPV systems encounter various issues that result in improper functioning of the GCPV systems, which needs to be resolved within a control scheme. The GCPV systems must exhibit rapid response during LVRT. Otherwise, they cannot provide grid support in a timely manner and in accordance with the grid code requirements. The DC-link voltage control can significantly affect the control dynamic response. In this paper, a control scheme is proposed to support grid voltage for single-stage GCPV systems during asymmetrical voltage drops. This control scheme addresses various control objectives within the framework of two optimization problems. As the second contribution, a flexible power point tracking (FPPT) algorithm is introduced to improve the dynamic response of the DC-link voltage control. The effectiveness of the proposed strategy is validated through testing on a real-time controller hardware-in-the-loop experimental platform.

title: Development of a Global Climate Model for Atmospheric Temperature using Machine Learning
authors: Daniel Okoh, Adero Awuor, George Ochieng, Paul Baki, John Bosco Habarulema, Babatunde Rabiu, Punyawi Jamjareegulgarn, Loretta Onuorah, Jonas Nzeagwu, Aderonke Akerele, Gabriel Ibeh, David Omole, James Ameh
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: This study presents a novel three-dimensional global model of atmospheric temperature developed using Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) trained on radio occultation (RO) data from the COSMIC I and COSMIC II satellite missions. Over 14.7 million quality-controlled profiles were used, providing approximately 9.5 billion data points that capture temperature variability across latitude, longitude, altitude (0-60 km), and time (2006-2025). The global domain was divided into 1,296 spatial grid cells (10° × 5°) to enable localized ANN training and ensure efficient handling of regional atmospheric dynamics. Model performance was evaluated through cross-validation and independent testing against radiosonde measurements from 684 stations worldwide. Results show mean absolute errors (MAE) of 1.5-4.5°C and root-mean-square errors (RMSE) of 2.5-6.5°C, with best performance in the tropical troposphere and increasing errors toward high latitudes. The model successfully reproduces key climatological structures (including the tropospheric lapse rate, stratospheric inversion, and seasonal hemispheric asymmetries), and accurately captures diurnal and annual thermal cycles. Long-term simulations (2006-2025) reveal a distinct tropospheric warming trend (∼ +0.07°C per year at 11 km) and a corresponding stratospheric cooling (∼ -0.03°C per year near 32 km), consistent with established satellite and reanalysis records. These results demonstrate that ANN-based frameworks can effectively model global atmospheric thermal structure and evolution, providing a scalable approach for future climate monitoring and forecasting applications.

title: Adaptive Granules-Based Semi-Supervised Feature Selection for Hybrid Data
authors: Zhenchao Yan, Songlin He, Jianhui Yu, Chase Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: The surge in online interactions and advancement in Big Data related techniques have generated vast amounts of hybrid data in the sense that the data are symbolic, numerical or missing features, and usually only a small number of data objects possess true labels due to high annotation costs. A necessary step of fully releasing the potential of these partially labeled hybrid data lies in feature selection, for which the neighborhood rough set (NRS) is an efficient mathematical method to apply. In NRS, setting proper neighborhood granules greatly influences the effectiveness and robustness of algorithms atop it. However, existing methods usually determine the optimal neighborhood radius of neighborhood granule via computationally intensive grid search, where the neighborhood radius for each object is the same, i.e., “unadaptive”. Some methods investigate adaptive granulation strategies, yet they inevitably hinge on preset parameters or a-prior knowledge. To tackle this problem, we propose an adaptive granules-enabled semi-supervised feature selection method that can adaptively generate suitable neighborhood radii for both labeled and unlabeled objects. The core idea lies in using the purity of decision labels as the threshold for granularity maximization construction. Then, by combining with neighborhood entropy and local density, a feature metric is designed to measure the feature significance. A semi-supervised feature selection algorithm is utilized to select feature subset by using the information from both labeled and unlabeled objects. Instead of hinging on expert knowledge, the proposed method only rely on the data per se. Experimental results on real-world datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the designed method and its superiority over other state-of-the-art.

title: Toward Robust UAV Swarm Coordination in Low-Altitude Networks: Online Learning Control under System and Channel Uncertainty
authors: Minjie Tang, Chenyuan Feng, Geyong Min, Kun Yang, Yongming Huang, Tony Q. S. Quek
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Robust tracking control of UAV swarms has attracted significant attention, yet most existing works assume reliable leader–follower communication and accurate system models. In practice, uncertain dynamics and unreliable wireless links can severely degrade swarm coordination. In this paper, we develop an online robust tracking control framework for UAV swarms operating over unreliable multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) channels. We first establish a dynamic model that captures coupled uncertainties arising from inter-UAV coordination and communication imperfections. Building on this model, we formulate the tracking problem and cast it as an ergodic optimal control problem for an auxiliary system that unifies system and channel variations. To address the resulting computational challenges, we derive an equivalent lower-dimensional representation that significantly reduces complexity. Feasibility and stability under this representation are guaranteed via controllability cone decomposition, contractive system analysis, and Lyapunov drift arguments. Based on the reduced-dimensional robust control solution, we further propose an online stochastic-approximation-based learning algorithm, whose almost sure convergence is established using the ordinary differential equation (ODE) method. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves improved tracking stability, faster convergence, and higher computational efficiency than benchmark schemes, particularly under stochastic channel conditions.

title: Homotopy-Guided Potential Games for Congestion-Aware Navigation_supp2-3675907.mp4
authors: Mohammed Irshadh Ismaaeel Sathyamangalam Imran
publish: N/A
abstract: We address the multi-agent motion planning problem where interactions, collisions, and congestion co-exist. Conventional game-theoretic planners capture interactions among agents but often converge to conservative, congested equilibria. Homotopy planners, on the other hand, can explore topologically distinct paths, but lack mechanisms to account for the interdependence of agents' future actions. We propose a unified framework that leverages homotopy classes as structured strategy sets within a receding-horizon setup. At each planning stage, a deterministic homotopy planner generates topologically distinct paths for each agent, conditioned on the joint configuration. To avoid intractable growth of candidate paths, we propose a simple heuristic filtering step that selects a top-K subset of the most suitable congestion-free joint strategies to ensure computational tractability. These serve as initializations for a potential game that enforces homotopy-consistent constraints and yields a generalized open-loop Nash equilibrium (OLNE), with penalties discouraging abrupt strategy shifts in a receding-horizon setting. Simulations with three agents demonstrate improved efficiency (faster completion) and enhanced safety (greater inter-agent clearance, leading to reduced congestion) compared to a local baseline and NH ORCA that do not reason about homotopies. Hardware trials with two robots and one human demonstrate robustness to irrational behaviors, where our method adapts by switching to alternative feasible equilibria while the baseline game fails.

title: The Intrinsic Shielding Effectiveness Testing Method for Small-size Samples Regardless of Shape
authors: Yanghui Wu, Yuyan Zhao, Fanghui Sang, Zhuoyan Li, Wenhua Gu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The intrinsic shielding effectiveness (SE) of materials is a critical metric for evaluating their electromagnetic protection capability. Currently, the most concerned frequency band is (1-18) GHz, and the shielding box method is the most intuitive and convenient testing method. However, existing SE measurement standards impose stringent requirements on sample geometry and dimensions, typically mandating square-shaped samples ≥ 300 mm × 300 mm. This constraint primarily aims to mitigate measurement errors induced by resonance effects from small apertures. In practice, limitations including application scenarios, fabrication processes and cost often prevent samples from meeting such stringent requirements. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a resonance-cancellation method for small samples with arbitrary shapes, which is based on an equivalent source construction model derived from the Huygens-Fresnel principle. It is a pure analytical correction method and does not request any additional measurement procedure. Experiment results complied with simulation pretty well, and showed a significantly reduction of the SE error from ±10 dB to ±2.5 dB.

title: IES ONCON 2025 [News in Industry Activities]
authors: Victor Huang
publish: IEEE Industrial Electronics Magazine
abstract: Provides society information that may include news, reviews or technical notes that should be of interest to practitioners and researchers.

title: DenseNet-121 Based Deep Learning Framework for Multiclass Skin Lesion Classification Using Dermoscopic Images
authors: Raghavendra Sridhar, Rashi Nimesh Kumar Dhenia, Ishva Jitendrakumar Kanani, Aditya Kumar
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: There has been an increase in the incidence of skin cancer all over the world, and it is considered a public health problem. It is essential to ensure that the diagnosis of skin lesions is made as soon and as accurately as possible in order to administer timely and effective treatment and improve the prognosis of the patient. Weighted Transfer learning using Deep Neural Networks for Skin Lesion Classification. Aims to build a high-performing classification model that can help dermatologists accurately identify skin lesions. DenseNet-121 is used because of its high accuracy compared to ResNet, VGG, DenseNet, and Inception, in the case of a large number of pre-trained convolutional neural networks (CNNs). Transfer learning methods are employed to retrain these networks, where the networks are fine-tuned using a large skin lesion image dataset, primarily the HAM10000 dataset, which contains all the different types of skin lesions. The results of the proposed approach are positive, and the validation accuracy is 98.85%. The results indicate that transfer learning is successful for skin lesion classification and is a useful tool to assist dermatologists in the diagnosis of a variety of skin conditions with high accuracy and efficiency. This research work will be further developed by the computer-aided diagnosis system and is anticipated to contribute to the improvement of patient care via the early and correct recognition of skin lesions.

title: Deep Q-Learning-Based Robotic Weed Detection and Removal in Precision Crop Management
authors: B. Shadaksharappa, N. Gurumurthy, R. Meenakshi, D. P. Sangeetha, M. Muthulekshmi, Ezhirkadhir Diwagaran R
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: This research examines the use of Deep Q-Learning (DQL) in robotic systems to identify and remove weeds in precision crop management. Conventional weed management techniques often exhibit inefficiency and environmental detriment, depending on physical labor or excessive chemical use. The proposed robotic system employs computer vision and reinforcement learning to automatically detect and categorize weeds while reducing agricultural damage. The system uses DQL to facilitate the robot's acquisition of optimum weed removal operations via environmental interactions, enhancing its performance over time. Experimental findings indicate the system's efficacy, attaining 97% accuracy in weed identification, a 75% decrease in herbicide use, and a 30% enhancement in weed removal efficiency. The findings demonstrate that the DQL-based robotic system improves the accuracy of weed control and promotes more sustainable agriculture practices. This strategy offers a viable alternative for sustainable and intelligent crop management by decreasing labor expenses, limiting pesticide use, and enhancing operational efficiency. Incorporating DQL-based robots into agricultural systems might transform weed management, providing a more efficient and eco-friendlier substitute for conventional techniques.

title: Techno-Ecologies of Migration: Integrating Blockchain and AI into Postcolonial Environmental Fiction Research
authors: Sushma Jaiswal, Rati Sood
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: This paper examines how postcolonial environmental fiction can be studied through the combined use of artificial intelligence and blockchain technologies. Migration in the context of ecological stress, rising seas, and depleted resources is central to many postcolonial societies whose histories of colonization and extraction continue to shape vulnerability. The selected fiction demonstrates how displacement is tied to environmental change, political disruption, and historical injustice. The study proposes that AI methods, such as text mining, sentiment analysis, and narrative mapping, can assist scholars in identifying recurring themes, symbolic structures, and affective dimensions within these narratives. At the same time, blockchain is considered as a tool for securing cultural archives, ensuring data permanence, and addressing issues of authorship and authenticity. Together, these technologies are framed as part of a "techno-ecology of migration," where digital infrastructures intersect with literary representations of mobility and environmental change. By analyzing a literary corpus from South Asia, Africa, and the Caribbean, this paper highlights both the possibilities and the limits of applying digital methods to postcolonial studies. It argues that while AI and blockchain provide new approaches to research and preservation, they must be engaged critically, with attention to bias, historical inequality, and the ecological footprint of technology itself.

title: Thermal Battery Modeling of Electrolytic Aluminum Loads for Demand Response
authors: Fengzhe Dai, Jing Wang, Zhaoxi Liu, Huan Quan, Wenhao Wang, Shaofei Wang, Shute Li, Liang Cheng
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Thermal-storage energy-intensive loads, such as electrolytic aluminum loads (EALs), constitute high-quality flexible resources for power system regulation. However, concerns regarding industrial privacy and operational safety make direct control of industrial users challenging to implement, thereby constraining the utilization of their flexibility potential. To overcome this limitation, an equivalent thermal battery model of EAL that explicitly incorporates thermal inertia is proposed. First, a thermodynamic model is established to capture the transient heat transfer dynamics within aluminum electrolytic cells. Based on the normal operating temperature range of the cells and external ambient temperature conditions, the baseline power consumption profile is characterized. Second, the energy balance relationships among key processes-including electric heating, raw material preheating, electro-thermal energy conversion, and heat dissipation through the cell shell-are formulated. By exploiting the inherent thermal inertia of normally operating electrolytic cells, the functional relationship between heating power and internal temperature is derived. Subsequently, an equivalent thermal battery model is developed to represent the external regulation characteristics of EAL, thereby preserving user privacy while reducing the complexity of EAL regulation. Case studies are conducted to verify the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed modeling framework.

title: Frequency-Domain Dielectric Characteristics of Oil-Paper Insulation under Different Types and Moisture Conditions
authors: Rongfu Zhong, Wanwei Wang, Jiaju Lin, Qiye Du, Congcong Liu, Jiahui Yang, Junjie Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: This study examines the effects of combining three types of insulating paper with two types of insulating oil on the dielectric spectra of oil-paper composites, with a particular emphasis on the influence of moisture absorption on the dielectric proper-ties of paper. Dielectric spectroscopy measurements were performed on samples with varying moisture contents, providing detailed frequency-dependent data on the real and imaginary components of complex capacitance as well as dielectric loss. The results demonstrate that moisture significantly affects the polarization behavior and dielectric loss of insulating paper, particularly in the low-frequency region, where both the real and imaginary parts of complex capacitance exhibit pronounced increases. Furthermore, the interaction between vegetable oil and paper is notably stronger than that of mineral oil, with veg-etable oil effectively enhancing the complex capacitance of paper, whereas the influence of mineral oil is relatively limited. These findings offer valuable theoretical insights for improving the dielectric performance of insulating materials and for op-timizing insulation systems in electrical equipment.

title: Digital Twin Platform for Ship Energy Efficiency and Environmental Monitoring Based on Internet of Things Technology
authors: Yongcheng Fan, Hongna Li, Chunhui Ma, Xu Zhen, Fanhong Meng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: This paper aims to introduce the design and implementation of intelligent Internet of Things system, including sensor selection and layout, MCU and core controller selection, communication module integration scheme, software development and system implementation and testing. In terms of sensor selection and layout, a variety of sensor types such as 18B20, DHT11, light sensor are combined to meet the monitoring needs of the system, and a reasonable sensor layout scheme is proposed. Different application scenarios and characteristics, such as STC89C52 and ESP32, are considered in the selection of microcontroller and core controller. The communication module integration scheme covers a variety of communication modes, and the appropriate communication module is selected according to the actual needs. In terms of software development, it selects the appropriate programming language and development environment, such as C/C++, JavaScript, wechat small program development tools and Node-RED, and describes the content of data collection, user interaction design and interface optimization. The system implementation and test part introduces hardware construction and connection, software development and debugging, system test scheme and result analysis, covering hardware test, software test and integration test.

title: User Response Pattern Mining and Short-Term Load Forecasting Based on the Price Elasticity of Demand
authors: Xuntao Shi, Jian Sun, Xiaobing Xiao, Yiyong Lei, Hao Yang, Miaomiao Zhou, Hongxin Lu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: In the context of energy transition, widespread electricity price incentives have intensified load fluctuations, demanding higher accuracy in short-term load forecasting. Existing methods often ignore heterogeneous user responses to price changes, limiting model adaptability. This paper proposes a short-term load forecasting method that integrates price elasticity coefficients to mine user response patterns. Our contributions are threefold: (1) clustering users into distinct groups via K-medoids based on historical load profiles; (2) quantifying the price sensitivity of each group using elasticity coefficients to reveal response patterns; (3) incorporating these coefficients into a Radial Basis Function Neural Network (RBFNN) and tailoring inputs by user group to enhance prediction accuracy and generalization. Experiments confirm that our approach effectively captures differentiated price responses and significantly improves forecasting performance, supporting demand-side management and resource optimization in power systems.

title: A Hybrid Wind Power Forecasting System Integrating Dynamic Model Selection and Data Augmentation
authors: Can Wang, Di Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Accurate wind power forecasting (WPF) is critical for integrating renewable energy into power grids; yet challenges persist due to the stochastic nature of weather variability. In recent years, machine learning methods have shown superior performance in WPF. This paper proposes a novel hybrid forecasting system that combines dynamic model selection (Wind-DS) with data augmentation techniques to enhance forecasting accuracy. The Wind-DS framework dynamically selects optimal models from a pool of candidates based on similarity metrics and competence regions, while leveraging three categories of data augmentation—time-domain, frequency-domain, and large language model (LLM)-based methods. These methods are systematically evaluated to demonstrate their effectiveness and robustness. Experimental results on multiple wind power datasets demonstrate that the proposed system achieves superior performance, with time-domain augmentation (e.g., noise injection) yielding the most significant improvements. The hybrid approach reduces reliance on costly energy storage systems and addresses the limitations of single-model forecasting, offering a scalable solution to grid stability.

title: Deep Learning-Based Convolutional Neural Network Approach for Smart Automated Classification and Sorting of Vegetables
authors: Shikha Khullar, Deepak Kumar, Perla Ramesh Babu, Sheri Ramchandra Reddy, Rajeswari Periyasamy, Smitha Sasi
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The classification of vegetables plays a critical role in modern agriculture, food supply chains, and smart farming practices. Accurately identification of plants like vegetables apart is key for automating the classification, ensuring safe food and helping the environment. This project introduces a CNN type of model based on deep learning to aid in correctly classifying Bean, Bitter Guard, Bottle Guard, Brinjal, Broccoli, Cabbage, Capsicum, Carrot, Cauliflower and Cucumber. Twelve thousand images, each representing one class, were collected from Kaggle and organized into training, validation and testing subsets containing 70%, 20% and 10% of images, respectively. To make the CNN perform and generalize better, both dropout layers and max-pooling were used to improve the underlying structure. High accuracy of 99% was found in the experimental results and the precision, recall and F1-scores for all classes were consistently above 0.98. Loss and accuracy plots indicated that my model converged smoothly without strongly overfitting. Looking at the confusion matrix, we observed that the model performed well, because the main spots on the matrix were black and other spots were small and scattered. It backs the objectives of the Sustainable Development Goals by supporting food security, good health, advancements in industry, responsible purchases, infrastructure and actions against climate change through enhanced food processing and supply methods. This research confirms that the model can be used in automated vegetable sorting, development of agricultural technologies and smart food systems, boosting precision agriculture and digital security of food.

title: AIoT Enabled Vehicle Classification Using Ensemble, Deep Learning, and Transformer Models
authors: Jakki Rajasekhar Ramana, Varun S Kumar, Sai Charan Das, Gowrishanker Manoj, Sruthy Anand
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Object detection and classification were the major breakthroughs in Artificial Intelligence, enabling automated recognition of objects and humans for applications such as surveillance and intelligent transportation systems. In this paper, we present a comparative study of multiple machine learning and deep learning models to develop an intelligent vehicle classification system for electric vehicle (EV) charging stations. The goal is to automate vehicle classification for improved resource utilization and reduced waiting times. We evaluated ensemble models, YOLOv11m, RetinaNet, Faster R-CNN, and a Vision Transformer (ViT) based model. Experimental results demonstrate that the Vision Transformer consistently outperforms the other approaches, achieving the highest classification accuracy across various test scenarios. Beyond EV scheduling, the proposed approach highlights potential applications in enhancing urban safety, intelligent transportation, and operational efficiency in smart city infrastructures.

title: Towards Sustainable Agriculture: Deep Learning-Based Potato Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Mandeep Kaur, Tapan Kant, Sahil Rampal, Pooja Chopra, Abhishek Rana, Mukesh Kumar
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Early and accurate detection of plant diseases is vital in protecting global food security and improving agricultural productivity. Potatoes are widely grown crops worldwide and highly vulnerable to various plant-diseases which threaten production levels and quality significantly. This paper introduces an automated deep learning system for potato leaf disease classification using EfficientNetB0 model, using powerful yet efficient deep neural networks for classification. This model was trained and evaluated using 5,236 high-resolution images obtained from Kaggle that had been classified into seven classes: Bacteria, Fungi, Healthy, Nematode Pest Pest Phytophthora and Virus. EfficientNetB0 architecture was trained on ImageNet before further enhancing it with a custom classification head to quickly learn complex visual patterns associated with various diseases; providing farmers and agronomists an accessible, quick, accurate means for real-time disease identification.

title: LoRaPathwayMapper: An Optimization Model for Long-Range Communication in IoT Networks
authors: S.Thangamayan, K. Naveen Kumar, Aswath Kulasekaran, Vigneshwar M, S. Saran Raj, C.K.Gurudhayal
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The fast growth in IoT has driven up the need for protocols that can communicate across long distances using low power and still work well wherever needed. LoRaPathwayMapper is the title of an intelligent framework that aims to boost long-range communication in IoT networks with LoRa technology. It combines multi-objective optimization, adaptive parameter changes and dynamic routing to support high communication in agriculture, urban monitoring and industrial Internet of Things (IoT) settings. The mapping engine in LoRaPathwayMapper adjusts the Spreading Factor (SF), Transmission Power (TP) and Channel Frequency (CF) each time considering how the channel conditions, energy use and interference change. Routing is done using reinforcement learning so that the best and most stable routes can be found to the gateways. The evaluation of the model included using a variety of realistic 2D/3D network layouts and different environment settings during many simulations. LoRaPathwayMapper was able to achieve a 97.8% Packet Delivery Ratio, provided better energy performance than the baseline (13.5 mJ per node), increased network lifetime to 124.8 hours and significantly lowered the number of collision events to only 3.1 per 1000 packets. Besides, its delay is just 145ms which allows smooth operation for real-time applications. The results support that LoRaPathwayMapper is capable of giving next-generation IoT systems a dependable, scalable and flexible connection network.

title: Vehicle Breakdown Prediction: An ML-Based System for Predicting Breakdown Using Ensemble Learning Technique
authors: Amit Gupta, Gaurav Saklani, Richa Gupta
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Some sudden breakdowns in vehicle can be result in possibility of accidents, notable disturbance, leading to highly cost repairs, and compromise with protection. Necessitating the evolution of a quick and trusty prediction system. Machine learning has come into view as influential tool for predicting such breakdowns, allowing for proactive maintenance activities. The paper presents the study of a prediction system that desired to identify, forecast and relieve these breakdowns, thereby increasing safety, reducing repair costs and chance of accidents. In this paper, we made an ensemble learning approach using Voting classifier that uses three algorithms to predict the breakdown condition of vehicle based on the engine sensor data. The receiver operating characteristic curve achieved an AUC score of 0.98, which showcase the model’s vigorous ability in differentiating between non breakdown and breakdown cases.

title: Generating Multi-Modal Knowledge Clues as an Image: Towards Improving Image-Sequence Reasoning with Assisted Visual Input
authors: Guanghui Ye, Huan Zhao, Yixian Shen, Jiaqi Li, Fengnan Li, Zhihua Jiang, Keqin Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Recent multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) have exhibited powerful abilities in addressing complex vision-language tasks such as image-sequence reasoning (ISR). However, significant challenges remain, e.g., it is still difficult for the MLLMs to fully capture and represent cross-image visual knowledge such as scene relations, attributes, and entity links between multiple images, which hinders them from better solving ISR. To alleviate these issues, we introduce a novel concept Visualized Knowledge Clue (VizKC) - synthetic images that encode key visual and external knowledge from a sequence of input images and are then used alongside the original input images within a multi-image MLLM to enhance reasoning performance. Accordingly, we propose an accompanying approach named VizKC-ISR, composed of two modules - VizKC generation and VizKC utilization. Specifically, in the generation module, VizKC-ISR follows a See-Find-Fuse pipeline: (i) “See - Scene Perception”, to construct an initial VizKC that incorporates scene relations of key visual entities detected from an original image; (ii) “Find - Knowledge Generation”, to generate enriched image captions with real-world knowledge and fine-grained entity details and then extract structured knowledge tuples from generated captions; (iii) “Fuse - Image Editing”, to introduce relevant knowledge tuples into the VizKC via iterative image editing. In the utilization module, we employ a multi-image MLLM (e.g., mPLUG-Owl3) to solve the VizKC-assisted ISR tasks by reasoning with generated knowledge clues. We evaluate VizKC-ISR on nine ISR benchmarks categorized into three multi-image scenarios. The results show that our VizKC-ISR performs best in all tasks, e.g., obtaining the highest average accuracy of 63.1% and surpassing the mPLUG-Owl3 baseline by 6.4 absolute points, due to the bridge between visually-grounded reasoning and multi-modal knowledge challenges.

title: A Rod-Driven Minimally Invasive Bioprinting Continuum Robotic System for Repairing Knee Joint Cartilage Defects_supp2-3670164.mp4
authors: Qian Tang
publish: N/A
abstract: Robot-assisted minimally invasive bioprinting for knee cartilage repair remains a significant clinical challenge. Ensuring bioprinting quality requires the robotic tip to stay perpendicular to the working surface. Rigid robots are limited in controlling tip orientation in the joint cavity, whereas continuum robots, with enhanced flexibility, provide promising solutions. In this article, a minimally invasive bioprinting continuum robot (BCR) system (BCRS) is proposed. First, a novel four-rod-driven double-layer rigid–flexible-coupled BCR is designed and fabricated, featuring enhanced dexterity to accommodate the spatial characteristics of the knee joint cavity. Second, a Lagrangian-based kinetostatic model of the BCR is derived using the geometrically nonlinear Euler–Bernoulli beam theory. Based on this model, an Eulerian analytical moment–equilibrium formulation is established to verify structural safety, and an open-loop feedforward controller is developed to precisely regulate the BCR tip pose. Finally, path-tracking and bioprinting experiments are performed, with results showing a root-mean-square error of 1.89 mm in position and 2.50<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula> in orientation. Minimally invasive perpendicular bioprinting within the knee joint cavity is achieved using the proposed BCRS, thereby reducing undesirable molding effects caused by bioink fluidity and providing an effective solution for cartilage repair.

title: GenDist: Generative-Driven Knowledge Distillation for Small Object Detection in Remote Sensing Images
authors: Zhuangzhuang Zhou, Xuewu Zhao, Xuezhuan Zhao, Yingying Zhu, Zunjin Zhao, Lingling Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Optimal efficiency is always the primary pursuit of the remote sensing community. However, the blurred appearance in small objects causes increasingly deeper and wider detectors, posing a significant challenge for deployment. Knowledge Distillation (KD), which can shorten the time from image acquisition to interpretation, has become a hotspot. In this paper, contrary to traditional feature imitation methods, we propose generative-driven knowledge distillation for small object detection (GenDist), which reconstructs teacher-like representations from the partially masked student features to achieve knowledge transfer. Masking during training strengthens the student’s ability to generalize in complex scenes. Specifically, GenDist contains three parts: Online Self-Correct Mask (O-SCM), Dynamic Activation Global Context (DAGC) and Sampling-Aligned Generation (SAG). First, O-SCM is utilized to identify informative masking regions that guide the student to reconstruct the teacher-like features. By integrating an online self-correction mechanism, it adaptively directs generative supervision toward regions that are ignored or misinterpreted by students. Then, DAGC refines teacher features via global context modeling and critical feature selection, facilitating more accurate object detail capture by the student model. Finally, SAG improves the quality of feature reconstruction by introducing the learnable offset sampling, enabling students to learn irregular areas such as the small instances’ edges and texture transitions. Our proposed method finds the sweet spot between precision and speed in an elegant way. GenDist yields stable performance gains for student models across the DIOR, AI-TOD, SODA-D, and SSDD datasets. For the lightweight detectors, it cuts the model’s parameter count by roughly 38.2%, while keeping accuracy loss under 3.9%.

title: Comprehensive Comparison of Ferrite-Assisted Synchronous Reluctance Motors for Five Rotor Structures
authors: Na Huang, Keda Wang, Wanning Li, Hao Mu, Zhengyu He, Guanghui Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: The ferrite-assisted synchronous reluctance motor (FaSynRM) does not rely on rare earth permanent magnets. With its high efficiency and low cost, it has broad application prospects in the industrial field. However, the motor has a variety of rotor structures, which show different multiphysical field characteristics. Most of the existing research focuses on the electromagnetic characteristics analysis of a specific rotor structure, and lacks comparative research on different rotor structures, especially the analysis of demagnetization characteristics under high overload conditions, resulting in the multiphysical field characteristics of many rotor structures are still unclear. Therefore, this article focuses on the multiphysical field characteristics of different rotors, including antidemagnetization characteristics, electromagnetic properties, loss characteristics and temperature distribution. First, the 75 kW, 1500 rpm FaSynRM motors with four rotor structures are introduced, and the influence of main design parameters on electromagnetic performance is analyzed comprehensively. In addition, this article also carried out multiobjective optimization of these four rotor structures. Then, the motor performance of four rotor structure optimization schemes is compared, including antidemagnetization characteristics, electromagnetic performance, loss and temperature distribution. Finally, in order to verify the correctness of the theoretical analysis, a full-size model was manufactured and tested.

title: MR_Go: A Magnetorheological Quadruped Robot for Energy-Efficient, High-Payload, and Impact-Tolerant Planetary Exploration_supp2-3675964.mp4
authors: Shuaishuai Sun
publish: N/A
abstract: Planetary exploration missions increasingly rely on agile robotic platforms capable of traversing unstructured and unpredictable terrain. While quadruped robots offer superior mobility compared to wheeled rovers, their deployment in long-duration and shock-load missions remains limited, due to the limitations of high power consumption, low payload-to-weight ratio, and poor resilience to impact and overloads with the conventional actuator. To address these challenges, we present the MR_Go, a magnetorheological (MR)-based quadruped robot featuring real-time joint damping modulation to enhance energy efficiency, impact mitigation, and load-bearing capability. Built upon the Unitree Go1 platform, MR_Go achieves a 400% increase in payload-to-mass ratio with only 40 W of MR activation power and can sustain 20 kg payloads without continuous motor engagement. Experiments conducted in lunar-analog terrain demonstrate that MR_Go extends mission battery life by 18%, reduces peak joint torque by 10% during an 80 cm cliff drop, and maintains superior thermal stability under prolonged loading. These results highlight the potential of MR-based hybrid actuation for enabling durable and energy-efficient quadruped systems suited for extended planetary operations.

title: A Human Vision Aided Method for Real-Time Foot Placement Prediction on Rough Terrains_supp1-3675906.mp4
authors: Chenglong Fu
publish: N/A
abstract: Human vision can instantaneously reflect motion intent during the execution of complex tasks. In rough terrain locomotion, intent prediction is achieved by foot placement prediction, which obtains intermediate gait parameters that indirectly reflect upcoming locomotion mode. This paper proposes a human vision aided framework to perform real-time foot placement prediction during dynamic walking on rough terrains, while precisely extracting step length, step height and foothold slope for the next step. In this paper, 3D human gaze is estimated by a depth-inertial eye tracking system. The Gaussian distribution-based gaze attention map is then used to model the temporal and spatial decay of human attention. To precisely measure gait parameters, a robust visual-inertial odometry algorithm is also employed to continuously estimate the camera pose. The real-time performance of the system is validated using data collected from online experiments, where five subjects are required to transverse rough terrains under different experimental setup. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves an average 97.4% intent prediction accuracy at 35% of the swing phase, with mean errors of 6.1 cm, 1.7 cm and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{2.6^\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula> for estimating step length, step height and foothold slope, respectively. These findings indicate that the real-time system have the potential to further improve the stability and safety of the control of walking assistive robots.

title: Separation and Reconstruction Method for Multicomponent Signals with Small Frequency Differences
authors: Lijun Liu, Mingliang Zhang, Hang Xu, Xiaoyi Liu, Zhian Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Modern radar protection systems often deploy decoys that transmit nonstationary waveforms sharing the same modulation scheme as the protected radar while operating at closely spaced carrier frequencies. Such composite observations are inherently challenging for component-wise time-frequency (TF) trajectory measurement and tracking, as the achievable TF resolution is fundamentally limited by finite data length and windowing. This paper proposes NKF-TFTE, a measurement driven framework that establishes a TF-augmented, multi-measurement state-space model for joint estimation and tracking of the instantaneous amplitudes (IAs) and instantaneous frequencies (IFs) of multiple closely spaced components, together with a dedicated reconstruction pipeline. Specifically, NKF-TFTE fuses time-domain in-phase and quadrature samples with a pseudo measurement of the IF slope extracted from the composite TF ridge, and exploits the shared modulation slope among components to improve identifiability under unresolved TF signatures. Based on the estimated trajectories, the reconstruction stage performs spline interpolated phase integration followed by coordinate descent based initial phase refinement, enabling high fidelity time-domain reconstruction with controlled phase-accumulation error. We further analyze the convergence behavior and estimation accuracy of NKF-TFTE, and derive its dominant per-recursion computational complexity. Simulations demonstrate that NKF-TFTE achieves improved trajectory resolution and reconstruction fidelity over representative baselines, while maintaining robust performance under measurement noise, modulation-slope mismatch, and non-Gaussian disturbances.

title: ADSIM: Novel Adaptive Deep-Stacked Intelligent Metasurfaces for AAV-Assisted Covert Communications
authors: Po-Chen Wu, Kai-Ten Feng, Zhi Ding, Jen-Ming Wu
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: This paper introduces a new class of multilayer metasurface, termed the adaptive deep-stacked intelligent meta-surface (ADSIM), and applies it to autonomous aerial vehicle (AAV)–assisted covert communication. Unlike existing reconfigurable surfaces , ADSIM explicitly captures recursive inter-layer reflection–transmission interactions, enabling unprecedented passive beamforming precision. Leveraging this capability, we formulate a joint optimization of BS beamforming, ADSIM configuration, and AAV three-dimensional (3D) positioning to maximize system energy efficiency (EE) under stringent covertness constraints. The resulting problem is highly non-convex due to multilayer wave-propagation coupling and discrete ADSIM control. To address this challenge, we propose a cross-entropy–assisted alternating optimization (CEAO) framework, featuring a cross-entropy optimization (CEO)-empowered alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM) module that efficiently handles the ADSIM configuration subproblem while strictly enforcing feasibility. Simulation results demonstrate the substantial gains achieved by ADSIM, yielding up to 189% EE improvement compared with systems without ADSIM configuration. Moreover, CEAO outperforms representative benchmarks, including naive-null artificial noise (AN), drop-rank, Q-learning, and heuristic schemes, by up to 107.3%, confirming its superiority and robustness for next-generation covert communication.

title: Energy Management and Optimal Operation of Light Rail Trains on Jerusalem's Proposed Green Line
authors: Maoz Eitan, Sebastián de la Torre
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: This work presents an integrated optimization framework for the operation of urban light rail systems, incorporating detailed traction dynamics, energy exchanges, battery behavior, and realistic infrastructure constraints into a unified mathematical model. Notably, the model accounts for the energy recovered through regenerative braking, significantly reducing operating costs. The formulation results in a non-linear problem solved efficiently with commercial solvers, and its flexible structure allows adaptation to different railway technologies, grid configurations, and operating strategies. The model was applied to the Jerusalem Light Rail Green Line using detailed topographic data and over 2,000 track segments covering a 32.7 km round trip, enabling full-cycle energy and operational assessment under realistic service conditions. Sixteen realistic operational scenarios were simulated, featuring cost or time minimization, onboard energy storage system (ESS) availability, regenerative braking, and different maximum speeds. Additionally, sensitivity analyses were carried out to quantify the influence of key parameters on operational performance and costs. Results show that time minimization produces small travel time reductions, while considerably increasing energy cost. In contrast, cost minimization yields a more balanced and sustainable trade-off between performance and efficiency. The best configuration corresponds to cost minimization with both regenerative braking and ESS. Detailed analysis reveals how the ESS smooths power fluctuations, enhances energy recovery, and limits purchases from the grid. Finally, the model was extended to full-day operation under realistic timetables with 5-minute frequencies; in this configuration, daily operating costs reach €8,273.52, with €270.14 saved thanks to regenerative braking. These results demonstrate the practical applicability of the framework and offer actionable insights and practical guidance for railway operators.

title: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)

title: Ethical AI Framework for Autonomous Decision Systems
authors: Pranay Chandur, Ranadheer Kovvuri, Srinivasarao Medam, Anusha Nerella, Rahul P Mahajan
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The proliferation of autonomous decision systems in areas of healthcare, finance, defence, and transportation is deeply ethically problematic in the areas of transparency, accountability, and fairness. Classic AI models will put a strong emphasis on the performance and scalability, but fail to consider the ethical and social consequences of autonomous decision-making. The present paper suggests an Ethical AI Framework of Autonomous Decision Systems, which combines fairness, explainability and human control concepts with adherence to changing legal standards. The framework consists of a multi-level design that integrates the modules of ethical reasoning, bias detection pipelines, and accountability systems to provide responsible autonomy. The proposed framework reduces the risk of unintended harm even though it maintains efficiency in the system, as normative constraints are introduced into the decision-making loop. Based on case studies and experimental validations, we have shown that the protection of ethics increases trust, decreases bias, and permits compliance with human values under complicated settings. The article helps fill the gap between technical efficiency and ethical responsibility, offering a starting point towards creating next-generation autonomous systems which are both intelligent and morally responsible.

title: Design and Simulation of Triple Band Microstrip Patch Antenna for Wireless Communication System
authors: Jenitha A, Tamil Vani R, Rishika R, Sonia M
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Radiation is pivotal in determining its performance. MPA is simpler to construct as it provides easy feeding and has low profile when contrasted with other type of antennas. it aims to create an antenna capable of operating across three distinct frequency ranges of 2.45 GHz, 4.5 GHz, and 5.725 GHz. The antenna structure is built using FR4 as the base material with the dimensions of 49.16x57.6x1.6mm3. Thus, the patch antenna system exhibits low profile and less weight which makes them heavier flexible and portable to use them in wireless applications. Ansys HFSS-23 is used for simulating the proposed antenna on the parameters of Return loss, VSWR (Voltage Standing Wave Ratio), Gain, Radiation Pattern.

title: Enhancing Real-Time Communication Security: Dual Encryption Based Web-Chat Application using Django
authors: Shivakumar M, Shreekrishna G S, Ramprasad Bharadwaj S N, Sinchana S, Shreyansh Mishra
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: This paper presents a Real-Time Secure Web-Based Chat Application developed using Django, integrating advanced encryption techniques to ensure confidential and tamper-proof communication. It employs a dual encryption approach using AES (CBC mode) and N-TEA, providing end-to-end security by encrypting messages before transmission and decrypting them only upon arrival. Unlike conventional chat applications, this system does not store chat data, preventing data footprints, breaches, or unauthorized access. Django Channels and WebSocket enable real-time messaging, ensuring seamless interaction. The application features a user-friendly interface with robust authentication mechanisms, including unique usernames for secure one-on-one chats. Additionally, the encryption process ensures resistance against man-in-the-middle (MITM) attacks and replay attacks, strengthening data integrity. The combination of strong cryptographic techniques, real-time communication, and zero-data retention policies makes this application a highly secure and efficient messaging solution for privacy-conscious users.

title: Deep Learning Method Based on Fine-Tuned ResNet50 and CT scan Imaging to Detect Kidney Diseases
authors: Vignesh Ramamoorthy. H, Shaheen Ebrahimkutty A. V, Rajeswari Periyasamy, Naresh Kumar Sripada, Ch. Mohan Sai Kumar, K N V Ramya Devi
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Kidney diseases such as cysts, stones, and tumors are among the most prevalent renal disorders, and early detection plays a crucial role in preventing complications and improving patient outcomes. Traditional diagnostic approaches, including CT-based assessments, often depend heavily on radiological expertise, which may lead to variability in interpretation and delays in diagnosis. To overcome these limitations, this study introduces a fine-tuned ResNet50 deep learning framework for automated kidney disease classification. The dataset, sourced from the open-source Kaggle platform, comprises 12,446 CT scan images categorized into four classes: cyst, normal, stone, and tumor. The proposed architecture leverages the pre-trained ResNet50 backbone for feature extraction, augmented with additional dense and dropout layers to enhance classification accuracy and reduce overfitting. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the model achieves an overall accuracy of 99%, with consistently high precision, recall, and F1-scores across all categories. Confusion matrix analysis confirms the robustness of the approach, showing minimal misclassifications, particularly in stone detection. Training and validation curves further validate stable convergence and strong generalization. The findings highlight the potential of the proposed framework as a reliable, efficient, and clinically relevant tool for automated kidney disease diagnosis

title: A Parallel Alternative for Energy-Efficient Neural Network Training and Inferencing
authors: Sudip K. Seal, Maksudul Alam, Jorge Ramirez, Sajal Dash, Hao Lu
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: Energy efficiency of training and inferencing with large neural network models is a critical challenge facing the future of sustainable large-scale machine learning workloads. This paper introduces an alternative strategy, called phantom parallelism, to minimize the net energy consumption of traditional tensor (model) parallelism, the most energy-inefficient component of large neural network training. The approach is presented in the context of feed-forward network architectures as a preliminary, but comprehensive, proof-of-principle study of the proposed methodology. We derive new forward and backward propagation operators for phantom parallelism, implement them as custom autograd operations within an end-to-end phantom parallel training pipeline and compare its parallel performance and energy-efficiency against those of conventional tensor parallel training pipelines. Formal analyses that predict lower bandwidth and FLOP counts are presented with supporting empirical results on up to 256 GPUs that corroborate these gains. Experiments are shown to deliver <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 50 \%$</tex> reduction in the energy consumed to train FFNs using the proposed phantom parallel approach when compared with conventional tensor parallel methods. Additionally, the proposed approach is shown to train smaller phantom models to the same model loss on smaller GPU counts as larger tensor parallel models on larger GPU counts offering the possibility for even greater energy savings.

title: Performance-Portable Optimization and Analysis of Multiple Right-Hand Sides in a Lattice QCD Solver
authors: Shiting Long, Gustavo Ramirez-Hidalgo, Stepan Nassyr, Jose Jimenez-Merchan, Andreas Frommer, Dirk Pleiter
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: Managing the high computational cost of iterative solvers for sparse linear systems is a known challenge in scientific computing. Moreover, scientific applications often face memory bandwidth constraints, making it critical to optimize data locality and enhance the efficiency of data transport. We extend the lattice QCD solver DD- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> AMG to incorporate multiple right-hand sides (rhs) for both the Wilson-Dirac operator evaluation and the GMRES solver, with and without odd-even preconditioning. To optimize auto-vectorization, we introduce a flexible interface that supports various data layouts and implement a new data layout for better SIMD utilization. We evaluate our optimizations on both x86 and Arm clusters, demonstrating performance portability with similar speedups. A key contribution of this work is the performance analysis of our optimizations, which reveals the complexity introduced by architectural constraints and compiler behavior. Additionally, we explore different implementations leveraging a new matrix instruction set for Arm called SME and provide an early assessment of its potential benefits.

title: Robotic Solutions for Sustainable Harvesting Using AI and IoT for Reducing Labor and Resource Waste in Farming
authors: M.Revathi, S Murugan, E.Sivanantham, R.Hemalatha, Dinesh Babu S, Jagadam Praveen Kumar
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The agriculture industry has increasing hurdles in fulfilling global food requirements while maintaining sustainability. Labor shortages and resource inefficiencies have emerged as significant issues, necessitating new solutions. This research examines the integrations of Artificial Intelligence (AI), the Internet of Things (IoT), and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) inside robotic systems for sustainable harvesting. DRL enables robots to enhance harvesting strategies by assimilating environmental data and adjusting their operations to optimize efficiency and reduce resource waste. IoT sensors provide instantaneous data on crop health, ambient conditions, and more variables, facilitating accurate and data-informed decision-making. Integrating AI and IoT enables robotic systems to execute autonomous harvesting with increased precision, lowering labor expenses, minimizing crop damage, and enhancing overall output. The proposed method emphasizes the automation of the harvesting process, guaranteeing that crops are collected at the best time while minimizing environmental effects. The system aims to enhance efficient and sustainable agricultural operations by integrating AI, IoT, and DRL. Using these technologies resolves labor-related issues, significantly contributes to resource conservation, improves crop yields, and advances sustainability in modern agriculture.

title: Reducing Fertilizer Runoff and Enhancing Crop Yield with Nano-Engineered Nutrients
authors: Nelson Lungu, Utpal Chandra De, Bibhuti Bhusan Dash, Lalbihari Barik, Tabrej Khan, Sudhansu Shekhar Patra
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The sustainability of agricultural practices depends on several types of underutilised fertilizers that are environmentally hazardous and impede agricultural productivity. Conventional fertilizers have low nutrient use efficiency, particularly in nitrogen, where losses are reported between 50 and 70% through runoff, volatilisation, and leaching. A nano-engineered complete nutrient delivery system with biodegradable polymer-coated nanoparticles for macronutrient-controlled release has been proposed in this study, with the evidence that the formulated nano-fertilizers reduce nutrients' runoff compared to conventional fertilizer applications by 65% while increasing crop yields by 28%. Nutrient cycling is optimised together with soil health indicators in multi-site field trials across diverse agricultural systems. Coating technology smartly delivers the proper nutrients at the right time, corresponding to plant growth stages for optimum absorption. Although capital costs are initially high, economic analysis demonstrates favourable cost-benefit ratios compared to traditional methods. Results indicate significantly reduced groundwater contamination and greenhouse gas emissions compared with standard fertilisation methods. This novel multi-nutrient release mechanism for simultaneous applications departs from existing controlled-release mechanisms under soil moisture conditions, pH, and temperature parameters. Statistically analysed data shows that the coefficient of variation for yield parameters has been kept below 15% over three growing seasons.

title: Research on Hybrid Modulation Strategies and Loss Balancing for Three-Level ANPC Converter
authors: Boyu Lin, Weibo Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: Active Neutral Point Clamped three-level converters find extensive application in high-power scenarios. However, under conventional modulation strategies, they suffer from severe imbalance in power dissipation and thermal generation between inner and outer tubes, thereby compromising system reliability. To address this issue, novel modulation techniques that do not increase hardware complexity require investigation. This paper provides an in-depth analysis of the operating principles and multiple typical operating modes of the ANPC three-level converter. Building upon this foundation, a hybrid modulation method is proposed that cyclically alternates and sequentially cascades two drive modulation strategies with complementary loss characteristics. This approach achieves dynamic transfer and balancing of switching device loss stresses by rotating high-loss components across different time periods. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared to conventional single-modulation strategies, the proposed hybrid modulation scheme effectively balances power dissipation between the inner and outer tubes, significantly improving the system's temperature distribution without necessitating increased switching frequencies or additional hardware. This provides an effective solution for enhancing the operational reliability and engineering applicability of high-power ANPC converters.

title: Research on Aerodynamic Interference and Flight Control of UAV in Confined Space
authors: Xutao Hu, Zhanfeng Ying, Wenbo Chen, Mingbo Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of flight stability control of four-rotor UAV in tunnel confined space, this paper establishes a aerodynamic simulation model based on CFD, and analyzes the aerodynamic interference characteristics under wall constraints. The simulation results show that the distribution of lift and rolling torque of the UAV in the tunnel section is independent of the rotor speed, and it is in good agreement with the experimental data of Monash University. On this basis, an integral backstepping position controller is designed, and its effectiveness is verified by control simulation. The results show that the control performance of the controller in hovering and trajectory tracking tasks is significantly better than that of the traditional PID controller, which provides an effective solution for the stability control of UAV in confined space.

title: A 48V High-Current Led Emergency Lighting System for Tunnel Engineering
authors: Yichen Deng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: This design presents a high-performance 48 V-to-12 V synchronous Buck switching power supply tailored for tunnel LED lighting applications. The core objectives focus on achieving high power output and efficiency, addressing the energy consumption and thermal challenges associated with prolonged continuous illumination in tunnels. The power solution employs advanced synchronous rectification technology, utilizing MOSFETs with low on-resistance and low-loss magnetic core inductors to minimize switching and conduction losses. Test results demonstrate that the power supply delivers a continuous output current of up to 30 A (corresponding to an output power of 360 W) under rated conditions, achieving a peak conversion efficiency of 95.6 %. Furthermore, the design incorporates a high-precision digital control strategy to ensure long-term stability and high reliability in the complex and harsh industrial environment of tunnels.

title: Predicting Human Cognitive Performance from Lifestyle Indicators Using LightGBM
authors: P. Sasikala, Tarun Kumar Kotteda, E. Uvarani, R. Abinaya, Sharmila S, C Srinivasan
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Human cognitive function is significantly influenced by lifestyle variables such sleep length, stress levels, screen usage, and dietary choices. This work uses the Human Cognitive Performance Analysis dataset from Kaggle, which has data from 80,000 samples, to predict cognitive performance using advanced machine learning (ML) methodologies. Using Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) for its rapidity, scalability, and improve prediction accuracy. Following meticulous preprocessing and feature engineering, our LightGBM model achieved a R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.94 and a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 3.57 in predicting standardized cognitive scores between 0 and 100. Model interpretability using SHAP values revealed that sleep length, stress levels, age, and screen time are the primary predictors. Individuals obtaining 7–9 hours of sleep, experiencing low stress, and engaging in modest screen exposure consistently had superior scores on cognitive evaluations. These results highlight the capability of interpretable ML algorithms to precisely predict cognitive function and provide practical lifestyle tips. The high- performance model establishes the groundwork for future progress.

title: Intelligent Energy-Aware Routing Framework Using Adaptive Q-Learning in Wireless Sensor Network
authors: Tarun Kumar, Dinesh Sharma, Amandeep Kaur, Aashdeep Singh
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: This Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) consist of geographically distributed sensor nodes with a limited energy capacity which gather and relay information to a main base station. They are utilized in different fields such as monitoring the environment, military operations, industrial control, and smart city technologies, and have spawned a great deal of interest and substantial research. Because sensor nodes are located in remote or difficult to access locations, replacing or recharging batteries is usually not practical. For this reason, prolonging the lifetime and reliability of the network requires the design of a smart mechanism to save energy, especially on the routing step. Using predefined rules is the backbone of static routing algorithms. This attribute makes them unsuitable for use in dynamic environments with constantly changing node energy levels and network topology. To address these constraints, this work proposes an adaptive routing algorithm based on Q-Learning. This is a type of reinforcement learning which allows each node to learn the best routing paths over time. The routing problem is treated as a learning problem where the sensor nodes take actions based on a reward function that combines residual energy, distance the node, and the number of available nodes. The method suggested here does not need any form of network architecture and can adapt to any behavioral and energy usage shifts of the nodes in real-time. This goal eliminates unnecessary transmission as well as routing through nodes with no energy while trying to balance energy consumption across the network. The self-learning at the node level through the Q-Learning routing method improves the adaptability, sustainability, and performance of the WSN in different settings thereby making the network more integrated.

title: IEEE IES Young Professionals2025 Annual Report: Inspiring Achievements of the IEEE IES Young Professionals [Students and Young Professionals News]
authors: Chathurika S. Wickramasinghe

abstract: Provides society information that may include news, reviews or technical notes that should be of interest to practitioners and researchers.

title: An Optimized Safety Protection Strategy for Backup Power Systems Based on Multi-Objective Collaborative Optimization
authors: Haowen Sun, Xuemei Zhang, Yanru Ren, Chen Li, Xudong Wang, Shuai Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: To mitigate the risk of backup power system overloads during automatic transfer switching, this paper proposes an optimized safety protection strategy based on multiobjective collaborative optimization. The methodology employs a two-stage process: first, all feasible power restoration topologies are systematically generated using graph theory, incorporating network simplification and Breadth-First Search algorithms. Second, a hybrid Genetic Algorithm-Particle Swarm Optimization (GA-PSO) is used to solve a multi-objective function—which minimizes load shedding, network losses, and switching operations under strict grid constraints—to identify the globally optimal strategy from the feasible set. Validation on a simulated 10kV distribution network under progressive load scenarios demonstrates that the strategy enables an adaptive, hierarchical decision-making process. This intelligent, tiered approach ensures operational security, significantly enhances power supply reliability, and prevents cascading failures during fault recovery.

title: Robust Secondary Control of AC Microgrids Against Unknown Disturbances via a Sliding-Mode-Based Nonlinear Observer
authors: Xiaoxuan Chen, Shihao Sun, Yuting Huang, Dianbo Chen, Yiping Han
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: As renewable penetration increases, islanded AC microgrids have become a critical interface between power systems and green energy sources. However, their voltageregulation performance is severely degraded by unknown secondary disturbances. To maintain tight voltage outputs during islanded operation, this paper proposes an observer-based robust distributed consensus strategy that integrates a sliding-modeenhanced nonlinear disturbance observer (NDO) with a sliding-surface-based secondary control law. Operating solely on measurements from neighboring units, the NDO reconstructs disturbances in finite time and feeds the estimate into the consensus protocol, achieving accurate voltage restoration without additional sensors or global communication. A distributed sliding surface guarantees finite-time convergence of both voltage and observation error, while Lyapunov analysis rigorously proves stability. Extensive MATLAB/Simulink tests under step, ramp, sinusoidal, and random disturbances show the proposed scheme reduces recovery time, suppresses steady-state error, and retains plug-and-play capability, outperforming conventional consensus methods and validating its practical applicability for resilient islanded microgrids.

title: Reducing Computational Burden in Congestion Probability Estimation Using Generalized Generation Distribution Factor
authors: Taisuke Kurosaki, Kenichi Kawabe
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: To achieve decarbonization, the application of renewable energy sources (RESs), such as wind and photovoltaic generators, is steadily increasing. However, it is inherently difficult to accurately predict the outputs of RES, which introduces significant uncertainty into electric power system operation. Forecasting errors in RESs and the subsequent activation of reserves can cause transmission line overloads, thereby posing a risk to system security. To reduce the computational burden of overload probability calculations, herein we propose a method to predict the transmission lines that can become overloaded and limit the number of scenarios to be considered in the probability calculation. The effectiveness of the proposed method was verified by numerical simulations, which demonstrated a significant reduction in computation time without compromising accuracy.

title: Spatio-Temporal Deep Learning for Accelerated Post-Disaster Power System Restoration
authors: Weili Li, Zhenwei Hu, Yi Jiang, Jing Zhao, Weixia Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Post-disaster power system restoration is a time-critical task, yet its underlying optimization problem, namely the mixed-integer linear program (MILP), which is too computationally intensive for rapid emergency decision-making. This paper proposes a spatio-temporal deep learning framework to accelerate the solution process via deep learning model reduction. We integrate a graph neural network (GNN) to learn the network's spatial topology and a long short-term memory (LSTM) network to capture the temporal dependencies of restoration sequences. The trained model predicts critical components and actions, allowing for a targeted reduction of variables and constraints in the original MILP. Validation on the IEEE 33-bus system demonstrates our method reduces computation time by over 17.3 % while maintaining near-optimal solutions with less than 1 % performance variation. This approach significantly enhances the speed and feasibility of emergency response operations.

title: Research on Anti-Maloperation Measures for Ratio Differential Protection of UHVDC Small Group AC Filters
authors: Zhixiong Huang, Hang Liu, Yunfeng Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Since the commissioning of a certain <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 800 \text{kV}$</tex> Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current (UHVDC) transmission project, multiple maloperation events of the ratio differential protection have occurred at the inverter station during the energization of C-type AC filters. To ensure the secure and stable operation of this UHVDC link and prevent further maloperations of the C-type filter ratio differential protection, this paper conducts an in-depth analysis of the causes. Based on theoretical analysis and simulation tests, it is conclusively determined that the maloperation is attributable to a significant deviation between the fundamental frequency component of the filter current extracted by the protection device at a low sampling frequency and the actual value, resulting from the influence of the high-frequency component in the switching inrush current. The anti-maloperation performance of the ratio differential protection during small group filter energization can be enhanced by measures such as increasing the sampling frequency of the protection device, raising the operating current or ratio braking coefficient, and incorporating an operating time delay.

title: Managing Charging Induced Grid Stress and Battery Degradation in Electric Taxi Fleets
authors: Michael Yuhas, Rajesh K. Ahir, Laksamana Vixell Tanjaya Hartono, Muhammad Dzaki Dwi Putranto, Arvind Easwaran, Suhono Harso Supangkat
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Operating fleets of electric vehicles (EVs) introduces several challenges, some of which are borne by the fleet operator, and some of which are borne by the power grid. To maximize short-term profit a fleet operator could always charge EVs at the maximum rate to ensure vehicles are ready to service ride demand. However, due to the stochastic nature of electricity demand, charging EVs at their maximum rate may potentially increase the grid stress and lead to overall instability. Furthermore, high-rate charging of EVs can accelerate battery degradation, thereby reducing the service lifespan of the fleet. This study aims to reconcile the conflicting incentives of fleet longevity, shortterm profitability, and grid stability by simulating a taxi fleet throughout its lifespan in relation to its charging policies and service conditions. We develop an EV fleet simulator to evaluate the battery degradation due to unpredictable charging and ride demand. Consequently, the impact on the power grid through the charging infrastructure is assessed due to these activities. This simulation utilizes publicly accessible real-world travel data from the NYC taxi dataset. We compare a baseline 80 – 20 fleet charging policy with a reinforcement learning-based policy designed to prolong the fleet's service life and alleviate grid stress. We monitor grid stress, battery degradation, and profitability over five years and find that our learned policy outperforms the baseline. This simulator enables fleet operators to assess the impact of different charging policies on these indicators to make informed decisions in the future.

title: Resilience Enhancement of Cyber-Physical Power Systems with Mobile Energy Resources
authors: Mohammad AlMuhaini
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: In recent years, power systems have undergone significant transformations due to the increased integration of information and communication technologies. Consequently, the power network is now considered as a highly interconnected cyber-physical infrastructure. Furthermore, the grid's vulnerability to extreme natural and cyber events has emerged as a critical area of research, driven by the rising frequency of such occurrences and their devastating impacts. Mobile Energy Resources (MERs), including energy storage and Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) electric vehicles, possess the capability to enhance the restoration process and reinforce resilience. This study proposes a Markov-based platform designed to model and assess the resilience of cyber-physical power systems, incorporating MER while accounting for major physical and cyber incidents. The proposed algorithm is optimized to resolve scalability challenges associated with larger systems, and the IEEE-14 bus system is utilized to illustrate the framework. Index Terms-resilience, cyber-physical, mobile resources.

authors: Ans Ahmad, Georgina Guillén, Ulla A. Saari, Liliane Carmagnac, Morgane Fritz, Ville-Veikko Uhlgren, Juho Hamari
