title: Visualization Tasks for Unlabeled Graphs
authors: Matt I. B. Oddo, Ryan Smith, Stephen Kobourov, Tamara Munzner
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: We investigate tasks that can be accomplished with unlabeled graphs, which are graphs with nodes that do not have persistent or semantically meaningful labels attached. New visualization techniques to represent unlabeled graphs have been proposed, but more understanding of unlabeled graph tasks is required before these techniques can be adequately evaluated. Some network visualization tasks apply to both labeled and unlabeled graphs, but many do not translate between these contexts. We propose a data abstraction model that distinguishes the Unlabeled context from the increasingly semantically rich Labeled, Attributed, and Augmented contexts. We filter tasks collected and gleaned from the literature according to our data abstraction and analyze the surfaced tasks, leading to a taxonomy of abstract tasks for unlabeled graphs. Our task taxonomy is organized according to the Target data under consideration, the Action intended by the user, and the Scope of the data at play. We show the descriptive power of this task abstraction by connecting to concrete examples from previous frameworks, and connecting these abstractions to real-world problems. To showcase the evaluative power of the taxonomy, we perform a preliminary assessment across 6 different network visualization idioms for each task. For each combination of task and visual encoding, we consider the effort required from viewers, the likelihood of task success, and how both factors vary between small-scale and large-scale graphs. Availability: Supplemental materials are available at osf.io/e23mr.

title: Analytical Model for Predicting Rotor Eddy Current Loss of PMSMs Considering Magnetic Saturation and Diffusion Effect
authors: Duy-Tinh Hoang, Manh-Dung Nguyen, Cheol Han, Yong-Joo Kim, Kyung-Hun Shin, Jang-Young Choi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion
abstract: Rotor eddy-current loss (RECL) in permanent-magnet synchronous machines (PMSMs) is strongly influenced by magnetic core saturation and diffusion effect (eddy-current reaction) in conducting parts. Many analytical models omit one of these, leading to errors at high load or under rich harmonics. This paper proposes a nonlinear hybrid analytical framework that couples a magnetostatic model (MSM) with a frequency-domain model (FDM) via an equivalent current-sheet (ECS) interface. The MSM computes the load-dependent magnetic field under nonlinear core permeability. Over one electrical period, the tangential field at the inner stator surface is sampled and decomposed by fast Fourier transform (FFT) into ECSs with explicit space-time orders. These ECSs are subsequently fed to the FDM, which solves the diffusion problem in conductive rotor regions, yielding per-harmonic RECL estimates. Compared with 2D finite-element analysis (FEA), the method maintains high accuracy across saturation levels and frequencies while requiring substantially lower computational effort. Experimental validation is carried out on a PMSM using a thermal-based method to infer rotor losses. The proposed model using a 3D correction shows good agreement with the test results. This model provides a transparent loss mechanism and is suitable for early-stage design studies where both saturation and diffusion are significant.

title: Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)

title: Eigenvalue Triangle: A Spectrum-Space Representation of Measurement Patterns for Correlation-Aware Network Analysis
authors: Zhuangyan Zhang, Xing He, Zhoujun Ma, Qian Ai, Robert Qiu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In active distribution networks (ADNs), operational flexibility and pervasive sensor imperfections may jointly result in non-typical, noisy, and asynchronous measurements, posing challenges to conventional correlation-aware network analysis (CANA) approaches that heavily rely on low-dimensional statistics such as pairwise Pearson correlation. To this end, this paper proposes the Eigenvalue Triangle (ET)—a geometric formulation grounded in Random Matrix Theory (RMT)—that jointly captures multi-node correlation and measurement noise within a unified high-dimensional spectrum space. By projecting the two dominant eigenvalues of the voltage correlation matrix into a bounded triangle, ET serves as a quantitative and theory-guided representation of measurement patterns, through which topology-induced correlation and signal–noise interplay can be visually and analytically associated. To handle temporal misalignment, a modified Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) algorithm is incorporated, completing the CANA framework in conjunction with ET. Case studies demonstrate that empirical ET patterns align well with theoretical predictions, validating the effectiveness of our CANA framework in capturing topology-induced correlations under noisy and asynchronous conditions. The spectrum-based ET perspective provides the conceptual and analytical basis for high-dimensional CANA beyond conventional low-dimensional methods, and enhances downstream tasks such as topology identification in ADNs.

title: Radial Eccentricity of Pole Wheels in Side-of-Shaft Rotary Magnetic Encoders
authors: Michael Ortner, Florian Slanovc, Daniel Markó, Heiko Knoll, Christian Egger, Stefan Kagerer, Yashar Mohammadi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Magnetic rotary encoders are widely used in industrial settings to measure the rotational position and speed of components like shafts and wheels. In bearingless side-of-shaft configurations, a magnetic pole wheel with alternating poles is mounted on the shaft, producing a varying magnetic field as it rotates. This field is monitored by adjacent stationary magnetic field sensors, whose output signals are used to determine the rotational state. Without mechanical fixtures, bearingless encoders enable flexible installation and non-contact measurement, but are more susceptible to radial eccentricity. Its subtle influence on the magnetic field is often underestimated—both in magnitude and complexity. This study analyzes eccentricity-induced errors in magnetic encoders, highlights the critical role of pole wheel manufacturing, and offers practical recommendations for simulating and mitigating the effects.

title: The 51st Annual Conference of the IEEE Industrial Electronics Society [Society News]
authors: Antonio Luque, Juan J. Rodriguez-Andina, Huijun Gao, Mo-Yuen Chow
publish: IEEE Industrial Electronics Magazine
abstract: Provides society information that may include news, reviews or technical notes that should be of interest to practitioners and researchers.

title: The Next-Generation Grid: Decentralized, Resilient, and Cyberphysical [Editor’s Column]
authors: Samir Kouro
publish: IEEE Industrial Electronics Magazine

title: Fault-Tolerant Fusion Framework for Robust State of Charge Estimation in Lithium-Ion Battery Packs
authors: Ruijian Yang, Zhendong Sun, Mingqiang Lin, Xingtao Liu, Ji Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries are widely adopted in electric vehicles due to the excellent safety and long cycle life. However, inconsistencies in capacity, internal resistance, and aging among individual cells are common throughout the battery lifecycle, which poses challenges for battery management systems in achieving accurate and reliable state of charge (SOC) estimation. In addition, to ensure robustness under sensor noise and abnormal conditions, fault-tolerant performance is increasingly critical for practical deployment. Here, we propose a data-driven and filter fusion-based algorithm with strong fault-tolerant capability for battery pack-level SOC estimation. First, representative cells are selected using a differential voltage method to reliably identify the cells most representative of the pack’s capacity and SOC. Subsequently, the voltage and current data from the selected cells serve as inputs to dual parallel gated recurrent unit networks, establishing a mapping between pack SOC and voltage-current characteristics. Finally, by integrating the model with a Kalman filter, a closed-loop estimation framework is constructed to suppress noise-induced deviations and enhance reliability under abnormal operating conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves accurate SOC estimation and maintains high performance under unknown operating conditions, sensor faults, and noise disturbances, offering strong fault tolerance and reliability for real-world applications.

title: Robust Blind Modulation Recognition via Entropy-based CFO-Adaptive Network
authors: Zixin Wang, Kai Chen, Hanmin Sheng, Wenjian Ma, Rui Cao, Zihan Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Automatic modulation recognition (AMR) plays a vital role in non-cooperative communication systems, yet carrier frequency offset (CFO) degrades its performance due to oscillator mismatches or Doppler shifts. While learning-based AMR methods have shown promising results under controlled conditions, they often overlook the effects of CFO. In realistic non-cooperative environments, the absence of pilot signals inevitably introduces CFO. Although blind synchronization techniques offer a potential solution, they typically introduce significant residual errors, reducing their practical effectiveness. To address this problem, we propose a novel blind compensation framework that jointly estimates and mitigates CFO while enabling end-to-end modulation recognition. The proposed method employs phase entropy minimization as an unsupervised objective to guide CFO correction and integrates an adaptive mechanism that selectively leverages uncertainty samples for online model refinement. This approach operates without prior knowledge of the signal structure, labeling, or protocol-specific parameters.

title: Design and Performance Evaluation of Single Frame Display Field Communication (SF-DFC) Based on Data Embedding Optimization
authors: Tae-min Kim, Pankaj Singh, Sung-Yoon Jung
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Display field communication (DFC) is an unobtrusive display-to-camera technology that transmits data within the frequency domain of images, ensuring that the embedded data are hidden and do not disrupt the viewing experience. The display embeds data into image frames, while the receiver captures the display and extracts it. The core of the proposed method in this paper is threefold. First, it addresses the shortcomings of existing DFC technology by eliminating the need for the reference (cover) image, similar to deep learning-based display-to-camera communication technology. This is crucial for improving data transmission efficiency. Secondly, the proposed method has relatively low algorithmic complexity compared to deep learning models, offering advantages in processing speed and making it suitable for implementation on small devices. Last, it allows for flexible adjustment of image size and can further increase data capacity through optimization. The effective embedding positions for data in real D2C environments, which are robust to AWGN, blur, and brightness noise, are identified through simulation. Furthermore, experiments conducted in real-world settings validate the effectiveness of the method by applying the BCH error correcting code. The proposed method identifies an effective embedding parameter of β<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∗</sup> = 0.13 through extensive simulations. Experimental results demonstrate that the system achieves high visual fidelity with a peak signal-to-noise ratio of 37–38 dB and an structural similarity index measure exceeding 0.93. Furthermore, the communication link remains highly reliable, maintaining a bit error rate below 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup> at transmission at distances up to 30 cm. Additionally, the data image was successfully decoded even when printed, demonstrating that it can be utilized similarly to QR codes in a print-to-camera environment.

title: Self-Adaptive Differential Evolution-Enhanced Quality-Diversity Optimization for Distributed Flexible Job-Shop Scheduling With Transportation_supp1-3670689.pdf
authors: Yi Xiang
publish: N/A
abstract: Distributed Flexible Job Shop Scheduling with Transportation Constraints (DFJSP-T) is widely used in manufacturing workshops across various industries, as it more accurately represents real-world production scenarios. However, many existing studies do not fully leverage critical problem features or domain knowledge, limiting the ability of current algorithms to find high-quality solutions for complex large-scale problems. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a Self-Adaptive Differential Evolution Enhanced Quality-Diversity Optimization (SADE-QD). The SADE-QD incorporates domain knowledge in three main ways: (1) it models the problem using machine idle and job transportation features, allowing the algorithm to retain solutions with diverse behaviors; (2) it introduces a knowledge-guided heuristic search strategy based on critical path to discover more high-quality solutions; and (3) it applies an self-adaptive differential evolution search method that leverages machine idle and transportation features to explore a broader range of solutions. This helps valuable information from the feature space contribute directly to the search process. Experiments on small, medium, and large scale benchmarks show that SADE-QD achieves an average makespan reduction of 12% compared to several recent state-of-the-art algorithms.

title: Design and Evaluation of a User Mental Model Driven Interface for Thermostatic Milk Warmers_supp2-3675743.pdf
authors: Long Chen
publish: N/A

title: Underdetermined Blind Source Separation via Weighted Simplex Shrinkage Regularization and Quantum Deep Image Prior_supp1-3673957.pdf
authors: Chia-Hsiang Lin
publish: N/A
abstract: As most optical satellites remotely acquire multispectral images (MSIs) with limited spatial resolution, multispectral unmixing (MU) becomes a critical signal processing technology for analyzing the pure material spectra for high-precision classification and identification. Unlike the widely investigated hyperspectral unmixing (HU) problem, MU is much more challenging as it corresponds to the underdetermined blind source separation (BSS) problem, where the number of sources is larger than the number of available multispectral bands. In this article, we transform MU into its overdetermined counterpart (i.e., HU) by inventing a radically new quantum deep image prior (QDIP), which relies on the virtual band-splitting task conducted on the observed MSI for generating the virtual hyperspectral image (HSI). Then, we perform HU on the virtual HSI to obtain the virtual hyperspectral sources. Though HU is overdetermined, it still suffers from the ill-posed issue, for which we employ the convex geometry structure of the HSI pixels to customize a weighted simplex shrinkage (WSS) regularizer to mitigate the ill-posedness. Finally, the virtual hyperspectral sources are spectrally downsampled to obtain the desired multispectral sources. The proposed geometry/quantum-empowered MU (GQ-μ) algorithm can also effectively obtain the spatial abundance distribution map for each source, where the geometric WSS regularization is adaptively and automatically controlled based on the sparsity pattern of the abundance tensor. Simulation and real-world data experiments demonstrate the practicality of our unsupervised GQ-μ algorithm for the challenging MU task. Ablation study demonstrates the strength of QDIP, not achieved by classical DIP, and validates the mechanics-inspired WSS geometry regularizer. The associated code will be available at https://github.com/IHCLab/GQ-mu.

title: Enhancing Microservice Performance: A Hybrid Approach using Caching and Batching Techniques
authors: P. Santhosh Kumar, T. Naresh, K Vinod Kumar, N. Ramesh Babu, K.Hari Babu, S.Riyaz Banu
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Nowadays, microservices play a major role in industries by enabling modular and scalable software design through inter-service communication. However, traditional communication methods—typically involving JSON-based API calls—can lead to high latency, increased network traffic, and redundant database queries. These inefficiencies become more prominent when services repeatedly request the same data or make multiple small API calls rather than batched requests. To address these issues, this paper explores two key optimization techniques: caching and batching, along with their combined effect. The objective is to analyze how these techniques individually and together improve performance in microservice-based systems. We conducted experiments under four scenarios: no optimization, caching only, batching only, and caching combined with batching. The implementation was carried out using Spring Boot, Redis for caching, and JPA for batch data access. The results demonstrate that caching alone reduces response time by up to 98.31%, batching alone by approximately 86.97%, and the hybrid approach of caching with batching offers consistent low latency with minimal database load. This confirms that a combined caching and batching strategy provides the most efficient and scalable solution for optimizing inter-service communication in microservices.

title: LegalIP-Detect: A Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Evaluating Patent Violations in E-Commerce Startup Ecosystem
authors: Preetha Rajesh, Aswathy Prakash G, Murugan Ramu, Dr.P.Shunmugapriya, Reshma Soman N, Dr.Thankamani.k
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Patent infringement detection continue issues are subject to severe restrictions, especially for the case of E-commerce Startup. Most importantly, the semantic interpretation of the legal and technical terms in the patents, is still an open issue for many AI models. Furthermore, human assessments of patent similarity are time-consuming and perspectives highly subjected and imperfect, which can lead to delayed and varied infringement interpretations. The lack of large, high quality annotated datasets also limits advancement of supervised learning techniques. The unique nature of innovation in e-commerce startups requires a composite similarity based on semantic, technical and bibliographic data. The objective of this study is to introduce a new Hybrid Deep Learning (LegalIP-Detect) Model for the E-commerce startup Ecosystem for efficient detection and assessment of patent infringement. The primary aim is to blend semantic similarity measures from state-of-the-art transformer-based NLP models with technical similarity obtained from IPC code co-occurrences and bibliographic information such as citations, inventor and assignee details. Through combining multimodal inputs in a composite weighted architecture, the system aims to improve accuracy compared to traditional mono-modal methods, taking into consideration the atypical innovation variance and patent diversity characteristic to E-commerce start-ups. The overall accuracy of the proposed LegalIP-Detect model is 99.20%.

title: Glove Based Low-Cost Braille Writer and Translator with Voice Feedback for Visually Impaired People
authors: Jesudas J., Vijaya Geetha R
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: Braille is important means for visually challenged persons. It is a famous writing system for blind or visually impaired peoples. This paper defines a new method for Braille writer with translator for visually challenged people with voice. It converts a document in Braille language to a text in English with voice alert. Braille writer contains microcontroller with many inactive components. To design such type of system to solve the problems exist in existing system. This paper also describes implementation of Braille to English text translator, which converts the Braille into the text format in English with voice. The proposed system is based on comparing the Braille dot position, conversion of Braille into text and voice from generated database for each Braille cell. As compared with existing system, this system has many advantages.

title: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Cloud Key Management Systems
authors: Somasundaram K, M. Gomathy Nayagam, M. Amanullah, S. Erana Veerappa Dinesh, Suresh Thangakrishnan M
publish: 2025 2nd Asian Conference on Intelligent Technologies (ACOIT)
abstract: The evolving potential disaster of quantum computing against the classical cryptographic methods requires the invention of immune post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) systems to cloud based key management system (CKMS). The proposed solution throughout this paper is considering the risk of existing public-key infrastructures when used in a cloud computing setup by incorporating lattice based post-quantum techniques i.e CRYSTALS-Kyber and CRYSTALS-Dilithium into cloud key management system structures. We came up with a prototype and simulated a hybrid CKMS structure encompassing classical and PQC protocols to secure backward contact and forward confidentiality. Benchmarking provides evidence that the PQC-enhanced CKMS registers 98.7 percent decrease of vulnerability to quantum attack vectors; its key generation time credit stays at 1.24 ms, and time to distribute keys at 5.6 ms, which is equivalent to RSA-2048 based systems. Comparative analysis provided an increase in computational efficiency by 34 percent and a decrease of bandwidth overhead by 22 percent using batch key encapsulation optimization. We have found that the given architecture does not only maintain the scalability of cloud environments but also implements long-term cryptographic resilience. The results show the feasibility and the strength of PQC on the integration into the existing cloud key management infrastructures.

title: Multi-Objective Loss Balancing in Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Fluid Flow Applications
authors: Afrah Farea, Saiful Khan, Mustafa Serdar Celebi
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data, and Analytics (HiPC)
abstract: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) have emerged as a promising machine learning approach for solving partial differential equations (PDEs). However, PINNs face significant challenges in balancing multi-objective losses, as multiple competing loss terms such as physics residuals, boundary conditions, and initial conditions must be appropriately weighted. While various loss balancing schemes have been proposed, they have been implemented within neural network architectures with fixed activation functions, and their effectiveness has been assessed using simpler PDEs. We hypothesize that the effectiveness of loss balancing schemes depends not only on the balancing strategy itself, but also on the loss function design and the neural network's inherent function approximation capabilities, which are influenced by the choice of activation function. In this paper, we extend existing solutions by incorporating trainable activation functions within the neural network architecture and evaluate the proposed approach on complex fluid flow applications modeled by the Navier-Stokes equations. Our evaluation across diverse Navier-Stokes problems demonstrates that this proposed solution achieves root mean square error (RMSE) improvements ranging from 7.4 % to 95.2 % across different scenarios. These findings highlight the importance of carefully designing the loss function and selecting activation functions for effective loss balancing.

title: A Novel Method for Calculating the Vulnerability Area of Voltage Dip in Distribution Networks Considering DG
authors: Zheng Yang, Yunjing Liu, Lizhong Lu, Xiaozhuo Guan, Zaifeng Li, Tiebin Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: Accurately characterizing the vulnerability area of voltage dip (VAOVD) is an essential component of voltage-dip assessment. In view of the insufficient computational accuracy and low efficiency of conventional area of vulnerability (AOV) calculation methods when considering the distributed generation (DG), this paper proposes a boundary-solving method for the AOV based on Kron reduction combined with an “optimization-iteration” scheme. First, by applying symmetrical component method, the AOV boundary equations under different fault types are derived. Then the distribution system is partitioned into dip subsystems and the AOV boundaries of each subsystem are solved using a Kron reduction combined “optimization-iteration” strategy. Finally, calculations of AOV on the IEEE 33-bus system verify the accuracy and high efficiency of the proposed method.

title: A High-Speed Low-Noise Fully Differential Operational Amplifier with Symmetric Switched-Capacitor Common-Mode Feedback
authors: Wenyi Zhang, Yingtao Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a high-speed, low-noise fully differential operational amplifier (opamp) featuring a symmetric switched-capacitor common-mode feedback (SC-CMFB) circuit. The proposed two-stage architecture consists of an input stage with a linear MOSFET CMFB for precise common-mode stabilization and an output stage employing a telescopic cascode amplifier integrated with the symmetric SC-CMFB. Key innovations include the complementary symmetric SC-CMFB structure, which doubles convergence speed and stabilizes load capacitance. Simulations yield a typical open-loop gain of 78 dB, gain-bandwidth product (GBW) of 470 MHz, input voltage noise below <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \text{nV} / \sqrt{\text{Hz}}$</tex> at 1 kHz and below <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \text{nV} / \sqrt{\text{Hz}}$</tex> at 1 MHz, power supply rejection ratio (PSRR) exceeding 58 dB at low frequencies, and slew rates (SR) surpassing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$470 ~\mathrm{V} / \mu \mathrm{s}$</tex> (rising) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$830 ~\mathrm{V} / \mu \mathrm{s}$</tex> (falling).

title: Design of Fast Interlock Control System for Quench Protection of ITER Magnet Cold Test Bench
authors: Haobin Zhan, Zhiquan Song, Guanghong Wang, Hua Li, Jie Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: The quench protection system provides a timely and effective protection mechanism for superconducting magnets, avoiding damage to the coils caused by untimely energy released when the magnet quenches. This paper presents the detailed design of the fast interlock control system for the quench protection of the ITER Magnet Cold Test Bench (MCTB). The control system scheme based on the NI CompactRIO platform is presented, and the control tasks are allocated to the real-time processor. The LabVIEW development environment is adopted, and the real-time module is leveraged to achieve hundreds of microsecond-level sampling and millisecond-level control response. The host computer communicates with the NI controller through the realtime system. Meanwhile, an optimized parallel non-preemptive priority scheduling algorithm is proposed. When receiving tasks with different priorities, this algorithm allocates resources according to priorities for fast response without affecting the program execution speed. A two-layer hierarchical architecture and redundancy voting mechanism are also applied in the control system, which is equipped with a dedicated diagnostic module for real-time system monitoring and fault diagnosis. The designed control system with high Safety Integrity Level (SIL) can meet the millisecond-level action and reliability requirements of the quench protection unit. Experimental results indicate that the system can respond to quench events and execute protection actions within hundreds of microseconds, demonstrate good real-time performance and reliability.

title:
SO
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    Concentration Prediction at Desulfurization Outlet Based on TA-LSTM
authors: Bo Peng, Xiuyong Zhao, Hui Yi, Fahua Zhu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Control Engineering (ICPECE)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of inaccurate prediction of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SO}_{2}$</tex> concentration at the outlet of the desulfurization system in thermal power plants due to its characteristics of large lag and strong disturbance, this paper proposes a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SO}_{2}$</tex> concentration prediction method at the desulfurization system outlet based on VMD-TA-LSTM. First, the DTW (Dynamic Time Warping) algorithm is used to calculate the delay variables between various features, and relevant features are selected through CIFE (Conditional Mutual Information Feature Selection). Then, VMD (Variational Mode Decomposition) is used to decompose the signal to eliminate high-frequency noise. Finally, the processed reconstructed data is put into the TA-LSTM model for prediction. The experimental results show that this model effectively solves the problem of inaccurate prediction caused by system lag and eliminates the impact of high-frequency noise in the original data on prediction performance. In addition, compared with other prediction models, MAE and RMSE are all improved, verifying that the model has higher prediction accuracy and better generalization ability.

title: Smart Grid Enabled Day-Ahead Energy Market (DAEM) Model for Customer-Owned Distributed Energy Resources
authors: Sai Krovvidi, Saifur Rahman, Yonael Teklu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The widespread implementation of smart grid infrastructure, combined with the extensive adoption of customerowned distributed energy resources (DER), is establishing a novel marketplace for electricity transactions. In this environment, consumers transition into active participants with the ability to select their energy providers. This study introduces a Day-Ahead Energy Market (DAEM) model facilitated by the smart grid, designed to support these transactions by integrating specific features of this emerging market. This paper makes two primary contributions: firstly, it outlines the DAEM framework and details electricity trading algorithms that utilize a new concept known as the buyer's Priority of Demand (PoD). Secondly, it details the realization of this market model using a MATLAB-based graphical user interface to verify the suggested concepts and trading logic. We anticipate that this market model will encourage the expansion of customer or community-owned distributed generation (DG), ultimately enhancing the reliability and power quality of the local grid.

title: Enhancement of Frequency Stabilization Capability of GFM Converters During Generation Loss Using Wide-Area Control Systems
authors: Tomoki Yokoi, Kenichi Kawabe, Yusuke Takarada
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: This study investigates the limitations of batterybased energy storage systems used as Grid-forming converters when the fault detection relies solely on the local voltage measurements. Using an RMS simulation of a generator trip in a multi-machine power system, the authors show that some converters fail to detect nearby faults or detect them with significant delays, resulting in insufficient contributions to frequency stabilization. The proposed approach incorporates a wide-area control system that transmits fault information to converters shortly after an event. The simulation results demonstrate that this approach enables timely emergency control actions, improving the frequency nadir and overall short-term frequency stability. Even when communication delays of up to 0.5 s are considered, improvements are observed if the delay is shorter than the time to the frequency nadir. The findings highlight that combining the local measurements with wide-area control signals enhances the reliability of the frequency support from Grid-forming converters and mitigates the risk of non-detection or delayed response in high renewable penetration grids.

title: Research on the Structure and Control Technology of Diesel Storage Combined Uninterruptible Power Supply System
authors: Cunping Wang, Yanxia Chen, Ruoxi Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: In response to the problem of slow cold start process of diesel generators, which may cause power interruption for electricity users, this paper proposed a supercapacitor based diesel storage combined uninterruptible power supply system structure. A power electronic device was installed between the diesel generator and the load, and it mainly includes a supercapacitor grid connected system and a thyristor. The supercapacitors were used to supply power to the load during the cold start process of diesel generators, thereby avoiding power interruption issues and achieving seamless switching between different power supplies. Besides, a joint control strategy for DC/DC converters and DC/AC inverters was proposed with the goal of maintaining stable DC bus voltage and outputting constant voltage and frequency AC voltage. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed system structure and control strategy were verified through simulation analysis.

title: Economic Dispatch of Integrated Energy Systems Coordinated with Communication Networks
authors: Rui Ma, Can Li, Jianing Niu, Zejing Guang, Xiaoxi Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: The increasing reliance on information and communication technology (ICT) for controlling complex integrated energy systems (IES) necessitates a paradigm shift from decoupled to coordinated cyber-physical dispatch. This paper proposes a holistic co-optimization framework for the economic dispatch of an IES, explicitly integrating the operational dynamics of electricity and heat networks with a performance-aware model of the communication network. The core innovation lies in modeling the communication network not as a static load, but as a dispatchable entity, where its power consumption is dynamically scheduled based on a quantitative trade-off model between energy use and Quality of Service (QoS), specifically end-to-end latency. The resulting large-scale Mixed-Integer Second-Order Cone Program (MISOCP) is solved to global optimality by leveraging SOCP relaxation and a branch-and-cut algorithm. Case studies conducted on a modified IEEE 33-bus power grid and a 32-node district heating network demonstrate the proposed model's effectiveness. Results show that the coordinated dispatch strategy achieves a 4.84% reduction in total operational cost compared to a conventional decoupled approach by intelligently managing the cyber-physical trade-offs. This confirms that treating communication as a flexible resource unlocks economic benefits and enhances overall system efficiency.

title: Application of Behavioral Economics Theory in the Design of National “Dual Carbon” Energy Policies
authors: Ye Feng, Jingyu Zhou, Yanzuo Chen, Shuhong Wu, Danhuang Dong, Junjia Yang, Yan Chai, Junchao Shi
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies - Asia (ISGT Asia)
abstract: To achieve the “dual carbon” goals, this paper constructs a “Behavior-Policy-Emissions reduction” (B-P-E) dynamic model based on behavioral economics theory. Through surveys of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 2 0 0}$</tex> households and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 1 2}$</tex> enterprises, key behavioral characteristics such as loss aversion, hyperbolic discounting, and social norm following are identified. The study finds that neglecting behavioral biases leads to a reduction in the effectiveness of traditional policies by approximately 10 % compared to rational expectations. Verified through multi-agent simulation, two types of behaviorally compatible policies - the synergy of “social norms-economic incentives” and the combination of “risk mitigation-signal guidance”-can enhance policy effectiveness by 17-43 percentage points. This research provides computable and simulatable methodological support and pathway references for promoting “dual carbon” policies from a “one-size-fits-all” approach towards “precision” policymaking.

title: Economic Model Predictive LFC Based on Dynamic Memory Event-Triggered Mechanism for Smart Grids With Bounded Disturbances
authors: Fang Yu, Xuanyu Zhao, Xiongbo Wan, Xing-Chen Shangguan, Leimin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: This article develops a robust economic model predictive control (EMPC) scheme for load frequency control (LFC) in multiarea smart grids with load disturbances. The proposed approach integrates the optimization of the cost and the utilization efficiency of network resources within a single EMPC framework. An economic cost function, including the generation cost and the LFC cost, is designed to minimize the involved costs. To optimize the utilization efficiency of network resources while maintaining satisfactory control performance, a new dynamic memory event-triggered mechanism (DMETM) is designed. By introducing a dynamic variable and an adaptively adjusting variable, the proposed DMETM adaptively adjusts triggering conditions using historical triggering information, thereby conserving network resources and reducing communication burden. A “min–max” EMPC optimization problem (OP) is developed, and it is transformed into an auxiliary OP based on linear matrix inequalities. The recursive feasibility of the auxiliary OP is proved, and the closed-loop system is also proven to satisfy input-to-state practical stability, guaranteeing robustness against the load disturbances. At last, a case study on a three-area smart grids verifies the effectiveness and the advantages of the proposed DMETM-based EMPC scheme and demonstrates its potential in enhancing the optimization of the economic cost and the utilization efficiency of network resources.
